  reference_genome_version varchar [note: 'Version of the reference genome used for read alignment and coverage computation (e.g., GRCh38, GRCh37/hg19, GRCm39). Critical for reproducibility and cross-sample comparability of coverage statistics. Must match the reference used in downstream variant calling pipelines.']
  resequencing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this library has been flagged for resequencing due to insufficient coverage or QC failure. When True, the library must be re-run on the sequencer before downstream bioinformatics analysis can proceed.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the manual QC review of this coverage statistic record was completed. Supports audit trail requirements under CLIA, CAP, and GxP regulations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the laboratory scientist or bioinformatician who performed the manual QC review of this coverage statistic record. Required for clinical assays under CLIA and CAP accreditation to maintain an auditable review trail.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this coverage statistic record originated. Supports data lineage and provenance tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Typically Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub for automated pipeline outputs or LabVantage LIMS for manually entered records.']
  stat_identifier varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for this coverage statistic record, following the COVSTAT-{alphanumeric} naming convention. Used for cross-system referencing and audit trail purposes in LIMS and BaseSpace.']
  target_region_bed_file varchar [note: 'Name or path of the BED file defining the target genomic regions used for on-target coverage calculations. Applicable for WES and targeted sequencing assays. Ensures coverage metrics are computed against the correct capture or amplicon panel definition.']
  target_region_size_bp varchar [note: 'Total size of the target genomic region in base pairs as defined by the BED file. Used as the denominator for coverage depth calculations. For WGS, this represents the effective genome size used in analysis.']
  total_reads varchar [note: 'Total number of sequencing reads generated for this library after demultiplexing. Represents the raw read count before any filtering or alignment steps. Used to assess sequencing yield and library loading efficiency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this coverage statistic record was last modified, such as after a QC review decision or recomputation. Supports audit trail requirements under GxP and ISO 13485. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory use classification of the assay producing this coverage statistic. RUO (Research Use Only), IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), or LDT (Laboratory Developed Test). Determines applicable regulatory reporting and QC requirements under FDA, CLIA, and IVDR frameworks.']

  Note: 'Transactional record storing per-library sequencing coverage statistics computed post-demultiplexing. Captures mean coverage depth (X), median coverage depth, coverage uniformity (%), fraction of bases at ≥10X, ≥20X, ≥30X, ≥100X thresholds, on-target rate (% for targeted/WES), duplication rate (%), insert size mean and standard deviation, GC bias metric, and coverage QC pass/fail against assay-specific thresholds. Enables assessment of whether a library meets minimum coverage requirements for downstream bioinformatics analysis.'
}

Table sequencing.index_set {
  index_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the sequencing index set record in the Genomics Biotech lakehouse. Primary key for the index_set master resource.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Index kits are batch-managed. Link enables lot traceability for quality investigations (index hopping, demux failures), recall management, expiry tracking, and GMP documentation. Removes denormalized ']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Index/adapter sets are sold as catalog items with specific SKUs. Critical for ordering, compatibility validation with library prep kits, regulatory documentation, and ensuring correct product versions']
  coa_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the Certificate of Analysis (COA) document associated with this index set lot, stored in Veeva Vault or MasterControl. Provides traceability to quality release documentation including purity, concentration, and sequence verification results.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Index kits are materials. Link enables inventory management, demand planning based on multiplexing forecasts, MRP planning, and supply chain integration for procurement and stock level optimization.']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Index sets can be nonconforming (failed validation, incorrect sequences, manufacturing defect, contamination). Labs track NCRs for supplier quality management, lot disposition, and regulatory reportin']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Index kit supplier tracking. Enables supplier quality management for index-related issues (index hopping, low demux rates), performance evaluation, and supplier qualification. Removes denormalized man']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Index sets are validated for use in multiplexed sequencing (cross-contamination testing, demultiplexing accuracy, color balance verification). Labs link index sets to validation studies for regulatory']
  adapter_sequence_read1 varchar [note: 'Full adapter sequence flanking the i7 index on the Read 1 side, used for adapter trimming in bioinformatics preprocessing pipelines. Stored in 5\'→3\' orientation per IUPAC convention.']
  adapter_sequence_read2 varchar [note: 'Full adapter sequence flanking the i5 index on the Read 2 side, used for adapter trimming in paired-end bioinformatics preprocessing pipelines. Null for single-end or single-index configurations.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the index set was formally approved for production use by the sequencing operations quality authority. Null if not yet approved. Triggers availability for pool design and run scheduling.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle and approval status of the index set within Genomics Biotech sequencing operations. \'pending_validation\' = under internal validation; \'validated\' = technically validated but not yet formally approved; \'approved\' = approved for production use; \'deprecated\' = superseded by a newer set, no new runs; \'retired\' = fully decommissioned.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the quality authority or sequencing operations manager who granted formal production approval for this index set. Required for GxP and ISO 13485 audit trails.']
  basespace_index_set_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned to this index set within Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub. Enables direct cross-reference between the Genomics Biotech lakehouse master record and the operational sequencing data management platform for run configuration and demultiplexing.']
  color_balance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the index set provides adequate color (fluorescence channel) balance across all sequencing cycles on two-channel or four-channel chemistry instruments. \'balanced\' = no spike-in required; \'unbalanced\' = color imbalance detected; \'requires_spike_in\' = PhiX or other spike-in control is mandatory. Prevents base-calling failures on patterned flow cells.']
  compatible_instruments varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sequencing instrument models validated for use with this index set (e.g., \'NovaSeq 6000, NextSeq 550, MiSeq\'). Ensures that run configuration and read cycle parameters are compatible with the index chemistry.']
  compatible_library_prep_kits varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of library preparation kit names or catalogue numbers validated for use with this index set (e.g., \'TruSeq DNA PCR-Free, TruSeq Stranded mRNA, Nextera DNA Flex\'). Managed by sequencing operations to prevent incompatible kit-index pairings during run setup.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this index set record was first created in the Genomics Biotech lakehouse data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GxP record integrity requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date on which the index set was deprecated or retired from active use. Null if still active. Used to enforce that no new sequencing runs are configured with deprecated index sets and to support historical traceability.']
  gmp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the index set was manufactured under Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) conditions as required for IVD or clinical use. True = GMP-manufactured; False = non-GMP (RUO grade). Drives quality management and supplier qualification requirements.']
  i5_sequence varchar [note: 'Nucleotide sequence of the i5 (read 2 side) index used in dual-index configurations. Null or empty for single-index sets. Stored as a representative or consensus sequence; individual per-sample i5 sequences are tracked at the library level.']
  i7_sequence varchar [note: 'Nucleotide sequence of the i7 (read 1 side) index used for sample identification during demultiplexing. Stored as a representative or consensus sequence for the set; individual per-sample i7 sequences are tracked at the library level. Validated against IUPAC nucleotide codes.']
  index_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unique index sequences (barcodes) available in the set. Determines the maximum multiplexing capacity — i.e., the maximum number of samples that can be pooled in a single sequencing run using this index set.']
  index_length_bp varchar [note: 'Length of the index sequence in base pairs (bp), typically 6, 8, 10, or 12 bp. Determines the read cycle configuration on the sequencer and affects the number of unique indices available in the set. Must match the sequencing run setup parameters.']
  index_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the indexing strategy used in the set. \'single\' = single-end index (i7 only); \'dual\' = dual-index (i7 + i5, may be combinatorial); \'UDI\' = Unique Dual Index (each sample has a unique i7+i5 pair, preventing index hopping); \'combinatorial\' = combinatorial dual index (i7 and i5 drawn from independent pools). Critical for demultiplexing configuration and index-hopping risk assessment.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Regulatory intended use classification for the index set. \'RUO\' = Research Use Only; \'IVD\' = In Vitro Diagnostic; \'LDT\' = Laboratory Developed Test; \'research\' = internal research; \'clinical\' = clinical genomics applications; \'agricultural\' = agricultural genomics; \'both\' = dual RUO and clinical use. Governs which workflows and regulatory submissions this index set may support. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RUO|IVD|LDT|research|clinical|agricultural|both — promote to reference product]']
  lims_index_set_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this index set in LabVantage LIMS, the system of record for sample tracking and laboratory workflow management. Enables traceability between the lakehouse master record and LIMS-managed library preparation and pool design workflows.']
  max_multiplex_samples varchar [note: 'Maximum number of samples that can be pooled (multiplexed) in a single sequencing run using this index set while maintaining the minimum Hamming distance threshold. Derived from the validated index set design; used in pool design planning and run capacity management.']
  min_hamming_distance varchar [note: 'The minimum Hamming distance between any two index sequences within the set. A higher value (typically ≥3) reduces the risk of index misassignment during demultiplexing. Critical quality parameter for pool design validation and collision prevention.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for sequencing operations scientists to record additional context, known limitations, special handling instructions, or validation observations for this index set that are not captured in structured fields.']
  phix_spike_in_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recommended percentage of PhiX control library to spike into the pool when using this index set, to correct for color imbalance or low-diversity libraries. Expressed as a percentage of total library pool volume (e.g., 1.00, 5.00, 20.00). Zero if no spike-in is required.']
  read_orientation varchar [note: 'Specifies the orientation in which the i5 index is read on the sequencer. Critical for correct demultiplexing on instruments with different flow cell orientations (e.g., NovaSeq v1.5 flow cells require reverse complement of i5 vs. standard orientation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: forward|reverse_complement|forward_reverse|reverse_complement_forward — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_clearance_number varchar [note: 'FDA 510(k) clearance number, CE-IVD certificate number, or equivalent regulatory authorization number if the index set is part of a cleared or approved IVD product. Null for RUO-only index sets. Required for regulatory submissions and audit trails.']
  sequencing_chemistry varchar [note: 'The sequencing chemistry type for which this index set is designed. Determines color balance requirements and base-calling algorithm compatibility. \'two_channel\' = Illumina two-channel SBS (e.g., NextSeq, NovaSeq); \'four_channel\' = Illumina four-channel SBS (e.g., MiSeq, HiSeq); \'ion_semiconductor\' = Ion Torrent; \'nanopore\' = Oxford Nanopore; \'other\' = other chemistry.']
  set_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code or catalogue number assigned to the index set by the manufacturer or internally by sequencing operations. Used as the business key for cross-system lookups and BOM references in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  set_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the sequencing index set as defined by the manufacturer or sequencing operations team (e.g., \'Illumina TruSeq CD Index Set A\', \'IDT xGen UDI-UMI Adapters\'). Used as the primary display label in library preparation workflows and pool design tools.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-specified shelf life of the index set reagents in months from the date of manufacture. Used for expiry tracking, inventory management, and ensuring reagents are not used beyond their validated stability window.']
  sop_reference varchar [note: 'Document number or identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing the use, handling, and storage of this index set. Links to the controlled document in MasterControl or Veeva Vault. Ensures procedural compliance during library preparation.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Required storage temperature for the index set reagents in degrees Celsius (e.g., -20.0, 4.0). Critical for reagent integrity, shelf-life compliance, and cold-chain management in the laboratory.']
  superseded_by_set_code varchar [note: 'Set code of the newer index set that replaces this one when it is deprecated. Enables migration guidance for sequencing operations teams transitioning from legacy to current index chemistries. Null if not superseded.']
  umi_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the index set includes Unique Molecular Identifiers (UMIs) integrated into the adapter design. UMIs enable PCR duplicate removal and error correction in downstream bioinformatics pipelines, critical for low-input and cfDNA applications.']
  umi_length_bp varchar [note: 'Length of the UMI sequence in base pairs when UMIs are included in the index set design (e.g., 8, 12 bp). Null if umi_included is False. Required for configuring UMI extraction steps in bioinformatics pipelines.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this index set record in the Genomics Biotech lakehouse. Tracks the last modification event for change control, data freshness monitoring, and GxP audit trail compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  validated_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the sequencing operations scientist or engineer who performed and signed off on the internal technical validation of this index set. Supports GxP audit trail requirements.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the index set completed internal technical validation by Genomics Biotech sequencing operations, confirming that demultiplexing performance, Hamming distance, and color balance meet acceptance criteria. Null if validation is pending.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the index set definition, following semantic versioning convention (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.1\', \'v1.0.3\'). Incremented when index sequences, Hamming distance parameters, or compatibility matrices are updated. Supports change control and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Controlled reference master for sequencing index (barcode) sets used in library multiplexing. Captures index set name, manufacturer, index type (single/dual/UDI/combinatorial), i7 and i5 sequences, index length (bp), compatible library prep kits and instruments, Hamming distance from nearest neighbor in the set, color balance properties, and approval status. Managed by sequencing operations to prevent index collisions during pool design and ensure reliable demultiplexing. Updated when new index chemistries are validated.'
}

Table sequencing.sequencing_protocol {
  sequencing_protocol_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the sequencing protocol record. Primary key for the sequencing_protocol data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: GxP-regulated sequencing protocols require documented approval by qualified personnel for regulatory submissions and CLIA/CAP compliance. Distinct from protocol_owner. Real process: protocol validatio']
  approved_kit_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Validated sequencing protocols specify approved/compatible kit catalog items. Essential for SOP compliance, regulatory submissions (FDA/IVDR), quality system requirements, and ensuring only validated ']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Changes to sequencing protocols (parameter updates, new applications, cycle count changes) require change control in GxP environments. Labs link protocol changes to change control records for validati']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.controlled_document. Business justification: Sequencing protocols ARE controlled documents in GxP environments. Every protocol must link to its document record for version control, training tracking, change control, and regulatory compliance. Fu']
  gene_annotation_track_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_annotation_track. Business justification: Protocols for RNA-seq and targeted gene panels specify which annotation version to use. Critical for protocol standardization, regulatory compliance, and ensuring consistent gene-level analysis across']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Sequencing protocols specify which reference genome build to use for alignment and analysis. Regulatory validation is build-specific; this link ensures protocol compliance and supports regulatory subm']
  genomic_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genomic_region. Business justification: Targeted sequencing protocols define the specific genomic regions to be captured. Essential for protocol validation, regulatory submissions, and ensuring all runs following protocol target the correct']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Sequencing protocols specify approved materials (reagent kits, consumables) for GMP compliance. Link enables procurement to validate material purchases against approved protocols and supports MRP plan']
  mdm_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.mdm_policy. Business justification: Sequencing protocols must comply with master data management policies (GxP validation requirements, CLIA quality standards, data retention mandates) - regulatory alignment requirement. Policies govern']
  metadata_schema_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.metadata_schema. Business justification: Sequencing protocols define required metadata fields (sample sheet structure, run parameters, QC thresholds) that must conform to standardized schemas for FAIR compliance, regulatory submissions, and ']
  rd_capitalization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.rd_capitalization. Business justification: Validated sequencing protocols achieving technical feasibility and regulatory approval represent capitalized R&D assets. Links protocol development milestones to capitalization decisions for accurate ']
  sop_document_id varchar [note: 'Document control identifier for the approved SOP governing execution of this sequencing protocol, as managed in MasterControl or Veeva Vault. Provides a direct link from the protocol record to the procedural document for GxP audit trails and training compliance.']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Sequencing protocols are validated through validation studies (method validation, protocol verification, performance qualification). Each protocol version must link to its validation study for regulat']
  variant_database_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.variant_database. Business justification: Clinical sequencing protocols specify which variant databases to use for interpretation (regulatory requirement). Essential for protocol validation, regulatory submissions, and ensuring consistent cli']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the protocol version received final approval from the qualified approver. Distinct from effective_date (when it becomes operational). Required for GxP audit trails and regulatory submissions. Captured from MasterControl electronic signature timestamp.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sequencing protocol within the GxP change control workflow. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Only protocols in \'approved\' status may be used for clinical or regulated sequencing runs. Managed in Veeva Vault and MasterControl.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the qualified person (QP) or scientific director who granted final approval for this protocol version in the GxP change control system. Supports regulatory audit trail requirements. Captured from MasterControl or Veeva Vault electronic signature records.']
  approved_library_prep_kits varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of approved library preparation kit names and catalog numbers validated for use with this sequencing protocol (e.g., \'Illumina DNA Prep (20018704), TruSeq DNA PCR-Free (20015963)\'). Only kits on this list may be used in GxP-regulated runs. Validated in Benchling and controlled in MasterControl.']
  cap_accredited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this protocol is covered under a CAP-accredited laboratory\'s scope of accreditation. True = included in CAP accreditation scope; False = not within CAP scope. Relevant for clinical genomics laboratories reporting patient results.']
  clia_validated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this protocol has been validated under CLIA requirements for clinical laboratory use. True = CLIA validation completed and documented; False = not CLIA validated (RUO or research only). Required for protocols used in LDT or clinical reporting contexts.']
  compatible_instrument_models varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sequencing instrument models validated for use with this protocol (e.g., \'NovaSeq 6000, NovaSeq X Plus, NextSeq 2000\'). Ensures run parameters are only applied to compatible hardware. Validated against instrument qualification records in LabVantage LIMS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sequencing protocol record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Populated automatically on insert and immutable thereafter. Supports GxP audit trail requirements.']
  demultiplexing_tool varchar [note: 'Approved software tool used to demultiplex raw BCL files into per-sample FASTQ files for this protocol. Governs the bioinformatics pipeline entry point. BCL Convert is the current Illumina-recommended tool; DRAGEN provides accelerated on-instrument demultiplexing.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this protocol version became (or becomes) effective for use in sequencing operations. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_FROM category. Protocols may not be used in regulated runs before this date. Managed through GxP change control in MasterControl.']
  flow_cell_type varchar [note: 'Specific flow cell cartridge type required by this protocol (e.g., \'S4 Flow Cell\', \'SP Flow Cell\', \'P2 Flow Cell\', \'25B Flow Cell\'). Determines lane count, cluster capacity, and maximum data output. Must match the instrument model and run configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: S1|S2|S4|SP|P1|P2|P3|25B|nano — promote to reference product]']
  gxp_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory practice classification applicable to runs executed under this protocol. GMP = Good Manufacturing Practice (production reagents); GLP = Good Laboratory Practice (non-clinical studies); GCP = Good Clinical Practice (clinical trials); non-GxP = research use. Determines documentation, validation, and audit requirements.']
  index1_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Number of cycles for the Index 1 (i7) read used in multiplexing to demultiplex samples on a shared flow cell. Typically 8 or 10 cycles. Required for all multiplexed library pools. Null for non-indexed single-sample runs.']
  index2_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Number of cycles for the Index 2 (i5) read used in dual-index multiplexing schemes. Typically 8 or 10 cycles. Null for single-index or non-indexed protocols. Dual indexing reduces index-hopping artifacts in high-multiplex runs.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the protocol\'s intended use. RUO = Research Use Only; IVD = In Vitro Diagnostic (FDA-cleared); LDT = Laboratory Developed Test (CLIA-regulated); CE-IVD = EU Conformité Européenne In Vitro Diagnostic. Determines applicable regulatory framework, labeling requirements, and permissible clinical use.']
  max_cluster_density decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable cluster density on the flow cell surface in K/mm². Exceeding this threshold causes cluster overlap (over-clustering), degrading base call quality and Q30 scores. Paired with min_cluster_density to define the acceptable loading range.']
  max_insert_size_bp varchar [note: 'Maximum acceptable DNA insert size in base pairs for library fragments. Inserts exceeding this threshold may indicate insufficient fragmentation, leading to reduced cluster density and read-through artifacts. Paired with min_insert_size_bp to define the acceptable library size range.']
  min_cluster_density decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable cluster density on the flow cell surface, expressed in thousands of clusters per square millimeter (K/mm²). Below this threshold, insufficient data yield is expected. Used as a pre-run and mid-run QC gate in Illumina BaseSpace.']
  min_insert_size_bp varchar [note: 'Minimum acceptable DNA insert size in base pairs for library fragments sequenced under this protocol. Insert sizes below this threshold indicate over-fragmentation and may degrade paired-end alignment quality. Used as a library QC acceptance criterion in LabVantage LIMS.']
  min_q30_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable percentage of bases with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (Q30), indicating ≥99.9% base call accuracy. Industry-standard QC gate for sequencing run acceptance. Runs falling below this threshold are flagged for investigation or resequencing. Typically ≥75% for most NGS applications.']
  min_yield_gb decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable total sequencing data yield in gigabases (Gb) per run or per sample lane, as required to achieve the target coverage depth. Runs not meeting this threshold trigger a QC failure and potential resequencing. Derived from target coverage, genome size, and read length.']
  mismatch_tolerance varchar [note: 'Number of allowable base mismatches in the index sequence during demultiplexing before a read is assigned to the \'undetermined\' bin. Typically 0 or 1. Higher values increase sample assignment rates but risk index cross-contamination in high-multiplex pools.']
  multiplexing_max_samples varchar [note: 'Maximum number of samples that can be pooled and multiplexed on a single flow cell lane under this protocol while maintaining the target coverage depth per sample. Governs library pooling decisions in LabVantage LIMS and run planning in BaseSpace.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the scientist or team lead responsible for maintaining and updating this sequencing protocol. The protocol owner initiates change control requests and is accountable for scientific validity. Sourced from Oracle Cloud HCM.']
  phi_data_generated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sequencing runs executed under this protocol generate Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined by HIPAA. True = PHI is generated (clinical patient samples); False = no PHI (research/RUO samples). Governs data handling, storage, and access control requirements for resulting FASTQ and downstream files.']
  protocol_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code identifying this protocol and version (e.g., WGS-PCR-v2.1). Used as the business identifier across LIMS, BaseSpace, and regulatory submissions. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  protocol_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the sequencing protocol (e.g., \'Whole Genome Sequencing PCR-Free 150bp Paired-End\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Used in reporting, SOP references, and customer-facing documentation.']
  r1_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Number of sequencing cycles for Read 1 (R1), determining the read length in base pairs for the forward read. Common values include 75, 100, 150, 250 cycles. Directly impacts data quality, coverage, and FASTQ file size. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  r2_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Number of sequencing cycles for Read 2 (R2) in paired-end configurations. Null for single-end protocols. Must match R1 cycle count for symmetric paired-end runs or may differ for asymmetric configurations. Governs reverse read length in base pairs.']
  read_configuration varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the sequencing run is configured for single-end or paired-end reads. Paired-end reads generate two reads per fragment (R1 and R2), improving alignment accuracy and variant calling. Directly governs flow cell programming and FASTQ output structure.']
  reference_genome_build varchar [note: 'Standard reference genome assembly version used for alignment and variant calling under this protocol (e.g., GRCh38 for human, GRCm39 for mouse). Ensures reproducibility and comparability of genomic results across runs and cohorts. Aligns with NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy requirements.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date on which this protocol version was retired or superseded and is no longer approved for new sequencing runs. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category. Null for currently active protocols. Historical runs executed under this protocol remain valid after retirement.']
  sequencing_application_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the genomic sequencing application this protocol supports. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Drives downstream bioinformatics pipeline selection and QC thresholds. WGS = Whole Genome Sequencing; WES = Whole Exome Sequencing; RNA-seq = RNA Sequencing.']
  sequencing_protocol_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the sequencing protocol, including its scientific rationale, key distinguishing parameters, intended sample types, and any special handling requirements. Authored by the protocol owner and reviewed during the GxP approval process.']
  sop_version varchar [note: 'Version of the SOP document referenced by sop_document_id that is current and approved for use with this protocol version. Ensures the protocol record is always linked to the correct procedural revision, supporting GxP change control.']
  strand_specificity varchar [note: 'Specifies the strand orientation of the sequencing library for RNA-seq and methylation protocols. Unstranded = no strand information preserved; Forward = sense strand; Reverse = antisense strand. Critical for accurate transcript quantification and differential expression analysis. Null for DNA-based protocols.']
  superseded_by_protocol_code varchar [note: 'Protocol code of the newer version that replaces this protocol upon retirement. Null for the current active version. Enables forward navigation in the protocol version chain for change control traceability and migration of active runs.']
  target_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Recommended mean sequencing coverage depth (in X, e.g., 30X for WGS, 100X for WES) required to meet analytical sensitivity thresholds for this protocol. Used to calculate required cluster density and flow cell loading. Governs pass/fail criteria for sequencing run QC.']
  target_panel_bed_file varchar [note: 'File path or artifact identifier for the BED (Browser Extensible Data) file defining the genomic target regions for targeted panel or WES protocols. Null for WGS protocols. Used by bioinformatics pipelines for on-target coverage calculation and variant calling region restriction. Stored in genomic artifact repository.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this sequencing protocol record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated automatically on every write. Supports change detection, incremental ETL processing, and GxP audit trail requirements.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the sequencing protocol (e.g., 2.1.0). Incremented upon any approved change to run parameters, kit compatibility, or QC thresholds. Supports GxP change control traceability.']

  Note: 'Reference master for approved sequencing protocols and run configurations. Captures protocol name, version, sequencing application type (WGS, WES, targeted panel, RNA-seq, amplicon, methylation), instrument compatibility, read configuration (single-end/paired-end), read lengths (R1/R2 cycles), index read cycles, recommended coverage target, minimum Q30 threshold, approved library prep kits, SOP document reference, effective date, and approval status. Governs which run parameters are permissible for each assay type and supports GxP compliance.'
}

Table sequencing.run_sample_sheet {
  run_sample_sheet_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the run sample sheet record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing instrument (e.g., NovaSeq, NextSeq, MiSeq) on which this sample sheet is loaded and executed. Drives instrument-specific configuration parameters.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory operator or scientist who submitted and authorized the sample sheet for the sequencing run. Required for GxP audit trail and operator accountability under CLIA and GMP regulations.']
  flow_cell_id varchar [note: 'The unique serial or barcode identifier of the physical flow cell cartridge used in this sequencing run. Enables traceability from sample sheet to physical consumable for GxP lot tracking and COA reconciliation.']
  sequencing_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_protocol. Business justification: Sample sheets configure sequencing runs according to approved protocols. run_sample_sheet currently has application_type, read_type, and cycle counts but no FK to sequencing_protocol. Adding sequencin']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing run that this sample sheet configures. Links the sample sheet to the parent run record in the sequencing domain.']
  adapter_read1 varchar [note: 'The adapter sequence to be trimmed from the 3\' end of Read 1 during BCLConvert processing (e.g., AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA for TruSeq). Correct adapter specification is critical for accurate read trimming and downstream alignment quality.']
  adapter_read2 varchar [note: 'The adapter sequence to be trimmed from the 3\' end of Read 2 in paired-end runs (e.g., AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT for TruSeq). Null for single-end runs. Must match the library preparation kit adapter chemistry.']
  application_type varchar [note: 'The genomic sequencing application configured for this run. Drives pipeline selection, adapter trimming, and analysis settings. Examples: WGS (Whole Genome Sequencing), WES (Whole Exome Sequencing), Targeted Panel, Amplicon, RNA-Seq, Methylation, 16S rRNA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|Targeted Panel|Amplicon|RNA-Seq|Methylation|16S rRNA|Custom — promote to reference product]']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sample sheet received final approval (electronic signature) for use in a GxP-controlled sequencing run. Distinct from submission timestamp; captures the authorization event in the document lifecycle.']
  basespace_run_code varchar [note: 'The unique run identifier assigned by Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub upon run registration or upload. Enables direct cross-reference between the internal data platform record and the BaseSpace cloud environment for FASTQ retrieval and run monitoring.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this run sample sheet record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking per GxP and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  demultiplexing_software varchar [note: 'The software tool configured to process this sample sheet for demultiplexing BCL files into per-sample FASTQ files. Options include BCLConvert (current Illumina standard), bcl2fastq (legacy), or DRAGEN (on-instrument or cloud). Determines compatible sample sheet sections and parameter keys.']
  demultiplexing_software_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the demultiplexing software (BCLConvert, bcl2fastq, or DRAGEN) specified in the sample sheet settings. Critical for reproducibility, pipeline validation, and regulatory submission documentation.']
  electronic_signature varchar [note: 'The electronic signature identifier or hash of the authorized signatory who approved this sample sheet for GxP-controlled runs. Required under 21 CFR Part 11 for electronic records in regulated environments. Stored as a reference token, not the raw signature credential.']
  experiment_name varchar [note: 'The experiment identifier or label captured in the [Header] section of the Illumina sample sheet. Provides a human-readable label for the run experiment, used for BaseSpace project organization and LIMS cross-referencing.']
  flow_cell_type varchar [note: 'The specific flow cell cartridge type loaded on the instrument for this run (e.g., SP, S1, S2, S4 for NovaSeq; P1, P2, P3 for NovaSeq X; High Output, Mid Output for NextSeq). Determines lane count, cluster density, and total output capacity.']
  gxp_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sample sheet is subject to GxP (Good Practice Regulations) document control requirements, including version control, electronic signature, and audit trail obligations under 21 CFR Part 11 and applicable GMP/GLP/GCP frameworks.']
  index1_cycles varchar [note: 'Number of cycles for Index 1 (i7 index read) used in multiplexed library demultiplexing. Typically 8 or 10 cycles. Determines the length of the i7 barcode sequence read during the indexing phase.']
  index2_cycles varchar [note: 'Number of cycles for Index 2 (i5 index read) used in dual-indexed multiplexed library demultiplexing. Typically 8 or 10 cycles. Null for single-indexed runs. Required for dual-index demultiplexing workflows.']
  index_adapter_kit varchar [note: 'The specific index adapter kit used for multiplexing (e.g., IDT for Illumina DNA/RNA UD Indexes, Nextera DNA CD Indexes). Defines the index sequence set and ensures correct barcode assignment during demultiplexing.']
  instrument_platform varchar [note: 'The Illumina sequencing instrument platform model for which this sample sheet is configured. Determines supported flow cell types, read lengths, and chemistry parameters. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NovaSeq 6000|NovaSeq X|NextSeq 550|NextSeq 2000|MiSeq|HiSeq 4000|iSeq 100 — promote to reference product]']
  investigator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the principal investigator or study lead responsible for the samples in this run. Captured in the [Header] section of the Illumina sample sheet. Used for accountability, reporting, and GxP traceability.']
  lane_count varchar [note: 'Number of flow cell lanes configured in this sample sheet. Determines how samples are distributed across lanes for multiplexing. Varies by flow cell type (e.g., 2 lanes for SP, 4 lanes for S2/S4 on NovaSeq 6000).']
  library_prep_kit varchar [note: 'The name and version of the library preparation kit used to prepare samples for sequencing (e.g., Illumina DNA Prep, TruSeq DNA PCR-Free, Nextera DNA Flex). Determines adapter sequences, index compatibility, and recommended run parameters.']
  lims_request_code varchar [note: 'The work order or sequencing request identifier from LabVantage LIMS that initiated this sample sheet. Provides bidirectional traceability between the sample sheet and the originating laboratory workflow request.']
  mask_short_reads varchar [note: 'The MaskShortReads BCLConvert setting specifying the read length threshold below which reads are masked (replaced with Ns) rather than discarded. Complements minimum_trimmed_read_length to control short-read handling behavior in FASTQ output.']
  minimum_trimmed_read_length varchar [note: 'The MinimumTrimmedReadLength BCLConvert setting specifying the minimum acceptable read length (in base pairs) after adapter trimming. Reads shorter than this threshold are discarded. Impacts FASTQ output quality and downstream alignment performance.']
  override_cycles_string varchar [note: 'The OverrideCycles parameter string in Illumina sample sheet v2 format (e.g., \'Y151;I8;I8;Y151\') that explicitly defines the cycle allocation for each read and index segment. Overrides default cycle settings and is used by BCLConvert for precise demultiplexing configuration.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'The project or study name associated with the samples in this sample sheet. Used for grouping FASTQ output files by project in BaseSpace and downstream bioinformatics pipelines. Maps to the \'Sample_Project\' column in the sample manifest.']
  read1_cycles varchar [note: 'Number of sequencing cycles for Read 1 (forward read). Directly determines the read length in base pairs for the primary read. Typical values range from 50 to 300 cycles depending on application and instrument platform.']
  read2_cycles varchar [note: 'Number of sequencing cycles for Read 2 (reverse read) in paired-end runs. Null for single-end runs. Must match Read 1 cycles for symmetric paired-end configurations or may differ for asymmetric designs.']
  read_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the sequencing run is configured as single-end (one read direction) or paired-end (forward and reverse reads). Paired-end reads improve alignment accuracy and are standard for WGS and WES applications.']
  reagent_kit_lot_number varchar [note: 'The lot number (LOT) of the sequencing reagent kit used for this run. Critical for GxP traceability, COA verification, and investigation of run failures attributable to reagent quality. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA QM batch records.']
  run_sample_sheet_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description field from the [Header] section of the Illumina sample sheet. May include run purpose, special instructions, or notes relevant to the sequencing configuration. Supports operational context for lab staff.']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Total number of samples listed in the sample manifest section of this sample sheet. Represents the multiplexing depth for the run. Used for capacity planning, lane loading calculations, and demultiplexing validation.']
  sample_sheet_format_version varchar [note: 'Version of the Illumina sample sheet format used (v1 or v2). v2 supports multi-section configuration including [Header], [Reads], [BCLConvert_Settings], and [BCLConvert_Data] sections. Determines downstream demultiplexing compatibility and parser behavior.']
  sample_sheet_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title assigned to the sample sheet file, typically following the Illumina naming convention (e.g., SampleSheet_RunID_YYYYMMDD). Serves as the externally-known business identifier for this document. Aligns with Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub sample sheet naming standards.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the sample sheet was submitted to the instrument or uploaded to Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this run sample sheet record. Supports change tracking and GxP audit trail requirements for controlled documents.']
  use_type varchar [note: 'Regulatory intended use classification for the samples in this run. RUO (Research Use Only) indicates non-clinical research; IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) indicates regulated diagnostic use; LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) indicates a lab-specific validated test; GMP indicates manufacturing-grade production run.']
  validation_error_summary varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of validation errors or warnings identified during automated sample sheet validation (e.g., index collision detected, invalid cycle count, missing required fields). Populated when validation_status is \'failed_validation\'. Supports rapid troubleshooting by lab operators.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle and validation state of the sample sheet. \'draft\' indicates in-preparation; \'pending_validation\' awaiting automated or manual QC check; \'validated\' passed all checks and approved for run execution; \'failed_validation\' contains errors blocking run start; \'superseded\' replaced by a corrected version; \'cancelled\' withdrawn before use. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_validation|validated|failed_validation|superseded|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Document version number of this sample sheet (e.g., v1.0, v1.1, v2.0). Incremented upon each revision or correction. Supports GxP document version control and supersession tracking. The combination of sample_sheet_name and version_number uniquely identifies a document revision.']

  Note: 'Transactional record representing the sample sheet submitted to configure a sequencing run on an Illumina instrument. Captures sample sheet format version (v1/v2), associated run and instrument, flow cell type, operator, submission timestamp, sample manifest (sample IDs, index assignments, project groupings), override cycles string, adapter sequences, application settings, and validation status. The sample sheet is the authoritative GxP-controlled input document that drives both instrument run setup and downstream demultiplexing configuration.'
}

Table sequencing.run_failure_event {
  run_failure_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each sequencing run failure or abort event record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing instrument on which the failed run was executed. Supports instrument reliability tracking, mean time between failures (MTBF) analysis, and preventive maintenance scheduling.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory operator or technician who initiated or was responsible for the failed sequencing run. Required for GxP deviation management and operator error root-cause analysis.']
  flow_cell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flow cell loaded on the instrument during the failed run. Supports flow cell lot-level failure trending, reagent quality investigation, and consumable reliability analysis.']
  primary_sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing run that experienced the failure or abort event. Links this failure record to the parent run entity for traceability and root-cause analysis.']
  support_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.support_case. Business justification: Run failures generate customer support cases for service labs. Links failure events to customer incident tracking, CAPA, rerun coordination, and SLA impact assessment. Essential for customer-facing op']
  affected_lanes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or range notation of flow cell lane numbers affected by the failure event (e.g., \'1,2,3\' or \'1-4\' or \'ALL\'). Enables partial-lane failure analysis and assessment of sample impact scope. Null if failure affected the entire flow cell uniformly.']
  affected_sample_count varchar [note: 'Number of samples whose sequencing data was lost or compromised as a result of this failure event. Drives impact assessment, customer notification decisions, and rerun prioritization. Sourced from the pool/library manifest associated with the failed run.']
  basespace_run_code varchar [note: 'The run identifier assigned by Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub for the failed run. Enables direct cross-reference to the BaseSpace run record for diagnostic log retrieval, run metrics review, and support case filing with Illumina.']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking this failure event to a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record in the quality management system (e.g., Veeva Vault). Null if no formal CAPA was initiated. Supports GxP deviation-to-CAPA traceability.']
  cluster_density_at_failure decimal(10,2) [note: 'Flow cell cluster density (in thousands of clusters per mm²) measured at or near the time of failure. Abnormal cluster density (too high or too low) is a key diagnostic indicator for library preparation issues, loading concentration errors, or flow cell defects.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the immediate corrective action taken in response to the failure event (e.g., instrument restart, reagent replacement, operator retraining). Distinct from the formal CAPA process; captures the on-the-spot remediation steps.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this run failure event record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the customer or project owner was formally notified of the failure event. Used to measure notification turnaround time (TAT) against SLA commitments and regulatory reporting timelines.']
  customer_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer or project owner was formally notified of the sequencing run failure and its impact on their samples or data deliverables. Supports SLA compliance tracking and customer communication audit trails.']
  deviation_report_number varchar [note: 'Formal deviation report number assigned in the quality management system (e.g., Veeva Vault) for this failure event. Distinct from the CAPA reference; the deviation report documents the non-conformance event itself, while CAPA tracks the systemic corrective action.']
  downtime_duration_min varchar [note: 'Total duration of instrument downtime in minutes attributable to this failure event, from failure detection to instrument return-to-service. Key input for instrument availability KPI calculation, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) reporting, and maintenance cost analysis.']
  estimated_data_loss_gb decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated volume of sequencing data (in gigabases, Gb) lost as a result of the failure event. Calculated based on cycles completed, affected lanes, and expected yield per lane. Key metric for assessing business impact and prioritizing rerun decisions. Serves as the QUANTITATIVE_RESULT for this non-monetary transaction.']
  failure_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause category for the sequencing run failure. Used for trend analysis, quality KPI reporting, and directing corrective action. Categories align with standard GxP deviation classification taxonomy.']
  failure_code varchar [note: 'Structured alphanumeric code identifying the specific failure mode within the failure category. Sourced from the instrument error code library or quality management system codebook (e.g., INS-00142, REA-00031). Enables precise failure mode trending and FMEA alignment.']
  failure_cycle varchar [note: 'The sequencing cycle number at which the failure or abort event occurred during the run. Critical for diagnosing instrument or chemistry issues at specific cycle positions (e.g., early cycles vs. late cycles). Null if failure occurred before sequencing commenced.']
  failure_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the sequencing run failure event, including observed symptoms, error messages, and contextual details captured at the time of detection. Supports root-cause investigation and deviation report authoring.']
  failure_detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the sequencing run failure or abort event was detected or reported. This is the principal real-world business event timestamp (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP) for this transaction, distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  failure_event_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this failure event, used in deviation reports, CAPA references, and quality management system cross-references (e.g., RFE-20240315-0042). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction.']
  failure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the failure event record within the quality management workflow. Tracks progression from initial detection through investigation, resolution, and closure. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this transaction.']
  gxp_impacting boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this failure event occurred in a GxP-regulated context (e.g., GMP, GLP, GCP) and therefore requires formal deviation management, CAPA, and regulatory documentation per applicable GxP guidelines. Drives regulatory reporting workflow routing.']
  instrument_error_message varchar [note: 'Verbatim error message or fault code string reported by the sequencing instrument at the time of failure. Sourced directly from instrument logs or Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub run diagnostics. Essential for instrument engineering root-cause analysis.']
  ivd_run boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the failed sequencing run was performed as part of an In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) or Clinical Laboratory Developed Test (LDT) workflow, as opposed to a Research Use Only (RUO) application. Determines applicable regulatory reporting obligations (FDA, IVDR, CLIA).']
  lims_event_code varchar [note: 'The event or non-conformance record identifier from LabVantage LIMS corresponding to this failure event. Supports bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational LIMS system of record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or observations recorded by the investigator, quality team, or instrument engineer during the failure event lifecycle. Captures supplementary context not covered by structured fields, such as environmental conditions, shift notes, or escalation history.']
  phred_score_at_failure decimal(5,2) [note: 'The mean Phred base quality score (Q-score) recorded at the cycle of failure or at the last completed quality checkpoint before failure. Provides quantitative evidence of data quality degradation leading up to the failure event. Null if failure occurred before quality metrics were generated.']
  reagent_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number of the sequencing reagent kit used during the failed run. Critical for reagent failure root-cause analysis, lot-level quality investigations, and supplier corrective action requests. Aligns with GMP lot traceability requirements.']
  rerun_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the failed sequencing run must be repeated to recover the lost data or meet the required coverage depth and quality thresholds. Drives rerun scheduling, resource allocation, and customer communication workflows.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the failure event was formally resolved and the record was closed in the quality management system. Used to calculate mean time to resolution (MTTR) and compliance with deviation closure SLA targets.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Confirmed root cause category determined after investigation of the failure event. Distinct from failure_category (which is the initial classification at detection); this field is populated after root-cause analysis is complete. Aligns with standard fishbone/Ishikawa root cause taxonomy.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the confirmed root cause identified through formal investigation. Populated after root-cause analysis is completed. Supports CAPA authoring, regulatory submission documentation, and institutional knowledge capture.']
  sample_quality_issue boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a sample quality problem (e.g., degraded DNA, low input concentration, contamination) was identified as a contributing factor to the run failure. Supports sample management quality trending and upstream LIMS feedback.']
  servicenow_ticket_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the ServiceNow incident or field service ticket created to manage the instrument repair or technical support response associated with this failure event. Enables cross-system traceability between quality events and IT/field service workflows.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the failure event based on its impact on data quality, patient safety, regulatory compliance, and business operations. Drives escalation workflows, notification requirements, and CAPA prioritization.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the instrument control software or run control application active at the time of the failure event. Required for software error root-cause analysis and version-specific defect tracking.']
  total_cycles_planned varchar [note: 'The total number of sequencing cycles that were planned for the run at the time of setup. Used in conjunction with failure_cycle to calculate the proportion of the run completed before failure, informing data loss estimation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this run failure event record was last modified. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for audit trail and change tracking purposes.']

  Note: 'Transactional record documenting a sequencing run failure or abort event. Captures failure event ID, run ID, failure category (instrument error, reagent failure, sample quality, operator error, software error), failure code, failure description, cycle at which failure occurred, affected lanes, estimated data loss (Gb), corrective action taken, rerun required flag, CAPA reference, and resolution timestamp. Feeds into quality management and instrument reliability tracking. Supports GxP deviation management.'
}

Table sequencing.basecall_model {
  basecall_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the basecalling algorithm model record. Primary key.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: Basecalling algorithms are software components of regulated IVD systems and must be traceable to the approval covering the complete system. FDA and EU MDR require software version control and approval']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Basecall model changes (version upgrades, algorithm updates, parameter tuning) require change control in regulated environments. Labs track model changes through change control for validation impact a']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or authority who approved this basecalling model for operational use.']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for regulatory submission (FDA 510(k), PMA, or CE-IVD) associated with this basecalling model.']
  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the validation protocol document used to validate this basecalling model.']
  software_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Basecalling software is a licensable product catalog item. Needed for license entitlement management, version control, regulatory validation documentation, and tracking which software SKU/version is a']
  superseded_basecall_model_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on basecall_model (superseded_basecall_model_id)']
  superseded_by_model_basecall_model_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer basecalling model that supersedes this version.']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Basecall models are validated before use in GxP environments (accuracy testing, performance qualification, software validation). Each model version must link to its validation study for regulatory com']
  accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured accuracy of the basecalling model expressed as a percentage, representing the proportion of correctly called bases.']
  algorithm_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying algorithm approach used for basecalling (e.g., neural network, machine learning, statistical).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this basecalling model was officially approved for use in production sequencing workflows.']
  ce_ivd_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the basecalling model has received CE-IVD certification for use in the European Union.']
  compatible_chemistry_versions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sequencing chemistry versions supported by this basecalling model.']
  compatible_instrument_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sequencing instrument platforms compatible with this basecalling model (e.g., NovaSeq, MiSeq, NextSeq).']
  computational_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of computational resources required to run this basecalling model (CPU, GPU, memory, storage).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this basecalling model record was first created in the system.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date when this basecalling model was deprecated and is no longer recommended for new sequencing runs.']
  developer_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or company that developed the basecalling model.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Web URL link to the official documentation for this basecalling model.']
  error_rate_percent decimal(5,3) [note: 'Measured error rate of the basecalling model expressed as a percentage, representing the proportion of incorrectly called bases.']
  fda_cleared boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the basecalling model has received FDA clearance or approval for clinical use in the United States.']
  gpu_accelerated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the basecalling model utilizes GPU acceleration for improved processing speed.']
  gxp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the basecalling model has been developed and validated under Good Practice regulations (GMP, GLP, GCP).']
  intended_use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the basecalling model indicating whether it is for Research Use Only (RUO), In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD), Laboratory Developed Test (LDT), clinical, or research applications.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Type of software license governing the use of this basecalling model.']
  mean_phred_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average Phred quality score produced by this basecalling model, indicating the confidence level of base calls.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the basecalling model used in operational systems and documentation.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the basecalling algorithm model.']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the basecalling model indicating its availability and approval for use in production sequencing workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes containing additional information, usage guidelines, or special considerations for this basecalling model.']
  processing_speed_gb_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Throughput speed of the basecalling model measured in gigabases processed per hour.']
  q30_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of bases called with Phred quality score of 30 or higher (99.9% accuracy) by this model.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when this basecalling model version was officially released for use.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when this basecalling model was retired and is no longer supported or available for use.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the software package that implements this basecalling model.']
  supports_real_time_basecalling boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the basecalling model can perform real-time basecalling during sequencing runs.']
  training_dataset_description varchar [note: 'Description of the dataset used to train the basecalling model, including sample types and sequencing conditions.']
  training_dataset_size_gb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Size of the training dataset in gigabytes used to develop the basecalling model.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this basecalling model record was last modified in the system.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date when the basecalling model successfully completed analytical validation studies.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation status of the basecalling model indicating whether it has passed required analytical validation studies.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the basecalling model following semantic versioning convention (e.g., 1.0, 2.1.3).']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of basecalling algorithm models and versions used to convert raw signal data to nucleotide sequences including accuracy and speed specifications'
}

Labs must document which approval covers']

Critical for procurement, cost-per-sample tracking, regulatory traceability, and compatibility validation with sca']
  array_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific array product type used in this hybridization run (SNP array, methylation array, CGH array, expression array).']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scanner or hybridization instrument used to perform the array hybridization and imaging.']
  clinical_evidence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_evidence. Required for tracing raw data to published performance claims and supporting regu']

Business justification: Array hybridization assays consume lab resources requiring cost center allocation for departmental cost tracking, project accounting, and accurate cost-per-sample calculations. Essential for genomics ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory technician or scientist who performed the array hybridization run.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: Array hybridizations are experimental procedures generating genotyping/expression data for specific experiments. Linking enables experiment-level data aggregation, QC assessment against experimental o']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Array-based genotyping uses probe coordinates defined in specific genome builds. Variant calling from arrays requires reference genome context for coordinate mapping and clinical interpretation of arr']
  installed_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.installed_instrument. Business justification: Array hybridization on customer-site scanners. Tracks instrument usage, utilization, and service delivery for customer-installed array scanning instruments.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Array experiments frequently support specific R&D projects or validation studies tracked via internal orders for project cost accumulation, budget management, and capitalization decisions. Standard ge']
  lims_workflow_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.lims_workflow_assignment. Business justification: Array hybridization processing is LIMS-tracked in genomics labs. Linking hybridization to workflow assignment enables tracking processing SLA compliance, operator accountability, batch-level quality c']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.opportunity. Business justification: Array hybridization services delivered for customer projects require opportunity linkage for service revenue tracking, project cost allocation, and billing. Standard in genomics service business where']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the research or clinical project under which this array hybridization was performed.']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Array hybridizations undergo QC (call rate, signal intensity, background intensity, signal-to-noise ratio). QC results determine sample disposition, reprocessing needs, and batch release. Standard arr']
  quality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.quality_assessment. Business justification: Array hybridizations assessed for call rate, signal intensity, background noise against manufacturer specifications - standard array QC workflow. Assessment results determine data release approval and']

Business justification: Failed array hybridizations (low call rate, high background, scanner errors) trigger quality issue logging for lab investigation and potential re-hybridization - standard lab QC workflow in genotyping']

  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or hybridization protocol version used for this run.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample loaded onto the array for hybridization. Links to the sample management domain.']

  technical_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.technical_requirement. Business justification: Array experiments designed to meet customer-specified technical requirements (array type, application, QC thresholds). Links experiment design to customer engagement specifications for acceptance crit']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study.
