Labs link validation hybridizations to study records for regulatory compli']
  analysis_software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the bioinformatics software used for initial quality control and genotype calling from raw intensity data.']
  array_barcode varchar [note: 'Unique barcode or serial number printed on the physical array chip used in this hybridization run.']
  array_position varchar [note: 'Position or well location on the array plate or chip where the sample was loaded (e.g., R01C01 for row 1 column 1).']
  background_intensity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average background fluorescence intensity measured in non-probe regions of the array, used to assess signal-to-noise ratio.']
  call_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of probes on the array that produced a valid genotype or methylation call, indicating data completeness.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Status of patient or research participant consent for genomic data generation and sharing from this array hybridization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this array hybridization record was first created in the system.']
  gxp_classification varchar [note: 'Good Practice regulation classification applicable to this array hybridization run: Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP), Good Laboratory Practice (GLP), Good Clinical Practice (GCP), or non-GxP.']
  hybridization_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total duration in hours that the sample was incubated on the array during the hybridization process.']
  hybridization_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the array hybridization process was completed and the array was ready for scanning.']
  hybridization_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the array hybridization process was initiated in the laboratory.']
  hybridization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the array hybridization run in the laboratory workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|completed|failed|aborted|qc_review|approved|rejected — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hybridization_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature in degrees Celsius at which the array hybridization was performed, critical for assay reproducibility.']
  input_dna_mass_ng decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total mass of DNA in nanograms used as input for the array hybridization reaction.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the array hybridization run: Research Use Only (RUO), In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD), Laboratory Developed Test (LDT), or clinical trial.']
  lims_run_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this array hybridization run in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) for cross-system traceability.']
  md5_checksum varchar [note: 'MD5 hash of the raw data file used to verify data integrity during transfer and storage.']
  mean_signal_intensity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average fluorescence signal intensity across all probes on the array, used as a quality metric.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional observations, deviations, or comments about the array hybridization run.']
  phi_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this array hybridization run contains Protected Health Information subject to HIPAA regulations.']
  probe_count varchar [note: 'Total number of probes present on the array chip used in this hybridization run.']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the array hybridization run failed quality control checks, if applicable.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Quality control outcome for the array hybridization run based on signal intensity, background noise, and other quality metrics.']
  raw_data_file_format varchar [note: 'File format of the raw intensity data output from the array scanner (e.g., IDAT for Illumina, CEL for Affymetrix).']
  raw_data_file_path varchar [note: 'File system path or cloud storage location where the raw intensity data (IDAT or CEL files) from the array scan is stored.']
  raw_data_file_size_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Size of the raw intensity data file in megabytes, used for storage planning and data transfer tracking.']
  run_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique identifier or barcode for the array hybridization run, used for tracking and reference in Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS).']
  run_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name assigned to the array hybridization run for easy identification and reporting purposes.']
  sample_concentration_ng_ul decimal(10,2) [note: 'Concentration of nucleic acid sample in nanograms per microliter prior to loading on the array.']
  sample_volume_ul decimal(8,2) [note: 'Volume of sample in microliters loaded onto the array for hybridization.']
  scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hybridized array was scanned to capture raw intensity data.']
  scanner_software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the scanner control software used to capture the array image and generate raw intensity data.']
  signal_to_noise_ratio decimal(8,2) [note: 'Ratio of mean signal intensity to background intensity, a key quality metric for array hybridization performance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this array hybridization record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of array-based hybridization runs for genotyping and methylation arrays — capturing array type (SNP array, methylation array, CGH array), hybridization protocol, sample loading, scanner assignment, and raw intensity data output references'
}

Table sequencing.file_quality_evaluation {
  file_quality_evaluation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this quality evaluation execution record. Primary key for the association.']
  fastq_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the FASTQ file being evaluated in this quality check execution']
  quality_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the quality rule definition applied in this evaluation']
  evaluated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system, service, or user that executed this quality evaluation. Typically an automated pipeline service account, but may be a bioinformatician for manual QC review.']
  execution_duration_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'The time in seconds required to execute this quality rule against this FASTQ file. Used for pipeline performance monitoring and optimization.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of why this rule failed or produced a warning for this file. Populated when rule_result is FAIL or WARNING. Supports troubleshooting and remediation.']
  measured_value decimal(15,4) [note: 'The actual measured metric value from this FASTQ file that was compared against the rule threshold. For example, if rule checks minimum read count, this is the actual read count observed. Unit and interpretation depend on the rule type.']
  pass_fail_status boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this file passed this specific quality rule. True = passed, False = failed. Simplified binary version of rule_result for filtering and aggregation.']
  pipeline_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the bioinformatics QC pipeline that executed this rule evaluation. Enables tracking of rule results across pipeline version changes and supports reproducibility.']
  remediation_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of any automated or manual remediation action taken in response to this rule failure. Examples: file re-sequenced, sample flagged for review, data excluded from analysis.']
  rule_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this quality rule was executed against this FASTQ file. Tracks when the evaluation occurred in the pipeline workflow.']
  rule_result varchar [note: 'The outcome of this quality rule evaluation. PASS = rule criteria met, FAIL = rule criteria not met, WARNING = borderline result, ERROR = execution error, SKIPPED = rule not applicable to this file type.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Quality Evaluation event between fastq_file and quality_rule. It captures the execution and results of automated data quality checks applied to FASTQ files during bioinformatics pipeline processing. Each record links one FASTQ file to one quality rule execution with measured values, pass/fail status, and pipeline version context that exist only at the intersection of a specific file being evaluated against a specific rule.. Existence Justification: In genomics bioinformatics operations, each FASTQ file is evaluated against multiple quality rules (minimum read count, Q30 threshold, contamination detection, duplication rate limits, adapter content checks), and each quality rule is applied to many FASTQ files as they are generated. The business actively manages these quality evaluation results as operational records - bioinformatics teams query which files failed which rules, track pass rates over time, and trigger remediation workflows based on specific rule failures.'
}

Table sequencing.array_type {
  array_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for array_type']
  superseded_array_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on array_type (superseded_array_type_id)']
  chemistry_version varchar [note: 'Version of the sequencing chemistry used by the array.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the array type record was first created.']
  data_format varchar [note: 'File format produced by the array (e.g., FASTQ, BCL).']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the array type, including use‑case notes.']
  flow_cell_type varchar [note: 'Designated flow cell format compatible with the array.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the array type is currently active for new runs.']
  library_prep_kit varchar [note: 'Standard library preparation kit recommended for this array type.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that manufactures the array platform.']
  max_output_gb_per_run decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum data output in gigabytes per sequencing run.']
  multiplexing_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of samples that can be multiplexed in a single run.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the array type (e.g., "Illumina NovaSeq 6000").']
  platform varchar [note: 'Specific instrument platform model associated with the array type.']
  read_length_bp varchar [note: 'Maximum base‑pair read length the array can generate.']
  recommended_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Suggested average sequencing depth (fold coverage) for optimal results.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory status of the array type for clinical use.']
  status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the array type.']
  supported_sample_type varchar [note: 'Type of biological sample the array is designed for (e.g., DNA, RNA, cfDNA).']
  technology varchar [note: 'Underlying sequencing technology (e.g., "Sequencing‑by‑Synthesis").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the array type record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for array_type. Referenced by array_type_id.'
}

Table sequencing.target_panel {
  target_panel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for target_panel']
  superseded_target_panel_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on target_panel (superseded_target_panel_id)']
  annotation_version varchar [note: 'Version of the genomic annotation set used for panel design.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when regulatory approval was granted.']
  audit_created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that initially created the record.']
  capture_method varchar [note: 'Laboratory method used to capture target regions.']
  clinical_significance varchar [note: 'Intended clinical use of the panel results.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the panel cost.']
  coverage_depth_target varchar [note: 'Desired average sequencing depth (X‑fold) for the panel.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the panel record was first created.']
  deprecated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the panel is officially deprecated.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date the panel was marked as deprecated.']
  design_reference varchar [note: 'External document or accession number describing the panel design.']
  disease_area varchar [note: 'Specific disease(s) or condition(s) the panel is intended to investigate.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the panel becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the panel is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  gene_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct genes targeted by the panel.']
  is_customizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the panel can be modified for custom gene sets.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Most recent date the panel design was reviewed for relevance.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that produced the capture reagents.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the target panel.']
  panel_category varchar [note: 'High‑level clinical or research domain the panel addresses.']
  panel_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used to reference the panel in downstream workflows.']
  panel_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Internal cost to produce or license the panel.']
  panel_file_checksum varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 checksum of the panel definition file for integrity verification.']
  panel_file_location varchar [note: 'URI or path to the stored panel definition file.']
  panel_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions for the panel.']
  panel_owner varchar [note: 'Internal team or department responsible for the panel.']
  panel_type varchar [note: 'Category of the panel based on sequencing strategy.']
  probe_type varchar [note: 'Molecular composition of the capture probes.']
  read_length_target varchar [note: 'Planned read length (bases) for sequencing runs using this panel.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory clearance state for clinical deployment.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date the panel was first released to customers.']
  sensitivity decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured sensitivity (percentage) of the panel in validation studies.']
  sequencing_technology varchar [note: 'Primary sequencing platform compatible with the panel.']
  specificity decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured specificity (percentage) of the panel in validation studies.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the panel.']
  strand_specific boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the panel captures strand‑specific information.']
  target_regions varchar [note: 'Textual description or BED‑style summary of genomic regions captured.']
  umi_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Unique Molecular Identifiers are used in the capture protocol.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the panel record.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful validation.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation state of the panel.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the panel design.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for target_panel. Referenced by target_panel_id.'
}

Business justification: Specimens are processed through manufacturing work orders for library preparation, sequencing runs, and array-based assays. This link enables tracking specimen-to-manufacturing traceability required f']
  a260_a280_ratio decimal(5,3) [note: 'Ratio of absorbance at 260 nm to 280 nm as measured by NanoDrop spectrophotometer, indicating nucleic acid purity. Acceptable range is 1.8–2.0 for DNA and ~2.0 for RNA. Values outside this range indicate protein or solvent contamination.']
  aliquot_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying this specimen as a specific aliquot derived from a parent specimen (e.g., aliquot 3 of 8 from a primary blood draw). Used for inventory management, depletion tracking, and ensuring equitable distribution of limited specimens across assays.']
  collection_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country in which the specimen was collected (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Required for GDPR cross-border data transfer compliance, biospecimen import/export regulatory tracking, and population genomics stratification.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'Standardized method used to collect the specimen from the subject. Influences pre-analytical variability, QC acceptance criteria, and downstream genomic assay performance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venipuncture|finger_prick|biopsy|swab|saliva_collection|urine_void|bone_marrow_aspirate|surgical_resection|liquid_biopsy|environmental_swab|other — promote to reference product]']
  collection_site_code varchar [note: 'Coded identifier for the anatomical site or environmental location from which the specimen was collected (e.g., left_arm_antecubital, liver_biopsy_segment_VI, tumor_margin). Aligns with SNOMED CT body site codes. Critical for oncology and multi-site clinical studies.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the specimen was physically collected from the subject, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Critical for pre-analytical stability calculations, TAT (Turnaround Time) tracking, and time-sensitive assay validity windows (e.g., cfDNA stability). Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE temporal anchor for this resource.']
  concentration_ng_ul decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of extracted nucleic acid (DNA or RNA) in nanograms per microliter (ng/µL), measured post-extraction by fluorometric or spectrophotometric methods (e.g., Qubit, NanoDrop). Used for library preparation input normalization and QC pass/fail determination.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the subject\'s informed consent for use of this specimen in research or clinical testing. Governs permissible downstream uses of the specimen and derived genomic data. Mandatory for all human-derived specimens under IRB, HIPAA, and GDPR requirements.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of physical container or tube in which the specimen is stored (e.g., EDTA tube, SST tube, PAXgene tube, cryovial, FFPE block). Affects pre-analytical processing requirements and downstream assay compatibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EDTA_tube|SST_tube|PAXgene_tube|cryovial|FFPE_block|microcentrifuge_tube|96_well_plate|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the specimen record was first created in LabVantage LIMS and ingested into the Genomics Biotech data lakehouse. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA tracking.']
  disposal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the specimen was permanently disposed of or destroyed, recorded in LabVantage LIMS. Populated only when lifecycle_status is \'disposed\'. Required for biorepository inventory reconciliation, regulatory audit trails, and biohazardous waste compliance documentation.']
  dna_integrity_number decimal(4,2) [note: 'DNA Integrity Number (DIN) score on a scale of 1-10 as measured by TapeStation, indicating genomic DNA quality and fragmentation level. Used for WGS and WES library preparation QC gating. Null for non-DNA specimens.']
  external_specimen_code varchar [note: 'Specimen identifier assigned by the submitting institution, clinic, or partner laboratory prior to receipt at Genomics Biotech. Used for cross-referencing with external Laboratory Information Systems (LIS) and Electronic Medical Records (EMR). May differ from the internal LIMS accession number.']
  freeze_thaw_count varchar [note: 'Number of freeze-thaw cycles the specimen has undergone since collection. Excessive freeze-thaw cycles degrade nucleic acid integrity and protein stability, impacting assay performance. Tracked in LabVantage LIMS for QC and specimen integrity assessment.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'Biosafety Level (BSL) classification of the specimen per CDC/NIH biosafety guidelines, indicating the containment requirements for safe handling, processing, and disposal. Drives PPE requirements, laboratory access controls, and waste disposal procedures.']
  label varchar [note: 'Human-readable label printed on the specimen container or tube, typically matching the LIMS accession number or a barcode-encoded identifier. Used for physical identification during laboratory handling, storage, and retrieval. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the specimen within the Genomics Biotech laboratory workflow lifecycle. Drives LIMS workflow routing, storage management, and chain-of-custody audit events. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: accessioned|in_processing|extracted|archived|depleted|disposed|rejected|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  lims_accession_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique accession number assigned by LabVantage LIMS at the point of specimen receipt. This is the primary business identifier used across laboratory workflows, requisition forms, and chain-of-custody documentation. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  mass_mg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Mass of the specimen in milligrams (mg) at the time of accessioning or most recent measurement. Applicable to solid specimens (tissue biopsies, FFPE blocks, dried blood spots). Null for liquid specimens where volume_ul is used instead.']
  matrix_type varchar [note: 'Physical matrix or medium in which the specimen is preserved or suspended (e.g., aqueous buffer, FFPE paraffin block, dried blood spot, frozen tissue). Determines storage temperature requirements, extraction chemistry, and analytical method compatibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aqueous|solid|semi-solid|dried|fixed|frozen|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nucleic_acid_type varchar [note: 'Type of nucleic acid present in or extracted from the specimen. Determines compatible sequencing library preparation protocols (e.g., WGS, WES, RNA-Seq, ctDNA panel). Null or not_applicable for unextracted specimens. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gDNA|cfDNA|RNA|total_RNA|mRNA|ctDNA|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  passage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the specimen or derived cell line has been sub-cultured or passaged. Relevant for cell-based specimens and primary cultures. Higher passage counts may indicate genomic drift and affect assay validity.']
  phi_deidentification_tier varchar [note: 'Specifies the de-identification method applied to this specimen record per HIPAA standards: Safe Harbor (removal of 18 identifiers), Expert Determination (statistical certification), Pseudonymisation (GDPR Article 4(5)), or Identified (PHI present). Drives data access tier controls and downstream sharing permissions.']
  phi_deidentified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) has been removed or de-identified from this specimen record in accordance with HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination methods. When True, the record may be used in broader research contexts without individual consent restrictions.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Processing priority assigned to the specimen, governing TAT (Turnaround Time) SLA targets and workflow queue position in LabVantage LIMS. STAT specimens require immediate processing; routine specimens follow standard scheduling.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the specimen was received and accessioned into the Genomics Biotech laboratory, recorded in LabVantage LIMS. Used to calculate transit time, pre-analytical cold-chain compliance, and TAT (Turnaround Time) SLA metrics.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Coded reason for specimen rejection at accessioning or QC review. Populated only when lifecycle_status is \'rejected\'. Used for pre-analytical quality monitoring, root cause analysis, and SOP improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insufficient_volume|hemolyzed|lipemic|clotted|wrong_container|mislabeled|temperature_excursion|expired|contaminated|other — promote to reference product]']
  rin_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'RNA Integrity Number (RIN) score on a scale of 1-10 as measured by Bioanalyzer or TapeStation, indicating RNA quality. A RIN score ≥7 is typically required for RNA-Seq library preparation. Null for non-RNA specimens.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the biological material type collected from the subject. Drives downstream workflow routing, extraction protocols, and storage conditions in LabVantage LIMS. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blood|tissue|saliva|urine|plasma|cfDNA|FFPE|bone_marrow|buccal_swab|stool|other — promote to reference product]']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Coded identifier for the physical storage location of the specimen within the biorepository (e.g., freezer ID, rack, shelf, box, position). Enables physical retrieval, inventory management, and chain-of-custody tracking in LabVantage LIMS.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Required or actual storage temperature for the specimen in degrees Celsius (e.g., -80, -20, 4, 25). Used for cold-chain compliance monitoring, specimen integrity assessment, and deviation management.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Further granular classification of the specimen within its primary type (e.g., for blood: whole_blood, PBMC, serum; for tissue: fresh_frozen, FFPE_block, FFPE_section; for cfDNA: cell_free_plasma). Enables precise protocol selection and QC thresholds in bioinformatics pipelines.']
  tumor_content_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage of tumor cells (neoplastic cellularity) in the specimen as assessed by pathology review. Critical for somatic variant calling sensitivity, LOD (Limit of Detection) calculations, and clinical oncology reporting. Null for non-oncology specimens.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the specimen record was most recently modified in LabVantage LIMS or the data lakehouse. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail compliance, and Silver layer incremental processing in Databricks.']
  use_restriction_code varchar [note: 'Coded data use restriction applicable to this specimen and its derived genomic data, aligned with GA4GH Data Use Ontology (DUO) codes (e.g., GRU: General Research Use, HMB: Health/Medical/Biomedical, DS: Disease-Specific). Governs permissible downstream research and data sharing activities.']
  use_type varchar [note: 'Intended use classification for the specimen: clinical diagnostic testing (IVD), research (RUO), internal QC (Quality Control), assay validation, proficiency testing (CAP/CLIA), or Research Use Only (RUO). Determines applicable regulatory framework, data handling requirements, and reporting obligations.']
  volume_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume of the specimen in microliters (µL) at the time of accessioning or most recent measurement. Used for aliquot planning, minimum volume QC checks, and assay feasibility assessment. Applicable to liquid specimens (blood, plasma, urine, saliva, cfDNA). Null for solid specimens where mass_mg is used instead.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a biological specimen collected from a subject (human, animal, or environmental source). Captures specimen identity, type classification (blood, tissue, saliva, FFPE, cfDNA, etc.), collection site, collection date/time, collection method, volume/mass, matrix type, PHI de-identification flag and tier, IRB protocol linkage, consent status, current lifecycle status (accessioned, in-processing, extracted, archived, depleted, disposed), and LIMS accession number from LabVantage. This is the SSOT for all biological material entering the genomics workflow and the anchor entity for chain-of-custody traceability.'
}

Table sample.aliquot {
  aliquot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the aliquot record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the aliquot data product within the sample domain. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a physical sub-divided specimen unit managed as an independent trackable resource in LabVantage LIMS.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.audit_finding. Business justification: Audit findings may cite specific aliquots (e.g., labeling errors, storage violations). Labs link findings to aliquots for evidence and corrective action.']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Change controls may affect aliquots (e.g., new container types, storage condition changes). Labs link change controls to affected aliquots for traceability and audit trails.']
  complaint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.complaint. Business justification: Complaints about aliquot quality (insufficient volume, wrong concentration) require linking to the specific aliquot for root cause analysis and corrective action.']
  container_id varchar [note: 'FK to sample.container']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Aliquots trigger deviations (volume discrepancies, labeling errors, storage violations). Labs link aliquots to deviations for traceability and corrective action. Critical for GxP compliance.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Aliquots are prepared and consumed for specific customer service orders (sequencing runs, array processing). Required for inventory consumption tracking, billing reconciliation, and linking lab materi']
  instrument_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_run. Business justification: Aliquots are loaded into specific instrument runs for sequencing/array analysis. Critical for sample-to-data lineage traceability, correlating aliquot QC with run performance, and investigating failed']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Aliquots failing specifications (concentration out of range, purity issues) are nonconformances requiring disposition decisions. Labs link aliquots to NCRs for traceability and corrective action.']
  parent_aliquot_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent aliquot when this record represents a secondary or tertiary sub-division (aliquot-of-aliquot). Null for first-generation aliquots derived directly from the specimen. Enables multi-level aliquot hierarchy traversal.']
  preparation_reagent_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Aliquots are prepared using specific reagent types (extraction kits, normalization buffers, stabilization reagents). Needed for protocol compliance verification, method validation studies, and ensurin']
  reagent_kit_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Aliquots are created using specific reagent kits (extraction, library prep). Business need: protocol compliance verification, batch effect analysis in sequencing QC, reagent lot traceability for faile']
  sample_consent_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) consent record governing the permissible uses of this aliquot. Ensures that downstream workflows (sequencing, research, clinical) are authorized under the applicable consent scope. Critical for HIPAA, GDPR, and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating parent specimen from which this aliquot was derived. Supports parent-child lineage traceability back to the source biological specimen. Null if this aliquot is a top-level sub-division directly from the primary specimen.']
  stability_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.stability_study. Business justification: Stability studies use aliquots as test samples at each time point. Labs maintain traceability to aliquots tested for regulatory compliance and shelf life determination.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location record defining the physical storage unit (freezer, refrigerator, ambient rack) where this aliquot is currently housed. Supports chain-of-custody and cold-chain compliance tracking.']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Validation studies use aliquots as test samples for repeatability, reproducibility, and accuracy studies. Labs maintain traceability to aliquots used for regulatory compliance.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_order. Business justification: Aliquots are direct inputs to manufacturing processes (library prep, QC runs, sequencing). This link supports material consumption tracking, work order costing, batch genealogy, and regulatory traceab']
  aliquot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the aliquot within the sub-division hierarchy. \'primary\' indicates first-generation aliquot from specimen; \'secondary\' and \'tertiary\' indicate downstream sub-divisions; \'pooled\' indicates a combined aliquot from multiple sources; \'reference\' indicates a reference material aliquot.']
  aliquoting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this aliquot was physically created from the parent specimen or parent aliquot. Represents the principal real-world business event timestamp for this entity. Used for pre-analytical variable tracking, stability calculations, and chain-of-custody documentation.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Unique barcode label affixed to the physical container holding this aliquot. Used for barcode-driven tracking, scanning at workstations, and chain-of-custody verification throughout laboratory workflows in LabVantage LIMS.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Date on which the original biological specimen was collected from the subject. Inherited from the parent specimen record. Critical for calculating specimen age, assessing pre-analytical variable impact, and meeting regulatory reporting timelines under CLIA and CAP.']
  concentration decimal(18,6) [note: 'Measured concentration of the biological analyte (e.g., DNA, RNA, protein) in the aliquot. The unit of measure is captured in concentration_unit. Critical for library preparation normalization in Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) workflows and Quality Control (QC) acceptance criteria.']
  concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the concentration value recorded in the concentration field. Common units include ng/µL (nanograms per microliter) for DNA/RNA quantification, nM (nanomolar) for library quantification, and copies/µL for digital Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) applications.']
  container_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the physical container holding the aliquot. \'in_use\' indicates container holds active aliquot material; \'empty\' indicates container has been emptied; \'discarded\' indicates container has been disposed of; \'pre_registered\' indicates container registered in LabVantage LIMS prior to receiving material (empty container inventory management).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this aliquot record was first created in the source LabVantage LIMS system and ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports audit trail requirements under GxP, CLIA, and SOX financial reporting for laboratory operations.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the aliquot was physically disposed of or destroyed per retention policy, consent withdrawal, or regulatory requirement. Populated when lifecycle_status transitions to \'disposed\' or \'destroyed\'. Required for audit trail completeness under GxP and HIPAA.']
  disposal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the disposal or destruction of the aliquot. Supports regulatory audit trails and consent management reporting. \'consent_withdrawn\' is critical for GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure and HIPAA consent revocation workflows.']
  dna_integrity_number decimal(4,2) [note: 'DNA Integrity Number (DIN) score measured by TapeStation, ranging from 1 (fully degraded) to 10 (fully intact). Applicable to genomic DNA aliquots. Used to assess suitability for Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS) and Whole Exome Sequencing (WES) library preparation. Null for non-DNA aliquots.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the aliquot is considered expired and should not be used in clinical or research workflows. Calculated based on specimen type, storage conditions, and validated stability data per Standard Operating Procedure (SOP). Triggers automated alerts in LabVantage LIMS for disposal or review.']
  freeze_thaw_count varchar [note: 'Number of freeze-thaw cycles the aliquot has undergone since creation. Excessive freeze-thaw cycles degrade nucleic acid integrity and protein activity. Used to enforce maximum freeze-thaw limits per Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) and to flag aliquots that may be unsuitable for sensitive assays.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this aliquot within the parent-child hierarchy. Level 1 indicates a direct sub-division of the primary specimen; level 2 indicates an aliquot-of-aliquot; and so on. Enables efficient hierarchy traversal queries and enforces maximum sub-division depth policies.']
  initial_volume_ul decimal(12,4) [note: 'Original volume of biological material dispensed into the aliquot container at the time of aliquoting, expressed in microliters (µL). Provides the baseline for volume consumption tracking and yield calculations.']
  is_phi_deidentified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) associated with this aliquot has been de-identified in accordance with HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination methods. True indicates de-identified; False indicates PHI is present and access controls apply. Governs data sharing eligibility for research and external collaborations.']
  label varchar [note: 'Human-readable label or name assigned to the aliquot for identification in laboratory workflows, reports, and LIMS displays. May include specimen accession number, aliquot sequence suffix, or study-specific naming conventions.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the aliquot in its operational lifecycle. \'available\' indicates ready for use; \'consumed\' indicates fully used in a workflow; \'depleted\' indicates volume exhausted; \'reserved\' indicates allocated to a pending workflow; \'quarantined\' indicates held pending Quality Control (QC) review; \'disposed\' indicates removed per retention policy; \'destroyed\' indicates intentionally destroyed per protocol. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|consumed|depleted|reserved|quarantined|disposed|destroyed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lims_aliquot_code varchar [note: 'The native aliquot identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS at the time of accessioning or aliquoting. Used as the operational cross-reference key between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the LIMS source system.']
  purity_ratio_260_230 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Absorbance ratio at 260nm to 230nm measured by spectrophotometry. Indicates contamination by chaotropic salts, EDTA, or carbohydrates. Acceptable range is typically 2.0–2.2. Complements the A260/A280 ratio for comprehensive nucleic acid Quality Control (QC) assessment.']
  purity_ratio_260_280 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Absorbance ratio at 260nm to 280nm measured by spectrophotometry (e.g., NanoDrop). Indicates nucleic acid purity; acceptable DNA purity is typically 1.8–2.0 and RNA purity 2.0–2.2. Values outside range may indicate protein or solvent contamination affecting downstream Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) performance.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Intended downstream use of the aliquot. Drives workflow routing in LabVantage LIMS and determines which bioinformatics pipelines or Quality Control (QC) processes the aliquot will enter. Values include sequencing (Next-Generation Sequencing/NGS workflows), qc (Quality Control testing), archive (long-term storage), backup (redundancy), assay_validation (Clinical Assay Validation), and research (Research and Development).']
  qc_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by laboratory personnel during Quality Control (QC) assessment. Documents observations, deviations, conditional pass justifications, or remediation actions taken. Supports audit trail requirements under Good Practice Regulations (GxP) and CLIA.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Result of the Quality Control (QC) assessment for this aliquot. \'pass\' indicates aliquot meets all acceptance criteria for its intended purpose; \'fail\' indicates one or more criteria not met; \'pending\' indicates QC not yet completed; \'conditional_pass\' indicates aliquot approved with documented exceptions; \'not_required\' indicates QC waived per Standard Operating Procedure (SOP).']
  rack_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the storage rack, plate, or box within the storage unit where this aliquot is positioned. Combined with well_position provides the precise physical location for robotic retrieval and manual locating in biobank operations.']
  rin_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'RNA Integrity Number (RIN) score measured by Bioanalyzer or TapeStation, ranging from 1 (fully degraded) to 10 (fully intact). Applicable to RNA aliquots only. Minimum RIN thresholds are defined per assay type (e.g., RIN ≥ 7 for RNA-seq library preparation). Null for non-RNA aliquots.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Biological specimen type of the aliquot material (e.g., whole blood, plasma, serum, saliva, tissue, FFPE, cell line, DNA extract, RNA extract, cfDNA). Inherited from the parent specimen and critical for downstream workflow routing and assay compatibility checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|saliva|tissue|ffpe|cell_line|dna_extract|rna_extract|cfdna|urine|csf|buffy_coat — promote to reference product]']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Required storage temperature for this aliquot in degrees Celsius (°C). Common values include -80°C (ultra-low for long-term nucleic acid storage), -20°C (standard freezer), 4°C (refrigerated), and ambient (15–25°C). Drives cold-chain compliance monitoring and storage assignment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this aliquot record in the source LabVantage LIMS system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit trail completeness under GxP and ISO 15189.']
  use_restriction_code varchar [note: 'Standardized data use restriction code governing permissible downstream uses of this aliquot and its derived data. Codes follow GA4GH/dbGaP conventions: GRU (General Research Use), HMB (Health/Medical/Biomedical), DS (Disease-Specific), POA (Population Origins/Ancestry), NPU (Non-Profit Use Only), RUO (Research Use Only). Enforced during data access governance reviews.']
  volume_ul decimal(12,4) [note: 'Current measured volume of biological material in the aliquot container, expressed in microliters (µL). Decremented as material is consumed in workflows. Critical for determining aliquot sufficiency for downstream assays such as Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) library preparation.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for volume fields (volume_ul and initial_volume_ul). Standard unit is microliters (µL) for most genomics laboratory workflows; milliliters (mL) may be used for larger biobank specimens.']
  well_position varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric position identifier within the storage rack, plate, or box (e.g., A01, B12, H08 for a 96-well plate layout). Used for precise physical localization of the aliquot within its storage container for robotic liquid handling and manual retrieval.']

  Note: 'Represents a physical sub-division of a parent specimen created during aliquoting workflows, including the container holding it. Tracks aliquot volume, concentration, unit of measure, container identity (barcode, type, manufacturer, lot number, material, volume capacity), position in storage rack/plate/well, aliquot purpose (sequencing, QC, archive, backup), current lifecycle status, and container status (in-use, empty, discarded). Maintains parent-child lineage back to the originating specimen and supports multi-level aliquot hierarchies (aliquot-of-aliquot). Also serves as the master record for empty container inventory when pre-registered in LabVantage. Managed with barcode-driven tracking.'
}

Table sample.accession {
  accession_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the accession record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — represents the formal intake event of a specimen into laboratory custody.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Accessions represent received samples from customer accounts. Required for order fulfillment tracking, billing reconciliation, customer-specific SLA enforcement, and regulatory classification routing.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Clinical accessioning creates billable events generating AR invoices. Essential for revenue cycle management, linking laboratory orders to billing, tracking days sales outstanding, and reconciling tes']
  authorized_orderer_id varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier (NPI) or internal identifier of the ordering physician or clinical provider who requisitioned the test. Used for result reporting, billing, and clinical communication. Classified as confidential.']
  clinical_evidence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_evidence. Business justification: Accessioned samples in clinical validation studies require linkage to evidence records for chain-of-custody documentation, protocol compliance verification, and audit trail completeness for regulatory']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory technician or scientist who performed the physical accessioning of the specimen. Required for chain-of-custody accountability and GxP audit trail compliance.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Accessioned samples are tagged with the intended reference genome at intake to ensure downstream pipeline compatibility and prevent misrouting. Required for sample routing decisions, assay selection, ']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating clinical or research order that triggered specimen collection and submission. Links the accession to the order management domain, enabling traceability from test requisition through laboratory intake.']
  lab_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory site that physically received and accessioned the specimen. Supports multi-site operations, routing logic, and site-level compliance reporting under CLIA and CAP.']
  lims_workflow_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the LabVantage workflow instance assigned to this accession for processing routing. Links the accession to the specific laboratory workflow queue (e.g., DNA extraction, library preparation, sequencing run setup) activated upon acceptance.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.opportunity. Business justification: Accessioned samples for commercial sequencing/diagnostic services (not research) must track to the sales opportunity for billable service tracking, revenue recognition, and customer invoicing. Core co']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the de-identified or pseudonymized patient/subject record associated with this specimen. Classified as restricted PHI under HIPAA. Used for clinical traceability while maintaining PHI separation per de-identification protocols.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Accessioned samples are collected under R&D projects. Linking accessions to projects enables project-level cohort analysis, R&D spend allocation to sample processing, IRB compliance reporting by proje']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample master record in the Sample Management domain. Establishes the chain-of-custody link between the accession intake event and the canonical sample identity record.']
  accession_number varchar [note: 'LIMS-generated, externally-visible unique identifier assigned to the specimen at the point of intake into LabVantage. Serves as the primary chain-of-custody reference number communicated to ordering clinicians, research teams, and downstream bioinformatics pipelines. Format: ACC-{YYYY}-{8-digit sequence}.']
  accession_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the accession record within LabVantage. Drives downstream routing: \'received\' = physically in lab but not yet reviewed; \'in_review\' = condition assessment underway; \'accepted\' = cleared for processing; \'rejected\' = specimen rejected and not processed; \'on_hold\' = pending additional information or consent; \'cancelled\' = order cancelled prior to processing.']
  accessioning_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the specimen was formally registered and accessioned into the LIMS by the receiving technician. May differ from receipt_timestamp if there is a delay between physical receipt and system registration. Used for TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations.']
  arrival_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the physical condition of the specimen upon arrival at the laboratory. \'acceptable\' = meets pre-analytical quality criteria; \'compromised\' = degraded but may still be processable with notation; \'rejected\' = fails minimum quality criteria and cannot be processed. Triggers rejection workflows and recollection requests when set to \'rejected\'.']
  assay_panel_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the genomic assay panel or test menu requested for this specimen (e.g., WGS, WES, NGS gene panel, SNP array, CRISPR screen). Drives workflow routing to the appropriate laboratory workstation and bioinformatics pipeline in LabVantage.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the biological specimen was collected from the patient or subject. Critical for pre-analytical stability assessments, TAT calculations, and clinical result interpretation. Distinct from receipt_timestamp.']
  collection_site varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the clinical facility, hospital, clinic, or research site where the specimen was collected from the patient. Supports multi-site clinical trial tracking, referring provider analytics, and logistics routing.']
  collection_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the specimen was collected from the patient or subject, including timezone. Used for time-sensitive pre-analytical stability calculations (e.g., RNA degradation, cell viability) and clinical result contextualization.']
  collector_name varchar [note: 'Name of the phlebotomist, nurse, or clinician who collected the specimen from the patient. Required for chain-of-custody documentation and pre-analytical error investigation. Classified as confidential business data.']
  condition_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of observed pre-analytical issues or anomalies noted at specimen receipt (e.g., hemolysis, lipemia, insufficient volume, broken container, temperature excursion, clotted sample). Supports root cause analysis and pre-analytical QC reporting.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Status of the patient or subject\'s informed consent for specimen use in research or clinical testing. \'consented\' = valid consent obtained; \'declined\' = subject declined consent; \'pending\' = consent process initiated but not completed; \'withdrawn\' = previously consented subject has withdrawn consent; \'not_required\' = clinical specimen exempt from research consent requirements.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of container or tube in which the specimen was collected and transported (e.g., EDTA tube, SST tube, PAXgene RNA tube, cryovial, FFPE block, dry swab). Determines extraction protocol eligibility and pre-analytical QC criteria. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: edta_tube|sst_tube|paxgene_rna|paxgene_dna|cryovial|ffpe_block|dry_swab|streck_tube|citrate_tube — promote to reference product]']
  courier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the courier or logistics provider that transported the specimen to the laboratory (e.g., FedEx, World Courier, BioMatik, internal courier). Used for vendor performance tracking and temperature excursion investigations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this accession record was first created in the source LIMS system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and GxP 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  external_requisition_number varchar [note: 'Test requisition number assigned by the ordering facility\'s own system (e.g., hospital LIS, EHR order number). Enables bidirectional reconciliation between the external ordering system and the internal LIMS accession record.']
  gxp_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accession falls under GxP (Good Practice Regulations) requirements including GMP, GLP, or GCP. True = GxP controls apply, requiring full audit trail, electronic signature compliance per 21 CFR Part 11, and enhanced documentation in MasterControl.']
  irb_protocol_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Institutional Review Board (IRB) protocol under which the specimen was collected and consented for research use. Required for research specimens to ensure ethical compliance. Null for purely clinical specimens not under a research protocol.']
  phi_deidentified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) associated with this accession has been de-identified per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination methods. True = PHI has been de-identified and the record may be used in downstream analytics without PHI restrictions; False = PHI is present and full access controls apply.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Processing priority assigned to the accession at intake. STAT (Short Turn-Around Time) specimens require immediate processing; routine specimens follow standard workflow queues; urgent specimens are expedited but below STAT; research specimens follow non-clinical timelines. Drives SLA (Service Level Agreement) enforcement and workflow queue prioritization in LabVantage.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the specimen was physically received at the laboratory, marking the transition from external courier custody to laboratory custody. This is the principal business event timestamp for the accession transaction. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  recollection_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recollection request has been issued to the ordering provider or collection site due to specimen rejection or insufficient quality. True = recollection has been requested; False = no recollection required.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accession is associated with a regulatory submission (e.g., FDA IND/NDA clinical trial specimen, CE-IVD validation study, EMA submission). True = specimen is part of a regulated submission context requiring enhanced documentation and audit trail.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason a specimen was rejected at accessioning. Populated only when arrival_condition = \'rejected\'. Used for pre-analytical QC trending, rejection rate KPI reporting, and recollection request generation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insufficient_volume|hemolysis|lipemia|clotted|wrong_tube|unlabeled|temperature_excursion|expired_container|broken_container|mislabeled — promote to reference product]']
  shipment_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Courier or carrier tracking number associated with the shipment in which the specimen was transported to the laboratory. Enables shipment-level chain-of-custody traceability and temperature excursion investigation via carrier records.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the LabVantage LIMS source system for this accession. Used for data lineage traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer and the operational system of record, supporting reconciliation and re-ingestion workflows.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the biological specimen material submitted for analysis (e.g., whole blood, FFPE tissue, saliva, buccal swab, plasma, serum, urine, bone marrow, CSF). Drives QC thresholds, extraction protocols, and assay eligibility rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|ffpe_tissue|saliva|buccal_swab|plasma|serum|urine|bone_marrow|csf|fresh_frozen_tissue — promote to reference product]']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Laboratory storage location code (freezer, rack, box, position) where the specimen was placed upon acceptance. Supports physical specimen retrieval, inventory management, and chain-of-custody continuity within the LIMS.']
  tat_target_hours varchar [note: 'Target Turnaround Time (TAT) in hours from specimen receipt to result delivery, as defined by the SLA (Service Level Agreement) for the assigned priority level and assay type. Used for TAT compliance monitoring and SLA breach alerting.']
  temperature_at_receipt_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured temperature of the specimen or shipping container at the time of receipt, expressed in degrees Celsius. Used to assess cold-chain compliance and flag potential temperature excursions that may affect specimen integrity and downstream assay validity.']
  transport_temperature_code varchar [note: 'Required transport temperature condition under which the specimen was shipped to the laboratory. Used to verify compliance with specimen stability requirements and to flag potential temperature excursions during transit.']
  tube_label_code varchar [note: 'Barcode or label identifier physically affixed to the specimen container at the time of collection or receipt. Used for physical specimen tracking, label verification at accessioning, and reconciliation against the LIMS accession number.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this accession record was most recently modified in the source LIMS system. Supports change detection in the Silver Layer ETL pipeline and GxP audit trail requirements.']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'Intended use classification of the specimen at the time of accessioning. \'clinical\' = IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) or LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) clinical testing; \'research\' = RUO (Research Use Only) investigation; \'QC\' = Quality Control specimen for assay performance monitoring; \'validation\' = analytical validation or clinical validation study specimen.']
  volume_received_ml decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured volume of the specimen received at the laboratory, expressed in millilitres. Used to assess adequacy for requested assays, trigger insufficient volume rejection flags, and support aliquoting planning. Represents the principal quantitative measurement of the accession transaction.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the volume_received_ml field. Typically mL for liquid specimens; mg or g for solid tissue specimens. Allows the schema to accommodate non-liquid specimen types without type conversion.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal intake and registration of a specimen into the laboratory information management system (LabVantage). Records accession number (LIMS-generated), receipt date/time, receiving lab site, courier/shipment reference, condition on arrival (acceptable, compromised, rejected), accessioning technician, priority level (STAT, routine), and linkage to the originating clinical order (FK to order domain). Represents the chain-of-custody entry point — the moment a specimen transitions from external custody to laboratory custody.'
}

Table sample.biobank_location {
  biobank_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a physical storage location within the biorepository hierarchy. Primary key for this master resource entity.']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Change controls often affect storage locations (e.g., equipment upgrades, temperature setpoint changes). Labs link change controls to affected locations for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Biobank storage equipment (ultra-low freezers, LN2 tanks, automated storage systems) are capitalized fixed assets requiring depreciation tracking. Essential for asset management, maintenance cost allo']
  parent_location_biobank_location_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent location in the biorepository hierarchy (e.g., a box\'s parent is a rack, a rack\'s parent is a shelf). Enables recursive hierarchy traversal for chain-of-custody and retrieval workflows.']
  access_restriction_level varchar [note: 'Physical and logical access control classification for this storage location. Governs who may retrieve specimens from this location. \'restricted\' and above require role-based authorization in LabVantage LIMS and physical access badge control. Relevant for PHI-linked specimens and BSL-classified materials.']
  backup_power_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage unit is connected to an uninterruptible power supply (UPS) or emergency generator backup. Critical for specimen integrity risk assessment and business continuity planning for irreplaceable biobank specimens.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Machine-readable barcode or 2D matrix code (e.g., DataMatrix, QR) affixed to the physical storage unit or position label. Scanned during specimen accessioning, retrieval, and chain-of-custody verification in LabVantage LIMS.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which this storage location was formally commissioned and made available for specimen storage following successful IQ/OQ/PQ qualification. Marks the start of the location\'s operational lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this biobank location record was first created in the system of record (LabVantage LIMS or enterprise data platform). Supports audit trail requirements under GxP, ISO 13485, and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which this storage location was permanently decommissioned and removed from active service. Null for locations still in service. Required for equipment lifecycle tracking and regulatory audit trails.']
  equipment_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer of the physical storage equipment (e.g., \'Thermo Fisher Scientific\', \'Panasonic\', \'Chart Industries\'). Required for vendor qualification records and regulatory equipment documentation.']
  equipment_model varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model designation of the physical storage equipment at this location (e.g., \'Thermo Scientific TSX Series -80°C Freezer\'). Used for equipment qualification documentation, spare parts management, and maintenance scheduling.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number of the physical storage equipment. Required for equipment qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ) documentation, warranty tracking, and regulatory audit trails per ISO 13485 and GxP requirements.']
  facility_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the physical facility or campus where this storage location resides (e.g., \'SDCA\' for San Diego Campus A). Links to the enterprise site master for regulatory site registration and GxP compliance reporting.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Level of this location within the biorepository physical hierarchy: facility → room → freezer → shelf → rack → box → position (well/slot). Enables hierarchical chain-of-custody traversal and capacity roll-up reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: facility|room|freezer|shelf|rack|box|position — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_cryogenic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location uses cryogenic conditions (liquid nitrogen vapor or immersion, or mechanical ultra-low temperature ≤ -130°C). Drives safety protocol assignment, PPE requirements, and cryogenic handling SOP routing.']
  is_gmp_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is subject to GMP regulations (e.g., for clinical trial specimens, IVD reagent intermediates, or GMP-grade biological materials). GMP-controlled locations require enhanced documentation, change control, and audit trail requirements.']
  is_phi_segregated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specimens stored at this location are physically segregated due to PHI linkage, requiring enhanced access controls and HIPAA-compliant chain-of-custody documentation. Supports de-identification workflow management.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent temperature calibration or verification performed on this storage unit. Required for GxP compliance and ISO 15189 accreditation. Tracked in MasterControl and SAP QM.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this biobank location record. Required for change control audit trails under FDA 21 CFR Part 11, ISO 13485, and GxP data integrity requirements.']
  lims_location_code varchar [note: 'Native location identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS for this storage position. Used for system-of-record cross-referencing, data lineage tracing, and integration reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the operational LIMS.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code assigned to this storage location within the biorepository management system (LabVantage LIMS). Used for labeling, chain-of-custody documentation, and specimen retrieval workflows. Example: FRZ-A01-SH3-RK2-BX05.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name for the storage location, used in operational reports and LIMS displays. Example: \'Freezer A1 – Shelf 3 – Rack 2 – Box 05\'.']
  monitoring_sensor_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the continuous temperature/humidity monitoring sensor assigned to this storage location. Links to the environmental monitoring system (EMS) for real-time excursion alerting and 21 CFR Part 11-compliant audit trails.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next temperature calibration or verification of this storage unit. Drives preventive maintenance work orders in SAP S/4HANA PM and compliance alerts in MasterControl.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or non-conformance observations associated with this storage location (e.g., \'Door seal replaced 2024-03-15\', \'Reserved for clinical trial XYZ specimens only\').']
  occupied_positions varchar [note: 'Current number of specimen positions occupied at this storage location as tracked by LabVantage LIMS. Enables real-time utilization monitoring and triggers capacity alerts when approaching full occupancy.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of this biobank storage location. \'active\' = available for specimen storage; \'maintenance\' = temporarily unavailable due to servicing; \'quarantine\' = specimens held pending investigation; \'decommissioned\' = permanently retired from service.']
  position_column varchar [note: 'Numeric column designator for a specific well or slot position within a box or rack (e.g., \'1\', \'12\'). Applicable at the position/well hierarchy level. Combined with position_row to form the full position address.']
  position_row varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric row designator for a specific well or slot position within a box or rack (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'H\'). Applicable at the position/well hierarchy level. Combined with position_column to form the full position address.']
  qualification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current GxP equipment qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ) expires and requalification is required. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling and regulatory compliance alerts in MasterControl.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'GxP qualification status of the storage equipment: \'qualified\' = Installation Qualification (IQ), Operational Qualification (OQ), and Performance Qualification (PQ) completed and approved; \'requalification_required\' = qualification expired or triggered by maintenance event. Mandatory for GMP/GLP-regulated specimen storage.']
  responsible_custodian varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the designated biorepository custodian or laboratory manager responsible for this storage location. Accountable for specimen integrity, access authorization, and regulatory compliance at this location.']
  rfid_tag varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier assigned to this storage unit for automated inventory scanning and real-time location tracking within the biorepository. Supports high-throughput specimen management workflows.']
  room_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory or storage room within the facility where this location physically resides. Used for access control, environmental monitoring zone assignment, and GxP area classification.']
  specimen_type_compatibility varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of specimen types approved for storage at this location (e.g., \'whole_blood,plasma,FFPE,DNA,RNA,cell_line\'). Enforced during specimen accessioning in LabVantage LIMS to prevent storage condition mismatches. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|FFPE|DNA|RNA|cell_line|tissue|urine|saliva — promote to reference product]']
  storage_medium varchar [note: 'Physical medium used for cryogenic or ambient storage at this location. Distinguishes mechanical freezer cooling from liquid nitrogen (LN2) vapor-phase or immersion-phase storage, which affects specimen viability protocols and safety requirements.']
  storage_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical storage unit at this location level (e.g., freezer, refrigerator, liquid nitrogen tank, ambient cabinet). Drives temperature monitoring SOP assignment and equipment maintenance scheduling.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Upper acceptable temperature limit in degrees Celsius for this storage location. Excursions above this threshold trigger alarms and non-conformance events per SOP.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Lower acceptable temperature limit in degrees Celsius for this storage location. Excursions below this threshold trigger alarms and non-conformance events per SOP.']
  temperature_setpoint_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Nominal target storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this location (e.g., -80.00 for ultra-low freezer, -196.00 for liquid nitrogen, 4.00 for refrigerator, 22.00 for room temperature). Critical for specimen integrity and regulatory compliance.']
  total_capacity_positions varchar [note: 'Maximum number of specimen positions (wells, slots, or tubes) this storage location can physically accommodate. Used for capacity planning, utilization reporting, and biorepository space management.']

  Note: 'Master data entity representing a physical storage location within the biorepository hierarchy — freezer, shelf, rack, box, and well/position. Captures location code, storage unit type, temperature setpoint (e.g., -80°C, -196°C LN2, 4°C, RT), facility site, capacity (total positions, occupied positions), and current operational status. Enables precise chain-of-custody tracking and retrieval of specimens and aliquots.'
}

Table sample.storage_event {
  storage_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each storage state change or custody transfer event record in the chain-of-custody audit trail. Primary key of the storage_event product.']
  aliquot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific aliquot (sub-divided portion of a specimen) involved in this storage event. Nullable when the event applies to the primary specimen rather than a derived aliquot. Populated from LabVantage LIMS aliquot tracking.']
  destination_location_biobank_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location (freezer, rack, shelf, box, or position) to which the specimen or aliquot was moved or placed. Null for disposal or external transfer events where no internal destination exists.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Storage events (temperature excursions, unauthorized access, location errors) trigger deviations. Labs document which storage event caused each deviation for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  irb_submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) protocol under which the specimen was collected and is being stored. Ensures that storage and custody actions are performed within the bounds of approved research protocols and informed consent agreements.']
  lims_workflow_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the LIMS-driven workflow task or work order that triggered or is associated with this storage event. Links the storage action to the broader laboratory workflow context (e.g., assay preparation, QC run, clinical testing batch).']
  primary_storage_biobank_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location (freezer, rack, shelf, box, or position) from which the specimen or aliquot was retrieved or transferred. Null for initial placement events where no prior location exists.']
  primary_storage_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory operator or technician who physically performed the storage event (placement, retrieval, relocation, or transfer). Sourced from Oracle Cloud HCM workforce records and LabVantage LIMS user registry.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary biological specimen (parent sample) involved in this storage event. Links to the specimen master record in LabVantage LIMS for full sample metadata and IRB consent context.']
  tertiary_storage_acknowledged_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who provided the electronic acknowledgment or e-signature for this storage event. May differ from the operator who performed the physical action, supporting dual-control and supervisory sign-off workflows required under GxP.']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the electronic acknowledgment or e-signature was applied to this storage event record. Distinct from the event_timestamp (when the physical action occurred). Required for FDA 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail completeness.']
  chain_of_custody_sequence varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing integer representing the ordinal position of this storage event within the complete chain-of-custody history for the specimen or aliquot. Enables ordered reconstruction of the full custody timeline for forensic genomics, legal, and regulatory audit purposes.']
  container_barcode varchar [note: 'The barcode or 2D matrix code printed on the physical container (tube, vial, plate, cryovial) holding the specimen or aliquot at the time of this storage event. Scanned by the operator during the event to confirm physical identity and prevent sample mix-up errors.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical container holding the specimen or aliquot during this storage event. Determines applicable storage conditions, handling protocols, and compatible storage unit configurations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this storage event record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Represents the record audit creation time, distinct from the event_timestamp which captures when the physical action occurred.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage event involved a deviation from the applicable SOP or standard storage protocol. True triggers a deviation investigation workflow in the quality management system (Veeva Vault or MasterControl) and requires documented justification.']
  electronic_acknowledgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storage event has received a valid electronic acknowledgment (e-signature) from the responsible operator or custodian in compliance with FDA 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements. True confirms the event is electronically signed and locked.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for this storage event, formatted as SE-{YYYY}-{YYYYMMDD}-{sequence}. Used in audit reports, chain-of-custody documentation, and regulatory submissions to uniquely reference a specific custody transfer or placement event.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the storage event record. Indicates whether the event has been completed, is pending electronic acknowledgment, was cancelled, encountered a failure condition, or is under supervisory review for compliance purposes.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise real-world date and time at which the storage state change or custody transfer physically occurred, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. This is the principal business event time, distinct from the record audit timestamps. Critical for chain-of-custody sequencing and GxP compliance.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Categorical discriminator identifying the nature of the storage state change. Drives chain-of-custody logic and audit trail classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial_placement|relocation|retrieval|return|inter_section_transfer|external_transfer|disposal|quarantine_placement — promote to reference product]']
  external_site_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external facility, partner laboratory, or clinical site to which the specimen or aliquot was transferred during an inter-site or external transfer event. Populated only for event_type = external_transfer. Supports chain-of-custody documentation for multi-site genomics studies.']
  external_transfer_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Shipping or courier tracking number associated with an external specimen transfer event. Enables end-to-end shipment traceability for specimens transferred to external sites, partner labs, or clinical collaborators. Populated only for event_type = external_transfer.']
  freeze_thaw_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of freeze-thaw cycles the specimen or aliquot has undergone up to and including this storage event. Excessive freeze-thaw cycles degrade nucleic acid and protein integrity; this count is critical for sample quality assessment in genomic and clinical workflows.']
  is_immutable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage event record has been locked and made immutable following electronic acknowledgment and audit finalization. Immutable records cannot be modified or deleted, satisfying FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and GxP requirements for tamper-evident audit trails.']
  lims_event_reference varchar [note: 'The native event or transaction identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS for this storage event.
