Enables bidirectional traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  phi_deidentified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specimen or aliquot involved in this storage event has been de-identified in accordance with HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination methods. True means PHI has been removed or anonymized; False means the specimen retains linkage to identifiable patient data.']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specimen or aliquot was placed into or removed from quarantine status as part of this storage event. Quarantined samples are restricted from use in assays or downstream workflows pending investigation or quality review.']
  quarantine_reason varchar [note: 'Standardized reason for placing the specimen or aliquot into quarantine. Populated only when quarantine_flag is True. Drives investigation workflows and determines the path to quarantine release or disposal.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining the business justification for the storage event. Used for operational reporting, compliance audits, and workflow analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: routine_storage|retrieval_for_assay|transfer_to_external_site|quarantine|inter_section_transfer|disposal_scheduled|emergency_retrieval — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context or justification for the storage event beyond the standardized reason code. Captures operator notes, special circumstances, or deviations from standard operating procedures (SOPs) that require documentation.']
  required_temp_max_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The maximum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the specimen or aliquot type involved in this event, as defined by the applicable SOP or specimen handling protocol. Used in conjunction with required_temp_min_c to define the acceptable temperature window.']
  required_temp_min_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The minimum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the specimen or aliquot type involved in this event, as defined by the applicable SOP or specimen handling protocol. Used to evaluate whether a temperature excursion occurred.']
  sop_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing the storage event type performed. Links to the controlled document in MasterControl or Veeva Vault for audit trail completeness and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this storage event was originated or ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between the lakehouse and upstream operational systems.']
  specimen_volume_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume of the specimen or aliquot in microliters (µL) at the time of this storage event. Captured to track volume consumption across retrieval events and to flag low-volume specimens that may be insufficient for planned assays.']
  storage_condition_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the required storage condition category for the specimen or aliquot at the destination location. Drives storage unit assignment logic and temperature monitoring alert thresholds in the LIMS and facility management systems.']
  temperature_at_event_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recorded ambient or storage unit temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of the storage event. Captured from connected monitoring systems or manually entered by the operator. Used to assess sample integrity and document temperature excursion conditions.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion (deviation from the required storage temperature range) was detected or reported in association with this storage event. True signals a potential sample integrity risk requiring investigation and documentation per GxP and CLIA requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this storage event record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Tracks the last update for audit trail completeness and data lineage purposes. Immutable records will not have an updated_timestamp after locking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every storage state change and custody transfer for a specimen or aliquot — initial placement, relocation, retrieval, return, and inter-section transfers. Captures event type, source location, destination location, event timestamp, operator ID, custodian identity, reason code (routine storage, retrieval for assay, transfer to external site, quarantine), temperature excursion flag, and electronic acknowledgment. Provides the full chain-of-custody audit trail required for GxP, CLIA, and forensic genomics compliance. Serves as the immutable custody log for all in-facility specimen movements.'
}

Table sample.sample_consent_record {
  sample_consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the informed consent record within the Genomics Biotech data platform. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — this entity represents a binding ethical and regulatory agreement governing specimen use.']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the consent form document as managed in Veeva Vault regulatory document management system. Enables traceability to the exact document version presented to the subject.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified staff member (e.g., clinical coordinator, genetic counselor, principal investigator) who administered and witnessed the informed consent process. Links to the workforce/personnel record.']
  nda_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Data Use Agreement (DUA) governing external sharing of genomic data derived from this specimen. Required when permitted_use_data_sharing is true. Managed in Veeva Vault.']
  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical or research study protocol under which the specimen was collected and consent was obtained. Links to the governing study protocol document in Veeva Vault.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological specimen governed by this consent record. Links the consent agreement to the physical sample in the LabVantage LIMS sample tracking system.']
  subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the de-identified research subject or patient from whom consent was obtained. Links to the subject/participant master record in the sample domain.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Informed consent documentation is required evidence in regulatory submissions for clinical validation studies, IRB compliance verification, and post-market surveillance plans. Essential for FDA/notifi']
  assent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assent was obtained from a minor subject in addition to parental/guardian consent. Applicable for pediatric genomics studies. Required for IRB compliance in pediatric research protocols.']
  biobank_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to long-term biobanking and storage of their specimen for future unspecified research use. Governs specimen retention policies in LabVantage LIMS.']
  consent_collection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which informed consent was obtained and documented. \'paper\' — wet ink signature; \'electronic\' — eConsent platform; \'verbal_with_waiver\' — IRB-approved verbal consent with waiver of written documentation; \'remote_electronic\' — remote eConsent; \'delegated\' — consent delegated to authorized representative.']
  consent_collection_site varchar [note: 'Name or code of the clinical or research site where informed consent was obtained (e.g., hospital, clinic, research center). Supports multi-site study management and site-level compliance reporting.']
  consent_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the research subject or patient provided signed informed consent. This is the principal business event date for the consent agreement and is required for regulatory compliance timelines.']
  consent_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the consent authorization expires and must be renewed or re-consented. Null for open-ended broad consents. Used to trigger re-consent workflows and enforce data use restrictions.']
  consent_form_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the IRB-approved informed consent form presented to and signed by the subject (e.g., v1.0, v2.1). Critical for regulatory audit trails to confirm the subject received the current approved version.']
  consent_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for documenting special circumstances, subject questions, deviations from standard consent process, or other relevant observations recorded during the consent encounter. Confidential — not for general access.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consent record. \'active\' — consent is valid and in force; \'withdrawn\' — subject has revoked consent; \'expired\' — consent period has lapsed without renewal; \'pending\' — awaiting subject signature or IRB approval; \'superseded\' — replaced by a newer consent version; \'revoked\' — administratively invalidated.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consent model applied. \'broad\' permits use across multiple future research purposes; \'specific\' restricts use to a defined study; \'tiered\' allows subject to select permitted uses; \'re_consent\' indicates updated consent obtained for changed scope; \'emergency_waiver\' denotes IRB-approved waiver; \'assent\' applies to minors. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broad|specific|tiered|re_consent|emergency_waiver|assent — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements under FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and GxP.']
  deidentification_status varchar [note: 'Current PHI de-identification status of the specimen and associated data. \'identified\' — PHI present; \'de_identified\' — HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination method applied; \'limited_dataset\' — HIPAA limited dataset with DUA; \'pseudonymized\' — GDPR pseudonymization applied; \'anonymized\' — irreversibly anonymized.']
  future_contact_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to being contacted for future research studies or re-consent requests. Governs subject outreach activities in Salesforce CRM.']
  gdpr_lawful_basis varchar [note: 'The GDPR Article 6/9 lawful basis under which personal and special-category genomic data is processed for subjects in EU/EEA jurisdictions. \'not_applicable\' for non-EU subjects. Required for GDPR compliance documentation.']
  incidental_findings_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to disclosure of incidental or secondary genomic findings identified during sequencing or analysis (e.g., ACMG secondary findings). Distinct from primary study results.']
  interpreter_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a language interpreter was used during the consent process. When true, additional interpreter documentation is required per GCP guidelines.']
  irb_protocol_number varchar [note: 'The unique protocol number assigned by the Institutional Review Board (IRB) governing the study or research program under which this consent was obtained. Required for regulatory traceability and audit.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code of the consent form presented to the subject (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr\', \'zh-CN\'). Ensures the subject received consent documentation in their primary language as required by FDA and ICH guidelines.']
  legally_authorized_representative boolean [note: 'Indicates whether consent was obtained from a Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) rather than the subject directly (e.g., for incapacitated adults or minors). Triggers additional documentation requirements.']
  lims_consent_record_code varchar [note: 'The native consent record identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS. Enables bidirectional traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS system of record.']
  permitted_use_clinical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has authorized use of their specimen for clinical diagnostic purposes, including In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) assay validation and Laboratory Developed Test (LDT) workflows under CLIA/CAP.']
  permitted_use_commercial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has authorized use of their specimen and genomic data for commercial product development, including reagent validation, instrument development, and product commercialization activities.']
  permitted_use_data_sharing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has authorized sharing of their de-identified genomic data with external parties, including public genomic databases (e.g., dbGaP, GEO), research consortia, or partner institutions under the NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy.']
  permitted_use_research boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has authorized use of their specimen and associated genomic data for internal Research and Development (R&D) purposes. Governs bioinformatics pipeline processing and variant analysis workflows.']
  phi_authorization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has provided explicit HIPAA authorization for use and disclosure of their Protected Health Information (PHI) in conjunction with specimen and genomic data processing.']
  re_consent_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which re-consent must be obtained from the subject. Null if re-consent is not required. Used to manage compliance timelines and subject outreach scheduling.']
  re_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a re-consent is required from the subject due to protocol amendments, expanded data use scope, or consent expiry. Triggers re-consent workflow in LabVantage LIMS and MasterControl.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory jurisdiction applicable to this consent record, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes. Determines which regulatory framework (FDA, EMA, IVDR, etc.) governs consent requirements and data handling obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|EU|GBR|CAN|AUS|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA|OTHER — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_of_results_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to receive individual genomic research results or incidental findings. Governs clinical reporting workflows and genetic counseling referral processes.']
  sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sponsoring organization or institution for the study under which consent was obtained. May be Genomics Biotech itself or an external sponsor for collaborative studies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Required for change tracking and audit trail compliance under FDA 21 CFR Part 11.']
  veeva_document_code varchar [note: 'The document identifier in Veeva Vault for the executed consent form document. Provides a direct link to the regulatory document management system for audit and inspection readiness.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'The date on which the subject formally withdrew consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. When populated, triggers downstream data quarantine and use-restriction enforcement per GDPR right-to-erasure and HIPAA requirements.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for consent withdrawal. Required for regulatory reporting and adverse event tracking. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: subject_request|adverse_event|protocol_deviation|lost_to_followup|deceased|administrative|other — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of IRB-approved informed consent obtained from a research subject or patient for specimen collection and use. Captures consent form version, IRB protocol number, consent date, consent type (broad, specific, re-consent), scope of permitted use (research, clinical, commercial, data sharing), withdrawal date if applicable, and PHI handling authorization. Links specimen to the governing ethical and regulatory framework. Supports HIPAA, GDPR, and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance.'
}

Table sample.subject {
  subject_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the biological source individual (research participant, patient, donor, or animal model) within the Genomics Biotech data platform. Primary key for the subject master record. Role: MASTER_PARTY.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Research subjects enrolled through customer accounts (clinical sites, research institutions). Required for multi-site study management, institutional consent tracking, data access control, and site-sp']
  family_id varchar [note: 'De-identified family or pedigree identifier linking related subjects (e.g., proband and family members in trio or family-based genomic studies). Enables inheritance pattern analysis, variant segregation studies, and familial genomics workflows such as WGS (Whole Genome Sequencing) trio analysis. Null for unrelated subjects.']
  gene_annotation_track_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_annotation_track. Business justification: Research subjects are analyzed with a consistent annotation track across longitudinal timepoints to ensure study reproducibility. Required for publication data freeze, cross-timepoint variant comparis']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Research subjects/cohorts are enrolled under a specific reference genome build to ensure consistency across longitudinal timepoints and multi-site studies. Required for study design, cross-study data ']
  irb_submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IRB or ethics committee protocol under which this subject was enrolled. Links the subject to the governing research protocol, consent framework, and permissible data use scope. Required for regulatory submissions, audit trails, and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance.']
  pharmacogenomics_marker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.pharmacogenomics_marker. Business justification: Research subjects are genotyped for PGx markers in drug response studies and clinical trials. Required for clinical trial stratification, GWAS, and pharmacogenomics outcomes research. Marker-level sub']
  age_at_enrollment varchar [note: 'Age of the subject in whole years at the time of study enrollment, derived from year_of_birth and enrollment_date at data ingestion. Stored as a pre-computed integer to support cohort age-stratification queries without requiring DOB. Enables pediatric vs. adult cohort segmentation and age-related genomic analyses.']
  ancestry_group varchar [note: 'Self-reported or genetically inferred ancestry/ethnicity group of the subject, used for population genomics stratification, Minor Allele Frequency (MAF) reference panel selection, and pharmacogenomics analyses. Stored as a controlled vocabulary term aligned with gnomAD and 1000 Genomes Project population labels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AFR|AMR|EAS|EUR|MID|SAS|ASH|LAT|OTH — promote to reference product]']
  biobank_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to long-term biobanking of residual specimens for future research use beyond the primary study. Governs specimen retention policies, storage duration limits, and permissible secondary use of aliquots in LabVantage LIMS.']
  biological_sex varchar [note: 'Biological sex of the subject as recorded at enrollment, distinct from gender identity. Used for sex-stratified genomic analyses, reference genome selection (e.g., chrX/chrY copy number), population genomics cohort stratification, and clinical assay interpretation.']
  collection_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the subject\'s specimens were collected. Drives GDPR data residency requirements, export control compliance for genomic data, and population genomics geographic stratification.']
  collection_site_code varchar [note: 'Coded identifier for the clinical or research site where the subject\'s specimens are collected. Used for multi-site study analytics, site-level QC (Quality Control) performance tracking, and regulatory site accountability. De-identified at the site level to avoid geographic re-identification of rare-disease subjects.']
  consent_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IRB-approved informed consent record governing the permissible uses of this subject\'s biological specimens and genomic data. Managed in the access-controlled consent management system (separate from this de-identified record). Enables traceability of data use to specific consent scope without embedding PHI in this product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subject record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under GxP, HIPAA, and GDPR, and enables data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  data_access_tier varchar [note: 'Permissible data access level for genomic data derived from this subject, aligned with NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy tiers. Open access data is publicly available; registered access requires user registration; controlled access requires dbGaP or equivalent approval; restricted access is limited to named investigators under a data use agreement (DUA).']
  deidentification_method varchar [note: 'The HIPAA-recognized or GDPR-compliant method applied to de-identify or pseudonymize the subject record. HIPAA Safe Harbor removes 18 specified identifiers; Expert Determination uses statistical analysis to certify re-identification risk is very small. Pseudonymization replaces direct identifiers with a code while retaining a linkage key in a separate access-controlled system. Drives data access tier assignment and downstream PHI handling rules.']
  deidentification_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the subject record\'s de-identification level per HIPAA and GDPR frameworks. Fully de-identified records have no re-identification pathway; pseudonymized records retain a coded linkage to PHI managed separately; limited dataset records retain certain geographic and date information under a data use agreement (DUA). Governs permissible downstream data uses and sharing.']
  disease_indication varchar [note: 'Primary disease indication or phenotype associated with the subject, encoded using a standardized ontology term (e.g., ICD-10, OMIM, HPO). Drives cohort assembly for clinical genomics studies, variant interpretation in clinical assay workflows, and regulatory submission stratification. Free-text or ontology code depending on study protocol.']
  disease_indication_ontology varchar [note: 'Ontology or coding system used to encode the disease_indication field (e.g., ICD-10, OMIM, HPO, SNOMED-CT). Enables interoperability across clinical, research, and regulatory systems and ensures consistent variant interpretation and cohort queries.']
  family_relationship varchar [note: 'Role of this subject within the family pedigree identified by family_id. Used for trio-based variant calling, de novo mutation detection, and inheritance pattern analysis in WGS/WES (Whole Exome Sequencing) workflows. Null for unrelated subjects.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR applies to this subject\'s data, based on the subject\'s collection country and data processing location. When True, GDPR Article 9 special category data protections apply to genetic data, and data subject rights (access, erasure, portability) must be honored. Drives EU data residency and transfer compliance workflows.']
  genomic_data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has provided explicit consent for their genomic data to be shared with external repositories (e.g., dbGaP, EGA, UK Biobank). Required for NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance and international data sharing agreements. When False, data must not be submitted to public or controlled-access repositories.']
  hipaa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether HIPAA Privacy Rule applies to this subject\'s data, based on whether the subject is a US patient or the data is held by a HIPAA-covered entity or business associate. When True, PHI handling, minimum necessary standard, and de-identification requirements apply.']
  incidental_findings_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to receive incidental or secondary genomic findings (e.g., ACMG SF v3.2 actionable secondary findings) identified during WGS/WES analysis. Drives the return-of-results workflow and clinical reporting obligations for pathogenic variants in medically actionable genes.']
  lims_subject_code varchar [note: 'Native subject identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS at the time of accessioning. Used for cross-system traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS workflow. Enables reconciliation and lineage tracking back to the system of record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for study coordinator or clinical staff annotations regarding the subject record, such as protocol deviations, special handling instructions, or enrollment exceptions. Must not contain direct PHI. Reviewed during data quality audits and IRB annual reviews.']
  phi_linkage_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PHI linkage record exists for this subject in the access-controlled consent and identity management system. When True, the subject_code can be re-linked to direct PHI by authorized personnel only. When False, the record is fully de-identified with no re-linkage pathway. Supports HIPAA Safe Harbor compliance auditing.']
  primary_assay_type varchar [note: 'The primary genomic assay type intended or performed for this subject\'s specimens. Drives bioinformatics pipeline selection, QC (Quality Control) metric thresholds, and data product routing. WGS (Whole Genome Sequencing), WES (Whole Exome Sequencing), targeted panel, SNP (Single Nucleotide Polymorphism) array, RNA-seq, and methylation array are the principal assay categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|SNP_array|RNA_seq|methylation_array|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recontact_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to being re-contacted for additional studies, incidental findings disclosure, or updated consent. Relevant for longitudinal genomics studies and return-of-results workflows. When False, no re-contact is permitted without a new consent process.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number for the subject record, incremented on each update. Supports optimistic concurrency control, SCD Type 2 history tracking, and GxP audit trail requirements for regulated genomics data. Version 1 represents the initial record creation.']
  reference_genome_build varchar [note: 'The reference genome assembly version used as the default for bioinformatics pipeline alignment and variant calling for this subject\'s specimens (e.g., GRCh38 for human, GRCm39 for mouse). Ensures consistency between sample metadata and downstream BAM/VCF (Variant Call Format) outputs. Critical for multi-cohort studies where genome build consistency is required.']
  reidentification_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level that the subject record could be re-linked to a specific individual, based on the de-identification method applied and residual quasi-identifiers present (e.g., rare disease, small geographic area, unusual ancestry). Determined by the privacy officer or expert determination process. Drives data access controls, sharing restrictions, and consent re-contact requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this subject record was originated or last updated. Enables data lineage traceability and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver Layer and upstream operational systems (primarily LabVantage LIMS for sample domain records).']
  species varchar [note: 'Biological species of the subject. Determines applicable reference genome, annotation pipeline, and regulatory classification (human vs. animal model). Critical for bioinformatics pipeline routing and IRB/IACUC compliance.']
  subject_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, de-identified alphanumeric code assigned to the subject at accessioning or enrollment. Used as the operational identifier across LIMS, bioinformatics pipelines, and clinical reports without exposing direct PHI. Equivalent to the participant ID or pseudonym in study documentation.']
  subject_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the biological source individual by their role in the study or clinical workflow. Drives consent requirements, regulatory handling, and data access controls. Research participants and patients require IRB/ethics board oversight; animal models require IACUC approval.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subject record in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental pipeline processing, and GxP audit trail compliance.']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory use classification for data and specimens derived from this subject. RUO (Research Use Only) specimens are not intended for clinical diagnosis; IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) specimens are used in validated clinical assays; LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) specimens are used in laboratory-developed clinical tests. Drives regulatory handling, labeling, and data access controls.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject formally withdrew consent or was removed from the study. Triggers downstream data use restriction workflows per HIPAA and GDPR right-to-erasure requirements. Populated only when subject_status is \'withdrawn\'.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for subject withdrawal from the study or clinical program, recorded when subject_status transitions to \'withdrawn\'. Supports regulatory audit trails, protocol deviation reporting, and IRB annual review documentation. Populated only when applicable.']
  year_of_birth varchar [note: 'Four-digit birth year of the subject. Stored instead of full date of birth (DOB) to comply with HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification requirements (45 CFR §164.514(b)(2)(i)), which prohibit retention of specific birth dates. Enables age-range calculations and cohort age-stratification for population genomics without re-identification risk.']

  Note: 'De-identified or pseudonymized master record of the biological source individual (research participant, patient, donor, or animal model) from whom specimens are collected. Stores subject code, species, biological sex, year of birth (not full DOB for de-identification), ethnicity/ancestry group (for population genomics), disease indication, de-identification method applied (HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination), de-identification tier (fully de-identified, pseudonymized, limited dataset), and re-identification risk level. Does NOT store direct PHI — PHI linkage is managed via consent_record and access-controlled separately per HIPAA Safe Harbor. Supports cohort membership tracking via external study enrollment references.'
}

Table sample.collection_event {
  collection_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the specimen collection event record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Collection events occur at customer sites. Essential for site-specific protocol compliance monitoring, collection kit logistics coordination, site performance metrics, and regulatory audit trails. Rea']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.campaign. Business justification: Collection events for biobank recruitment campaigns or population genomics studies must link to marketing campaign for effectiveness measurement (specimen collection volume vs. campaign spend), lead g']
  clinical_evidence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_evidence. Business justification: Clinical specimen collection events in validation studies must be linked to evidence records for protocol compliance verification, data integrity audits, and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  collection_kit_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Collection events use specific collection kit products (PAXgene, Tempus, saliva kits). Critical for tracking kit performance across collection sites, supplier quality monitoring, and correlating pre-a']
  collection_kit_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Collection events use specific collection kit products. Currently denormalized as collection_kit_sku. Business need: kit lot traceability for regulatory compliance, collection protocol compliance veri']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) document in MasterControl that governed this collection event. Ensures traceability to the approved procedure version used, supporting GxP compliance and audit readiness.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Collection events incur field operations costs (phlebotomy labor, collection kits, logistics) charged to cost centers. Necessary for clinical trial budgeting, site cost allocation, and per-patient cos']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trained personnel (phlebotomist, nurse, field technician, or research coordinator) who performed the physical specimen collection. Used for competency tracking, deviation investigation, and chain-of-custody documentation.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Collection kits are materials with SKUs and lot numbers. Linking enables kit inventory management, automated consumption tracking, lot traceability for specimen provenance, and recall management. Remo']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the research study, clinical trial, or diagnostic project under which this collection event was performed. Enables cohort-level aggregation and study-specific pre-analytical QC reporting.']
  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) approved protocol under which this collection event was conducted. Mandatory for research specimens; ensures regulatory traceability of consent and ethical approval.']
  sample_consent_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the informed consent record obtained from the subject prior to collection. Links to the IRB consent management entity. Mandatory for research and clinical genomics specimens; supports HIPAA, GDPR, and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary biological sample record created as a result of this collection event. Links to the sample master entity in the Sample Management domain for downstream LIMS workflow assignments, aliquoting, and storage tracking.']
  subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the human subject, patient, or research participant from whom the specimen was collected. Links to the subject/patient master record. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category. Classified restricted/pii_identifier as it directly identifies a research or clinical participant.']
  training_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.training_record. Business justification: Training records document that collectors are qualified to perform collections. Labs link training to collection events for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical or research study visit during which this collection event occurred. Supports longitudinal sample tracking and time-point-based genomic analysis (e.g., baseline vs. post-treatment comparisons).']
  anatomical_site varchar [note: 'Standardized anatomical location from which the specimen was collected (e.g., left antecubital vein, right breast core, nasopharynx). Encoded using SNOMED CT or ICD-10 topography codes where applicable. Critical for oncology and clinical genomics interpretation.']
  collection_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country where the specimen was collected. Required for international regulatory compliance (GDPR, IVDR), cross-border data transfer governance, and geographic cohort stratification.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Calendar date (yyyy-MM-dd) on which the specimen was collected. Stored separately from collection_timestamp to support date-level partitioning, reporting, and cases where only the date (not exact time) is known or required.']
  collection_event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the collection event, assigned by LabVantage LIMS at accessioning. Used for chain-of-custody documentation, deviation reports, and cross-system traceability. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  collection_kit_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number of the collection kit (e.g., blood draw tube, biopsy kit, saliva collection kit, liquid biopsy tube) used during the collection event. Critical for pre-analytical QC, reagent traceability, and recall management. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM lot tracking.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'Standardized method used to obtain the biological specimen. Drives pre-analytical QC interpretation and NGS library preparation protocol selection. Values: venipuncture (blood draw), biopsy (tissue), swab (mucosal/surface), saliva_kit (self-collected saliva), liquid_biopsy (cell-free DNA from blood), fine_needle_aspiration (FNA cytology).']
  collection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional collector observations, special circumstances, or contextual notes recorded at the time of collection that do not fit into structured fields. Supports clinical interpretation and deviation investigation.']
  collection_order_number varchar [note: 'External order or requisition number from the ordering system (e.g., SAP SD sales order, clinical order management system) that triggered this collection event. Enables end-to-end traceability from customer order to specimen collection.']
  collection_site_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical, research, or environmental site where the specimen was collected (e.g., hospital phlebotomy unit, field sampling location, clinical trial site). Links to the site/location master record.']
  collection_sop_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the SOP document used at the time of collection (e.g., v1.2, v3.0). Stored alongside the SOP ID to capture the exact approved version in effect, as SOP versions change over time.']
  collection_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient or transport temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of or immediately following specimen collection. Used to assess cold-chain compliance and pre-analytical stability. Critical for cfDNA liquid biopsy and RNA specimens.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the physical specimen was collected from the subject or environment. This is the principal real-world event time and is critical for pre-analytical time calculations (e.g., time-to-processing, cold-chain compliance). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  collection_tube_count varchar [note: 'Number of collection tubes or containers used during the collection event. Relevant for blood draw events with multiple tube types (EDTA, SST, Streck cfDNA). Supports aliquot planning and chain-of-custody reconciliation.']
  collection_urgency varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the collection event that governs Turnaround Time (TAT) expectations and workflow prioritization in LabVantage LIMS. \'stat\' = immediate priority; \'urgent\' = same-day; \'routine\' = standard TAT.']
  collection_volume_ml decimal(8,3) [note: 'Volume of specimen collected, expressed in millilitres (mL). Used for pre-analytical QC assessment (e.g., short draw detection), yield estimation, and aliquot planning. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category (non-monetary transaction).']
  collection_volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the collection volume field. Typically mL for liquid specimens; mg or g for solid tissue specimens; uL for micro-volume collections.']
  collector_certification_number varchar [note: 'Professional certification or license number of the individual who performed the collection (e.g., phlebotomy certification, nursing license). Required for CLIA and CAP compliance documentation and chain-of-custody integrity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the collection event record was first created in LabVantage LIMS. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA tracking. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  deviation_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the pre-analytical deviation observed during the collection event (e.g., \'Specimen hemolyzed due to difficult venipuncture\', \'Collection tube inverted fewer than required times\'). Populated only when deviation_flag is True.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-analytical deviation occurred during this collection event (e.g., hemolysis, incorrect tube, delayed processing, temperature excursion). True = deviation recorded; False = no deviation. Triggers downstream QC review and may affect NGS data quality interpretation.']
  deviation_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the pre-analytical deviation type. Used for trending, root cause analysis, and QC reporting. Populated only when deviation_flag is True.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the collection event workflow. \'scheduled\' = collection planned but not yet performed; \'in_progress\' = collector has initiated; \'completed\' = specimen successfully collected and handed off; \'cancelled\' = event voided before collection; \'deviated\' = completed with documented pre-analytical deviation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  fasting_status varchar [note: 'Pre-analytical fasting status of the subject at the time of collection. Relevant for metabolomics, lipid panels, and certain genomic assays where fasting state affects analyte stability and downstream data quality interpretation.']
  hemolysis_index varchar [note: 'Semi-quantitative assessment of hemolysis in the collected blood specimen, graded on a standardized scale (none, 1+ to 4+). Hemolysis is a key pre-analytical variable that degrades DNA/RNA quality and affects NGS library preparation success rates.']
  lims_accession_number varchar [note: 'Accession number assigned by LabVantage LIMS at the time of specimen receipt and registration. Serves as the primary LIMS-side identifier for chain-of-custody tracking and workflow assignment.']
  phi_deidentified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) associated with this collection event has been de-identified per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination method. True = de-identified; False = identifiable PHI present. Governs data access controls and downstream sharing permissions.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the specimen was received at the processing laboratory. Used to calculate time-in-transit and total pre-analytical time (collection to receipt). Critical for TAT reporting and cold-chain compliance verification.']
  scheduled_collection_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for the collection event as defined in the study protocol or clinical order. Compared against collection_date to calculate collection compliance and protocol adherence metrics.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Biological specimen type collected. Determines downstream processing protocols, storage conditions, and applicable NGS assay types (e.g., WGS, WES, liquid biopsy ctDNA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|buffy_coat|saliva|tissue_ffpe|tissue_fresh_frozen|buccal_swab|urine|csf|stool|bone_marrow|amniotic_fluid — promote to reference product]']
  time_to_processing_minutes varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in minutes between specimen collection timestamp and the start of primary processing (e.g., centrifugation, nucleic acid extraction). A critical pre-analytical variable for NGS data quality; exceeding thresholds may trigger QC flags or deviation records.']
  transport_condition varchar [note: 'Required transport temperature condition for the collected specimen from collection site to processing laboratory. Drives cold-chain logistics and is compared against actual temperature readings for pre-analytical QC.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the collection event record in LabVantage LIMS. Supports audit trail requirements and Silver Layer incremental load detection. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory use classification of the specimen collected. RUO = Research Use Only; IVD = In Vitro Diagnostic; LDT = Laboratory Developed Test; clinical_trial = collected under an approved clinical trial protocol; quality_control = internal QC specimen. Determines applicable regulatory framework (FDA, CLIA, CAP) and data handling requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of the physical specimen collection activity. Captures collection date/time, collection site (anatomical location or environmental site), collection method (venipuncture, biopsy, swab, saliva kit, liquid biopsy), collector ID, collection kit lot number, pre-analytical conditions (fasting status, time from collection to processing), and any collection deviations. Provides the pre-analytical metadata critical for NGS data quality interpretation.'
}

  lims_workflow_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the workflow assignment record in LabVantage LIMS. Primary key for tracking specimen or aliquot assignment to laboratory workflows.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: LIMS workflows for IVD assays must operate under approved regulatory scope (CLIA, CE-IVD, FDA clearance). Required for compliance verification, audit trails, and ensuring workflows match approved inte']

Business justification: Workflows consume specific assay kits (sequencing reagents, array chips, library prep kits). Business need: protocol compliance verification, cost allocation per sample, inventory consumption tracking']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory instrument or equipment assigned for this workflow execution. Links to instrument master record for utilization tracking and maintenance correlation.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the processing batch when multiple samples are processed together. Format: BATCH-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX. Enables batch-level Quality Control (QC) tracking and efficiency analysis.']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Change controls may affect workflows (e.g., new protocols, instrument changes). Labs link change controls to affected workflow assignments for traceability and regulatory compliance.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Workflow execution errors (missed steps, timing violations, instrument failures) generate deviations. Labs link workflow assignments to deviations for process control and CAPA.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: LIMS workflows execute research protocols. Linking workflow assignments to experiments enables study data package assembly, protocol deviation tracking, audit trail completeness for GxP studies, and e']
  gene_annotation_track_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_annotation_track. Business justification: Workflow assignments specify which annotation track to use for variant calling and interpretation. Annotation version is locked during assay validation and must remain consistent for regulatory compli']

Required for gene-specific assay routing, pharmacogenomics panel execution, and']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Workflow assignments (WGS, WES, targeted panels) are configured for a specific reference genome build. Bioinformatics pipelines, alignment indices, and variant calling parameters are genome-specific. ']
  genomic_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genomic_region. Business justification: Workflow assignments specify target regions for sequencing (panel-based testing, exome capture). Required for panel-based test routing, QC region selection, and coverage uniformity assessment. Region-']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Workflow assignments execute lab tasks to fulfill customer orders. Critical for TAT tracking, SLA compliance monitoring, billable activity tracking, and linking lab operations to revenue. Standard in ']

Essential for run sheet generation, capacity planning, workflow completion tracking, and linking planned work to executed runs. Alr']

Business justification: PGx workflow assignments target specific markers for genotyping (star allele calling, diplotype determination).   quaternary_lims_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or system account that last modified this workflow assignment record. System-generated audit field for accountability and compliance tracking.']

  tertiary_lims_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or system account that created this workflow assignment record. System-generated audit field for accountability and compliance tracking.']
  training_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.training_record. Business justification: Training records document that operators are qualified to perform specific workflows. Labs link training to workflow assignments for GxP compliance and audit trails.']

Required for CAP/CLIA compliance,']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_order. Business justification: LIMS workflow assignments trigger and track manufacturing work orders for sample processing operations. This link enables MES-LIMS integration, production scheduling, resource allocation, electronic b']
  workflow_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Workflow assignments consume labor, reagents, and instrument time charged to specific cost centers. Critical for activity-based costing, variance analysis, and operational expense tracking. Existing c']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory workflow or assay protocol definition. Links to the workflow master record defining the procedure steps and requirements.']
  workflow_reagent_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Workflows (sequencing runs, array processing, qPCR assays) consume specific reagent lots. Critical for cost accounting, correlating assay performance with reagent lot quality, protocol validation stud']
  actual_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual elapsed processing time in hours from start to completion. Calculated from actual_start_timestamp to completion_timestamp. Used for performance analysis and process improvement.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when workflow processing actually began. Populated when status transitions to in-progress. Used for actual Turnaround Time (TAT) measurement.']
  assay_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific assay or test protocol within the workflow type. Format: AAA-NNNN where AAA is assay category and NNNN is protocol version number.']
  assay_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the assay or test protocol. Examples: Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), Single Nucleotide Polymorphism (SNP) Array, Quantitative Polymerase Chain Reaction (qPCR).']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the workflow assignment. Format: WFA-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX where YYYYMMDD is assignment date and XXXXXX is sequential counter.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sample was assigned to this workflow queue. Marks the start of the workflow assignment lifecycle for Turnaround Time (TAT) calculation.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this workflow assignment is billable to customer or research grant. True for billable clinical or contract research workflows, false for internal Quality Control (QC) or Research and Development (R&D) workflows.']
  cap_accredited_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this workflow was performed in a College of American Pathologists (CAP) accredited laboratory section. True for CAP-accredited workflows, false otherwise.']
  clia_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this workflow execution meets Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) regulatory requirements for clinical testing. True for CLIA-compliant workflows, false for Research Use Only (RUO) workflows.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when workflow processing was completed successfully. Populated when status transitions to completed. Used for actual Turnaround Time (TAT) reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this workflow assignment record was first created in LabVantage LIMS. System-generated audit field for data lineage and compliance tracking.']
  expected_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard processing time in hours for this workflow type based on Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) specifications. Used for capacity planning and bottleneck identification.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why workflow failed. Populated only when workflow_status is failed. Used for Quality Assurance (QA) investigation and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) tracking.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why workflow is on-hold. Populated only when workflow_status is on_hold. Enables root cause analysis of processing delays and bottleneck identification.']
  irb_protocol_number varchar [note: 'Institutional Review Board (IRB) protocol identifier for research samples. Required for human subject research to ensure ethical compliance. Format: IRB-YYYY-NNNNNN. Null for non-research clinical samples.']
  lab_section varchar [note: 'Organizational unit or department within the laboratory responsible for executing this workflow. Enables workload distribution and resource allocation tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this workflow assignment record was last updated. System-generated audit field for change tracking and data lineage.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special handling instructions, protocol deviations, or operational observations during workflow execution. Used for audit trail and knowledge transfer.']
  phi_present_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) is associated with this workflow assignment. True for identifiable clinical samples, false for de-identified or Research Use Only (RUO) samples. Drives Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) compliance controls.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Processing priority for the workflow assignment. STAT indicates immediate processing required, urgent for expedited processing, routine for standard Turnaround Time (TAT), research for non-time-sensitive Research Use Only (RUO) samples.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or protocol document used for this workflow execution. Format: vX.Y.Z for semantic versioning. Ensures regulatory compliance and audit trail.']
  qc_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from Quality Control (QC) reviewer documenting review findings, deviations, or conditional pass justifications. Required for failed or conditional_pass QC status.']
  qc_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when Quality Control (QC) review was completed. Populated when qc_status transitions from pending to passed, failed, or conditional_pass.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Quality Control (QC) review status for the workflow output. Pending indicates awaiting QC review, passed for QC approved, failed for QC rejected, conditional_pass for acceptable with documented deviations.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory category of the workflow. In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) for FDA/CE-IVD approved tests, Research Use Only (RUO) for non-diagnostic research, Laboratory Developed Test (LDT) for Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) validated tests, clinical trial for Institutional Review Board (IRB) approved studies.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for workflow processing to begin. Used for capacity planning and workload scheduling across laboratory sections.']
  sla_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time by which workflow must be completed to meet Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments. Calculated based on priority level and workflow type Turnaround Time (TAT) standards.']
  workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the workflow assignment in its lifecycle. Queued indicates awaiting processing, in-progress for active work, completed for successful finish, on-hold for temporary suspension, failed for technical failure, cancelled for business cancellation.']
  workflow_type varchar [note: 'Category of laboratory workflow assigned. Defines the high-level process type: library preparation for Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS), nucleic acid extraction, Quality Control (QC) analysis, clinical diagnostic assay, or research use only assay.']

  Note: 'Tracks the assignment of a specimen or aliquot to a specific laboratory workflow or assay queue within LabVantage LIMS. Records workflow type (library prep, DNA extraction, QC, clinical assay, research assay), assigned lab section, priority, scheduled start date, SLA due date, assigned technician, and current workflow status (queued, in-progress, completed, on-hold, failed). Enables turnaround-time monitoring, workload balancing, and bottleneck identification across the sample processing pipeline. Supports multi-step workflow chains where one assignment completion triggers the next.'
}

Table sample.extraction {
  extraction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the nucleic acid extraction record. Primary key.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the automated extraction instrument used (e.g., QIAcube, KingFisher Flex). Null if manual extraction.']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Change controls may affect extraction methods (e.g., new kits, protocol updates). Labs link change controls to affected extractions for traceability and regulatory compliance.']
  clinical_evidence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_evidence. Business justification: Extraction protocols used in clinical validation studies must be traceable to evidence packages for method validation, protocol compliance verification, and regulatory review of analytical procedures.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Extraction activities consume significant reagent kits, labor, and instrument time allocated to cost centers. Required for COGS calculation in clinical labs and R&D expense tracking in research operat']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Extraction failures (low yield, contamination, protocol deviations) are common quality events. Labs link extractions to deviations for process improvement and regulatory documentation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory technician or scientist who performed the extraction.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: Extractions execute experimental protocols. Linking extraction to experiment enables protocol compliance verification, data lineage for publications, assay validation documentation, and regulatory sub']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: DNA/RNA extractions are prepared for sequencing against a target reference genome. Extraction protocol parameters (input amount, elution volume) and QC thresholds are optimized for the intended genome']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Extractions are performed as part of customer service orders (sequencing, genotyping). Links lab processing steps to commercial transactions for billing, quality tracking, and regulatory documentation']
  kit_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Extraction uses specific kit products. Currently denormalized as extraction_kit_name. Business need: kit lot traceability for regulatory audits, cost-per-extraction calculation for pricing models, inv']
  kit_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Extraction uses specific reagent kit lots. Essential for correlating extraction yield/quality with reagent lot performance, investigating out-of-specification results, and maintaining GxP traceability']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Extraction kits are materials with material numbers in ERP/LIMS systems. Linking enables inventory consumption tracking, automated reordering, kit lot traceability for regulatory submissions, and CoA ']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source specimen or aliquot from which nucleic acid was extracted.']
  training_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.training_record. Business justification: Training records document that operators are qualified to perform extractions. Labs link training to extraction activities for audit trails and regulatory compliance.']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Extraction method validations use real extraction runs to demonstrate performance. Labs track which extractions were part of validation studies for regulatory submissions and method qualification.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_order. Business justification: Nucleic acid extraction is a controlled manufacturing operation executed via work orders in GMP/GLP environments. This link supports batch record documentation, process validation, deviation tracking,']
  a260_a230_ratio decimal(5,3) [note: 'Absorbance ratio at 260nm and 230nm wavelengths, indicating contamination by organic compounds or chaotropic salts. Expected range is 2.0-2.2 for pure nucleic acid.']
  a260_a280_ratio decimal(5,3) [note: 'Absorbance ratio at 260nm and 280nm wavelengths, indicating nucleic acid purity. Pure DNA typically has ratio ~1.8, pure RNA ~2.0. Lower values indicate protein contamination.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Unique barcode identifier assigned to the extracted nucleic acid aliquot for tracking and traceability.']
  concentration_ng_per_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Concentration of extracted nucleic acid measured in nanograms per microliter (ng/µL), typically measured by spectrophotometry or fluorometry.']
  contamination_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether contamination was detected during or after extraction (True = contamination detected, False = no contamination).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this extraction record was first created in the LIMS system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  dv200_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of RNA fragments greater than 200 nucleotides, used as quality metric for degraded RNA samples (e.g., FFPE). Values >30% are typically acceptable for NGS.']
  elution_volume_ul decimal(10,2) [note: 'Final elution volume of the extracted nucleic acid, measured in microliters (µL).']
  extraction_date date [note: 'Date when the nucleic acid extraction was performed, in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  extraction_method varchar [note: 'The nucleic acid extraction methodology applied to the specimen (e.g., column-based, magnetic bead, phenol-chloroform, manual, automated, salting-out).']
  extraction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the extraction process was completed, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  input_mass_mg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mass of input material used for extraction (applicable for solid tissue specimens), measured in milligrams (mg).']
  input_material_type varchar [note: 'Type of biological specimen used as input for extraction (e.g., whole blood, plasma, serum, tissue, saliva, buccal swab, urine, cerebrospinal fluid, FFPE, fresh frozen). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|tissue|saliva|buccal_swab|urine|csf|ffpe|fresh_frozen — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  input_volume_ul decimal(10,2) [note: 'Volume of input material used for extraction, measured in microliters (µL).']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Intended downstream application for the extracted nucleic acid (e.g., NGS library preparation, qPCR, array, ddPCR, storage, research, clinical testing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ngs_library_prep|qpcr|array|ddpcr|storage|research|clinical_testing — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this extraction record was last modified in the LIMS system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator notes, observations, or deviations from standard protocol during the extraction process.']
  nucleic_acid_type varchar [note: 'Type of nucleic acid extracted: DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid), RNA (ribonucleic acid), cfDNA (cell-free DNA), gDNA (genomic DNA), total nucleic acid, or mRNA (messenger RNA).']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or protocol used for extraction, ensuring traceability to documented procedures.']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the extraction failed QC criteria (e.g., low yield, poor purity ratios, degradation). Null if QC passed.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Quality control determination for the extraction: pass (meets all QC criteria), fail (does not meet QC criteria), pending (QC not yet complete), conditional_pass (marginal quality, proceed with caution).']
  quantification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to quantify the extracted nucleic acid (e.g., NanoDrop spectrophotometry, Qubit fluorometry, Bioanalyzer, TapeStation, qPCR, PicoGreen).']
  rin_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'RNA Integrity Number score (1-10 scale) indicating RNA quality and degradation level. Applicable only for RNA extractions. Higher scores indicate better integrity.']
  run_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch or run in which this extraction was performed, linking multiple extractions processed together.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical storage location of the extracted nucleic acid (e.g., freezer ID, rack position, box number) for inventory and retrieval.']
  storage_temperature_c varchar [note: 'Storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the extracted nucleic acid (e.g., -20°C for DNA, -80°C for RNA).']
  total_yield_ng decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total yield of extracted nucleic acid in nanograms (ng), calculated as concentration multiplied by elution volume.']

  Note: 'Records the nucleic acid extraction process applied to a specimen or aliquot — producing DNA, RNA, cfDNA, gDNA, or total nucleic acid. Captures extraction method (column-based, magnetic bead, manual, automated), instrument used, extraction kit lot number, input material type and volume, elution volume, yield (ng/µL), purity ratios (A260/A280, A260/A230), extraction date, operator, and pass/fail QC determination. Critical pre-analytical step linking specimen to library preparation in the NGS workflow.'
}

Table sample.qc_measurement {
  qc_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality control measurement record. Primary key for the qc_measurement data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory instrument used to perform the QC measurement. Links to instrument master data for calibration and maintenance tracking.']
  clinical_evidence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_evidence. Business justification: QC measurements from validation studies form the analytical performance data (precision, accuracy, LOD) in clinical evidence packages for regulatory submissions. Direct input to regulatory claims.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: QC measurements validate experimental protocols and assay performance. Linking measurements to experiments supports assay development validation, protocol optimization cycles, analytical performance s']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: QC measurements (fragment size distribution, coverage uniformity, GC bias) are evaluated against genome-specific expectations. Different reference genomes have different sequence complexity profiles a']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: QC measurements validate specimens meet customer order specifications (concentration, purity, integrity). Required for acceptance criteria verification, billing justification, and customer reporting. ']
  instrument_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch or run in which this measurement was performed. Groups multiple measurements performed together for batch-level QC and traceability.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: QC reagents and assay kits are materials. Linking enables reagent lot traceability, validation of QC results against reagent specifications, automated inventory depletion, and regulatory compliance (2']
  measurement_reagent_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: QC measurements (Qubit quantification, Bioanalyzer sizing, qPCR validation) use specific reagent/standard lots. Essential for assessing measurement validity, investigating out-of-specification results']
  original_measurement_qc_measurement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original QC measurement record if this is a repeat measurement. Enables traceability of repeat testing and comparison of results.']
  primary_qc_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory technician or operator who performed the QC measurement. Links to workforce/employee master data for accountability and training tracking.']
  qc_reagent_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: QC measurements use specific reagent products (Qubit assays, Bioanalyzer chips, TapeStation reagents). Business need: measurement validity verification, reagent lot correlation with QC failures, regul']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample or aliquot on which this QC measurement was performed. Links to the sample master record in LabVantage LIMS.']
  a260_a230_ratio decimal(10,4) [note: 'Ratio of absorbance at 260 nm to 230 nm, used to detect contamination from carbohydrates, salts, or phenol. Expected range is 2.0-2.2 for pure nucleic acids.']
  a260_a280_ratio decimal(10,4) [note: 'Ratio of absorbance at 260 nm to 280 nm, used to assess nucleic acid purity. Pure DNA typically has a ratio of ~1.8, pure RNA ~2.0. Values outside this range indicate contamination.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement was reviewed and approved by quality assurance personnel. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument used for this measurement was last calibrated. Ensures measurement accuracy and compliance with quality standards. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  concentration_ng_ul decimal(18,6) [note: 'Nucleic acid concentration measured in nanograms per microliter. Commonly used for DNA and RNA quantification via Qubit or NanoDrop.']
  control_sample_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this measurement was performed on a control sample (positive control, negative control, or reference standard) rather than a patient or research specimen. True indicates control.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Type of control sample used in the measurement, if applicable. Defines the role of the control in the quality assurance process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this QC measurement record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  din_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'DNA Integrity Number (DIN) score ranging from 1 (highly degraded) to 10 (highly intact). Measured via TapeStation electrophoresis. Assesses genomic DNA fragmentation.']
  extraction_method varchar [note: 'Method or kit used to extract nucleic acids from the specimen prior to QC measurement. Relevant for DNA/RNA quality assessment. May reference a specific protocol or commercial kit.']
  fragment_size_bp varchar [note: 'Average or modal fragment size of nucleic acid measured in base pairs. Determined via electrophoresis (Bioanalyzer, TapeStation, or Fragment Analyzer). Critical for library prep QC.']
  fragment_size_distribution varchar [note: 'Textual or encoded representation of the fragment size distribution profile. May include peak positions, distribution shape, or reference to an electropherogram file.']
  lims_record_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this measurement in the source Laboratory Information Management System (LabVantage LIMS). Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation.']
  lod_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the measured value is below the Limit of Detection (LOD) for the assay. True indicates below LOD. Critical for clinical and research interpretation.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Quantitative result of the QC measurement. The numeric value obtained from the instrument or assay. Precision supports high-resolution measurements.']
  measurement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by the operator regarding the measurement. May include observations about sample quality, instrument behavior, or deviations from SOP (Standard Operating Procedure).']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the QC measurement. Tracks the measurement from initiation through completion or failure.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the QC measurement was performed. Critical for traceability and TAT (Turnaround Time) tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Type of quality control measurement performed on the specimen. Defines the analytical method used for quality assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qubit_fluorometry|nanodrop_spectrophotometry|bioanalyzer_electrophoresis|tapestation_electrophoresis|din_score|rin_score|fragment_analyzer|qpcr_quantification|gel_electrophoresis — promote to reference product]']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this QC measurement record was last modified. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  pass_fail_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the measured value falls within the acceptable reference range. True indicates pass, False indicates fail. Drives pre-analytical QC gating decisions.']
  reference_range_lower decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower bound of the acceptable reference range for this measurement type. Used to determine pass/fail status and quality gating decisions.']
  reference_range_upper decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper bound of the acceptable reference range for this measurement type. Used to determine pass/fail status and quality gating decisions.']
  repeat_measurement_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this measurement is a repeat or retest of a previous measurement. True indicates repeat. Used for tracking measurement reproducibility and troubleshooting.']
  rin_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'RNA Integrity Number (RIN) score ranging from 1 (completely degraded) to 10 (fully intact). Measured via Bioanalyzer or TapeStation electrophoresis. Critical for RNA-seq quality.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Type of biological specimen or nucleic acid material on which the QC measurement was performed. Defines the material being assessed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|saliva|tissue|dna|rna|cdna|library|amplicon|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Storage temperature or condition of the specimen prior to QC measurement. Impacts nucleic acid stability and quality. Critical for pre-analytical quality assessment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the measured value. Standardized units ensure consistent interpretation across laboratories and workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ng_ul|ng|ug|pg|ng_ml|a260_a280_ratio|rin|din|bp|percent — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Captures quantitative and qualitative QC measurements performed on specimens, aliquots, or extracted nucleic acids at any stage of the sample lifecycle. Records measurement type (Qubit fluorometry, NanoDrop spectrophotometry, Bioanalyzer/TapeStation electrophoresis, DIN score, RIN score, fragment size distribution), instrument ID, reagent lot, measured value, unit, reference range, pass/fail flag, and measurement timestamp. Supports LOD tracking and pre-analytical QC gating decisions.'
}

Table sample.disposal_event {
  disposal_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each specimen or aliquot disposal event record in the Sample Management domain. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  aliquot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific aliquot being disposed of, when disposal applies to a sub-divided portion rather than the whole specimen. Nullable when the entire specimen is disposed.']
