  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Disposal activities incur waste management costs (biohazard disposal, autoclave operations, contractor fees) charged to cost centers. Required for environmental health and safety budgeting, regulatory']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Improper disposal (wrong method, missing documentation, unauthorized disposal) triggers deviations. Labs link disposal events to deviation records for audit trails and regulatory compliance.']
  gdpr_erasure_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure request that mandated this disposal event. Applicable for EU subject samples. Supports GDPR compliance documentation and data subject rights management.']
  primary_disposal_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified personnel (scientist, lab manager, or compliance officer) who formally authorized the disposal event. Required for GxP and CLIA chain-of-custody documentation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category.']
  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IRB protocol under which the specimen was collected and is now being disposed. Required for IRB protocol closure documentation and consent-withdrawal-driven disposals.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent specimen record that is being disposed of. Links the disposal event to the originating biological sample in the Sample Management domain.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical storage location (freezer, rack, position) from which the specimen/aliquot was retrieved for disposal. Required for chain-of-custody documentation and inventory reconciliation.']
  tertiary_disposal_witness_personnel_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified witness who observed and attested to the disposal procedure. Required for controlled substance or high-value specimen disposals under GxP and certain IRB protocols.']
  veeva_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the corresponding disposal authorization or destruction certificate document stored in Veeva Vault Quality Management system. Enables cross-system traceability for regulatory submissions.']
  autoclave_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the autoclave sterilization cycle used for disposal when disposal_method is autoclave. Links to autoclave validation and equipment qualification records for GxP compliance.']
  biosafety_level varchar [note: 'Biosafety Level (BSL) classification of the specimen being disposed, determining the required containment and disposal procedures. Governs environmental health and safety compliance for disposal method selection.']
  certificate_of_destruction_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the Certificate of Destruction (COD) issued upon confirmed disposal. Serves as the primary regulatory evidence document for GxP, CLIA, and IRB protocol closure audits.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the authorizing personnel formally confirmed and attested to the physical destruction of the specimen/aliquot. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature compliance.']
  consent_withdrawal_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the subject consent withdrawal request that triggered this disposal event. Applicable when reason_code is consent_withdrawal. Supports GDPR Article 17 (right to erasure) and HIPAA compliance documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disposal event record was first created in the system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Required for GxP electronic records audit trail compliance (21 CFR Part 11).']
  destruction_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether physical destruction of the specimen/aliquot has been formally confirmed by the authorizing personnel. Required for GxP, CLIA, and IRB protocol closure documentation. True = destruction confirmed; False = pending confirmation.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the physical disposal or destruction of the specimen/aliquot was performed. Used for regulatory reporting, retention schedule compliance, and GxP audit trails. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (date-level precision).']
  disposal_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the disposal event, used in GxP documentation, IRB protocol closure records, and chain-of-custody audit trails. Format: DSP-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'The physical or chemical method used to destroy or dispose of the specimen/aliquot. Governs environmental health and safety compliance, biosafety level requirements, and regulatory documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: incineration|autoclave|chemical_inactivation|return_to_subject|biohazard_waste|landfill_approved — promote to reference product if additional methods are required]']
  disposal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, observations, or exceptions recorded during the disposal event. Supports GxP deviation documentation and chain-of-custody completeness.']
  disposal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the disposal event workflow. Tracks progression from initiation through physical destruction and regulatory confirmation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  disposal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the disposal action was executed. Provides sub-day precision required for chain-of-custody audit trails and GxP electronic records compliance (21 CFR Part 11).']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory or manufacturing facility where the disposal event was physically performed. Supports multi-site regulatory compliance reporting and GxP site-level audit trails.']
  irb_closure_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Institutional Review Board (IRB) has been formally notified of the disposal event for protocol closure purposes. True = notification sent; False = notification pending. Required for IRB protocol closure compliance.']
  lims_disposal_task_code varchar [note: 'Native task or workflow identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS for this disposal event. Enables bidirectional traceability between the Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS system of record.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot Number (LOT) of the reagent or consumable used in the disposal process (e.g., chemical inactivation reagent lot). Supports GxP traceability and reagent recall management.']
  phi_deidentified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) associated with this specimen was de-identified prior to disposal processing. True = PHI de-identified; False = PHI still linked. Supports HIPAA Safe Harbor and Expert Determination de-identification compliance.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business or regulatory reason triggering the disposal event. Drives IRB protocol closure, consent management workflows, and GxP deviation reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: consent_withdrawal|expiry|volume_exhausted|contamination|protocol_completion|regulatory_hold_release — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context or justification for the disposal event beyond the standardized reason code. Supports GxP deviation documentation and IRB protocol closure notes.']
  regulatory_compliance_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulatory requirement, SOP number, or governing standard under which this disposal event is being executed (e.g., SOP-LAB-0042, 21 CFR Part 11, ISO 15189:2022 Section 5.7). Supports regulatory submission and audit readiness.']
  remaining_volume_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume of biological material remaining in the specimen/aliquot container after partial disposal, expressed in microliters (µL). Zero indicates complete disposal. Supports inventory reconciliation and chain-of-custody documentation.']
  sample_use_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intended use of the specimen being disposed: Clinical (IVD/LDT), Research (RUO), or Quality Control (QC). Determines applicable regulatory framework (CLIA, FDA IVD, IRB) for disposal documentation requirements.']
  sop_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing the disposal method and process. Links to the document control system (MasterControl) for version-controlled procedure traceability.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this disposal event record originated (e.g., LabVantage LIMS, SAP S/4HANA QM, MasterControl). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the biological specimen type being disposed. Determines applicable biosafety handling requirements, disposal method eligibility, and regulatory reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blood|saliva|tissue|urine|csf|buccal_swab|plasma|serum|ffpe|cell_line — promote to reference product]']
  temperature_at_disposal_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient or process temperature (in degrees Celsius) recorded at the time of disposal. Relevant for autoclave validation, chemical inactivation efficacy, and cold-chain break documentation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disposal event record was last modified. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Required for GxP electronic records audit trail and change control documentation.']
  volume_disposed_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume of biological material disposed of, expressed in microliters (µL). Used for inventory reconciliation, chain-of-custody completeness verification, and QC audit trails confirming full or partial disposal.']
  waste_contractor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the licensed third-party waste disposal contractor responsible for off-site incineration or biohazardous waste processing. Required for EPA chain-of-custody and vendor qualification records.']
  waste_manifest_number varchar [note: 'Hazardous or biohazardous waste manifest tracking number issued by the waste disposal contractor or environmental health and safety department. Required for EPA and state environmental compliance documentation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of the formal disposal or destruction of a specimen or aliquot at end-of-life. Captures disposal date, disposal method (incineration, autoclave, chemical inactivation, return to subject), reason code (consent withdrawal, expiry, volume exhausted, contamination, protocol completion), authorizing personnel, regulatory compliance reference, and confirmation of destruction. Required for GxP, CLIA, and IRB protocol closure documentation.'
}

Table sample.shipment_manifest {
  shipment_manifest_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the shipment manifest record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key. Entity Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete business event (inter-site or inter-partner specimen transfer) with a clear lifecycle.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Shipment issues (damaged specimens, temperature excursions, missing samples) generate deviations. Labs track which shipment caused each deviation for root cause analysis and supplier management.']
  lims_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Native shipment record identifier from LabVantage LIMS, the system of record for sample tracking and laboratory workflow. Enables bidirectional traceability between the Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS source system.']
  nda_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the executed Data Transfer Agreement (DTA) or Material Transfer Agreement (MTA) governing the transfer of specimens and associated genomic data to the destination partner. Required for CRO, biobank, and external partner transfers.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.opportunity. Business justification: Sample shipments for commercial projects (pharma collaborations, clinical trial sample returns) must link to opportunity for logistics cost allocation, service delivery confirmation, and customer bill']
  origin_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Shipments originate from customer sites (return shipments, inter-site transfers). Critical for chain-of-custody documentation, logistics coordination, regulatory compliance, and temperature excursion ']
  prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory chain-of-custody for specimen shipments requires tracking the qualified employee who prepared the manifest. GxP audits and FDA inspections verify personnel qualifications for specimen handl']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical or research study associated with the specimens in this shipment. Links the manifest to the study master for protocol compliance, specimen accountability, and regulatory reporting.']
  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the IRB protocol under which the specimens being shipped were collected or are being transferred. Ensures that inter-site specimen transfers comply with the approved IRB consent scope and research authorization.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: International specimen shipments for clinical trials require regulatory submission linkage for import/export permits, customs documentation, and compliance with international transfer regulations (CIT']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: External specimen shipments often involve supplier/vendor relationships (contract labs, biobanks, logistics providers). Linking enables vendor performance tracking (on-time delivery, cold chain compli']
  actual_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the destination site confirmed physical receipt of the shipment. Closes the chain-of-custody loop and triggers downstream LIMS accessioning workflows. Null until receipt is confirmed.']
  authorized_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the supervisor or quality officer who authorized the release and dispatch of the shipment. Required for GxP-compliant release workflows and regulatory audit trails.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier or courier responsible for transporting the shipment (e.g., \'FedEx\', \'World Courier\', \'Marken\'). Used for carrier performance tracking and exception management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the shipment manifest record was first created in the system. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Supports GxP audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Customs or export declaration reference number assigned by the relevant customs authority for international shipments. Required for import/export regulatory compliance and cross-border chain-of-custody documentation.']
  dangerous_goods_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains materials classified as dangerous goods under IATA regulations (e.g., dry ice UN 1845, biological substances Category B UN 3373, or infectious substances Category A UN 2814). True = dangerous goods declaration required.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country to which the shipment is destined (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Required for import/export regulatory compliance, customs documentation, and GDPR/HIPAA cross-border data transfer assessments.']
  destination_site_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the site, laboratory, clinical site, CRO, or biobank that is the intended recipient of the shipment. References the internal site/facility master.']
  dry_ice_weight_kg decimal(6,3) [note: 'Weight of dry ice (solid CO2) in kilograms included in the shipment packaging. Required for IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations compliance declarations when dry ice is used as a refrigerant (UN 1845). Applicable only when packaging_type = \'dry_ice\'.']
  expected_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date by which the shipment is expected to arrive at the destination site. Used for SLA monitoring, lab scheduling, and proactive exception management when shipments are delayed.']
  import_export_permit_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued permit or license number required for cross-border shipment of biological specimens, GMOs, or controlled substances. Supports regulatory compliance for international transfers under CITES, CDC/USDA select agent regulations, or national biosafety authorities.']
  manifest_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable manifest reference number assigned at the time of shipment creation (e.g., SM-2024-000123). Used for chain-of-custody documentation, regulatory submissions, and cross-site reconciliation. Sourced from LabVantage LIMS shipment module. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  manifest_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shipment manifest. \'draft\' = manifest created but not yet dispatched; \'in_transit\' = carrier has collected and shipment is en route; \'delivered\' = receipt confirmed at destination; \'cancelled\' = shipment voided before dispatch; \'exception\' = shipment flagged for temperature deviation, loss, or damage. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country from which the shipment originates (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Required for import/export regulatory compliance and customs documentation.']
  origin_site_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the site, laboratory, or facility from which the specimens are dispatched. References the internal site/facility master. Used for inter-site chain-of-custody and import/export compliance.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Type of temperature-controlled packaging used for the shipment. \'dry_ice\' = solid CO2 for frozen specimens; \'ln2_shipper\' = liquid nitrogen dry shipper for ultra-low temperature; \'ambient\' = room temperature; \'refrigerated\' = 2–8°C cold pack; \'controlled_ambient\' = validated ambient range packaging. Critical for specimen integrity and IATA compliance.']
  phi_deidentified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all Protected Health Information (PHI) associated with the specimens in this shipment has been de-identified in accordance with HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination methods prior to transfer. True = de-identified; False = PHI present, full HIPAA safeguards apply.']
  receipt_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the shipment condition recorded by the receiving laboratory upon physical receipt. \'acceptable\' = specimens received in satisfactory condition; \'compromised\' = packaging or specimen integrity concerns noted; \'rejected\' = shipment rejected and returned; \'pending_review\' = condition under investigation. Drives specimen disposition workflow in LabVantage LIMS.']
  receipt_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the receiving laboratory at the time of receipt, documenting any observed discrepancies, packaging damage, specimen count mismatches, or temperature excursion details. Supports non-conformance investigation and regulatory reporting.']
  received_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the laboratory staff member who confirmed receipt of the shipment at the destination site. Required for chain-of-custody documentation and GxP audit trails.']
  shipment_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is outbound (specimens leaving the originating site) or inbound (specimens arriving at the receiving site). Drives chain-of-custody workflow and import/export regulatory compliance checks.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the purpose of the shipment. \'clinical\' = patient specimens for diagnostic or clinical trial use; \'research\' = RUO specimens; \'qc\' = Quality Control reference materials; \'biobank_transfer\' = long-term storage transfer; \'cro_transfer\' = transfer to/from a Contract Research Organization. Drives regulatory handling requirements and IRB consent validation.']
  shipped_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the shipment was physically dispatched by the originating site and collected by the carrier. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (date-level precision for manifest planning and SLA tracking).']
  shipped_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the shipment was handed over to the carrier. Provides exact chain-of-custody event time for regulatory audit trails and temperature excursion analysis.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling, storage, or processing instructions for the carrier and receiving laboratory (e.g., \'Keep upright\', \'Do not X-ray\', \'Priority handling required\'). Communicated on the physical manifest and shipping label.']
  specimen_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual specimens or aliquots included in this shipment manifest. Used for receipt reconciliation and chain-of-custody verification at the destination site. QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.']
  temp_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion (deviation outside the validated acceptable range) was detected during transit. True = excursion detected; False = no excursion. Triggers specimen integrity review and potential quarantine workflow in LabVantage LIMS.']
  temp_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature recorded by the temperature monitoring device during transit, in degrees Celsius. Used to assess cold-chain integrity and determine whether specimens experienced a temperature excursion above the acceptable range.']
  temp_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature recorded by the temperature monitoring device during transit, in degrees Celsius. Used to assess cold-chain integrity and determine whether specimens experienced a temperature excursion below the acceptable range.']
  temp_monitor_device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the temperature monitoring device (e.g., data logger, indicator strip, or IoT sensor) placed inside the shipment packaging. Used to retrieve temperature excursion records and validate specimen integrity upon receipt.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number or airway bill number for the shipment. Enables real-time shipment tracking and chain-of-custody verification with the logistics provider.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations dangerous goods classification number applicable to the shipment contents (e.g., UN3373 for Biological Substance Category B, UN2814 for Infectious Substance Category A, UN1845 for dry ice). Required on shipping labels and documentation when dangerous_goods_flag = True.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the shipment manifest record was most recently modified. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Supports GxP audit trail requirements, change tracking, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Records the outbound or inbound transfer of specimens or aliquots between sites, labs, or external partners (CROs, clinical sites, biobanks). Captures manifest number, shipment direction (inbound/outbound), origin site, destination site, carrier, tracking number, packaging type (dry ice, LN2 shipper, ambient), number of specimens, temperature monitoring device ID, shipped date, expected arrival date, and actual receipt confirmation. Supports inter-site chain-of-custody and import/export regulatory compliance.'
}

Table sample.container {
  container_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical container record in the LabVantage LIMS. Primary key for the container master entity.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Containers (tubes, plates, cryovials) are catalog products. Currently denormalized as manufacturer/catalog_number. Business need: inventory management, cost tracking per sample, compatibility verifica']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Containers (tubes, plates, vials) are consumable materials with material numbers. Linking enables consumable inventory tracking, automated procurement, cost allocation, and supplier management. Remove']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample or aliquot currently held within this container. Links container to the sample domain for chain-of-custody traceability.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical storage location (freezer, rack, shelf, position) where this container is currently housed. Supports barcode-driven inventory management.']
  accessioned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the container was first registered and accessioned into the LabVantage LIMS system. Marks the start of the container lifecycle and chain-of-custody record. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Unique machine-readable barcode label affixed to the physical container. Used for barcode-driven sample tracking and automated liquid handling workflows in LabVantage LIMS. Must be globally unique across all container inventory.']
  cap_color varchar [note: 'Color of the container cap or closure used for visual specimen type identification per laboratory SOP (e.g., red = serum, purple = EDTA whole blood, yellow = SST). Supports pre-analytical sorting and specimen type verification at accessioning.']
  chain_of_custody_status varchar [note: 'Current integrity status of the chain-of-custody record for this container. Intact = unbroken custody from collection to current location; Broken = a custody gap or anomaly has been detected; Under Review = custody discrepancy under investigation; Verified = custody independently confirmed.']
  concentration_ng_ul decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured concentration of the nucleic acid or analyte within the container expressed in nanograms per microliter (ng/µL). Captured at accessioning or post-QC measurement. Used for library preparation input normalization and downstream NGS workflow eligibility.']
  container_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the container. Governs whether the container is available for use, actively holding a specimen, reserved for a workflow, or has been removed from inventory. Drives container inventory management and QC workflows.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Physical form factor classification of the container. Drives liquid handling automation compatibility, storage configuration, and workflow routing in LabVantage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tube|plate|vial|cryovial|microplate_well|strip_tube|cartridge — promote to reference product]']
  current_volume_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Current volume of specimen or reagent held within the container expressed in microliters (µL). Updated by LabVantage LIMS upon each aliquoting, dispensing, or consumption event. Enables remaining volume tracking for sample sufficiency checks.']
  discarded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the container was formally discarded or destroyed in LabVantage LIMS. Null if the container has not been discarded. Required for chain-of-custody closure, biohazardous waste disposal documentation, and regulatory audit trails.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of the container and its contents upon discard or destruction. Required for biohazardous waste compliance documentation and environmental health and safety (EHS) reporting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Manufacturer-specified expiration date for the container (e.g., sterility expiry, additive expiry for collection tubes with anticoagulants or preservatives). After this date the container must not be used for specimen collection or storage per GMP and ISO 13485 requirements.']
  external_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by an external system, partner laboratory, or originating institution (e.g., hospital LIMS ID, biobank accession number). Enables cross-system reconciliation and chain-of-custody linkage.']
  freeze_thaw_count varchar [note: 'Number of freeze-thaw cycles the container and its contents have undergone. Tracked to assess specimen integrity degradation risk. Exceeding the maximum allowable freeze-thaw cycles per SOP may trigger QC review or specimen disqualification.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Intended use classification of the container and its contents. Determines applicable regulatory framework: clinical specimens are subject to CLIA/CAP and IVD regulations; research specimens fall under RUO designation; QC specimens support internal quality programs; manufacturing specimens are governed by GMP.']
  irb_protocol_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Institutional Review Board (IRB) approved protocol under which the specimen in this container was collected. Required for research use specimens to ensure informed consent compliance. Links container to the governing research ethics approval.']
  is_dnase_rnase_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container is certified DNase- and RNase-free (True), ensuring nucleic acid integrity for genomic and transcriptomic applications. Critical for NGS library preparation, RNA sequencing, and qPCR workflows.']
  is_low_bind boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container has a low-binding surface treatment (True) to minimize nucleic acid or protein adsorption to container walls. Important for low-input NGS library preparation and single-cell genomics workflows.']
  is_phi_deidentified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container label and associated metadata have been de-identified to remove Protected Health Information (PHI) per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination method. True = de-identified; False = identifiable PHI present. Governs data access controls and downstream analytics eligibility.']
  is_sterile boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container was manufactured and certified as sterile (True) or is non-sterile (False). Determines eligibility for clinical specimen collection, cell culture applications, and GMP manufacturing workflows.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the container record in LabVantage LIMS. Used for audit trail maintenance, data synchronization, and change detection in the Databricks Silver layer. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  lims_container_code varchar [note: 'Native container identifier assigned by the LabVantage LIMS source system. Preserved for bidirectional reconciliation between the Databricks Silver layer and the operational LIMS. Distinct from the surrogate container_id.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer lot or batch number for the container. Critical for traceability, recall management, and COA verification under GMP and ISO 13485 quality management requirements.']
  material varchar [note: 'Primary material composition of the container (e.g., polypropylene, glass, polystyrene). Determines chemical compatibility with specimen types, sterilization method eligibility, and storage temperature range.']
  max_freeze_thaw_cycles varchar [note: 'Maximum number of freeze-thaw cycles permitted for the specimen type held in this container before the sample is considered compromised per laboratory SOP. Drives automated QC alerts in LabVantage when freeze_thaw_count is exceeded.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for laboratory staff to record observations, anomalies, handling instructions, or special conditions associated with the container (e.g., visible hemolysis, lipemia, container damage, special handling requirements). Supports chain-of-custody documentation.']
  plate_position varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric well position identifier for containers that are wells within a microplate or array plate (e.g., A01, H12, P24). Null for standalone tubes and vials. Enables plate-map tracking and automated liquid handling coordination.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Current Quality Control (QC) assessment status of the container contents. Pending = QC not yet performed; Passed = contents meet acceptance criteria; Failed = contents do not meet acceptance criteria; Waived = QC waived per SOP exception; Not Required = QC not applicable for this container type.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the container lot was received into laboratory inventory from the manufacturer or supplier. Used for FIFO inventory management, shelf-life calculation, and incoming QC inspection records in SAP MM.']
  ruo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container is designated for Research Use Only (RUO) and is not intended for use in clinical diagnostic procedures. True = RUO; False = clinical or IVD use. Required for FDA regulatory compliance labeling and downstream workflow routing.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the biological specimen held in the container (e.g., whole blood, FFPE tissue, saliva, buccal swab, plasma, serum, DNA extract, RNA extract, cell line). Drives pre-analytical handling rules, storage temperature requirements, and assay eligibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|dna_extract|rna_extract|ffpe_tissue|saliva|buccal_swab|cell_line|urine — promote to reference product]']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature for the container and its contents expressed in degrees Celsius. Used to validate storage location assignment and trigger temperature excursion alerts. Typical values: 4°C (refrigerator), 25°C (ambient), -20°C (freezer).']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature for the container and its contents expressed in degrees Celsius. Used to validate storage location assignment and trigger alerts for temperature excursions. Typical values: -196°C (liquid nitrogen), -80°C (ultra-low freezer), -20°C (standard freezer).']
  tube_format varchar [note: 'Standardized format designation of the container (e.g., 1.5 mL Eppendorf, 96-well plate, 384-well plate). Determines compatibility with automated liquid handling instruments, centrifuge rotors, and storage rack configurations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 0.2ml|0.5ml|1.5ml|2.0ml|5.0ml|15ml|50ml|96_well|384_well — promote to reference product]']
  volume_capacity_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity of the container expressed in microliters (µL). Used for liquid handling automation, aliquot volume validation, and storage planning. Typical values range from 0.2 µL (PCR strip tube) to 50,000 µL (50 mL conical).']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a physical container (tube, plate, vial, cryovial, microplate well) used to hold a specimen or aliquot. Captures container barcode, container type, manufacturer, lot number, material (polypropylene, glass), volume capacity, current contents reference, and container status (empty, in-use, discarded). Enables barcode-driven sample tracking and container inventory management within LabVantage.'
}

Table sample.temperature_excursion {
  temperature_excursion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each temperature excursion event record in the Genomics Biotech cold-chain monitoring system. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — a discrete GxP cold-chain compliance event with a clear lifecycle (detected, assessed, dispositioned, closed).']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Temperature excursions are a specific type of quality deviation requiring investigation and CAPA. Labs create deviation records for excursions to document impact assessment and corrective action.']
  disposition_authorized_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Temperature excursion disposition decisions (use/quarantine/discard specimens) require documented approval by qualified personnel with appropriate training and authority. Critical for GxP compliance, ']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specimen shipment manifest affected by the temperature excursion. Populated when the excursion occurs during transit rather than at a fixed storage location. Mutually exclusive with storage_location_id in most cases.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical storage location (freezer, refrigerator, cryogenic tank, cold room) where the excursion occurred. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category (resource reference to the affected storage asset). Populated when the excursion is location-based rather than shipment-based.']
  affected_sample_type varchar [note: 'Predominant biological specimen type(s) affected by the excursion (e.g., whole blood, plasma, FFPE tissue, DNA extract, RNA extract, cell line). Informs the biological stability risk assessment since different specimen types have different thermal sensitivity profiles. Free text to accommodate diverse specimen types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|dna_extract|rna_extract|ffpe_tissue|cell_line|urine|csf — promote to reference product]']
  affected_specimen_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual biological specimens (samples, aliquots, tubes) present in the affected storage location or shipment at the time of the excursion. Used for impact scope assessment, risk quantification, and regulatory reporting. Populated during impact assessment phase.']
  alert_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which the automated alert notification was sent to responsible personnel (lab manager, QA, on-call staff) following excursion detection. Used to measure response time and assess SLA compliance for cold-chain incident response. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  capa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated CAPA record in the quality management system (Veeva Vault or MasterControl) initiated as a result of this excursion. Enables cross-system traceability between the excursion event and the corrective action workflow.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) is required as a result of this temperature excursion. Driven by excursion severity category, recurrence pattern, and QA assessment. True triggers CAPA initiation in the quality management system (Veeva Vault or MasterControl).']
  closure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which the temperature excursion record was formally closed in the quality management system, indicating all required actions (impact assessment, disposition, CAPA initiation if required) have been completed and reviewed. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the immediate corrective action taken in response to the excursion (e.g., specimens transferred to backup freezer, equipment serviced, power restored, dry ice added). Distinct from the formal CAPA; captures the real-time remediation steps.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the temperature excursion record was first created in the system (LabVantage LIMS or integrated cold-chain monitoring platform). May differ from detection_timestamp if there is a lag in data entry. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world timestamp at which the temperature excursion was first detected by the monitoring device or alert system. This is the principal business event time for the excursion. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  deviation_report_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal deviation report filed in the quality management system (Veeva Vault or MasterControl) documenting this temperature excursion. Required for GxP compliance, regulatory inspection readiness, and quality trending.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'The formal disposition decision for the affected specimens following impact assessment. Use-as-is: specimens deemed unaffected; Conditional use: specimens usable with documented justification; Quarantine: specimens held pending further evaluation; Retest required: specimens require QC retesting before use; Discard: specimens deemed compromised and destroyed.']
  disposition_rationale varchar [note: 'Scientific and regulatory justification for the disposition decision, including reference to stability data, specimen type thermal sensitivity, excursion magnitude, and duration. Required for GxP audit trail and regulatory submission support.']
  disposition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which the formal disposition decision was made and recorded by the authorized reviewer. Establishes the timeline for regulatory audit purposes and tracks TAT (Turnaround Time) for excursion resolution. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the temperature excursion in minutes, calculated from excursion_start_timestamp to excursion_end_timestamp. Stored as a business field to support threshold-based disposition rules and regulatory reporting without requiring runtime calculation. Null if excursion is ongoing.']
  excursion_category varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the excursion based on magnitude and duration relative to defined thresholds. Drives escalation protocols, CAPA requirements, and regulatory notification obligations. Minor: within acceptable tolerance; Critical: significant risk to specimen integrity.']
  excursion_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which the temperature returned within the acceptable range, marking the end of the excursion event. Null if the excursion is still ongoing. Used together with excursion_start_timestamp to determine total duration. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  excursion_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable business identifier for the excursion event (e.g., EXC-2024-000123). Used in deviation reports, CAPA records, regulatory submissions, and customer communications. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  excursion_source varchar [note: 'The source or mechanism by which the temperature excursion was detected. Automated monitor: continuous monitoring system alarm; Manual check: discovered during routine temperature log review; Shipment logger: data logger downloaded upon receipt; IoT sensor: real-time IoT platform alert; Third-party courier: reported by logistics provider.']
  excursion_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which the temperature first breached the defined alert threshold, marking the beginning of the excursion event. Used to calculate excursion duration and assess cumulative thermal exposure. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  excursion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the temperature excursion event workflow. Progresses from initial detection through impact assessment and disposition decision to closure. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  excursion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the excursion by the nature of the temperature deviation. High temperature indicates warming above upper threshold; low temperature indicates cooling below lower threshold; freeze-thaw indicates unintended freeze/thaw cycle; rapid change indicates rate-of-change violation; prolonged drift indicates slow sustained deviation.']
  impact_assessment_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the impact assessment conducted to evaluate the effect of the temperature excursion on specimen integrity, assay validity, and downstream test results. Documents the scientific rationale used to support the disposition decision.']
  lims_excursion_ref varchar [note: 'The source system reference identifier for this excursion record as assigned by LabVantage LIMS. Enables bidirectional traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer analytical record and the operational LIMS source of record for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  lower_threshold_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The lower temperature alert threshold configured on the monitoring device at the time of the excursion, expressed in degrees Celsius. Documents the threshold that was breached to trigger the excursion alert. Essential for audit trail and regulatory review.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The highest temperature value recorded by the monitoring device during the excursion event, expressed in degrees Celsius. Critical for assessing heat degradation risk for biological specimens, nucleic acids, and reagents. Used in impact assessment and disposition decision logic. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.']
  mean_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The mean kinetic temperature (MKT) recorded during the excursion period, expressed in degrees Celsius. MKT is the single calculated temperature that, if maintained constantly, would produce the same thermal degradation as the actual variable temperature conditions experienced. Used in pharmaceutical and biological specimen stability assessments per ICH Q1A.']
  min_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The lowest temperature value recorded by the monitoring device during the excursion event, expressed in degrees Celsius. Critical for assessing freeze risk for biological specimens and reagents. Used in impact assessment and disposition decision logic.']
  monitoring_device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the temperature monitoring device (data logger, IoT sensor, continuous monitoring system) that recorded the excursion. Used for equipment traceability, calibration verification, and audit trail. Maps to the instrument/device asset register.']
  prior_excursion_number varchar [note: 'The excursion_number of the most recent prior excursion at the same location or device, populated when recurrence_flag is True. Enables trend analysis and supports CAPA root cause investigation by linking related excursion events.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this excursion is a recurrence of a previously documented excursion at the same storage location or on the same monitoring device within a defined lookback period. Recurrent excursions trigger mandatory CAPA escalation and may indicate systemic equipment or process failure.']
  regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this temperature excursion meets the criteria for mandatory regulatory reporting to a governing body (e.g., FDA, EMA, CAP, CLIA). Determined during impact assessment based on specimen type, intended use (IVD, clinical, GMP), and excursion severity.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which a responsible person acknowledged the excursion alert and initiated a response action (e.g., moved specimens, initiated repair, escalated). Used to calculate response time KPI and assess GxP compliance with cold-chain SOP requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorized root cause of the temperature excursion as determined during investigation. Used for trending, CAPA identification, and preventive action planning. Populated after investigation is complete. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|power_outage|door_left_open|improper_loading|calibration_drift|ambient_condition|transport_delay|human_error|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the identified root cause of the temperature excursion, as documented during the deviation investigation. Complements root_cause_category with specific contextual details for CAPA and regulatory review.']
  storage_condition_required varchar [note: 'The required storage temperature condition for the affected specimens or materials at the time of the excursion. Provides context for assessing the severity of the deviation relative to the required storage specification.']
  threshold_breached varchar [note: 'Indicates which configured alert threshold was breached during the excursion: upper threshold (overheating), lower threshold (overcooling/freeze), or both (e.g., during a freeze-thaw cycle). Drives the type of impact assessment required.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the temperature excursion record, including status changes, disposition updates, or impact assessment revisions. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  upper_threshold_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The upper temperature alert threshold configured on the monitoring device at the time of the excursion, expressed in degrees Celsius. Documents the threshold that was breached to trigger the excursion alert. Essential for audit trail and regulatory review.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a detected temperature excursion event affecting a storage location or shipment containing specimens. Captures excursion start and end timestamps, affected location or shipment manifest, minimum and maximum temperatures recorded, duration of excursion, monitoring device ID, alert threshold breached, impact assessment (specimens affected count), and disposition decision (use as-is, quarantine, discard). Critical for GxP cold-chain compliance and specimen integrity assurance.'
}

Table sample.run_sample {
  run_sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this specimen-run assignment record. Primary key for the run_sample association.']
  instrument_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the instrument run in which this specimen was sequenced. References the run execution record.']
  qc_reviewed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory scientist or bioinformatician who reviewed the QC metrics and assigned the run_qc_status for this specimen-run pairing.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the biological specimen that was loaded into this sequencing run. References the core specimen master entity.']
  actual_coverage_depth decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual average sequencing coverage depth achieved for this specimen in this run, calculated from actual_reads_generated and reference genome size. Compared against target_coverage_depth to assess run success.']
  actual_reads_generated varchar [note: 'Total number of sequencing reads (or read pairs for paired-end) generated for this specimen in this run, as determined by demultiplexing. Used to calculate actual coverage and assess run balance. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specimen was assigned to this run in the LIMS system. Captures when the multiplexing decision was made.']
  demultiplexed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when demultiplexing completed for this specimen, producing specimen-specific FASTQ files. Marks when per-specimen data became available.']
  duplicate_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of reads identified as PCR duplicates for this specimen. High duplicate rates indicate over-amplification during library prep or low library complexity.']
  fastq_file_path varchar [note: 'File system path or cloud storage URI to the demultiplexed FASTQ files for this specimen from this run. Links sequencing data to downstream bioinformatics pipelines.']
  index_sequence varchar [note: 'Unique index barcode sequence (or dual-index pair) assigned to this specimen for multiplexed sequencing. Used by demultiplexing software to separate reads by specimen. Format: single index (ACGTACGT) or dual index (ACGTACGT+TGCATGCA). Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  lane_number varchar [note: 'Physical lane number on the flow cell where this specimen was loaded (1-8 for HiSeq/NovaSeq). Critical for demultiplexing and per-lane quality assessment. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  loading_concentration_nm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Molar concentration (nanomolar, nM) of this specimen\'s library at the time of loading into the sequencing run. Influences cluster density and read balance across multiplexed specimens. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  mean_insert_size varchar [note: 'Average insert size (distance between paired-end reads) for this specimen\'s library in base pairs. Used to assess library preparation quality and detect size selection issues.']
  percent_aligned decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of this specimen\'s reads that successfully aligned to the reference genome. Low alignment rates indicate contamination, adapter issues, or sample quality problems.']
  percent_q30 decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of bases with Phred quality score of 30 or higher (≥99.9% accuracy) for this specimen\'s reads in this run. Key quality metric for per-specimen QC assessment. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  pool_position varchar [note: 'Position or slot number of this specimen within the multiplexed pool loaded into the lane. Used for tracking specimen order during library pooling. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  qc_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when QC review was completed and run_qc_status was assigned for this specimen-run pairing.']
  resequencing_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this specimen requires re-sequencing due to insufficient coverage, quality issues, or run failure. Drives downstream workflow decisions.']
  run_qc_status varchar [note: 'Quality control status for this specimen in this run: pass (meets quality thresholds), fail (requires re-sequencing), warning (marginal quality), pending (QC not yet complete). Drives re-sequencing decisions. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  target_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Planned average sequencing coverage depth (e.g., 30X for WGS, 100X for WES) for this specific specimen in this run. May differ from the run-level target if specimens have different coverage requirements. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of a biological specimen to a specific instrument sequencing run. In genomics operations, multiple specimens are routinely multiplexed into a single sequencing run (8-96+ specimens per run), and individual specimens may be re-sequenced across multiple runs for additional coverage, validation, or QC failure recovery. Each record captures the lane assignment, index sequences for demultiplexing, loading concentration, per-specimen read counts, quality metrics, and QC status for each specimen-run pairing. This is the core operational entity that enables multiplexed sequencing workflows and per-specimen quality tracking across the sequencing lifecycle.. Existence Justification: In genomics sequencing operations, specimens and instrument runs have a genuine many-to-many relationship. Multiple specimens (8-96+) are routinely multiplexed into a single sequencing run to maximize instrument utilization and reduce per-specimen cost. Individual specimens are frequently re-sequenced across multiple runs when initial runs fail QC, when additional coverage is needed, or for validation purposes. The business actively manages these specimen-run assignments as operational entities with specific lane assignments, index sequences, loading concentrations, and per-specimen quality metrics.'
}

Table sample.sample_enrollment {
  sample_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sample_enrollment']
  cohort_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.cohort. Business justification: Cohort is a master reference for study groups. Adding cohort_id to sample_enrollment links each enrollment to its cohort, eliminating the siloed cohort table and enabling cohort‑based reporting.']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this subject-project enrollment record']
  principal_investigator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee record of the Principal Investigator responsible for this subject\'s enrollment at the enrolling site']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the R&D research project in which the subject is enrolled']
  study_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external study enrollment record linking this subject to a specific clinical or research study cohort. Supports cohort membership tracking, multi-study subject participation, and study-level analytics without duplicating study metadata in this product. [Moved from subject: This attribute in the subject product is a reference to an external study enrollment record. Since we are now modeling enrollment as an explicit association between subject and project, this attribute becomes redundant and should be replaced by the enrollment association itself. The enrollment association IS the study enrollment record.]']
  subject_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the de-identified biological subject enrolled in this research project']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date on which this subject completed all protocol-required activities for this project. Null if enrollment is still active or was withdrawn.']
  cohort_assignment varchar [note: 'The specific cohort, arm, or subgroup to which this subject was assigned within this research project (e.g., \'Cohort A\', \'Treatment Arm\', \'Control Group\'). Used for stratification and analysis.']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the IRB-approved informed consent form that the subject signed for this specific project enrollment. Tracks consent document version for regulatory compliance.']
  eligibility_confirmed_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject\'s eligibility for this project was confirmed against inclusion/exclusion criteria']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject was formally enrolled in the study or clinical program and informed consent was obtained. Used for cohort timeline analysis, study milestone tracking, and regulatory submission timelines. Stored as date (not full DOB) to comply with HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification requirements. [Moved from subject: This attribute currently exists on the subject product but represents the date of enrollment into a specific study/project, not a property of the subject itself. A subject can be enrolled in multiple projects at different dates, so enrollment_date belongs to each subject-project enrollment relationship, not to the subject master record.]']
  expected_completion_date date [note: 'Projected date for this subject to complete all protocol-required activities for this project enrollment']
  randomization_code varchar [note: 'Blinded randomization code assigned to this subject for this project enrollment, used in randomized controlled trials. Access restricted per study blinding protocol.']
  sample_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject was formally enrolled into this specific research project, as recorded in the study enrollment system']
  sample_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this subject\'s enrollment in this project: active (currently enrolled), withdrawn (subject withdrew consent), completed (subject completed study protocol), screen_failed (did not meet eligibility), lost_to_followup (contact lost)']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the clinical or research site where this subject was enrolled into this project, supporting multi-site study tracking']
  study_arm varchar [note: 'The study arm or treatment group assignment for this subject within this project, particularly relevant for interventional or comparative studies (e.g., \'Arm 1: CRISPR Treatment\', \'Arm 2: Standard of Care\')']
  subject_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subject record within the study or clinical program. Active subjects are eligible for ongoing specimen collection and data use. Withdrawn subjects have revoked consent and data use must be restricted per IRB protocol. Deceased and lost-to-follow-up statuses affect cohort completeness reporting and TAT (Turnaround Time) analytics. [Moved from subject: If subject_status represents enrollment lifecycle status (active, withdrawn, completed), it is specific to each project enrollment, not a universal subject property. A subject can be \'active\' in one project and \'completed\' in another simultaneously. This should move to the enrollment association as \'status\'.]']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject withdrew from this specific research project or was removed from the study. Null if enrollment is still active or completed normally.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for subject withdrawal from this specific project enrollment, recorded when status changes to withdrawn (e.g., \'adverse event\', \'subject request\', \'protocol violation\', \'investigator decision\')']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment event between a biological subject and an R&D research project. It captures the operational relationship when a de-identified subject is enrolled into a specific research study or clinical program, tracking consent, cohort assignment, study arm allocation, and enrollment lifecycle. Each record links one subject to one project with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific enrollment relationship, supporting longitudinal tracking, multi-protocol participation, and IRB-compliant consent management.. Existence Justification: In genomics research operations, subjects participate in multiple research projects over time (longitudinal studies, cross-protocol participation, multi-indication studies), and research projects enroll multiple subjects to form cohorts. The business actively manages enrollment as an operational entity with lifecycle tracking (active, withdrawn, completed), cohort assignment, study arm allocation, consent version management, and IRB-compliant withdrawal tracking. Research coordinators and PIs query \'show me all enrollments for this project by cohort\' and \'what is this subject\'s enrollment history across studies\' as core operational workflows.'
}

Table sample.aliquot_consumption {
  aliquot_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each aliquot consumption record']
  aliquot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific aliquot consumed in this experiment']
  consumed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory technician or scientist who physically consumed/used the aliquot in the experiment. Required for GxP audit trail and accountability.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the experiment in which this aliquot was consumed']
  consumption_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by laboratory personnel regarding the consumption event (e.g., observations, deviations, quality issues). Part of the experimental record.']
  consumption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the aliquot was consumed or added to the experiment. Used for material traceability, experimental timeline documentation, and GxP compliance.']
  experiment_role varchar [note: 'The functional role this aliquot plays in the experimental design (e.g., sample, control, calibrator, blank, standard, qc_reference). Critical for understanding experimental setup and data interpretation.']
  lims_transaction_code varchar [note: 'The native transaction identifier from LabVantage LIMS that recorded this consumption event. Enables traceability back to source system for audit purposes.']
  plate_position varchar [note: 'Well position on the experimental plate where this aliquot was placed (e.g., A01, B12). Critical for plate-based assays, liquid handling automation, and result mapping.']
  replicate_number varchar [note: 'Sequential identifier for technical or biological replicates within the experiment (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Used to group and analyze replicate measurements for statistical validation.']
  volume_consumed_ul decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volume of biological material consumed from the aliquot for this specific experiment, measured in microliters. Critical for inventory depletion tracking and experimental documentation.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the consumption event between aliquot and experiment. It captures the operational record of biological material usage in scientific experiments, tracking volume consumed, timing, experimental role, and technical replicate information. Each record links one aliquot to one experiment with attributes that exist only in the context of this consumption relationship. Critical for material tracking, experimental traceability, and compliance with GxP documentation requirements.. Existence Justification: In genomics laboratory operations, aliquots are routinely consumed across multiple experiments for purposes including technical replicates, method validation, quality control testing, and multi-assay workflows. Conversely, experiments consume multiple aliquots representing different samples, controls, standards, and replicates within the experimental design. The business actively manages consumption records with specific volume tracking, timing, experimental role assignment, and plate positioning—all attributes that belong to the consumption event itself, not to either the aliquot or experiment independently.'
}

Table sample.cohort {
  cohort_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cohort']

  study_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the study associated with the cohort.']
  age_range_max varchar [note: 'Maximum age (in years) of participants in the cohort.']
  age_range_min varchar [note: 'Minimum age (in years) of participants in the cohort.']
  cohort_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the cohort used in study documentation.']
  cohort_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the cohort.']
  cohort_type varchar [note: 'Category of cohort based on study design.']
  consent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether informed consent is required for samples in the cohort.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cohort record was initially captured.']
  data_access_level varchar [note: 'Level of access permitted for cohort data.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days data will be retained before archival or deletion.']
  deidentification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether samples are de-identified for privacy.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the cohort purpose and composition.']
  disease_focus varchar [note: 'Primary disease or condition the cohort targets.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the cohort is retired or expires.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the cohort becomes active for data collection.']
  ethnicity_inclusion varchar [note: 'Ethnicity groups included in the cohort.']
  exclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Text describing criteria for sample exclusion from the cohort.']
  gender_inclusion varchar [note: 'Gender categories included in the cohort.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) covered by the cohort.']
  inclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Text describing criteria for sample inclusion in the cohort.']
  last_data_update_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent data refresh for the cohort.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes about the cohort.']
  phenotype varchar [note: 'Phenotypic characteristic(s) defining the cohort.']
  privacy_tier varchar [note: 'Privacy classification tier for the cohort data.']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Number of biological samples assigned to the cohort.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cohort.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cohort record.']
  creation_date date [note: 'Date the cohort record was first created.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for cohort. Referenced by cohort_id.'
}

Table sample.lims_workflow {
  lims_workflow_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lims_workflow']
  parent_lims_workflow_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lims_workflow (parent_lims_workflow_id)']
  average_step_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean duration of a single step based on historical runs.']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the workflow must satisfy regulatory compliance (e.g., GLP, GCP).']
  cost_estimate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary cost to execute the workflow per sample.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the workflow record was first created in the system.']
  deidentification_flag boolean [note: 'Specifies whether PHI associated with samples processed by this workflow must be de‑identified.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the workflow purpose and scope.']
  disposal_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing final disposal of samples after workflow completion.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the workflow is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the workflow becomes valid for use.']
  estimated_total_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected total runtime for the workflow from start to finish.']
  irb_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Institutional Review Board consent is required for samples processed under this workflow.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the workflow is executed automatically by the LIMS.']
  is_valid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the workflow definition has passed all validation checks.']
  last_executed_by varchar [note: 'User or system account that triggered the most recent execution.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the workflow was most recently run.']
  max_step_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Longest observed duration for any step in the workflow.']
  min_step_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Shortest observed duration for any step in the workflow.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or operational notes captured by the workflow owner.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns the workflow.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person responsible for the workflow.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the workflow for scheduling purposes.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Type(s) of biological specimen the workflow is designed to handle.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the workflow definition.']
  step_count varchar [note: 'Total number of discrete steps defined in the workflow.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Designated storage area or freezer code where samples are kept during the workflow.']
  trigger_event varchar [note: 'Event that initiates the workflow execution.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the workflow record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the workflow definition.']
  workflow_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification grouping (e.g., clinical, research, quality‑control).']
  workflow_code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the workflow across systems.']
  workflow_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the workflow used in LIMS UI and reports.']
  workflow_type varchar [note: 'Category of the workflow (e.g., sample ingestion, library preparation, sequencing, analysis, quality control).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for lims_workflow. Referenced by workflow_id.'
}

Essential for tracking instrument operating costs (consumables, maintenance, labo']

  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Instruments are physically assigned to organizational units (labs, facilities) for asset tracking, compliance audits, space planning, and budget allocation. Essential for GxP facility management and r']
  site_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address.
