Component probe/primer coordinates, target gene lists, and clinical cla']
  kit_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual component reagent catalog item. Links to the specific reagent, enzyme, buffer, adapter, or consumable that is part of the kit Bill of Materials (BOM).']
  parent_kit_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent multi-component reagent kit catalog item. Links to the master kit product definition in the reagent catalog.']
  parent_kit_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Kit parent must reference the commercial catalog item for BOM costing, pricing rollup, and product configuration management. Enables multi-level product structures and kit-to-catalog synchronization f']
  primary_kit_substitute_component_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an approved substitute component catalog item. Used when the primary component is unavailable or discontinued. Substitution must be validated and documented per Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) change control procedures.']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the validation study that qualified this component for inclusion in the kit. Links to Research and Development (R&D) documentation, analytical validation reports, and regulatory submission packages.']
  assembly_instruction varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions for incorporating this component into the kit during manufacturing assembly. Includes handling precautions, placement requirements, and Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) references for Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) compliance.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) change control record that authorized the addition, modification, or removal of this component from the kit Bill of Materials (BOM). Required for regulatory audit trails and quality system compliance.']
  component_cost_usd decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost of the component in United States Dollars (USD). Used for kit Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation, pricing analysis, and Profit and Loss (P&L) reporting. Confidential business data.']
  component_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'Quantity of the component included in one unit of the parent kit. Used for kit assembly, inventory consumption calculations, and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) batch record reconciliation.']
  component_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the component within the kit workflow. Defines the biochemical or operational purpose of the component in library preparation, Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR), or sequencing protocols.']
  component_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this component within the kit Bill of Materials (BOM). Used for assembly instructions, kit documentation, and Certificate of Analysis (COA) reporting.']
  component_stability_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the component remains stable within the kit under specified storage conditions. Used to calculate kit expiry date based on the shortest-lived component. Critical for In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) and Research Use Only (RUO) product labeling.']
  component_storage_requirement varchar [note: 'Storage temperature requirement for this component within the kit. Drives warehouse zoning, kit assembly logistics, and shipping validation. Must align with the most restrictive component requirement for the overall kit.']
  component_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the component quantity. Aligns with reagent catalog specifications and manufacturing Bill of Materials (BOM) standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ml|ul|mg|ug|units|reactions|wells|tubes — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  component_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the component reagent. Tracks formulation changes, protocol updates, and component substitutions over the kit lifecycle. Critical for Lot (LOT) traceability and regulatory submissions.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this kit component association record. Used for audit trails, accountability, and Good Practice (GxP) compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this kit component association record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails, data lineage tracking, and Good Practice (GxP) compliance.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this component association was discontinued or superseded in the kit Bill of Materials (BOM). Null for currently active components. Used for version control and regulatory change documentation.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this component association became active in the kit Bill of Materials (BOM). Used for version control, change management, and historical traceability of kit composition changes.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'Safety Data Sheet (SDS) hazard classification for this component. Drives kit labeling, shipping requirements, workplace safety protocols, and regulatory compliance with Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) and Globally Harmonized System (GHS) standards.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this kit component association is currently active and in use. False for historical or discontinued component associations. Used for filtering current Bill of Materials (BOM) configurations.']
  is_critical_component boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component is critical to kit performance and requires enhanced Quality Control (QC) testing, Certificate of Analysis (COA) documentation, and Lot (LOT) traceability. True for components that directly impact assay performance or regulatory compliance.']
  is_substitutable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component can be substituted with an alternative component during kit assembly without requiring protocol changes or revalidation. Used for supply chain flexibility and manufacturing continuity planning.']
  lot_traceability_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual Lot (LOT) numbers for this component must be tracked and recorded for each assembled kit. True for critical components requiring full traceability per Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) and In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) regulations.']
  maximum_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum acceptable quantity of the component for kit release. Used for Quality Control (QC) inspection and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) batch record verification. Values above this threshold trigger non-conformance investigations.']
  minimum_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum acceptable quantity of the component for kit release. Used for Quality Control (QC) inspection and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) batch record verification. Values below this threshold trigger non-conformance investigations.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this kit component association record. Used for audit trails, accountability, and Good Practice (GxP) compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this kit component association record was last modified. Used for audit trails, change tracking, and Good Practice (GxP) compliance.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory approval status of this component within the kit for In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) or Research Use Only (RUO) use. Approved components have passed validation studies and are cleared for commercial distribution. Pending components are under review. Discontinued components are being phased out. Restricted components have geographic or use limitations.']
  requires_separate_shipment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component must be shipped separately from the main kit due to storage incompatibility, hazardous material regulations, or customs restrictions. True for components requiring dry ice, liquid nitrogen, or dangerous goods handling.']

  Note: 'Association entity defining the bill-of-materials (BOM) composition of multi-component reagent kits — capturing the parent kit catalog item, each component reagent catalog item, component quantity, unit of measure, component role (e.g., enzyme, buffer, adapter, index plate), and component version. Supports kit assembly work orders, component substitution management, and traceability of individual component lots within assembled kits. Enables downstream LOT-level traceability for each kit component.'
}

Table reagent.disposal_record {
  disposal_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reagent disposal record. Primary key.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: Disposal of regulated reagents (especially IVD products) must comply with approval conditions and jurisdictional waste regulations. Links disposal to the approval governing the product\'s regulatory st']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master reagent or consumable product being disposed.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Disposal may result from quality deviations (OOS, contamination, handling errors). Linking disposal to originating deviation provides complete audit trail for regulatory inspections and CAPA effective']
  disposal_authorization_id varchar [note: 'The authorization or approval identifier issued by quality assurance or management permitting the disposal action.']
  disposal_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Disposal events charge to cost centers for waste management cost tracking, environmental compliance reporting, and variance analysis of expired/failed inventory. Enables financial analysis of disposal']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator or technician who executed the disposal activity.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: GMP-compliant disposal tracking requires linking disposal records to lot master. Currently disposal_record has lot_number as STRING but no FK to lot table. Adding lot_id enables regulatory traceabilit']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Disposal often results from NCRs (failed QC, expired, damaged). Linking disposal to originating NCR provides complete audit trail for regulatory inspections and disposition justification in GMP enviro']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Research-phase reagents disposed after project completion require project traceability for cost accounting, grant reporting, and compliance documentation. Enables project closeout reconciliation and w']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Disposal records track waste by SKU for cost accounting (scrap variance), environmental compliance reporting, and manufacturing yield analysis. Required for COGS adjustment and sustainability metrics ']
  authorized_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the quality assurance or management personnel who authorized the disposal.']
  coa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the Certificate of Analysis for the disposed lot, linking disposal to original quality documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the disposal record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the disposal cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  disposal_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cost incurred for the disposal activity, including vendor fees and internal labor.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'The date on which the reagent disposal was executed.']
  disposal_location varchar [note: 'The physical location or facility where the disposal was performed (e.g., warehouse, disposal room, external vendor site).']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'The method used to dispose of the reagent (e.g., incineration, chemical neutralization, biohazard waste, autoclave sterilization).']
  disposal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or special instructions related to the disposal activity.']
  disposal_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for disposal (e.g., expired, failed quality control, product recall, damage, contamination, obsolescence).']
  disposal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the disposal record (e.g., pending approval, approved, completed, cancelled).']
  disposal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the disposal activity was completed, supporting audit trail requirements.']
  disposal_vendor varchar [note: 'Name of the external waste disposal vendor or contractor, if applicable.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'The environmental permit or license number under which the disposal is authorized, if required by local regulations.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the reagent lot being disposed, if disposal reason is expiration.']
  gmp_release_status varchar [note: 'The GMP release status of the lot at the time of disposal (e.g., released, quarantined, rejected, blocked).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified the disposal record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the disposal record was last modified.']
  qc_failure_code varchar [note: 'The quality control failure code or defect classification if disposal is due to failed QC testing.']
  quantity_disposed decimal(15,3) [note: 'The numeric quantity of reagent or consumable disposed in this transaction.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'The recall identifier if the disposal is part of a product recall action.']
  regulatory_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this disposal record is subject to regulatory inspection or audit review.']
  sds_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the Safety Data Sheet document for the reagent, ensuring proper handling and disposal procedures are followed.']
  storage_location_prior varchar [note: 'The storage location or bin where the reagent was held immediately before disposal (e.g., quarantine area, expired goods zone).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the disposed quantity is measured (e.g., milliliters, grams, units, vials). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mL|L|g|kg|mg|units|vials|plates — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  waste_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the waste material for environmental and safety compliance (e.g., hazardous, biohazardous, non-hazardous, chemical, radioactive).']
  waste_manifest_number varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for the waste manifest document that tracks the disposal from generation to final treatment or disposal site.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the disposal record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record documenting the controlled disposal of expired, failed, or recalled reagent lots — capturing disposal date, lot number, quantity disposed, disposal method (incineration, chemical neutralization, biohazard waste), regulatory waste classification, disposal authorization, operator ID, and waste manifest reference. Supports GMP material disposition, environmental health and safety (EHS) compliance, and regulatory inspection documentation.'
}

Table reagent.recall_event {
  recall_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the recall event record. Primary key.']
  affected_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Recalls must identify which customers received affected lots for notification and retrieval. Regulatory requirement (FDA 21 CFR Part 7) for traceability. Supports recall effectiveness checks and custo']
  affected_rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Recalls of research-grade reagents require project impact assessment to determine if active research is affected and whether experiments need repeating. Critical for research continuity planning and r']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.capa. Business justification: Recalls trigger CAPAs for root cause analysis and preventive action. Required for regulatory compliance (FDA, ISO 13485) and continuous improvement. Standard genomics biotech quality management proces']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Eliminates silo - recall_event currently has no FK relationships. Every recall event is initiated for a specific reagent catalog item. Currently recall_event has affected_product_sku and affected_prod']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Recalls are issued against catalog products for customer notification and regulatory reporting (FDA/EMA). Required to identify affected SKUs, customer shipments, and replacement product offerings. Fou']
  field_safety_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.field_safety_action. Business justification: Recalls generate field safety actions (FSN, FSCA) that document customer notification, product recovery, and effectiveness checks. Direct operational link required for CAPA tracking, regulatory report']
  internal_investigation_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this recall event to the internal quality investigation or non-conformance report that triggered the recall.']
  responsible_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Recall investigations and remediation costs are charged to responsible cost centers. Critical for financial impact analysis, insurance claims processing, quality cost reporting, and management account']
  responsible_party_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Recall events require internal employee ownership for CAPA execution, regulatory reporting (FDA/EMA), and customer communication. Links to employee record for contact information, organizational accou']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Product recalls often trigger mandatory regulatory submissions (field safety corrective actions, adverse event reports to FDA/notified bodies). Links recall event to the submission documenting regulat']
  adverse_event_count varchar [note: 'Number of reported adverse events (injuries, incorrect results, patient harm) associated with the recalled product.']
  affected_lot_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of reagent lot numbers affected by this recall event. Critical for traceability and scope determination.']
  affected_product_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier of the reagent product affected by the recall. Links to the product catalog for detailed product information.']
  affected_unit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of product units (kits, vials, flow cells, etc.) affected by the recall across all lots and distribution channels.']
  capa_completion_date date [note: 'Date when all corrective and preventive actions were verified as complete and effective. Null if CAPA is still in progress.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when all recall activities were completed, including retrieval, disposition, and customer notification. Null if recall is still ongoing.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed description of corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) implemented to address the root cause and prevent recurrence of the issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recall event record was first created in the system.']
  customer_complaint_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer complaints received related to the defect or issue that led to the recall event.']
  customer_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of customer notification activities: not_started (notifications pending), in_progress (notifications being sent), or completed (all customers notified).']
  disposition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of retrieved recalled products: destruction (incineration/disposal), rework (repair/reprocess), return_to_vendor, or quarantine (hold for investigation).']
  distribution_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of product distribution affected by the recall: internal_only (internal labs), domestic (within country), international (multiple countries), or global (worldwide distribution).']
  estimated_financial_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total financial impact of the recall event in USD, including product replacement costs, retrieval logistics, customer credits, and regulatory penalties.']
  health_hazard_evaluation varchar [note: 'Assessment of the potential health risk posed by the defective product, including probability and severity of adverse events. Used to determine recall classification.']
  initiation_date date [note: 'Date when the recall event was officially initiated and announced to stakeholders.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recall event record was last updated or modified.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or username of the person who last modified this recall event record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date when initial customer notifications were sent regarding the recall event.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to notify affected customers and stakeholders about the recall event.']
  press_release_date date [note: 'Date when the public press release was issued. Null if no press release was issued.']
  press_release_issued boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a public press release was issued for this recall event. True if press release was issued, False otherwise.']
  recall_class varchar [note: 'FDA recall classification indicating the severity of the health hazard: Class I (serious injury or death), Class II (temporary or reversible adverse health consequences), Class III (unlikely to cause adverse health consequences).']
  recall_effectiveness_check_date date [note: 'Date when the recall effectiveness check was conducted to verify that the recall achieved its intended purpose and all affected products were retrieved.']
  recall_effectiveness_result varchar [note: 'Result of the recall effectiveness check: effective (recall goals met), partially_effective (some goals met), ineffective (recall goals not met), or pending (check not yet completed).']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique recall identifier assigned by the organization or regulatory authority for tracking and reference purposes.']
  recall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recall event: initiated (recall announced), ongoing (retrieval in progress), completed (all actions finished), terminated (recall stopped), or closed (regulatory closure achieved).']
  recall_type varchar [note: 'Type of recall action: voluntary (initiated by manufacturer), mandatory (required by regulatory authority), field correction (on-site correction without removal), or market withdrawal (removal for non-safety reasons).']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory authority overseeing the recall: FDA (United States), EMA (European Union), MHRA (United Kingdom), TGA (Australia), Health Canada, or PMDA (Japan).']
  regulatory_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the regulatory report submitted to authorities (e.g., FDA MedWatch report number, IVDR vigilance report ID).']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the recall event was officially reported to the regulatory authority.']
  retrieval_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of affected units successfully retrieved, calculated as (units_retrieved / affected_unit_count) * 100. Key performance indicator for recall effectiveness.']
  root_cause_summary varchar [note: 'Detailed summary of the root cause analysis findings that led to the recall, including manufacturing defects, contamination, labeling errors, or quality control failures.']
  units_retrieved varchar [note: 'Number of affected product units successfully retrieved from customers and internal inventory as of the current status update.']

  Note: 'Transactional record managing reagent lot recall or field correction events — capturing recall initiation date, recall class (I/II/III per FDA), affected lot numbers, root cause summary, scope of distribution (internal labs, external customers), notification status, affected unit count, retrieval status, and regulatory reporting reference (FDA MedWatch, IVDR vigilance). Supports post-market surveillance, regulatory reporting obligations, and customer notification workflows.'
}

Table reagent.inventory_transaction {
  inventory_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory transaction record. Primary key for the inventory transaction entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Sequencing instrument, array scanner, or laboratory equipment that consumed the reagent. Critical for tracking flow cell usage, library preparation kit consumption, and instrument-specific reagent utilization.']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reagent catalog item involved in this transaction. Links to the master reagent definition including product specifications and regulatory classification.']
  destination_location_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Storage location to which the reagent was moved. Null for goods issue or consumption transactions where material leaves inventory. References the storage location master data including facility, room, and environmental controls.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User or employee who executed or authorized the inventory transaction. Critical for accountability, audit trails, and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) compliance.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the research experiment or assay run that consumed the reagent. Enables tracking of reagent usage in Research Use Only (RUO) and Laboratory Developed Test (LDT) workflows.']
  gmp_batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Batch record identifier for Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) regulated manufacturing operations. Links reagent consumption to specific production batches for regulatory compliance and traceability.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Inventory movements trigger general ledger postings for inventory valuation changes, COGS recognition, and financial statement accuracy. Fundamental accounting control linking physical inventory trans']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Every inventory movement (goods receipt, transfer, consumption, adjustment) must be traceable to the specific lot being moved. Currently inventory_transaction has lot_number as STRING but no FK to lot']
  primary_inventory_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Storage location from which the reagent was moved. Null for goods receipt transactions where material enters inventory from external sources. References the storage location master data including facility, room, and environmental controls.']
  reversal_inventory_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inventory_transaction (reversal_inventory_transaction_id)']
  reversed_transaction_inventory_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original inventory transaction that this record reverses or cancels. Null for original transactions. Populated for reversal entries to maintain audit trail.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: All inventory movements (receipts, issues, transfers, adjustments) are recorded against SKUs. Foundation of perpetual inventory, COGS calculation, and inventory reconciliation in genomics ERP systems.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Vendor or supplier from whom the reagent was received. Populated for goods receipt transactions. Used for supplier performance tracking and supply chain analytics.']
  coa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Certificate of Analysis (COA) document number associated with the lot involved in this transaction. Provides traceability to quality test results and specifications compliance.']
  cold_chain_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory transaction maintained required temperature controls throughout the movement. True indicates temperature was maintained within specification. False indicates potential temperature excursion requiring investigation.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which the inventory transaction is charged. Used for internal cost allocation and departmental expense tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inventory transaction record was first created in the system. Audit field for record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction value, such as USD, EUR, or GBP. Supports multi-currency inventory valuation for global operations.']
  document_date date [note: 'Date on the source document that triggered this inventory transaction, such as the date on a goods receipt note or transfer order.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the reagent lot at the time of transaction. Critical for First Expired First Out (FEFO) inventory management and preventing use of expired materials in Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) environments.']
  gmp_release_status varchar [note: 'Quality control release status of the lot at the time of transaction. Released indicates Quality Assurance (QA) approved for use. Quarantine indicates hold pending quality testing. Rejected indicates failed Quality Control (QC) and restricted from use. Pending QC indicates testing in progress. Conditional release indicates approved for specific limited use cases.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'Internal order or project number for cost tracking purposes. Used when reagent consumption needs to be allocated to specific research projects, validation studies, or manufacturing campaigns.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this inventory transaction record. Audit field for accountability and compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inventory transaction record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  movement_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the inventory movement, such as production consumption, quality control testing, research and development (R&D) use, expired material disposal, or inventory adjustment.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Accounting date on which the inventory transaction is posted to the financial ledger. May differ from transaction timestamp for period-end adjustments or backdated corrections.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number associated with goods receipt transactions. Links inventory receipt to procurement process for three-way matching and accounts payable processing.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of reagent moved in this transaction. Positive values represent increases to destination location. Negative values represent decreases from source location. Precision supports fractional units for liquid reagents and consumables.']
  reference_document_line_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the reference document that corresponds to this inventory transaction. Used when a single document contains multiple material movements.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the source transaction that triggered this inventory movement, such as purchase order number, work order number, or transfer order number. Provides audit trail to originating business event.']
  reference_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of source document that triggered or authorized this inventory transaction. Enables traceability back to originating business process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase_order|work_order|transfer_order|sales_order|quality_order|adjustment_document|disposal_record — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction is a reversal or cancellation of a previous inventory movement. True indicates this is a correcting entry. False indicates this is an original transaction.']
  sds_reference_number varchar [note: 'Safety Data Sheet (SDS) document number for the reagent. Required for hazardous material handling, storage compliance, and environmental health and safety (EHS) management.']
  system_source varchar [note: 'Source system or application that originated the inventory transaction record, such as SAP, LIMS, or warehouse management system. Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius recorded during the inventory transaction or transfer. Used for cold chain monitoring and temperature-sensitive reagent quality assurance.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius recorded during the inventory transaction or transfer. Used for cold chain monitoring and temperature-sensitive reagent quality assurance.']
  total_transaction_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total financial value of the inventory transaction calculated as quantity multiplied by unit cost. Used for financial reporting, inventory valuation, and cost accounting.']
  transaction_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments providing additional context about the inventory transaction, such as special handling instructions, deviation explanations, or operational observations.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transaction number or document number assigned to this inventory movement event. Used for traceability and audit purposes.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory transaction was executed in the operational system. Represents the actual business event time of the inventory movement.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory movement type. Goods receipt represents incoming stock from suppliers or manufacturing. Goods issue represents outbound movements. Transfer posting represents internal location changes. Consumption represents usage in experiments or production. Adjustment represents inventory corrections. Return represents material returned to stock. Disposal represents waste or expired material removal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: goods_receipt|goods_issue|transfer_posting|consumption|adjustment|return|disposal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of measure for the reagent at the time of transaction. Used for inventory valuation and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation. May represent standard cost, moving average price, or actual purchase price depending on valuation method.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the transaction quantity is expressed, such as milliliters (mL), grams (g), units (EA), kits (KIT), or flow cells (FC). Must align with the reagent catalog base unit or approved alternate units.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this inventory transaction record. Audit field for accountability and compliance.']

  Note: 'Unified transactional record of all reagent inventory movements — goods receipts from suppliers or manufacturing, internal transfers between storage locations, and consumption events. Captures transaction type, source/destination, quantity, lot reference, and timestamp.'
}

Table reagent.tag_assignment {
  tag_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this reagent-tag assignment record. Primary key for the association.']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the reagent catalog entry being tagged for classification and discovery']
  catalog_tag_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the controlled vocabulary tag being applied to the reagent']
  assigned_by varchar [note: 'Username or system identifier of the user or automated process that created this tag assignment. Used for audit and accountability.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The date when this tag was assigned to the reagent catalog entry. Tracks when the classification was applied.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining the rationale for this tag assignment, special considerations, or review comments. Used for documentation and knowledge transfer.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this tag assignment. Active assignments are used for search. Pending_review assignments require steward approval. Deprecated assignments are historical.']
  classification_algorithm varchar [note: 'The algorithm or method used to assign this tag. Examples: manual_curation, ml_classifier_v2, rule_based_engine, ontology_mapping. Enables traceability and quality control.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric confidence score (0.0 to 1.0) indicating the certainty or relevance of this tag assignment. Used for machine-learning-based tagging and quality assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this tag assignment is effective and should be used for search and discovery. Supports temporal validity of classifications.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this tag assignment is effective. Null for currently active assignments. Supports tag deprecation and reclassification workflows.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and synchronization.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when this tag assignment was reviewed and approved by a data steward. Null for unreviewed assignments.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Username of the data steward who reviewed and approved this tag assignment. Null for auto-approved or pending assignments.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the classification assignment between reagent_catalog and catalog_tag. It captures the application of controlled vocabulary tags to reagent products for data catalog discovery, FAIR compliance, and faceted search. Each record links one reagent catalog entry to one catalog tag with metadata about the assignment confidence, algorithm used, and temporal validity of the classification.. Existence Justification: In genomics biotech data governance operations, reagent catalog entries are tagged with multiple controlled vocabulary tags for FAIR-compliant discovery, faceted search, and regulatory classification (e.g., GMP-Critical, Cold-Chain, Hazardous, RUO, IVD). Each tag can be applied to many reagents across different product families. The business actively manages these tag assignments through data stewardship workflows, tracking assignment confidence, classification algorithms, temporal validity, and approval status.'
}

Table reagent.disposal_authorization {
  disposal_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for disposal_authorization']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reagent batch linked to this disposal authorization.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier for the person or role that authorized the disposal.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier for the disposal location.']
  superseded_disposal_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on disposal_authorization (superseded_disposal_authorization_id)']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the disposal authorization was approved.']
  authorization_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the disposal authorization by regulatory or internal systems.']
  authorization_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the waste the authorization covers.']
  authorized_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or department that granted the disposal authorization.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the authorization against regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the disposal authorization record was first created in the system.']
  disposal_location varchar [note: 'Designated facility or site where the waste will be disposed.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method prescribed for the safe disposal of the waste.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or code for the supporting regulatory document.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the disposal authorization becomes legally effective.']
  expiration_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the authorization was terminated or expired.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the disposal authorization expires or is no longer valid.']
  hazardous_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waste is classified as hazardous.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or safety review of the authorization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions related to the disposal authorization.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing agency that issued or oversees the disposal authorization.']
  required_documentation varchar [note: 'Documentation that must accompany the disposal process.']
  revocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authorization was revoked, if applicable.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the disposal authorization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the disposal authorization record.']
  waste_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the waste type covered by the authorization.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for disposal_authorization. Referenced by disposal_authorization_id.'
}

Table reagent.facility {
  facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for facility']
  parent_facility_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on facility (parent_facility_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the facility.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building, etc.).']
  backup_capacity_kw decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum kilowatt capacity of the backup power system.']
  building_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total usable square footage of the facility.']
  capacity_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of reagent/storage units the facility can hold.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the facility is located.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date the facility ceased operations (null if still active).']
  code varchar [note: 'Internal alphanumeric code used to reference the facility in systems.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of compliance certifications held by the facility.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for facility communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for facility communications.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the facility resides.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the facility record was first created.']
  current_occupancy_units varchar [note: 'Current number of reagent/storage units occupying the facility.']
  floor_number varchar [note: 'Floor on which the facility is located within a multi‑story building.']
  gmp_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the facility meets Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) standards.']
  humidity_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility maintains controlled humidity levels.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the facility (decimal degrees).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the facility (decimal degrees).']
  maintenance_schedule varchar [note: 'Frequency of routine maintenance activities.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the facility (e.g., "Main Sequencing Lab").']
  next_audit_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance audit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or observations.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date the facility commenced operations.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational condition of the facility.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the facility.']
  power_backup boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility has an uninterruptible power supply.']
  responsible_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manager accountable for the facility.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Security classification of the facility.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the facility location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the facility.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility maintains controlled temperature conditions.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the facility (e.g., "America/New_York").']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the facility based on its primary function.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the facility record.']
  waste_disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used for hazardous waste disposal at the facility.']
  zone varchar [note: 'Designated zone or area within the facility (e.g., "Cold Room", "QC Lab").']

  Note: 'Master reference table for facility. Referenced by facility_id.'
}

Table bioinformatics.pipeline {
  pipeline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each bioinformatics pipeline definition record in the master registry. Primary key for the pipeline data product.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.campaign. Business justification: Marketing campaigns promote specific pipeline capabilities (e.g., \'New CNV Detection Pipeline\' launch campaign). Campaign effectiveness is measured by pipeline adoption, trial requests, and usage metr']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Validated bioinformatics pipelines are commercialized as catalog products (e.g., Illumina DRAGEN, 10x Genomics Cell Ranger). Business need: product catalog management, revenue recognition, regulatory ']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the GxP validation documentation package stored in Veeva Vault (e.g., IQ/OQ/PQ validation report document ID). Links the pipeline definition to its formal validation evidence for regulatory audits.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Bioinformatics pipelines consume compute resources that must be charged to specific cost centers for departmental budget tracking, chargeback allocation, and financial reporting. Essential for operati']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the employee or team responsible for maintaining, validating, and supporting this pipeline. The pipeline owner is accountable for GxP compliance, version control, and change management.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the authoritative reference genome record in the Reference Genomics domain. Ensures pipeline-to-genome linkage for data lineage and reproducibility tracking.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Bioinformatics pipeline development projects are tracked via internal orders for cost accumulation, budget control, and capitalization decisions. Links development costs to financial settlement rules ']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_model. Business justification: Bioinformatics pipelines are validated for specific sequencer platforms (e.g., NovaSeq 6000 vs MiSeq). This link enforces platform-specific pipeline qualification requirements, ensures correct algorit']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Clinical/diagnostic pipelines are locked to specific regulatory submissions (510k, CE-IVD) for version control and post-market surveillance. New attribute needed to track which submission authorized e']
  algorithm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary algorithm or tool used by the pipeline (e.g., \'BWA-MEM\', \'STAR\', \'GATK HaplotypeCaller\', \'DeepVariant\', \'VEP\', \'ANNOVAR\'). Identifies the computational engine for reproducibility and regulatory documentation.']
  algorithm_parameters varchar [note: 'JSON-serialized string of key algorithm configuration parameters used by the pipeline (e.g., GATK HaplotypeCaller ploidy, BWA-MEM seed length, DeepVariant model type). Stored as a structured string for auditability and reproducibility. Required for GxP validation documentation.']
  algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version of the underlying algorithm or tool (e.g., \'BWA 0.7.17\', \'GATK 4.3.0.0\', \'DeepVariant 1.5\'). Distinct from the pipeline version — the same pipeline version may reference a specific algorithm version. Required for GxP validation and regulatory submissions.']
  annotation_database varchar [note: 'Name and version of the variant annotation database(s) used by the pipeline (e.g., \'ClinVar 2024-01\', \'gnomAD v3.1.2\', \'dbSNP 156\', \'COSMIC v97\'). Determines the clinical and population-level context applied to called variants.']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'Supported sequencing assay type(s) for which this pipeline is designed (e.g., WGS — Whole Genome Sequencing, WES — Whole Exome Sequencing, targeted panel, amplicon, RNA-seq). Determines pipeline applicability to incoming sample runs.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason for the creation of this pipeline version (e.g., \'Updated reference genome to GRCh38\', \'Algorithm parameter tuning for improved sensitivity\', \'Bug fix in CNV caller\'). Required for GxP change control and audit trail.']
  cnv_detection_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pipeline includes Copy Number Variation (CNV) detection capability. CNV analysis identifies genomic regions with abnormal copy numbers, relevant for oncology and rare disease diagnostics.']
  container_image varchar [note: 'Docker or Singularity container image reference (repository:tag) used to package and execute the pipeline (e.g., \'genomics-biotech/bwa-pipeline:1.0.0\'). Ensures reproducibility and environment consistency across HPC and cloud runs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pipeline record was first created in the master registry. Supports audit trail requirements under GxP 21 CFR Part 11 and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this pipeline version is approved and available for production use. Pipelines should not be used in production runs before this date. Supports GxP change control and regulatory traceability.']
  estimated_runtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated wall-clock runtime in hours for a standard pipeline execution on a typical input dataset. Used for TAT (Turnaround Time) planning, SLA commitments, and HPC/cloud job scheduling.']
  execution_environment varchar [note: 'Compute environment where the pipeline is designed to execute (HPC — High-Performance Computing cluster, AWS cloud, on-premise servers, hybrid, or Databricks). Drives infrastructure provisioning and cost allocation.']
  gxp_validation_status varchar [note: 'GxP validation status of the pipeline indicating whether it has been formally validated for use in regulated (GMP/GLP/GCP) environments. Pipelines used in IVD or clinical assay workflows must be validated per FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and ISO 13485 requirements.']
  input_data_format varchar [note: 'Primary input file format accepted by the pipeline (e.g., FASTQ for raw sequencing reads, BAM — Binary Alignment Map for aligned reads, CRAM, VCF — Variant Call Format). Defines upstream data dependencies and ingestion requirements.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Regulatory intended use classification of the pipeline: RUO (Research Use Only), IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), LDT (Laboratory Developed Test), research, clinical, or manufacturing QC. Determines applicable regulatory framework and validation requirements.']
  is_parallelizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pipeline supports parallel execution across multiple compute nodes or shards (e.g., scatter-gather parallelism in WDL). Parallelizable pipelines can be scaled horizontally on HPC or AWS for reduced TAT.']
  maf_threshold decimal(10,6) [note: 'Minor Allele Frequency (MAF) threshold used by the pipeline to filter common variants from rare variant analysis. Variants with MAF above this threshold may be excluded from clinical reporting. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.01 = 1%).']
  max_cpu_cores varchar [note: 'Maximum number of CPU cores allocated per pipeline execution job. Used for HPC/AWS resource planning, cost estimation, and job scheduling configuration.']
  max_memory_gb varchar [note: 'Maximum RAM memory in gigabytes (GB) required per pipeline execution job. Critical for HPC cluster and AWS instance type selection and cost management.']
  min_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Minimum sequencing coverage depth (read depth, expressed as integer fold-coverage, e.g., 30x) required for the pipeline to produce reliable results. Used in QC gating and assay validation documentation.']
  min_phred_score varchar [note: 'Minimum Phred quality score threshold applied during pipeline execution for base quality filtering. Phred scores represent the probability of an incorrect base call; higher values indicate higher quality. Typical threshold is Q20 or Q30.']
  min_read_length varchar [note: 'Minimum sequencing read length in base pairs (bp) required for this pipeline to function correctly. Ensures compatibility with sequencing instrument output and library preparation protocols.']
  multiplexing_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pipeline supports multiplexed sample processing (multiple samples pooled on a single flow cell and demultiplexed during processing). True if multiplexing/demultiplexing is handled within the pipeline.']
  output_data_format varchar [note: 'Primary output file format produced by the pipeline (e.g., BAM/CRAM for alignment, VCF/gVCF — Genomic VCF for variant calls, BED, TSV). Defines downstream data consumption and annotation pipeline compatibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BAM|CRAM|VCF|gVCF|BED|TSV|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name of the bioinformatics team or organizational unit responsible for this pipeline (e.g., \'Clinical Bioinformatics\', \'Research Genomics\', \'NGS Platform Engineering\'). Supports organizational accountability and resource planning.']
  pipeline_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique business identifier for the pipeline (e.g., \'NGS-ALIGN-BWA-001\', \'VC-GATK-WGS-002\'). Used in SOPs, regulatory submissions, and cross-system references.']
  pipeline_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the pipeline\'s purpose, methodology, and key capabilities. Provides context for bioinformaticians, clinical scientists, and regulatory reviewers who need to understand the pipeline\'s function without reading the source code.']
  pipeline_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the bioinformatics pipeline (e.g., \'BWA-MEM Alignment Pipeline\', \'GATK Variant Calling Pipeline\', \'VEP Annotation Pipeline\'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and user interfaces.']
  pipeline_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pipeline definition (draft, active, deprecated, retired, under_validation). Controls whether the pipeline is available for production runs. Active pipelines are cleared for use; deprecated/retired pipelines are preserved for historical traceability.']
  pipeline_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the pipeline by its primary computational function (e.g., alignment, variant calling, annotation, CNV/SNP analysis, MAF calculation, QC). Drives workflow routing and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alignment|variant_calling|annotation|cnv_analysis|snp_analysis|maf_calculation|qc|other — promote to reference product]']
  reference_genome_assembly varchar [note: 'Name and version of the reference genome assembly used by this pipeline (e.g., \'GRCh38\', \'GRCh37/hg19\', \'GRCm39\'). Critical for variant calling accuracy, annotation consistency, and regulatory traceability. Links to the Reference Genomics data domain.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date on which this pipeline version was retired or deprecated and is no longer available for new production runs. Historical runs executed under this version remain traceable. Nullable for active pipelines.']
  snp_detection_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pipeline includes Single Nucleotide Polymorphism (SNP) detection and genotyping capability. SNP analysis is foundational for population genomics, pharmacogenomics, and GWAS studies.']
  sop_reference varchar [note: 'Document number or identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing the use and execution of this pipeline (e.g., \'SOP-BIO-0042-v3\'). Required for GxP compliance and CLIA/CAP laboratory accreditation.']
  source_repository_url varchar [note: 'URL of the version-controlled source code repository for this pipeline (e.g., GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket repository URL). Enables code traceability, change history review, and CI/CD integration for GxP-compliant software development.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this pipeline record. Required for GxP audit trail compliance and change management tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  variant_calling_lod decimal(10,4) [note: 'Limit of Detection (LOD) for variant calling expressed as the minimum variant allele frequency (VAF) detectable by this pipeline. Critical for somatic variant calling pipelines and clinical assay validation documentation.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the pipeline (e.g., \'1.0.0\', \'2.3.1\'). Tracks iterative changes to algorithms, parameters, or reference genome compatibility. Critical for GxP validation traceability and reproducibility of genomic results.']
  workflow_language varchar [note: 'Workflow definition language used to implement the pipeline (e.g., WDL — Workflow Description Language, Nextflow, Snakemake, CWL — Common Workflow Language). Determines tooling requirements for execution and portability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WDL|Nextflow|Snakemake|CWL|Bash|Python|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master registry of all bioinformatics computational pipelines used at Genomics Biotech — including NGS alignment pipelines (BWA, STAR), variant calling pipelines (GATK, DeepVariant), CNV/SNP analysis pipelines, annotation pipelines (VEP, ANNOVAR), and MAF calculation workflows. Captures pipeline name, version, algorithm parameters, supported assay types (WGS, WES, targeted), reference genome compatibility, GxP validation status, and pipeline owner. This is the authoritative catalog of all pipeline definitions.'
}

Table bioinformatics.pipeline_version {
  pipeline_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each versioned record of a bioinformatics pipeline in the authoritative master registry. Primary key for the pipeline_version entity.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Each validated pipeline version corresponds to a specific product release for commercial distribution. Business need: version-specific regulatory clearances (FDA 510(k) tied to exact software version)']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the formal change control record in MasterControl or Veeva Vault that authorized the creation or modification of this pipeline version (e.g., \'CC-2024-00456\'). Mandatory for GxP-controlled pipelines to maintain a complete audit trail of version changes.']
  gene_annotation_track_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_annotation_track. Business justification: Pipeline versions must specify which annotation track version (GENCODE, RefSeq, Ensembl) for reproducibility, regulatory validation documentation, reanalysis consistency, and GxP-controlled pipeline q']
  pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.pipeline. Business justification: Master-version normalization pattern. pipeline_version is described as \'versioned lifecycle record\' and pipeline is \'Master registry\'. pipeline_version currently has pipeline_code and pipeline_name as']
  predecessor_version_pipeline_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pipeline_version_id of the immediately preceding version of this pipeline. Enables traversal of the full version lineage chain from the earliest to the current version, supporting data lineage tracking and regulatory audit trails.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Specific pipeline versions are referenced in regulatory submissions to establish analytical validity. Regulatory_submission_ids exists as text array but proper FK enables traceability for change contr']
  validated_reagent_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Pipeline validation studies qualify specific reagent SKUs (e.g., "validated with Illumina TruSeq DNA PCR-Free kit catalog #20015963"). Change control process requires revalidation when reagent formula']
  validation_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the formal validation report or protocol document stored in Veeva Vault (e.g., \'VV-VAL-2024-00123\'). Links this pipeline version to its regulatory validation evidence package for audit and regulatory submission purposes.']
  algorithm_config_json varchar [note: 'JSON-serialized configuration document capturing all algorithm parameter definitions for this pipeline version, including parameter name, data type, default value, allowed range, and GxP-controlled flag for each parameter (e.g., minimum Phred Score threshold, minimum coverage depth, MAF cutoff, LOD threshold). Stored as a STRING to avoid complex types per schema rules. Enables full parameter reproducibility and change-control auditing.']
  aligner_tool varchar [note: 'Name and version of the read alignment tool used to produce BAM files in this pipeline version (e.g., \'BWA-MEM2 2.2.1\', \'STAR 2.7.10\', \'Bowtie2 2.5.1\'). Determines alignment accuracy, BAM format characteristics, and downstream variant calling performance.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pipeline version received formal approval for production use. Distinct from validation_date (which records scientific validation completion) — this records the organizational authorization event. Required for GxP electronic signature audit trail.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the authorized signatory (e.g., Head of Bioinformatics, Quality Assurance Manager) who formally approved this pipeline version for production use. Required for GxP electronic signature compliance under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  benchling_entry_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the Benchling R&D Notebook entry documenting the scientific design, development rationale, and experimental validation data for this pipeline version. Provides traceability from the computational pipeline back to the laboratory science.']
  changelog_summary varchar [note: 'Human-readable summary of changes introduced in this pipeline version compared to the immediately preceding version. Documents algorithm updates, parameter changes, bug fixes, new assay support, or reference genome updates. Essential for regulatory submissions and scientific reproducibility.']
  container_image_digest varchar [note: 'Cryptographic SHA-256 digest of the container image, providing immutable integrity verification of the pipeline\'s computational environment. Ensures that the exact same software stack is used across all runs referencing this version, supporting GxP audit trail requirements.']
  container_image_uri varchar [note: 'Fully-qualified URI of the Docker or Singularity container image encapsulating this pipeline version\'s runtime environment (e.g., \'registry.genomicsbiotech.com/pipelines/wgs-gvc:3.2.1\'). Ensures computational reproducibility by pinning all software dependencies. Required for GxP-compliant pipeline execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pipeline version record was first created in the authoritative master registry. Supports audit trail requirements under GxP and ISO 13485. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this pipeline version is approved and authorized for production use. Defines the start of the version\'s active period in the pipeline lifecycle. Required for regulatory traceability and change control documentation.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Date on which this pipeline version\'s authorization expires or is superseded. Null indicates the version remains active with no planned end date. Used for lifecycle management, regulatory compliance, and preventing use of expired pipeline versions in clinical workflows.']
  estimated_runtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected wall-clock execution time in hours for a standard run of this pipeline version on the designated compute profile. Used for Turnaround Time (TAT) planning, SLA commitments, and compute cost estimation.']
  git_commit_hash varchar [note: 'Full 40-character SHA-1 Git commit hash uniquely identifying the exact source code state for this pipeline version. Provides immutable, cryptographically verifiable traceability to the precise codebase used, supporting GxP audit trail and reproducibility requirements.']
  gxp_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pipeline version is subject to Good Practice (GxP) regulations (GMP, GLP, GCP), requiring formal change control, validation documentation, and audit trail maintenance. True = GxP-controlled pipeline requiring full validation lifecycle; False = non-GxP pipeline (e.g., exploratory R&D).']
  hpc_compute_profile varchar [note: 'Designated High-Performance Computing (HPC) or cloud compute execution profile for this pipeline version. Specifies the orchestration environment: AWS Batch, AWS EC2, HPC SLURM cluster, HPC LSF cluster, local execution, or hybrid. Drives resource allocation and job scheduling configurations.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this pipeline version has been formally deprecated and superseded by a newer version. True = deprecated (new pipeline runs should not use this version); False = not deprecated. Distinct from \'retired\' status — deprecated versions may still be used for legacy run reproduction.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this pipeline version record. Required for change control auditing and data lineage tracking in GxP-regulated environments. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  lod_sensitivity decimal(10,6) [note: 'Analytical Limit of Detection (LOD) expressed as the minimum variant allele fraction detectable by this pipeline version at a defined confidence level. A key analytical performance characteristic for clinical and IVD-validated pipelines, reported in regulatory submissions and validation documentation.']
  maf_threshold decimal(10,6) [note: 'Minimum Minor Allele Frequency (MAF) cutoff applied during variant filtering in this pipeline version. Variants with MAF below this threshold may be classified as rare or filtered depending on pipeline configuration. Critical parameter for population genetics and clinical variant interpretation.']
  min_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Minimum sequencing coverage depth (read depth, expressed as integer fold-coverage, e.g., 30x) required for this pipeline version to produce a valid result. Values below this threshold trigger QC failure flags in pipeline runs. A key quality parameter for WGS and WES assays.']
  min_cpu_cores varchar [note: 'Minimum number of CPU cores required to execute this pipeline version. Used for compute resource planning, job scheduling, and cost estimation on HPC/AWS infrastructure.']
  min_memory_gb varchar [note: 'Minimum RAM in gigabytes required to execute this pipeline version. Critical for HPC/AWS job scheduling, instance type selection, and preventing out-of-memory failures during large WGS runs.']
  min_phred_score varchar [note: 'Minimum Phred quality score threshold applied during base quality filtering in this pipeline version. Phred scores represent the probability of an incorrect base call (Q20 = 1% error rate, Q30 = 0.1% error rate). Directly impacts variant calling sensitivity and specificity.']
  output_format varchar [note: 'Primary file format produced as the final output of this pipeline version. VCF = Variant Call Format; BAM = Binary Alignment Map; FASTQ = raw sequence data; CRAM = compressed BAM; GVCF = Genomic VCF for joint genotyping. Determines downstream compatibility with analysis tools and data consumers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VCF|BAM|FASTQ|CRAM|BED|TSV|GVCF — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name of the bioinformatics or R&D team responsible for developing and maintaining this pipeline version (e.g., \'Clinical Bioinformatics\', \'Research Genomics\', \'Array Analysis\'). Used for organizational accountability, resource planning, and support routing.']
  pipeline_category varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the pipeline by its primary computational purpose. Drives routing logic, validation requirements, and reporting groupings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alignment|variant_calling|annotation|cnv_analysis|maf_calculation|array_processing|demultiplexing|other — promote to reference product]']
  pipeline_owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the bioinformatics scientist or team lead who is the designated owner and accountable party for this pipeline version. Responsible for validation, change control, and scientific integrity of the pipeline.']
  pipeline_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this specific pipeline version. \'draft\' = under development; \'active\' = approved and in production use; \'deprecated\' = superseded by a newer version but still permitted for legacy runs; \'retired\' = no longer permitted for new pipeline runs; \'under_review\' = undergoing validation or change control review.']
  reference_genome_build varchar [note: 'Primary reference genome assembly version this pipeline version is configured to align against (e.g., GRCh38, T2T-CHM13). Critical for reproducibility, variant calling accuracy, and cross-study comparability. Determines coordinate system for BAM/VCF outputs.']
  regulatory_submission_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory submission identifiers (FDA 510(k), PMA, De Novo, EMA IVDR submission numbers) in which this pipeline version is referenced or cited (e.g., \'K240001,DEN230045\'). Enables traceability between pipeline versions and regulatory filings.']
  semantic_version varchar [note: 'Semantic versioning string (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) for this pipeline release, following SemVer 2.0 conventions (e.g., \'3.2.1\'). MAJOR increments indicate breaking algorithm changes; MINOR indicate new features; PATCH indicate bug fixes. Enables reproducibility tracking from FASTQ to final genomic output.']
  source_repository_url varchar [note: 'URL of the version-controlled source code repository (e.g., GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket) for this pipeline version, including the specific branch or tag reference (e.g., \'https://github.com/genomicsbiotech/pipelines/tree/v3.2.1\'). Enables full code reproducibility and supports IP management.']
  supported_assay_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of genomic assay types this pipeline version is designed and validated to process (e.g., \'WGS,WES,targeted_panel\'). Assay types include WGS (Whole Genome Sequencing), WES (Whole Exome Sequencing), targeted panel, array-based, amplicon, RNA-Seq, single-cell. Drives compatibility checks at pipeline run initiation.']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory use classification governing how this pipeline version may be deployed. RUO = Research Use Only; IVD = In Vitro Diagnostic (FDA-cleared/approved); LDT = Laboratory Developed Test; CE-IVD = Conformité Européenne In Vitro Diagnostic (EU market). Determines permissible clinical and commercial applications.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which formal validation of this pipeline version was completed and documented. Required for IVD, LDT, and GxP-controlled pipelines. Supports regulatory submission timelines and audit readiness.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory validation classification of this pipeline version. \'unvalidated\' = no formal validation performed; \'ruo_validated\' = validated for Research Use Only (RUO) applications; \'ivd_validated\' = validated as an In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) per FDA/IVDR requirements; \'clinical_validated\' = validated for clinical reporting under CLIA/CAP standards.']
  variant_caller_tool varchar [note: 'Name and version of the primary variant calling algorithm/tool used in this pipeline version (e.g., \'GATK HaplotypeCaller 4.4.0\', \'DeepVariant 1.5\', \'Strelka2 2.9.10\'). Directly determines the VCF output format and variant calling characteristics. Essential for reproducibility and regulatory documentation.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master registry and versioned lifecycle record of all bioinformatics computational pipelines at Genomics Biotech. Each record represents a specific version of a pipeline — capturing pipeline identity (name, owner, category: alignment/variant-calling/annotation/CNV/MAF), supported assay types (WGS, WES, targeted panel, array), semantic version number, algorithm parameter configurations (name, type, default, allowed range, GxP-controlled flag), container image references (Docker/Singularity), reference genome compatibility (GRCh38, T2T), changelog, validation status (RUO vs IVD-validated), deprecation flag, and effective date range. Serves as the single source of truth for pipeline identity and the reproducibility-enabling version history from FASTQ to final output. All pipeline_run records reference this entity.'
}

Table bioinformatics.bioinformatics_pipeline_run {
  bioinformatics_pipeline_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual pipeline execution record in the bioinformatics domain. Primary key for the pipeline_run data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Clinical and commercial sequencing runs are performed for customer accounts (pharma partners, clinical labs, research institutions). Essential for revenue recognition, SLA compliance tracking, regulat']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_asset. Business justification: Track which physical sequencer generated analyzed data. Critical for instrument-specific bias correction algorithms, troubleshooting systematic quality issues, regulatory audit trails, and correlating']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.audit. Business justification: Regulatory audits (FDA, CAP, CLIA) review specific pipeline runs for GxP compliance, data integrity, electronic signature adherence, and audit trail completeness. Auditors sample pipeline runs to veri']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the batch grouping under which this pipeline run was submitted. Supports cohort-level monitoring, batch-level result delivery, and aggregate SLA reporting across multiple samples processed together.']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Pipeline runs consume specific sequencing reagents (library prep kits, flow cell reagents). Critical for QC root cause analysis when variant calling failures correlate to reagent lot quality issues. R']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each pipeline execution incurs compute costs that must be allocated to requesting departments\' cost centers for accurate chargeback, budget variance analysis, and financial planning. Critical for IT c']
  demux_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.demux_result. Business justification: Pipelines consume demultiplexed FASTQ files as input, fundamental data flow relationship. This link enables tracking from demultiplexing through analysis, required for troubleshooting index hopping, u']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Pipeline run failures, QC breaches, or anomalous results trigger quality deviations in GxP environments. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance, CAPA tracking, and regulatory audit trails. Links compu']

Business justification: Field Application Scientists execute pipeline runs on-site during customer engagements to demonstrate capabilities, troubleshoot customer protocols, or support method development. FAS activity reports']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific reference genome assembly (e.g., GRCh38, GRCh37/hg19, T2T-CHM13) used as the alignment and variant calling baseline for this run. Critical for reproducibility, result interpretation, and regulatory submission documentation.']
  installed_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.installed_instrument. Business justification: Sequencing runs executed on customer-installed instruments (at customer sites or in service labs) must be tracked to the specific instrument for utilization analytics, warranty/service contract valida']
  instrument_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_run. Business justification: Every bioinformatics pipeline processes sequencing data from a specific instrument run. This link is essential for data provenance, quality troubleshooting, regulatory traceability (21 CFR Part 11), a']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Extends reagent traceability to lot level. Clinical genomics labs must document exact reagent lot used in each sequencing run for CLIA compliance and FDA submissions. Enables batch-level investigation']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Pipeline runs consume specific reagents, kits, and consumables (library prep kits, sequencing reagents, capture panels) tracked as materials. Essential for inventory management, cost allocation per ru']

Business justification: Clinical/diagnostic pipeline runs are frequently executed to support specific sales opportunities (customer demos, proof-of-concept studies, validation for prospects). Sales operations track which tec']
