  pipeline_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific versioned bioinformatics pipeline definition (e.g., alignment, variant calling, annotation, CNV/SNP analysis) that was executed in this run. Enables reproducibility audits and version-controlled result comparison.']
  quality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.quality_assessment. Business justification: Pipeline runs generate genomic data assets (VCF, BAM files) that undergo systematic quality assessment for FAIR compliance, regulatory submission readiness, and data sharing. Links run outputs to form']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample or sample batch that triggered this pipeline execution. Links the computational run back to the physical specimen tracked in LabVantage LIMS for end-to-end traceability from wet lab to genomic result.']
  cloud_job_reference varchar [note: 'The native job identifier assigned by the compute platform (e.g., AWS Batch Job ID, HPC SLURM Job ID) for this pipeline execution. Used for cross-referencing with infrastructure cost billing records and compute platform logs.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the pipeline run reached a terminal state (completed, failed, or cancelled). Used to calculate wall-clock duration, TAT (Turnaround Time), and SLA compliance reporting.']
  compute_environment varchar [note: 'The infrastructure platform on which this pipeline run was executed (e.g., HPC cluster, AWS cloud, GCP). Used for cost attribution, performance benchmarking across environments, and capacity planning.']
  cpu_core_hours decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total CPU core-hours consumed by this pipeline run across all compute nodes. Primary metric for cloud/HPC cost attribution, capacity planning, and per-sample computational cost reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pipeline_run record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit record creation timestamp for GxP electronic record compliance and data lineage tracking.']
  data_lineage_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier of the parent pipeline run from which this run was derived or re-triggered (e.g., a reprocessing run referencing the original failed run). Enables full data lineage chain reconstruction from raw FASTQ to final interpreted result.']
  error_category varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the failure type when a pipeline run does not complete successfully. Enables systematic failure triage, root cause analysis, and trend reporting on pipeline reliability.']
  error_message_summary varchar [note: 'Truncated human-readable summary of the error message or exception captured at pipeline failure. Supports first-level failure triage without requiring access to full log files. Maximum 2000 characters.']
  estimated_compute_cost_usd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Estimated monetary cost in US Dollars for the compute resources consumed by this pipeline run, derived from CPU core-hours, memory, and storage metrics against cloud/HPC pricing. Used for per-sample cost attribution, project budget tracking, and COGS reporting.']
  exit_code varchar [note: 'Numeric exit code returned by the pipeline process upon termination. Exit code 0 indicates successful completion; non-zero codes indicate specific failure modes. Used for automated failure classification and triage routing.']
  input_fastq_path varchar [note: 'Cloud or HPC storage path to the input FASTQ file(s) consumed by this pipeline run. FASTQ is the raw sequencing data format produced by NGS instruments. Required for data lineage tracking, reprocessing, and audit trail from raw data to result.']
  mean_coverage_depth decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of sequencing reads aligned to each base position in the target region or genome. A key QC metric indicating sequencing depth adequacy. Minimum thresholds vary by assay type (e.g., 30x for WGS, 100x for targeted panels).']
  output_bam_path varchar [note: 'Cloud or HPC storage path to the output BAM (Binary Alignment Map) file produced by alignment pipeline runs. BAM is the standard compressed binary format for aligned sequencing reads. Used for downstream variant calling and data lineage.']
  output_report_path varchar [note: 'Cloud or HPC storage path to the final analytical report or summary output artifact (e.g., QC report, annotation summary, CNV report) generated by this pipeline run. Used for result delivery to downstream consumers and regulatory documentation.']
  output_vcf_path varchar [note: 'Cloud or HPC storage path to the output VCF (Variant Call Format) file produced by variant calling pipeline runs. VCF is the standard format for representing SNPs, indels, and structural variants. Central artifact for clinical and research genomic interpretation.']
  peak_memory_gb decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum RAM (in gigabytes) consumed at any point during this pipeline run. Used for resource right-sizing, out-of-memory failure diagnosis, and HPC/cloud instance type optimization.']
  percent_bases_above_q30 decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sequencing bases with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher, indicating 99.9% base call accuracy. A primary sequencing QC metric used to assess run quality before downstream analysis. Industry standard threshold is ≥80%.']
  percent_reads_mapped decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total sequencing reads that successfully aligned to the reference genome. A key alignment QC metric; low mapping rates indicate sample quality issues, contamination, or reference genome mismatch.']
  pipeline_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bioinformatics pipeline category executed in this run. Drives downstream routing, result schema expectations, and domain-specific SLA thresholds. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alignment|variant_calling|annotation|cnv_analysis|snp_genotyping|methylation|rna_seq|gene_editing_qc — promote to reference product]']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the specific QC metric(s) that caused this pipeline run to fail QC assessment (e.g., \'Mean coverage depth 18x below 30x threshold\', \'Mapping rate 62% below 80% threshold\'). Populated only when qc_pass_flag is False.']
  qc_pass_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this pipeline run passed all defined QC thresholds (coverage depth, mapping rate, Phred score, variant count sanity checks). Drives automated downstream routing — only QC-passed runs proceed to clinical or research reporting.']
  regulatory_use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the intended use of this pipeline run\'s outputs. IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) runs are subject to FDA/IVDR validation requirements; RUO (Research Use Only) runs have different compliance obligations. Drives audit trail depth and result release controls.']
  reprocessing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pipeline run is a reprocessing of a previously executed run (e.g., triggered by QC failure, pipeline version upgrade, or parameter correction). When True, data_lineage_run_id should reference the original run.']
  run_accession_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier assigned to this pipeline execution at submission time. Used for cross-system traceability, SLA tracking, and audit references across LIMS, BaseSpace, and downstream reporting systems.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pipeline execution. Drives operational dashboards, SLA breach alerting, failure triage workflows, and batch-level completion monitoring. Transitions: queued → running → completed | failed | cancelled.']
  sequencing_assay_type varchar [note: 'The type of genomic sequencing assay whose data is being processed in this pipeline run (e.g., WGS — Whole Genome Sequencing, WES — Whole Exome Sequencing, targeted panel). Determines applicable pipeline parameters, QC thresholds, and regulatory classification.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pipeline run exceeded its defined SLA target TAT. Populated upon run completion. Drives SLA breach alerting, escalation workflows in ServiceNow, and operational performance reporting.']
  sla_target_tat_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The contracted or internally defined SLA target for Turnaround Time (TAT) in hours for this pipeline run, based on assay type and regulatory classification. Used to assess SLA compliance by comparing against actual wall-clock duration.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the pipeline run transitioned from queued to actively executing on the compute environment. Used to calculate queue wait time (start_timestamp minus submission_timestamp) and actual wall-clock execution duration.']
  storage_consumed_gb decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total intermediate and output storage consumed by this pipeline run in gigabytes. Includes FASTQ inputs, BAM alignment files, VCF outputs, and annotation artifacts. Used for storage cost attribution and capacity planning.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pipeline run was submitted to the compute queue. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for SLA measurement start, TAT (Turnaround Time) calculation, and queue wait-time analytics.']
  total_reads_count varchar [note: 'Total number of sequencing reads processed by this pipeline run. Used as a primary input volume metric for QC assessment, cost normalization, and performance benchmarking across pipeline runs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pipeline_run record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail completeness per GxP requirements.']
  variant_count varchar [note: 'Total number of genomic variants (SNPs, indels, CNVs) identified and reported in the VCF output of this pipeline run. Used for QC sanity checks, result completeness validation, and cohort-level variant burden analytics.']
  wall_clock_duration_sec varchar [note: 'Total elapsed real-world time in seconds from pipeline start to completion. Used for performance benchmarking, SLA compliance verification, and identifying pipeline runs that exceed expected execution time thresholds.']
  workflow_engine varchar [note: 'The bioinformatics workflow orchestration engine used to execute this pipeline run (e.g., Nextflow, Snakemake, CWL, WDL/Cromwell). Relevant for reproducibility documentation, debugging, and regulatory submission of computational methods.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each individual execution of a bioinformatics pipeline — capturing pipeline_version reference, triggering sample or batch identifier, compute environment (HPC cluster/AWS/GCP), job submission and completion timestamps, wall-clock duration, CPU/memory peak consumption, run status (queued, running, completed, failed, cancelled), exit code, error message summary, output artifact locations, batch grouping reference, and initiating user/system. Central operational log for all pipeline executions supporting SLA tracking, failure triage, batch-level monitoring, cost attribution, and cohort-level result delivery.'
}

Table bioinformatics.pipeline_run_step {
  pipeline_run_step_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each granular step-level execution record within a bioinformatics pipeline run. Primary key for this entity.']
  bioinformatics_pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent pipeline run that this step belongs to. Establishes the header-to-line relationship between the overall pipeline execution and its individual tool invocations.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Individual pipeline step failures (variant caller crash, alignment tool error, annotation service timeout) trigger quality deviations in validated environments. Enables granular root cause analysis an']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reference genome assembly used in this step (e.g., GRCh38, GRCh37/hg19, GRCm39). Critical for variant calling accuracy, annotation consistency, and cross-study comparability.']
  compute_cost_usd decimal(12,6) [note: 'Estimated or actual cloud/HPC compute cost in US dollars attributed to this step execution, based on instance type, duration, and resource consumption. Supports COGS allocation, R&D cost tracking, and pipeline ROI analysis.']
  compute_environment varchar [note: 'Compute infrastructure environment where this step was executed (e.g., AWS Batch, HPC SLURM cluster, local workstation). Enables environment-specific performance benchmarking and cost comparison.']
  container_image varchar [note: 'Docker or Singularity container image URI and tag used to execute this step (e.g., \'broadinstitute/gatk:4.2.6.1\'). Ensures computational environment reproducibility and supports GxP validation of the execution environment.']
  cpu_cores_requested varchar [note: 'Number of CPU cores requested for this step from the HPC/cloud compute scheduler. Used for resource planning, cost attribution, and pipeline optimization.']
  cpu_cores_used varchar [note: 'Actual number of CPU cores consumed by this step during execution, as reported by the compute scheduler. Enables resource efficiency analysis and right-sizing of future pipeline runs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pipeline run step record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per GxP and FDA 21 CFR Part 11.']
  data_lineage_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA-256) of the step\'s input files, parameters, and tool version, enabling deterministic reproducibility verification and tamper detection for GxP and regulatory audit purposes.']
  disk_io_read_gb decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total data read from storage in gigabytes during this step\'s execution. Used for storage cost attribution, I/O bottleneck analysis, and pipeline performance optimization.']
  disk_io_write_gb decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total data written to storage in gigabytes during this step\'s execution. Used for storage cost attribution, output size estimation, and pipeline performance optimization.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Wall-clock execution time of this pipeline step in seconds, as recorded by the job scheduler. Used for HPC/cloud cost attribution, performance profiling, and pipeline optimization.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Captured error or exception message from the tool\'s standard error output when the step fails. Enables rapid root cause analysis and debugging without requiring access to raw compute logs.']
  exit_code varchar [note: 'Numeric exit code returned by the tool process upon completion (0 = success; non-zero = failure). Essential for automated failure detection, root cause analysis, and pipeline debugging.']
  input_file_format varchar [note: 'Standard genomic file format of the primary input consumed by this step (e.g., FASTQ for raw reads, BAM for aligned reads, VCF for variants). Supports data lineage classification and format validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FASTQ|BAM|CRAM|VCF|GVCF|BED|TSV|FASTA|BCF — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  input_file_paths varchar [note: 'Semicolon-delimited list of input file URIs consumed by this step (e.g., S3 paths to FASTQ, BAM, or VCF files). Enables data lineage tracing from raw sequencing data through each transformation step.']
  instance_type varchar [note: 'Cloud or HPC instance/node type used to execute this step (e.g., \'r5.4xlarge\', \'c5.2xlarge\' for AWS EC2). Enables cost attribution, performance benchmarking, and optimal instance selection for future runs.']
  is_gxp_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this step is classified as GxP-critical (True), meaning it falls within the validated computational workflow for clinical or regulatory submissions and requires enhanced audit trail and change control.']
  log_file_path varchar [note: 'URI path to the full execution log file for this step (e.g., S3 path to CloudWatch log export or SLURM job log). Enables detailed debugging and GxP audit trail access.']
  memory_requested_gb decimal(10,3) [note: 'Amount of RAM in gigabytes requested for this step from the compute scheduler. Used for resource planning and cost attribution in HPC/AWS environments.']
  memory_used_gb decimal(10,3) [note: 'Peak RAM consumption in gigabytes recorded during this step\'s execution. Enables memory efficiency analysis, out-of-memory failure diagnosis, and right-sizing recommendations.']
  output_file_format varchar [note: 'Standard genomic file format of the primary output produced by this step (e.g., BAM for alignment, VCF/GVCF for variant calling, annotated TSV for annotation steps). Enables format-aware downstream processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BAM|CRAM|VCF|GVCF|BED|TSV|FASTA|BCF|HTML — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  output_file_paths varchar [note: 'Semicolon-delimited list of output file URIs produced by this step (e.g., S3 paths to aligned BAM, called VCF, or annotated TSV files). Supports downstream step input resolution and data lineage documentation.']
  parameters_json varchar [note: 'JSON-serialized string of all command-line parameters and configuration settings applied to the tool during this step execution (e.g., minimum base quality, ploidy, emit-ref-confidence mode). Required for GxP reproducibility and regulatory submission.']
  pipeline_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the overall bioinformatics pipeline definition in which this step is defined (e.g., \'2.4.1\'). Denormalized from the parent pipeline run for rapid step-level lineage queries and regulatory traceability.']
  qc_metrics_json varchar [note: 'JSON-serialized string of key QC metrics produced by this step (e.g., Phred score distribution, coverage depth, duplication rate, alignment rate, transition/transversion ratio). Enables automated QC gating and quality reporting.']
  qc_pass_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this step\'s output passed all defined QC thresholds (True = pass, False = fail). Drives automated pipeline gating decisions and flags samples requiring manual review.']
  reference_genome_build varchar [note: 'Human-readable identifier of the reference genome assembly build used in this step (e.g., GRCh38, hg19). Stored denormalized for rapid querying and cross-step lineage validation without joining to the reference genome table. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GRCh38|GRCh37|hg19|hg38|GRCm39|GRCm38|T2T-CHM13 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this step has been retried following a failure. Tracks transient infrastructure failures (e.g., AWS spot instance preemption) and informs pipeline reliability metrics.']
  spot_instance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this step was executed on a cloud spot/preemptible instance (True) or an on-demand instance (False). Spot instances reduce cost but increase retry risk; used for cost-reliability trade-off analysis.']
  step_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pipeline step completed or terminated execution. Combined with step_start_timestamp to derive step duration for performance benchmarking and TAT reporting.']
  step_execution_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for this step execution as assigned by the HPC/cloud job scheduler (e.g., AWS Batch job ID, SLURM job ID, or Nextflow process hash). Used for cross-referencing with compute infrastructure logs.']
  step_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the pipeline step (e.g., \'BWA-MEM Alignment\', \'GATK HaplotypeCaller\', \'Picard MarkDuplicates\', \'VEP Annotation\', \'Trimmomatic QC\'). Used for display, reporting, and debugging.']
  step_order varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this step within the parent pipeline run, defining the execution order and enabling reconstruction of the full data lineage chain from raw FASTQ input to final genomic result.']
  step_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pipeline step began execution on the compute infrastructure. Used for duration calculation, performance profiling, and TAT (Turnaround Time) analysis.']
  step_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pipeline step execution. Drives monitoring dashboards, alerting workflows, and SLA tracking for bioinformatics pipeline operations.']
  step_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the pipeline step indicating the category of genomic processing performed. Enables performance profiling and resource benchmarking by step category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alignment|variant_calling|annotation|qc|trimming|demultiplexing|cnv_analysis|snp_genotyping|library_prep_qc|indexing|phasing|imputation — promote to reference product]']
  tool_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bioinformatics software tool invoked during this step (e.g., BWA-MEM, GATK, Picard, STAR, Samtools, VEP, DeepVariant, Strelka2). Critical for reproducibility, audit, and regulatory submission documentation.']
  tool_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version string of the bioinformatics tool used in this step (e.g., \'4.2.6.1\' for GATK, \'0.7.17\' for BWA). Required for GxP compliance, reproducibility, and regulatory submission traceability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pipeline run step record was last modified in the data platform. Required for change tracking, incremental data loading, and GxP audit trail compliance.']
  workflow_engine varchar [note: 'Bioinformatics workflow management system used to orchestrate and execute this step (e.g., Nextflow, Snakemake, CWL, WDL/Cromwell). Relevant for reproducibility documentation and regulatory submission.']

  Note: 'Granular step-level execution records within a pipeline run — capturing individual tool invocations (e.g., BWA-MEM alignment, GATK HaplotypeCaller, Picard MarkDuplicates, VEP annotation), step order, input/output file paths, tool version, parameters applied, step status, duration, and resource consumption. Enables fine-grained debugging, performance profiling, and data lineage tracing at the tool level.'
}

Table bioinformatics.compute_job {
  compute_job_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each compute job record in the bioinformatics pipeline ecosystem. Primary key for the compute_job data product.']
  bioinformatics_pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bioinformatics pipeline run that spawned this compute job. Links the job to its parent pipeline execution context for data lineage tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Compute jobs represent billable HPC/cloud resource consumption that finance tracks against departmental budgets via cost centers. Enables accurate cost allocation, budget monitoring, and showback/char']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Compute infrastructure failures (spot instance preemption, storage corruption, network errors) in GxP environments trigger quality deviations. Required for data integrity investigations and CAPA when ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: HPC compute jobs are submitted by bioinformaticians/computational scientists. Required for resource usage reports by employee, cost center attribution, HPC quota management, and GxP audit trails track']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reference genome assembly used by this compute job (e.g., GRCh38, GRCh37, hg19). Critical for data lineage, reproducibility, and variant interpretation accuracy.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Compute resources for R&D projects are charged to internal orders for proper cost allocation, enabling capitalization tracking and settlement to fixed assets or expense accounts based on project outco']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational team or department that owns this compute job. Used for resource quota enforcement, capacity planning, and chargeback allocation.']
  pipeline_run_step_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.pipeline_run_step. Business justification: Compute jobs are spawned at the pipeline STEP level, not just the run level. A compute job executes a specific step (alignment, variant calling, etc.) within a pipeline run. compute_job currently has ']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the research or clinical project under which this compute job is executed. Used for chargeback reporting, budget tracking, and resource quota allocation.']
  algorithm_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary bioinformatics algorithm or tool executed by this job (e.g., BWA-MEM, GATK HaplotypeCaller, DeepVariant, CNVkit, STAR). Used for method traceability and regulatory documentation.']
  algorithm_version varchar [note: 'The version of the bioinformatics algorithm or tool used in this job (e.g., BWA 0.7.17, GATK 4.3.0). Required for reproducibility, method validation, and regulatory submission traceability.']
  aws_region varchar [note: 'The AWS geographic region where this cloud compute job was executed (e.g., us-east-1, eu-west-1). Used for data residency compliance, latency analysis, and regional cost optimization.']
  compute_environment varchar [note: 'The compute infrastructure platform on which this job was executed. Distinguishes between on-premises HPC schedulers (SLURM, LSF, PBS) and cloud batch services (AWS Batch, GCE, Azure). Critical for cost governance and capacity planning.']
  container_image varchar [note: 'The Docker or Singularity container image URI used to execute this job (e.g., registry/bwa:0.7.17). Ensures software environment reproducibility and supports GxP validation of computational methods.']
  cpu_cores_actual varchar [note: 'The actual number of CPU cores consumed by the job during execution as reported by the scheduler or cloud monitoring service. Used for resource utilization analysis and right-sizing recommendations.']
  cpu_cores_requested varchar [note: 'The number of CPU cores requested by the job at submission time. Compared against actual CPU usage to identify over-provisioning and optimize resource requests for future runs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this compute job record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and GxP compliance documentation.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the compute job completed, failed, or was terminated. Used to calculate wall-clock duration and measure pipeline turnaround time (TAT).']
  error_message varchar [note: 'The primary error message or exception captured from the job\'s standard error output upon failure. Used for root cause analysis, pipeline debugging, and incident reporting.']
  estimated_cost_usd decimal(12,4) [note: 'The estimated monetary cost in US dollars for this compute job, calculated based on instance type, pricing model, and wall-clock duration. Used for cloud cost governance, chargeback reporting, and budget tracking against project allocations.']
  exit_code varchar [note: 'The numeric exit code returned by the job process upon termination. Exit code 0 indicates successful completion; non-zero codes indicate specific failure modes. Used for automated failure classification and retry logic.']
  gpu_count_requested varchar [note: 'The number of GPU accelerators requested by the job at submission time. Relevant for deep learning-based variant calling, structural variant analysis, and AI/ML genomics workloads.']
  hpc_node_name varchar [note: 'The hostname or identifier of the HPC compute node on which this job executed. Used for hardware failure correlation, node performance analysis, and infrastructure capacity planning.']
  input_data_path varchar [note: 'The storage path or URI of the primary input data consumed by this job (e.g., S3 bucket path to FASTQ files, BAM files). Used for data lineage tracking from raw FASTQ to interpreted genomic results.']
  instance_type varchar [note: 'The specific compute instance or node type used for job execution (e.g., EC2 instance type such as r5.4xlarge, or HPC node class such as highmem_node). Used for cost estimation and resource optimization analysis.']
  is_spot_preempted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job was interrupted due to spot instance reclamation by the cloud provider. Used to measure spot preemption rates, assess cost-reliability trade-offs, and inform spot usage policies.']
  job_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the compute job, typically reflecting the pipeline step or algorithm being executed (e.g., bwa_alignment, gatk_variant_calling, cnv_analysis).']
  job_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the compute job within the scheduler or cloud batch system. Drives operational monitoring, SLA tracking, and pipeline retry logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|queued|running|completed|failed|cancelled|timeout|preempted — promote to reference product]']
  job_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bioinformatics workload executed by this job. Drives resource profiling and cost allocation by workload category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alignment|variant_calling|annotation|cnv_analysis|qc|demultiplexing|assembly|other — promote to reference product]']
  memory_actual_gb decimal(10,2) [note: 'The peak RAM consumption in gigabytes recorded during job execution. Used for right-sizing analysis, out-of-memory root cause investigation, and capacity planning.']
  memory_requested_gb decimal(10,2) [note: 'The amount of RAM in gigabytes requested by the job at submission time. Compared against actual memory usage to detect over-provisioning and prevent out-of-memory failures in future runs.']
  output_data_path varchar [note: 'The storage path or URI where this job\'s primary output data was written (e.g., S3 path to BAM, VCF, or annotation files). Used for downstream pipeline step chaining and data lineage documentation.']
  pipeline_version varchar [note: 'The semantic version identifier of the bioinformatics pipeline that spawned this compute job (e.g., v2.4.1). Essential for reproducibility, audit trails, and regulatory submission documentation.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'The pricing or allocation model under which the compute instance was provisioned. Spot instances offer cost savings but risk preemption; on-demand and reserved instances provide reliability. Drives cloud cost governance and budget forecasting.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'The business priority tier assigned to this compute job, governing scheduling precedence within the queue. Clinical and regulatory jobs typically receive critical or high priority; exploratory R&D jobs receive standard or low.']
  queue_name varchar [note: 'The name of the HPC job queue or cloud batch queue to which this job was submitted (e.g., high_priority, standard, low_priority, gpu_queue). Used for capacity planning and queue performance analysis.']
  quota_cpu_core_hours_consumed decimal(12,4) [note: 'The number of CPU core-hours consumed by this job, counted against the team or project\'s allocated compute quota. Used for quota enforcement, capacity planning, and fair-share scheduling.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this compute job has been retried following a failure or preemption. Used to identify unstable pipeline steps, spot instance preemption rates, and resource contention patterns.']
  scheduler_job_reference varchar [note: 'The native job identifier assigned by the HPC job scheduler or cloud batch service (e.g., SLURM job ID, LSF job ID, PBS job ID, AWS Batch job ID). Used to cross-reference with scheduler logs and cloud billing records.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the compute job began actual execution on the assigned compute resource. Used to calculate queue wait time (start minus submission) and actual wall-clock duration.']
  storage_iops_requested varchar [note: 'The number of storage input/output operations per second (IOPS) requested by the job. Critical for I/O-intensive genomics workloads such as BAM sorting, FASTQ processing, and large VCF annotation.']
  storage_read_gb decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total data volume in gigabytes read from storage during job execution. Used for data egress cost estimation, storage performance analysis, and pipeline I/O profiling.']
  storage_write_gb decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total data volume in gigabytes written to storage during job execution. Used for storage capacity planning, data lifecycle management, and pipeline output sizing.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the compute job was submitted to the scheduler or cloud batch service. Used to calculate queue wait time and measure scheduling latency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this compute job record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  wall_clock_seconds varchar [note: 'The actual elapsed real-world time in seconds from job start to job end. Distinct from CPU time; used for SLA compliance, pipeline TAT reporting, and resource efficiency analysis.']
  workflow_engine varchar [note: 'The workflow orchestration engine that submitted and manages this compute job (e.g., Nextflow, Snakemake, CWL, WDL, Apache Airflow). Used for pipeline provenance and reproducibility documentation.']

  Note: 'Tracks individual HPC cluster and cloud compute jobs spawned by pipeline runs — including job scheduler ID (SLURM/LSF/PBS/AWS Batch), compute environment details (HPC node, EC2/GCE instance type, spot vs on-demand), requested and actual resources (CPU cores, memory GB, GPU, storage IOPS), job queue name and priority tier, timing (submission, start, end, wall-clock), exit status and error output, cost estimate (USD), and resource quota consumption against team/project allocations. Supports resource optimization, capacity planning, cloud cost governance, budget tracking, and chargeback reporting.'
}

Table bioinformatics.alignment_result {
  alignment_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each BAM/CRAM alignment result record produced by a bioinformatics pipeline run. Primary key for this entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — this entity represents a discrete computational event (alignment job output) with its own lifecycle, QC status, and lineage.']
  bioinformatics_pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bioinformatics pipeline run that produced this alignment result. Links the alignment output back to the specific pipeline execution context, enabling full data lineage from raw FASTQ input to aligned output. Satisfies PARTY_REFERENCE / RESOURCE_REFERENCE category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  flow_cell_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sequencing Flow Cell used to generate the raw reads for this alignment. The Flow Cell is the consumable cartridge on which sequencing chemistry occurs. Traceability to the flow cell enables investigation of batch-level sequencing failures, reagent lot issues, and instrument performance trends.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reference genome assembly used for alignment (e.g., GRCh38/hg38, T2T-CHM13). The choice of reference genome directly impacts variant calling accuracy and clinical interpretation; this linkage is mandatory for reproducibility and regulatory traceability.']
  genomic_artifact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.genomic_artifact. Business justification: alignment_result is METADATA about an alignment (QC metrics, statistics). The actual BAM/CRAM file is a genomic_artifact. alignment_result currently has output_file_path, output_file_format, output_fi']
  library_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.library. Business justification: Alignment results are library-specific outputs required for library QC, coverage analysis, and prep troubleshooting. Labs use this to identify failed libraries, optimize prep protocols, and determine ']
  library_prep_reagent_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Alignment QC metrics (duplicate rate, insert size distribution, GC bias) are library prep chemistry-dependent. Troubleshooting workflow: when coverage uniformity degrades, correlate to library prep re']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Alignment results depend on upstream sample QC (DNA integrity, concentration, purity) performed in laboratory. Poor alignment metrics often trace to failed sample QC. Enables integrated quality invest']
  reviewed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Alignment QC results require qualified bioinformatician review before downstream variant calling, especially for clinical/GxP pipelines. Required for quality gate enforcement, personnel qualification ']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample that was sequenced and aligned. Establishes traceability from the alignment result back to the originating sample, supporting clinical reporting, regulatory audit, and downstream variant analysis workflows.']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_run. Business justification: Alignment results must trace to originating sequencing run for quality control, coverage analysis, and reprocessing decisions. Essential for clinical laboratories to validate alignment quality against']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Alignment QC metrics (coverage depth, mapping rate) establish analytical validity in regulatory submissions for NGS-based IVDs. New attribute links alignment results to submissions for performance cla']
  aligner_tool varchar [note: 'Name of the alignment software tool used to produce this result (e.g., BWA-MEM, BWA-MEM2, DRAGEN, STAR, Bowtie2, minimap2). The choice of aligner affects sensitivity, specificity, and computational cost. Required for reproducibility and regulatory method validation documentation.']
  aligner_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version string of the aligner tool used (e.g., 0.7.17, 2.2.1). Version tracking is mandatory for bioinformatics method validation, regulatory submissions (FDA, EMA), and reproducibility of clinical genomic results. A version change may require re-validation under ISO 15189 and CLIA.']
  alignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the alignment job completed and the BAM/CRAM output file was finalized. Represents the principal real-world business event time for this record. Used for Turnaround Time (TAT) calculations, SLA monitoring, and pipeline performance analytics. Satisfies BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  chimeric_read_rate_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of reads flagged as chimeric (reads where the two ends map to different chromosomes or distant genomic locations). Elevated chimeric rates indicate library preparation artifacts, structural variant signals, or sequencing errors. Monitored as a QC metric in clinical WGS and structural variant detection workflows.']
  compute_platform varchar [note: 'Compute infrastructure platform on which the alignment job was executed: AWS (Amazon Web Services), on-premise High-Performance Computing (HPC) cluster, Azure, GCP, or Illumina DRAGEN server. Required for cost allocation, performance benchmarking, and infrastructure capacity planning.']
  compute_runtime_seconds varchar [note: 'Wall-clock time in seconds taken to complete the alignment job from job start to job completion. Used for pipeline performance monitoring, SLA compliance tracking, HPC/cloud resource optimization, and cost modeling. Enables identification of performance regressions across aligner versions or reference genome builds.']
  coverage_uniformity_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of target bases covered at ≥20% of the mean coverage depth, measuring the uniformity of sequencing coverage across the target region. High uniformity indicates consistent library preparation and sequencing performance. Low uniformity may indicate GC bias or capture inefficiency in WES/panel assays.']
  cpu_cores_used varchar [note: 'Number of CPU cores allocated and used for the alignment job. Together with compute_runtime_seconds and memory_used_gb, enables accurate cloud compute cost attribution per alignment result and supports capacity planning for HPC/AWS infrastructure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this alignment result record was first ingested into the Silver Layer data lakehouse. Distinct from alignment_timestamp (job completion time). Used for data lineage, audit trails, and ETL monitoring. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  duplicate_rate_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of mapped reads flagged as PCR or optical duplicates (duplicate_reads / mapped_reads × 100). High duplicate rates reduce effective coverage depth and can introduce variant calling bias. Typically assessed using Picard MarkDuplicates or equivalent. Threshold violations trigger QC warning or failure.']
  gc_content_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of guanine (G) and cytosine (C) bases in the aligned reads. GC content deviating significantly from the expected genomic average (~41% for human) indicates GC bias in library preparation or PCR amplification, which can cause uneven coverage and affect Copy Number Variation (CNV) analysis accuracy.']
  insert_size_std_dev_bp decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard deviation of the insert size distribution in base pairs. A narrow distribution indicates consistent library preparation; a wide distribution may indicate mixed fragment populations or library quality issues. Used alongside median_insert_size_bp for comprehensive library QC assessment.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Regulatory intended use classification for this alignment result: Research Use Only (RUO), In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD), Laboratory Developed Test (LDT), clinical research, agricultural genomics, or companion diagnostic. Determines applicable regulatory framework (FDA, IVDR, CLIA) and data handling requirements including PHI/HIPAA obligations.']
  is_reanalysis boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this alignment result is a reanalysis of a previously aligned sample (True) or an original first-pass alignment (False). Reanalyses may be triggered by reference genome updates, aligner version upgrades, QC failures, or clinical reinterpretation requests. Supports data lineage and version management.']
  mapped_reads varchar [note: 'Number of sequencing reads that successfully mapped to the reference genome. Together with total_reads, this is used to compute the mapping rate. Low mapped read counts may indicate sample contamination, library preparation failure, or reference genome mismatch.']
  mapping_rate_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of total reads that successfully mapped to the reference genome (mapped_reads / total_reads × 100). A primary alignment QC metric; typical acceptable thresholds are ≥90% for human WGS. Values below threshold trigger QC failure and potential re-sequencing.']
  mean_base_quality_score decimal(6,3) [note: 'Mean Phred Score across all bases in the aligned reads. The Phred Score is a logarithmic measure of base call accuracy (Q30 = 99.9% accuracy). A key sequencing run quality metric; Q30 ≥ 80% is a standard threshold for clinical-grade sequencing. Directly impacts variant calling sensitivity and specificity.']
  mean_coverage_depth decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average number of sequencing reads covering each base position in the target region or genome (Coverage Depth). Expressed as a fold value (e.g., 30×, 100×). A fundamental QC metric determining the statistical power for variant detection; minimum thresholds vary by application (e.g., 30× for WGS, 100× for somatic panels).']
  median_insert_size_bp varchar [note: 'Median length of the DNA fragment insert in base pairs (bp) as measured from the aligned paired-end reads. Insert size distribution is a key library preparation QC metric; unexpected values indicate library degradation, adapter contamination, or incorrect size selection. Affects read overlap and variant calling accuracy.']
  memory_used_gb decimal(10,3) [note: 'Peak memory (RAM) consumed in gigabytes during the alignment job execution. Memory usage varies significantly by aligner (e.g., BWA-MEM2 requires ~60 GB for human WGS). Used for cloud instance type selection, cost optimization, and pipeline resource configuration management.']
  pct_bases_above_10x decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 10× reads. A standard coverage adequacy metric used in clinical genomics reporting to confirm that the majority of the target region has sufficient depth for reliable variant calling. Commonly reported in clinical assay validation and CAP/CLIA compliance.']
  pct_bases_above_30x decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 30× reads. The 30× threshold is the standard minimum for clinical Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS) and is required by many regulatory and accreditation bodies. Distinct from pct_bases_above_10x as it represents a higher stringency clinical threshold.']
  pct_q30_bases decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of sequenced bases with a Phred Score of 30 or higher (≥Q30), indicating ≥99.9% base call accuracy. The Q30 percentage is the primary sequencing run quality KPI reported by Illumina instruments and BaseSpace. Minimum thresholds (typically ≥75–80%) are defined in clinical assay SOPs and regulatory submissions.']
  properly_paired_rate_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of read pairs where both reads mapped to the reference genome in the expected orientation and within the expected insert size range. Low properly paired rates indicate library preparation issues, adapter contamination, or reference genome incompatibility. A standard SAMtools flagstat metric.']
  qc_review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by a bioinformatics scientist or QC reviewer during manual review of this alignment result. Captures the rationale for QC status overrides, observations about anomalous metrics, or instructions for downstream processing. Required for GxP audit trail and CLIA/CAP documentation.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Overall Quality Control (QC) pass/fail/warning determination for this alignment result based on evaluation of all QC metrics against defined thresholds. Drives downstream workflow routing: pass results proceed to variant calling; fail results trigger re-sequencing or sample rejection. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  read_length_bp varchar [note: 'Length of sequencing reads in base pairs (e.g., 150 bp for standard Illumina short-read, 10,000+ bp for long-read platforms). Read length affects alignment accuracy, variant detection capability (especially for structural variants and repeat regions), and computational resource requirements.']
  reference_genome_build varchar [note: 'Human-readable label of the reference genome assembly build used for alignment (e.g., GRCh38/hg38, T2T-CHM13). Stored denormalized here for rapid querying and reporting without joining to the reference genome dimension. Critical for clinical interpretation — variant coordinates are build-specific.']
  result_identifier varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable business identifier for this alignment result (e.g., ALN-2024-00123456). Used in LIMS reports, bioinformatics dashboards, and regulatory submissions to uniquely identify an alignment output without exposing surrogate keys. Satisfies BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  sequencing_platform varchar [note: 'Sequencing instrument platform used to generate the raw reads (e.g., Illumina NovaSeq, PacBio Revio, Oxford Nanopore PromethION). Platform determines read characteristics (length, error profile), applicable alignment tools, and QC thresholds. Required for method validation documentation and regulatory submissions.']
  sequencing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sequencing assay type for which this alignment was performed: Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), targeted panel, RNA-seq, amplicon, or metagenomics. Determines applicable QC thresholds, coverage requirements, and downstream analysis workflows. Satisfies CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.']
  strand_balance_ratio decimal(8,6) [note: 'Ratio of reads mapping to the forward strand versus the reverse strand (forward_reads / total_mapped_reads). Expected value is approximately 0.5 for unstranded libraries. Significant deviation indicates strand bias, which can introduce systematic variant calling errors, particularly for SNP and indel detection.']
  total_reads varchar [note: 'Total number of sequencing reads in the input FASTQ file(s) submitted for alignment. Reflects sequencing depth and library complexity. Used to assess whether sufficient input data was provided for the intended application (e.g., WGS, WES, targeted panel). Key QC input metric.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this alignment result record was last modified, such as when QC status was updated after manual review or when supplementary metrics were appended. Supports audit trail and data governance requirements. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']

  Note: 'Stores the output metadata and QC metrics for each BAM/CRAM alignment produced by a pipeline run — including aligned BAM/CRAM file path, reference genome used (GRCh38/hg38, T2T), aligner tool and version, total reads, mapped reads percentage, duplicate rate, mean coverage depth, uniformity of coverage, insert size distribution, Phred score distribution, and alignment QC pass/fail status. Core output artifact of the alignment stage.'
}

Table bioinformatics.variant_call_result {
  variant_call_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each variant call result record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete genomic analysis event (variant detection/genotyping run output) with a clear lifecycle from raw FASTQ to interpreted result.']
  bioinformatics_pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bioinformatics pipeline run that produced this variant call result. Links the result to the specific computational execution context, HPC/cloud job, and algorithm parameters used.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reference genome assembly used for alignment and variant calling (e.g., GRCh38, GRCh37/hg19, GRCm39). Critical for reproducibility and cross-study comparability.']
  genomic_artifact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.genomic_artifact. Business justification: variant_call_result is METADATA about variant calling (QC metrics, counts, statistics). The actual VCF file is a genomic_artifact. variant_call_result has vcf_file_path and genotype_file_path as STRIN']
  library_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.library. Business justification: Variant calls are generated per library, essential for clinical reporting and sample identification. This link enables library-level variant QC, contamination detection, and sample swap identification']
  library_prep_reagent_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Variant calling sensitivity and Ti/Tv ratio vary by library prep chemistry (PCR-free vs. PCR-plus kits have different error profiles). Clinical validation studies stratify analytical performance by re']
  prior_result_variant_call_result_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous variant call result record that this result supersedes or is a reanalysis of. Enables data lineage chain tracking from original to reanalyzed results. Null for original (non-reanalysis) results.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample that was analyzed to produce this variant call result. Enables traceability from genomic result back to the physical sample in the LIMS.']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_run. Business justification: Variant calls require provenance to sequencing run for clinical validation, regulatory submissions, and quality assessment. CAP/CLIA labs must demonstrate traceability from variant calls back to raw s']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Clinical variant calling pipelines must reference the validation study establishing analytical performance (LOD, sensitivity, specificity) for regulatory use. Required for CLIA/CAP compliance and clin']
  alt_allele varchar [note: 'Alternate (non-reference) allele sequence observed at the variant locus (e.g., T, ACGT). Follows VCF specification ALT field conventions. Required for variant interpretation, zygosity determination, and clinical reporting.']
  annotation_pipeline_version varchar [note: 'Version of the variant annotation pipeline applied to this call set (e.g., VEP, ANNOVAR, SnpEff). Tracks which annotation databases and logic were used, critical for clinical interpretation reproducibility and regulatory audit.']
  array_probe_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the array probe used to genotype this locus (applicable to array-based SNP genotyping only, e.g., Illumina Infinium probe ID). Null for NGS-based variant calls. Enables probe-level QC and array manifest cross-referencing.']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the genomic assay that generated this result. Supports workflow routing for GWAS, pharmacogenomics, oncology, clinical interpretation, ancestry, and agricultural genomics use cases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|SNP_array|CNV_array|pharmacogenomics|oncology|ancestry|agricultural — promote to reference product]']
  call_method varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the technology and analytical approach used to generate this result: NGS variant calling (WGS/WES), array-based SNP genotyping, targeted panel sequencing, or targeted genotyping. Drives downstream interpretation logic and QC thresholds.']
  call_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of loci or probes that received a valid genotype call (range 0.0000–1.0000). A key QC metric for array genotyping; samples below threshold (typically <0.97) are flagged for reanalysis. Also applicable to targeted panel completeness.']
  call_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world timestamp when the variant calling or genotyping pipeline completed and this result was generated. Principal business event time for this transaction. Used for turnaround time (TAT) calculations and SLA monitoring.']
  caller_tool_name varchar [note: 'Name of the variant calling or genotyping software tool used (e.g., GATK HaplotypeCaller, DeepVariant, Strelka2, GenomeStudio, Illumina DRAGEN). Essential for reproducibility, audit, and regulatory submission documentation.']
  caller_tool_version varchar [note: 'Software version of the variant calling or genotyping tool (e.g., 4.2.6.1 for GATK). Required for regulatory submissions, audit trails, and ensuring reproducibility of results across pipeline versions.']
  chromosome varchar [note: 'Chromosome identifier for the primary or representative variant locus in this result record (e.g., chr1, chrX, chrMT). For multi-variant result summaries, this field captures the primary locus; individual variant details are in the VCF file.']
  cnv_count varchar [note: 'Number of copy number variation (CNV) events detected in this result. Applicable to WGS, WES, and array-based CNV analysis workflows. Null for targeted SNP-only panels.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this variant call result record was first ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking per GxP and regulatory requirements.']
  dbsnp_concordance_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of called variants that are present in the dbSNP database (range 0.0000–1.0000). A QC benchmark for variant calling accuracy; high concordance (>0.95 for WGS) indicates reliable calling performance.']
  filter_criteria_applied varchar [note: 'Description or identifier of the variant filtering criteria applied to this call set (e.g., VQSR, hard-filter thresholds, panel-specific filters). Documents the post-calling filtering logic for audit, reproducibility, and regulatory submission.']
  genomic_position varchar [note: '1-based genomic coordinate of the primary or representative variant locus on the reference genome. Used for locus-level QC reporting, targeted panel coverage verification, and clinical variant lookup.']
  genotype_quality_score decimal(8,2) [note: 'Phred-scaled genotype quality score (GQ) for the primary variant call, representing confidence in the assigned genotype. Follows VCF FORMAT/GQ field conventions. Used for variant-level QC filtering and clinical reporting thresholds.']
  indel_count varchar [note: 'Number of small insertion and deletion (INDEL) variants called in this result. INDELs are a distinct variant class from SNPs and require separate QC thresholds and clinical interpretation rules.']
  is_clinical_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this variant call result has passed all QC gates and regulatory checks and is eligible for inclusion in a clinical genomics report. True = reportable; False = not reportable (failed QC, RUO designation, or pending review).']
  maf_threshold_applied decimal(6,5) [note: 'The minor allele frequency (MAF) cutoff applied during variant filtering or GWAS QC for this result (e.g., 0.01 = 1%). Variants below this threshold may be excluded from population-level analyses. Null if no MAF filter was applied.']
  mean_base_quality_score decimal(6,2) [note: 'Mean Phred quality score across all sequenced bases for this sample run. Phred score reflects base-calling accuracy (Q30 = 99.9% accuracy). A core NGS QC metric; samples below Q30 threshold may require resequencing.']
  mean_coverage_depth decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average sequencing coverage depth (mean number of reads aligned per base) across the target region for this sample and run. A fundamental NGS QC metric; minimum thresholds vary by assay (e.g., 30x for WGS, 100x for targeted oncology panels).']
  pct_bases_above_20x decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of target bases achieving at least 20x sequencing coverage depth (range 0.0000–1.0000). A standard NGS QC threshold metric used to assess adequacy of coverage for reliable variant detection.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Overall QC pass/fail status for this variant call result. Drives clinical reporting eligibility, downstream analysis inclusion/exclusion, and regulatory compliance gating. A \'fail\' or \'flagged\' result must not be released for clinical interpretation without review.']
  reanalysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this result was generated as a reanalysis of a previously processed sample (e.g., due to updated pipeline version, reference genome, or QC failure). True = reanalysis; False = original analysis. Supports data lineage and version tracking.']
  ref_allele varchar [note: 'Reference allele sequence at the variant locus as defined in the reference genome (e.g., A, GCTA). Follows VCF specification REF field conventions. Required for variant interpretation and clinical reporting.']
  regulatory_use_designation varchar [note: 'Regulatory designation governing the intended use of this result: In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD), Research Use Only (RUO), Laboratory Developed Test (LDT), or CE-IVD (EU conformity). Determines applicable regulatory reporting obligations and data handling requirements.']
  result_identifier varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this variant call result record (e.g., VCR-WGS001-20240115). Used in reports, LIMS cross-references, and regulatory submissions to uniquely identify the result output.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this variant call result record. Controls whether the result is available for clinical reporting, downstream analytics, or has been superseded by a reanalysis. Distinct from QC status.']
  rs_number varchar [note: 'dbSNP reference SNP identifier (rsID) for the primary or representative variant in this result (e.g., rs334 for the sickle cell HbS variant). Enables cross-study variant lookup, pharmacogenomics mapping, and clinical database concordance checks.']
  snp_count varchar [note: 'Number of single nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) variants called in this result. Used for QC benchmarking, GWAS analysis, and pharmacogenomics reporting.']
  ti_tv_ratio decimal(6,4) [note: 'Ratio of transition (Ti) to transversion (Tv) SNP variants in this call set. A critical QC metric for NGS variant calling: expected ~2.0–2.1 for WGS, ~3.0–3.3 for WES coding regions. Deviations indicate potential calling errors or contamination.']
  total_genotypes_called varchar [note: 'Total number of individual genotype calls made in this result. Primarily applicable to array-based SNP genotyping where each probe position yields a discrete genotype call. Distinct from variant count in NGS workflows.']
  total_variants_called varchar [note: 'Total count of all variant sites (SNPs, INDELs, CNVs, structural variants) called in this result before any filtering. Primary quantitative output metric for NGS variant calling runs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this variant call result record (e.g., QC status update, annotation refresh, reanalysis flag). Required for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  use_case_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the intended use case for this variant call result. Determines applicable regulatory framework (IVD/RUO/LDT), data access controls, and downstream workflow routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clinical|research|agricultural|pharmacogenomics|oncology|ancestry|gwas|ruo — promote to reference product]']
  variants_pass_filter_count varchar [note: 'Number of variants that passed all applied quality filters and are included in the final reportable call set. Distinct from total_variants_called which includes pre-filter counts.']
  zygosity varchar [note: 'Zygosity classification of the primary variant call: homozygous reference, heterozygous, homozygous alternate, hemizygous (X-linked in males), or no-call. Critical for clinical interpretation, inheritance pattern analysis, and pharmacogenomics dosing.']

  Note: 'Unified output record for all variant detection and genotyping results per sample per pipeline run — covering NGS variant calling (WGS/WES/targeted), array-based SNP genotyping, and targeted genotyping panels. Captures VCF/genotype file path, caller tool and version, call method discriminator (NGS variant calling, array genotyping, targeted panel), total variants/genotypes called, SNP/INDEL counts, individual genotype calls (rsID, chromosome, position, REF/ALT, zygosity, quality score, array probe ID where applicable), Ti/Tv ratio, dbSNP concordance, call rate, coverage at locus, QC pass/fail status, and filtering criteria applied. Supports GWAS, pharmacogenomics, oncology, clinical interpretation, ancestry, and agricultural genomics workflows.'
}

Table bioinformatics.variant_annotation {
  variant_annotation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each variant annotation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a genomic variant annotation artifact with its own identity, lifecycle, and rich set of interpreted attributes derived from annotation pipelines.']
  bioinformatics_pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bioinformatics annotation pipeline run that produced this annotation record. Enables data lineage tracing from raw FASTQ through to interpreted genomic results.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reference genome assembly used for alignment and variant calling (e.g., GRCh38, GRCh37/hg19). Critical for reproducibility and cross-study comparability.']
  glossary_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.glossary_term. Business justification: Variant consequence types (missense, frameshift), clinical significance terms (pathogenic, benign), and ACMG classifications require standardized glossary definitions for consistent interpretation acr']
  pharmacogenomics_marker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.pharmacogenomics_marker. Business justification: Clinical variant interpretation workflows must link identified variants to known PGx markers for automated clinical reporting, drug-gene interaction alerts, FDA/EMA PGx label compliance, and pharmacog']
  reviewed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Clinical variant annotations require expert review by qualified scientists (ABMGG-certified genetic counselors, clinical laboratory directors) before clinical reporting. Required for CAP/CLIA complian']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample from which the sequencing data and variant call originated. Provides traceability from annotation back to the source specimen managed in the LIMS.']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_run. Business justification: Annotations inherit sequencing run context from variant calls for clinical reporting and quality assessment. This link enables run-level annotation QC, batch effect analysis, and regulatory audit trai']
  transcript_model_id varchar [note: 'Ensembl or RefSeq transcript identifier used as the basis for consequence annotation (e.g., ENST00000357654, NM_007294). Specifies which transcript isoform the HGVS notation and consequence type are reported against.']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Annotation pipelines used for clinical variant interpretation require validation demonstrating database version control, annotation accuracy, and ACMG classification consistency. Links annotation to v']
  variant_call_result_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source VCF record from which this annotation was derived. Links the annotation back to the raw variant call produced by the variant calling pipeline.']
  variant_database_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.variant_database_version. Business justification: Regulatory audit trails and clinical report versioning require tracking which specific ClinVar/dbSNP/gnomAD version was used for each variant classification. Supports reanalysis decisions when databas']
  acmg_classification varchar [note: 'Variant classification assigned per ACMG/AMP 2015 guidelines for germline variants (Pathogenic, Likely Pathogenic, Variant of Uncertain Significance (VUS), Likely Benign, Benign). The authoritative classification for clinical genetic testing reports and regulatory submissions.']
  acmg_criteria_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of ACMG/AMP evidence criteria codes applied to support the variant classification (e.g., PVS1|PS3|PM2|PP3). Provides the evidence basis for the classification decision and supports audit and regulatory review.']
  alt_allele varchar [note: 'The alternate (non-reference) base(s) observed at the variant position. Defines the variant allele relative to the reference genome. May include symbolic alleles for structural variants.']
  annotation_db_version varchar [note: 'Version of the annotation database(s) used during annotation (e.g., ClinVar 2024-01, gnomAD v3.1.2, dbSNP b156). Required for regulatory submissions and reproducibility of clinical-grade annotations.']
  annotation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the annotation record: pending (awaiting annotation), completed (annotation finalized), failed (annotation pipeline error), review_required (flagged for manual curation), superseded (replaced by a newer annotation run).']
  annotation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the annotation pipeline completed and this annotation record was generated. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the annotation lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  annotation_tool varchar [note: 'Name of the annotation pipeline tool used to generate this annotation record (VEP=Ensembl Variant Effect Predictor, ANNOVAR, SnpEff). Enables tool-specific interpretation and reproducibility tracking.']
  annotation_tool_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the annotation tool used (e.g., VEP 108.0, ANNOVAR 2023-01-12). Critical for reproducibility, regulatory submissions, and audit trails per GxP and CLIA requirements.']
  cadd_phred_score decimal(6,3) [note: 'Combined Annotation Dependent Depletion (CADD) Phred-scaled score integrating multiple functional annotations to predict variant deleteriousness. Scores ≥20 indicate top 1% most deleterious variants. Used for variant prioritization in research and clinical pipelines.']
  chromosome varchar [note: 'Chromosome on which the variant is located, using standard UCSC notation (e.g., chr1, chrX, chrM). Fundamental genomic coordinate for variant localization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: chr1|chr2|chr3|chr4|chr5|chr6|chr7|chr8|chr9|chr10|chr11|chr12|chr13|chr14|chr15|chr16|chr17|chr18|chr19|chr20|chr21|chr22|chrX|chrY|chrM — promote to reference product]']
  clinvar_accession varchar [note: 'ClinVar Reference Variant (RCV) accession number uniquely identifying the variant-condition record in the ClinVar database. Enables direct cross-referencing to the authoritative clinical variant database for regulatory submissions.']
  clinvar_classification varchar [note: 'Clinical significance classification of the variant as curated in the NCBI ClinVar database (Pathogenic, Likely Pathogenic, Uncertain Significance (VUS), Likely Benign, Benign). Central to clinical reporting and IVD/LDT assay interpretation.']
  consequence_type varchar [note: 'Sequence Ontology (SO) term describing the predicted molecular consequence of the variant on the transcript (e.g., missense_variant, frameshift_variant, stop_gained, splice_site_variant). Primary driver of clinical significance classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: missense_variant|frameshift_variant|stop_gained|stop_lost|splice_donor_variant|splice_acceptor_variant|splice_site_variant|synonymous_variant|intron_variant|5_prime_UTR_variant|3_prime_UTR_variant|upstream_gene_variant|downstream_gene_variant|intergenic_variant — promote to reference product]']
  cosmic_accession varchar [note: 'Catalogue of Somatic Mutations in Cancer (COSMIC) identifier for the variant (e.g., COSM476). Relevant for oncology applications, somatic variant interpretation, and OncoKB tier assignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this annotation record was first inserted into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and GxP/CLIA compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  dbsnp_accession varchar [note: 'NCBI dbSNP reference SNP identifier (rsID) for the variant (e.g., rs80357906). Standard cross-reference identifier used in clinical reports, publications, and regulatory submissions.']
  exon_number varchar [note: 'Exon number within the canonical transcript where the variant is located (e.g., \'14/23\' indicating exon 14 of 23). Reported as a string to accommodate fractional or range notation. Used in clinical reports and splice-site analysis.']
  gene_symbol varchar [note: 'Official HGNC (HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee) gene symbol for the gene in which the variant is located (e.g., BRCA1, TP53, EGFR). Primary human-readable identifier for gene-level reporting and clinical interpretation.']
  gnomad_maf decimal(10,8) [note: 'Minor Allele Frequency (MAF) of the variant in the Genome Aggregation Database (gnomAD) global population. Used to assess variant rarity and support pathogenicity classification per ACMG/AMP criteria (BA1, BS1, PM2).']
  gnomad_population varchar [note: 'gnomAD population subgroup for which the MAF is reported (e.g., AFR=African, AMR=Latino/Admixed American, EAS=East Asian, NFE=Non-Finnish European). Required for population-stratified allele frequency interpretation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AFR|AMR|ASJ|EAS|FIN|NFE|OTH|SAS — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hgvs_coding varchar [note: 'Human Genome Variation Society (HGVS) notation describing the variant at the coding DNA sequence level (e.g., NM_007294.4:c.5266dupC). Standard notation for clinical reporting, regulatory submissions, and variant database submissions.']
  hgvs_protein varchar [note: 'HGVS notation describing the predicted amino acid change at the protein level (e.g., NP_009225.1:p.Gln1756Profs). Required for clinical variant reporting and interpretation of functional impact.']
  impact_severity varchar [note: 'Predicted functional impact severity of the variant as classified by the annotation tool (HIGH, MODERATE, LOW, MODIFIER per SnpEff/VEP convention). Used for variant prioritization in clinical and research workflows.']
  intron_number varchar [note: 'Intron number within the canonical transcript where the variant is located (e.g., \'5/22\'). Populated for intronic and splice-site variants. Used in clinical reports and splicing impact assessment.']
  is_canonical_transcript boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this annotation record is based on the canonical (primary) transcript for the gene as defined by Ensembl or MANE Select. Canonical transcript annotations are used for primary clinical reporting; non-canonical records are retained for completeness.']
  is_somatic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the variant is classified as somatic (True) or germline (False). Drives routing to oncology vs. hereditary disease interpretation workflows, and determines applicable regulatory and reporting frameworks.']
  oncokb_mutation_effect varchar [note: 'OncoKB-curated functional effect of the somatic mutation on the gene product (Gain-of-function, Loss-of-function, Neutral, Switch-of-function, Unknown). Supports oncology biomarker reporting and therapeutic decision support.']
  oncokb_tier varchar [note: 'OncoKB evidence-based therapeutic actionability tier for the somatic variant (Level 1=FDA-approved biomarker, Level 2=Standard care, Level 3A/3B=Investigational, Level 4=Biological evidence, R1/R2=Resistance). Critical for oncology precision medicine and clinical trial matching. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3A|3B|4|R1|R2 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  polyphen2_prediction varchar [note: 'Categorical PolyPhen-2 prediction for the amino acid substitution (probably damaging, possibly damaging, benign, unknown). Provides human-readable interpretation of the PolyPhen-2 score for clinical reporting.']
  polyphen2_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Polymorphism Phenotyping v2 (PolyPhen-2) score predicting the impact of an amino acid substitution on protein structure and function (0=benign, 1=probably damaging). Used as evidence for ACMG/AMP pathogenicity criteria (PP3, BP4).']
  position varchar [note: '1-based genomic coordinate (POS) on the chromosome where the variant is located, as defined in the VCF specification. Used for precise variant localization and cross-database lookups.']
  ref_allele varchar [note: 'The reference base(s) at the variant position as defined in the reference genome assembly. Used in conjunction with alt_allele to fully characterize the variant.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Level of expert review applied to the variant classification (e.g., not_reviewed, single_submitter, expert_panel, practice_guideline). Mirrors ClinVar review status levels and indicates confidence in the reported classification for clinical use.']
  sift_prediction varchar [note: 'Categorical SIFT prediction for the amino acid substitution (deleterious or tolerated, with confidence qualifier). Provides human-readable interpretation of the SIFT score for clinical reporting.']
  sift_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Sorting Intolerant From Tolerant (SIFT) score predicting whether an amino acid substitution affects protein function (0=deleterious, 1=tolerated). Used as evidence for ACMG/AMP pathogenicity criteria (PP3, BP4).']
  strand varchar [note: 'DNA strand orientation of the gene/transcript relative to the reference genome (+ for forward/sense strand, - for reverse/antisense strand). Required for correct HGVS notation and splice-site interpretation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this annotation record was last modified (e.g., re-annotation, manual curation update, status change). Required for change tracking and GxP audit trail compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  variant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the variant by its molecular nature: Single Nucleotide Polymorphism (SNP), Insertion/Deletion (INDEL), Multi-Nucleotide Polymorphism (MNP), Copy Number Variation (CNV), Structural Variant (SV), or gene Fusion. Drives downstream clinical and research interpretation workflows.']

  Note: 'Captures per-variant annotation records produced by annotation pipelines (VEP, ANNOVAR, SnpEff) — including chromosome, position, REF/ALT alleles, gene symbol, transcript ID, consequence type (missense, frameshift, splice-site), HGVS notation, ClinVar classification, gnomAD MAF, SIFT/PolyPhen scores, OncoKB tier, and annotation pipeline version. Provides the interpreted genomic context for each called variant.'
}

Table bioinformatics.cnv_result {
  cnv_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each CNV detection result record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE — represents a single CNV call output line produced by a bioinformatics pipeline run for a given sample.']
  alignment_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.alignment_result. Business justification: CNV detection consumes alignment results (BAM files) as input. cnv_result has input_bam_path as STRING path, but the BAM file is produced by an alignment_result record. This FK establishes the data li']
  bioinformatics_pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent bioinformatics pipeline run that produced this CNV result. Acts as the HEADER_REFERENCE linking this result line to its execution context (pipeline version, compute parameters, reference genome).']
  compute_job_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HPC cluster or AWS cloud compute job that executed the CNV calling step. Enables compute resource traceability, cost attribution, and job re-execution for reproducibility. Sourced from AWS Batch, Databricks Jobs, or on-premise HPC scheduler.']
  gene_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_model. Business justification: CNV clinical interpretation requires identifying which genes overlap the CNV region for pathogenicity assessment per ACMG CNV guidelines, clinical reporting, and haploinsufficiency/triplosensitivity s']
  genomic_artifact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.genomic_artifact. Business justification: cnv_result is METADATA about CNV detection (CNV calls, QC metrics).
