  regulatory_use_classification varchar [note: 'Intended regulatory use classification of the pipeline being validated. IVD: In Vitro Diagnostic cleared/approved by FDA. LDT: Laboratory Developed Test under CLIA oversight. RUO: Research Use Only, not for clinical use. CE-IVD: EU conformity-marked IVD. EUA: Emergency Use Authorization.']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Total number of samples (or synthetic datasets) processed during the validation study. Determines statistical power of the validation and is reviewed by regulatory bodies to assess adequacy of the study design.']
  sensitivity_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Measured analytical sensitivity (true positive rate) of the pipeline expressed as a percentage (0–100). Calculated as TP / (TP + FN) × 100 across the validation sample set. Key regulatory performance metric for IVD/LDT claims.']
  specificity_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Measured analytical specificity (true negative rate) of the pipeline expressed as a percentage (0–100). Calculated as TN / (TN + FP) × 100 across the validation sample set. Key regulatory performance metric for IVD/LDT claims.']
  study_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name of the validation study (e.g., \'SNV/Indel Analytical Sensitivity Validation — TumorScan v3.2\'). Used in reports, regulatory submissions, and internal tracking.']
  study_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the validation study (e.g., PVS-2024-0042). Used in regulatory submissions, SOPs, and cross-functional communications. Sourced from Veeva Vault document control numbering convention.']
  study_start_date date [note: 'The date on which formal execution of the validation study commenced (first sample or dataset processed per the study protocol). Distinct from the protocol authoring date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pipeline validation study record was most recently modified. Required for change tracking, audit trail compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the validation study. Draft: study protocol authored but not initiated. In-progress: active execution. Passed: all acceptance criteria met. Failed: one or more criteria not met. On-hold: execution paused. Cancelled: study terminated without completion.']
  validation_type varchar [note: 'Category of analytical validation being performed. Analytical sensitivity measures LOD; analytical specificity measures cross-reactivity; reproducibility measures inter-run/inter-site consistency; accuracy measures concordance with reference method; precision measures intra-run variability; interference measures matrix effects. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: analytical_sensitivity|analytical_specificity|reproducibility|accuracy|precision|interference|repeatability|comparability — promote to reference product]']
  variant_types_evaluated varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of genomic variant classes evaluated in this validation study (e.g., SNP, Indel, CNV, SV, fusion). Defines the analytical scope of the validation and maps to regulatory performance claims.']
  veeva_vault_study_reference varchar [note: 'System identifier for the validation study record as registered in Veeva Vault Quality Management System. Enables direct cross-system traceability between the analytics lakehouse record and the regulated document management system.']

  Note: 'Master record of formal analytical validation studies conducted on bioinformatics pipelines for IVD/LDT regulatory compliance — including study name, pipeline version validated, assay type, validation type (analytical sensitivity, specificity, reproducibility, accuracy), study protocol reference, validation status (in-progress, passed, failed), LOD established, regulatory submission reference (FDA, CE-IVD), and validation completion date. Required for CLIA/CAP and FDA IVD pipeline qualification.'
}

Table bioinformatics.snp_genotype_result {
  snp_genotype_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each SNP genotyping result record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: EVENT_LOG — this entity represents an immutable observation/measurement of a genotype call at a specific genomic locus produced by a genotyping pipeline run.']
  array_hybridization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the instrument or array genotyping run (e.g., Illumina array scan or sequencing flow cell run) that generated the raw signal data from which this SNP call was derived. Distinct from the bioinformatics pipeline run.']
  bioinformatics_pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bioinformatics pipeline run that produced this SNP genotype result. Links the result to its computational provenance, algorithm version, and compute parameters. Serves as the EVENT_SOURCE_REFERENCE for this event log entity.']
  gene_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_model. Business justification: Genotype results are routinely aggregated and reported by gene for pharmacogenomics panels, ancestry analysis, GWAS studies, and clinical variant interpretation. Supports gene-level QC, haplotype phas']
  glossary_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.glossary_term. Business justification: SNP genotype classifications (homozygous, heterozygous), pharmacogenomics phenotypes (poor metabolizer, rapid metabolizer), and functional consequences require controlled terminology for clinical deci']
  library_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.library. Business justification: Genotype calls are library-specific, needed for sample identity verification and contamination detection. This link enables library-level genotype concordance checks, sample swap detection, and qualit']
  reviewed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Clinical genotyping results require qualified personnel review (CLIA-certified laboratory directors, ABMGG-certified genetic counselors) before patient reporting. Required for CAP/CLIA compliance, per']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample that was genotyped. Links the SNP result to the originating specimen tracked in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS), enabling traceability from raw sample to interpreted genomic result.']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_run. Business justification: Genotyping results require sequencing run context for array-based and sequencing-based genotyping workflows. This link enables run-level genotype QC, call rate analysis, and batch effect detection. Es']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Genotyping assays used for clinical or CLIA purposes require validation studies demonstrating accuracy, precision, and concordance with reference methods. Links genotype result to validated method for']
  allele_depth_alt varchar [note: 'Number of sequencing reads supporting the alternate allele at this locus (AD field, ALT component in VCF). Null for array-based genotyping. Used alongside allele_depth_ref to compute allele balance ratio and validate variant calls.']
  allele_depth_ref varchar [note: 'Number of sequencing reads supporting the reference allele at this locus (AD field, REF component in VCF). Null for array-based genotyping. Used to calculate allele balance, detect strand bias, and validate heterozygous calls in sequencing-based workflows.']
  alternate_allele varchar [note: 'The alternate (non-reference) allele nucleotide sequence observed at this locus. Stored in IUPAC nucleotide notation per VCF specification. Used for genotype call interpretation, allele frequency calculations, and pharmacogenomics classification.']
  ancestry_panel_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SNP is designated as an Ancestry Informative Marker (AIM) in the genotyping panel design. True if the SNP is used for population stratification, ancestry inference, or admixture analysis. Supports agricultural genomics breed identification and human ancestry product workflows.']
  assay_use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory use classification of the genotyping assay that produced this result: IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic — cleared/approved for clinical use), RUO (Research Use Only — not for clinical diagnosis), LDT (Laboratory Developed Test — validated in-house), or research (internal R&D use). Governs data handling, reporting obligations, and regulatory submission requirements.']
  call_rate decimal(7,6) [note: 'Proportion of samples (or loci, depending on context) for which a confident genotype call was made at this SNP position, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1. A key Quality Control (QC) metric for array-based genotyping; loci with call rate below threshold (e.g., <0.95) are typically excluded from downstream analysis.']
  call_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the genotype call was computationally generated by the bioinformatics pipeline. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this result record. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations.']
  chromosome varchar [note: 'Chromosome on which the SNP locus resides, using standard UCSC/Ensembl notation (e.g., chr1, chrX, chrMT). Required for genomic coordinate-based queries, CNV/SNP regional analysis, and reference genome alignment validation.']
  clinical_significance varchar [note: 'Clinical significance classification of this SNP variant as reported in ClinVar or assigned by the clinical interpretation pipeline. Uses ACMG/AMP variant classification terminology. Applicable for clinical genomics and IVD assay workflows. Null for research-use-only (RUO) results without clinical interpretation.']
  coverage_depth varchar [note: 'The number of sequencing reads covering this SNP locus (read depth / coverage depth) for sequencing-based genotyping. Null for array-based genotyping. A key QC metric; loci with coverage below threshold (e.g., <10x) may yield unreliable genotype calls. Used in WGS and WES workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this SNP genotype result record was first created and persisted in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp for audit trail and data lineage purposes. Immutable after initial insert.']
  data_use_restriction varchar [note: 'The data use restriction code governing permissible downstream uses of this genomic result, as defined by the GA4GH Data Use Ontology (DUO) and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy. Values: GRU (General Research Use), HMB (Health/Medical/Biomedical), DS (Disease-Specific), POA (Population Origins/Ancestry), NRES (No Restrictions), no_restriction. Governs GWAS, pharmacogenomics, and agricultural genomics data sharing compliance.']
  functional_consequence varchar [note: 'Predicted functional consequence of the SNP on gene function as annotated by the variant annotation pipeline (e.g., missense_variant, synonymous_variant, intron_variant, 3_prime_UTR_variant, stop_gained). Uses Sequence Ontology (SO) terms as standardized by Ensembl VEP and ANNOVAR. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: missense_variant|synonymous_variant|intron_variant|3_prime_UTR_variant|stop_gained|splice_region_variant|intergenic_variant|other — promote to reference product]']
  genotype_call varchar [note: 'The diploid genotype call at this locus expressed as allele1/allele2 (e.g., A/A, A/G, G/G) using VCF-compatible notation. Represents the observed allelic state of the sample at this SNP position. Null indicates a no-call.']
  genotype_class varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the genotype call: homozygous_ref (both alleles match reference), heterozygous (one REF, one ALT allele), homozygous_alt (both alleles are ALT), or no_call (insufficient data for a confident call). Used for GWAS, pharmacogenomics stratification, and ancestry analysis.']
  genotype_posterior_probability decimal(10,8) [note: 'The posterior probability of the reported genotype call, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1. Derived from Bayesian genotype likelihood models (e.g., BEAGLE imputation, array intensity clustering). Higher values indicate greater statistical confidence in the call. Used for imputation quality filtering.']
  genotype_quality_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Phred-scaled genotype quality score (GQ) representing the confidence in the genotype call, analogous to the Phred Score used in sequencing. Higher values indicate greater confidence. Typically ranges 0–99 for array-based calls and 0–99+ for sequencing-based calls. Used for call filtering and QC thresholding.']
  genotyping_method varchar [note: 'The technology or assay method used to generate this SNP genotype call. Values: array (SNP microarray), wgs (Whole Genome Sequencing), wes (Whole Exome Sequencing), targeted_panel (targeted sequencing panel), qpcr (quantitative PCR-based genotyping), other. Drives downstream analytical workflow selection and QC thresholds.']
  gwas_catalog_trait varchar [note: 'The disease or phenotypic trait associated with this SNP as catalogued in the NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog (e.g., type 2 diabetes, breast cancer, LDL cholesterol). Null if no GWAS association is recorded. Enables phenotype-driven SNP filtering and reporting for research and clinical genomics applications.']
  haplotype_block_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier for the linkage disequilibrium (LD) haplotype block in which this SNP resides, as defined by the reference haplotype panel. Used for haplotype-based pharmacogenomics star allele calling (e.g., CYP2D6, CYP2C19), ancestry inference, and LD-aware GWAS analysis.']
  hardy_weinberg_p_value decimal(15,10) [note: 'P-value from the Hardy-Weinberg Equilibrium (HWE) exact test for this SNP locus within the study population. Significant deviation from HWE (e.g., p < 1e-6) may indicate genotyping error, population stratification, or selection pressure. A standard QC metric in GWAS and population genomics workflows.']
  imputation_r2_score decimal(10,8) [note: 'The imputation quality metric (R2 or INFO score) for imputed SNP calls, representing the estimated squared correlation between imputed and true genotypes. Ranges 0–1; values below threshold (e.g., <0.3) indicate poor imputation quality. Null for directly genotyped variants. Standard metric from MINIMAC/BEAGLE imputation tools.']
  is_imputed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SNP genotype call was statistically imputed (True) rather than directly measured by the assay (False). Imputed calls are inferred from haplotype reference panels (e.g., 1000 Genomes, TOPMed) and carry additional uncertainty. Critical for distinguishing directly genotyped from imputed variants in downstream analyses.']
  minor_allele_frequency decimal(10,8) [note: 'The frequency of the less common allele at this SNP locus within the study cohort or population, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 0.5. A critical QC and analytical metric for GWAS, population stratification, and pharmacogenomics studies. SNPs with MAF below threshold (e.g., <0.01) may be excluded from association analyses.']
  pharmacogenomics_phenotype varchar [note: 'The pharmacogenomics metabolizer or response phenotype associated with this SNP genotype call (e.g., poor_metabolizer, intermediate_metabolizer, normal_metabolizer, ultrarapid_metabolizer for CYP450 genes). Derived from CPIC or PharmGKB guidelines. Null for non-pharmacogenomics SNPs. Supports precision medicine drug dosing applications.']
  position varchar [note: '1-based genomic coordinate (base pair position) of the SNP locus on the reference chromosome. Used for positional queries, regional filtering, linkage disequilibrium analysis, and integration with annotation databases such as ClinVar and gnomAD.']
  probe_identifier varchar [note: 'The array probe or bead identifier used to interrogate this SNP locus in array-based genotyping platforms (e.g., Illumina SNP array probe ID). Null for sequencing-based genotyping. Enables traceability to the physical array design, probe performance metrics, and manifest file.']
  reference_allele varchar [note: 'The reference allele nucleotide sequence at this locus as defined by the reference genome assembly (e.g., GRCh38). Stored in IUPAC nucleotide notation. Required for genotype interpretation, variant effect prediction, and VCF-format compatibility.']
  reference_genome_assembly varchar [note: 'The reference genome assembly version used for alignment and coordinate assignment of this SNP locus (e.g., GRCh38, GRCh37/hg19). Critical for genomic coordinate interpretation, cross-study harmonization, and liftover operations. Positions are assembly-specific and must not be compared across assemblies without liftover.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this SNP genotype result record: preliminary (initial call pending review), final (reviewed and released), amended (corrected after final release), cancelled (result voided), failed_qc (result failed Quality Control thresholds and is excluded from analysis). Governs data release and downstream consumption eligibility.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when a qualified scientist or clinical reviewer approved or amended this genotype result, transitioning it to final or amended status. Null for preliminary or failed_qc results. Required for GxP audit trail and CLIA/CAP laboratory compliance.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scientist, bioinformatician, or clinical laboratory director who reviewed and approved this genotype result. Stored as an employee or user identifier string. Required for GxP electronic signature compliance and CLIA/CAP laboratory director accountability.']
  rs_number varchar [note: 'NCBI dbSNP reference SNP cluster identifier (rsID) for the variant locus. Provides a globally standardized identifier for the SNP, enabling cross-study comparisons, GWAS catalog lookups, pharmacogenomics database queries, and ancestry panel alignment. Example: rs429358.']
  source_file_name varchar [note: 'The name of the source data file (e.g., VCF, FASTQ, IDAT, GTC) from which this SNP genotype result was extracted during pipeline processing. Supports data lineage tracing from the Silver Layer record back to the raw data file in the Bronze Layer or instrument output. Examples: sample001.vcf.gz, sample001.idat.']
  strand varchar [note: 'The DNA strand orientation of the SNP locus relative to the reference genome: forward (+) or reverse (-). Required for correct allele interpretation in array-based genotyping where probe strand orientation affects allele assignment, and for cross-platform harmonization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this SNP genotype result record was last modified (e.g., status change, amendment, QC flag update). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED timestamp. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change data capture.']
  variant_filter_status varchar [note: 'Quality Control (QC) filter status for this SNP genotype result, analogous to the FILTER field in VCF format. PASS indicates the locus passed all QC thresholds. Filtered values indicate the specific QC criterion that failed (low quality score, low call rate, low MAF, Hardy-Weinberg Equilibrium deviation, strand bias). Used to exclude low-quality calls from downstream analyses. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PASS|filtered_low_quality|filtered_low_call_rate|filtered_low_maf|filtered_hwe|filtered_strand_bias|other — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Stores SNP genotyping results produced by array-based or sequencing-based genotyping pipelines — including SNP identifier (rsID), chromosome, position, REF/ALT alleles, genotype call (homozygous REF, heterozygous, homozygous ALT), genotype quality score, call rate, array probe ID or sequencing coverage at locus, and genotyping pipeline run reference. Supports GWAS, pharmacogenomics, ancestry, and agricultural genomics workflows.'
}

Table bioinformatics.artifact_tag_assignment {
  artifact_tag_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each artifact-tag assignment record']
  catalog_tag_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the catalog tag being applied']
  genomic_artifact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the genomic artifact being tagged']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of this tag assignment. Values: pending, approved, rejected, under_review. Captured from the detection phase relationship data.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tag assignment was approved']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the data steward who approved this tag assignment, if approval was required']
  assigned_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user or system that assigned this tag to the artifact']
  assignment_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tag was assigned to the genomic artifact. Captured from the detection phase relationship data.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this tag assignment, including rationale or context']
  classification_algorithm varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the algorithm or process used to assign this tag (e.g., manual, rule-based, ML model name). Captured from the detection phase relationship data.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Confidence score (0.0 to 1.0) indicating the certainty or quality of this tag assignment, particularly relevant for machine-generated tags. Captured from the detection phase relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assignment record was first created in the system']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this tag assignment is effective and should be used for discovery and access control. Captured from the detection phase relationship data.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this tag assignment is effective. Null for currently active assignments. Captured from the detection phase relationship data.']
  is_system_generated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tag assignment was automatically generated by the system or manually assigned by a user']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assignment record was last modified']
  review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this tag assignment requires human review or validation, particularly for automated assignments. Captured from the detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the metadata tagging relationship between genomic artifacts and catalog tags. It captures the assignment of controlled vocabulary tags to genomic data assets for discovery, access control, compliance reporting, and FAIR principle alignment. Each record links one genomic artifact to one catalog tag with assignment metadata including who tagged, when, confidence score, approval status, and effective dates.. Existence Justification: In genomics bioinformatics operations, genomic artifacts (FASTQ, BAM, VCF files) receive multiple catalog tags for discovery, access control, and compliance (e.g., WGS, clinical-grade, PHI, FAIR-compliant). Each artifact has many tags, and each tag applies to many artifacts. The business actively manages these tag assignments with approval workflows, confidence scoring, effective dates, and audit trails. This is operational metadata management, not analytical correlation.'
}

Table bioinformatics.pipeline_authorization {
  pipeline_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each pipeline authorization record linking a customer account to a specific pipeline version']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account receiving authorization to use this pipeline version']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the master service agreement or contract document governing this pipeline authorization. Links to contract management system.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user (regulatory affairs, bioinformatics lead, or account manager) who approved this customer-pipeline authorization. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  pipeline_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific versioned pipeline being authorized for customer use']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of this customer-pipeline authorization. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data. Values: pending, approved, rejected, suspended, expired.']
  authorization_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special conditions, restrictions, or context for this customer-pipeline authorization. Examples: customer-specific validation protocol references, usage restrictions, or renewal conditions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this authorization record was created in the system']
  customer_specific_parameters varchar [note: 'JSON-serialized configuration document capturing customer-specific parameter overrides or constraints for this pipeline version. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data. Examples: custom coverage thresholds, customer-mandated quality filters, or restricted output formats per customer contract.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this customer is authorized to use this pipeline version under their contract. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this customer authorization expires or must be renewed. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data. Null indicates indefinite authorization subject to contract terms.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this authorization record was last modified']
  usage_tier varchar [note: 'Tier of usage authorized for this customer-pipeline combination. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data. Values: RUO (research use only), IVD (in vitro diagnostic), clinical_validated, research_only, full_production. Governs regulatory compliance and liability.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date when this specific customer completed validation of this pipeline version for their use case. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual authorization and validation agreement between a specific pipeline version and a customer account. It captures customer-specific validation status, approved usage scope, effective dates per customer contract, and customer-specific parameter overrides that exist only in the context of this relationship. Each record links one pipeline_version to one account with regulatory and contractual attributes governing how that customer may use that specific pipeline version.. Existence Justification: In genomics biotechnology operations, multiple customer accounts use the same validated pipeline versions (e.g., a standard DRAGEN germline pipeline used by 50+ clinical labs for their respective patient cohorts), AND each customer account uses multiple pipeline versions (e.g., a biopharma partner uses germline, somatic, RNA-seq, and CNV pipelines across different research programs). The relationship represents a contractual and regulatory authorization that the business actively manages with customer-specific validation status, approved usage scope, effective dates per customer contract, and customer-specific parameter overrides.'
}

Table clinical.test_catalog {
  test_catalog_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for test_catalog']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: IVD tests in the catalog must reference their FDA 510(k), De Novo, or CE-IVD regulatory approval to be offered clinically. Required for CLIA compliance and test menu management. Domain experts expect ']
  clia_accreditation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.clia_accreditation. Business justification: Each test in the IVD catalog operates under specific CLIA accreditation credentials and scope. Currently test_catalog has clia_accreditation_scope as a string, but this should be normalized to FK to t']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Test catalog items are assigned to performing laboratory cost centers for standard costing, profitability analysis by test type, and transfer pricing between legal entities. Enables cost center P&L re']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Clinical tests must specify reference genome for variant calling and coordinate systems. Required for FDA/CAP regulatory compliance, result reproducibility, and data exchange. Reference_genome_build t']
  metadata_schema_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.metadata_schema. Business justification: Test catalogs must conform to metadata schemas (HL7 FHIR, GA4GH standards) for regulatory compliance and data interoperability. CAP/CLIA require structured test definitions. Essential for validating t']
  primary_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: IVD test catalog entries specify primary reagent kit/material required for each assay. Enables procurement planning, inventory management, test costing, and regulatory traceability of material specifi']
  process_validation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.process_validation. Business justification: FDA QSR requires process validation for IVD manufacturing. Test catalog must reference validation protocols to demonstrate analytical performance claims are supported by validated manufacturing proces']
  product_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: IVD test catalog items are commercial products that should reference the product catalog. This enables pricing, ordering, and inventory management for clinical test kits. The test_code becomes redunda']
  production_routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_routing. Business justification: IVD test kits are manufactured products requiring validated production routings for GMP compliance (21 CFR Part 820). Links test catalog entries to their manufacturing process definitions for regulato']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Clinical tests originate from R&D projects. Required for 21 CFR Part 820 design history file traceability, validation documentation linkage, and regulatory submission (510(k)/PMA) evidence. Domain exp']
  retention_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.retention_policy. Business justification: Test catalog entries are subject to retention policies defining how long test definitions, validation data, and historical versions must be retained. Regulatory record retention per 21 CFR Part 11 req']
  analytical_sensitivity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Analytical performance metric: percentage of true positive results correctly identified by the assay (true positive rate).']
  analytical_specificity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Analytical performance metric: percentage of true negative results correctly identified by the assay (true negative rate).']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the genomic assay methodology. WGS=Whole Genome Sequencing, WES=Whole Exome Sequencing, targeted_panel=focused gene panel, SNP_array=Single Nucleotide Polymorphism array, CRISPR_based=CRISPR gene editing assay, qPCR=Quantitative Polymerase Chain Reaction, NGS_panel=Next-Generation Sequencing panel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|SNP_array|CRISPR_based|qPCR|NGS_panel|cytogenetic|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assay_version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the assay configuration (e.g., v1.0, v2.1). Incremented when assay parameters, panel content, or pipeline change.']
  cap_accreditation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test is performed in a CAP-accredited laboratory. True=CAP accredited, False=not CAP accredited.']
  change_rationale varchar [note: 'Business and technical justification for the assay version change (e.g., improved sensitivity, updated gene panel, regulatory requirement, pipeline optimization).']
  clinical_validity_evidence varchar [note: 'Summary of evidence supporting the clinical validity of the test (e.g., peer-reviewed publications, clinical trial data, professional guidelines).']
  cpt_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit CPT billing code used for reimbursement and insurance claims for the clinical test.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this test catalog record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test catalog record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this assay version became active and available for clinical ordering.']
  intended_use_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the test. IVD=In Vitro Diagnostic (FDA-cleared or CE-marked for clinical use), RUO=Research Use Only (not for clinical decision-making), LDT=Laboratory Developed Test (CLIA-validated for clinical use).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this test catalog record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test catalog record was last updated.']
  limit_of_detection varchar [note: 'Analytical performance metric: the lowest quantity of analyte that can be reliably detected. Expressed with units (e.g., 5% variant allele frequency, 10 copies/mL).']
  ordering_physician_specialty varchar [note: 'Target physician specialties for which this test is intended (e.g., Medical Oncology, Genetic Counseling, Cardiology, Pediatrics). Multiple specialties may be listed.']
  panel_gene_list_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the gene panel content (applicable to targeted panels). Tracks which genes are included in this assay version.']
  phi_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test workflow and data handling are compliant with PHI protection requirements. True=compliant, False=not compliant.']
  pipeline_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the bioinformatics analysis pipeline used for data processing, variant calling, and annotation.']
  price_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'List price of the clinical test in USD. Used for quotations, billing, and revenue recognition.']
  reflex_testing_rules varchar [note: 'Conditional logic for automatic follow-up testing based on initial results (e.g., if variant detected in gene X, reflex to confirmatory Sanger sequencing).']
  reimbursement_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test is eligible for insurance reimbursement. True=eligible, False=not eligible (patient pay only).']
  retired_date date [note: 'Date when this assay version was retired and no longer available for new orders. Nullable for currently active versions.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Accepted biological specimen types for the assay (e.g., whole blood, saliva, buccal swab, tissue biopsy, FFPE tissue, plasma, serum). Multiple types may be listed as comma-separated values.']
  test_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the clinical assay, including methodology, clinical indications, and intended use statement.']
  test_menu_category varchar [note: 'Organizational category for grouping tests in the clinical test menu (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Rare Disease, Pharmacogenomics, Infectious Disease).']
  test_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the clinical assay as displayed in test menus, requisition forms, and clinical reports.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test in the catalog. active=available for ordering, inactive=temporarily unavailable, pending_approval=awaiting regulatory or internal approval, discontinued=permanently retired, under_review=undergoing quality or compliance review.']
  turnaround_time_hours varchar [note: 'Committed turnaround time from specimen receipt to report delivery, measured in hours. Used for SLA tracking and customer expectations.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation state of the assay version. validated=analytical and clinical validation complete, in_validation=validation studies in progress, pending_validation=awaiting validation, failed_validation=did not meet acceptance criteria, not_required=validation not applicable.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master catalog of all IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) and LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) clinical assays offered by Genomics Biotech, including versioned assay configurations. Captures test code, test name, assay type (WGS, WES, targeted panel, SNP array, CRISPR-based), intended use (RUO vs IVD), regulatory clearance status (FDA 510(k)/PMA, CE-IVD), CLIA/CAP accreditation scope, specimen types accepted, TAT commitments, CPT billing codes, assay version history (version number, effective/retired dates, reference genome build, panel gene list version, pipeline version, validation status, change rationale), and reflex testing rules. SSOT for the clinical test menu and assay version traceability.'
}

Table clinical.assay_version {
  assay_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each version of a clinical assay configuration. Primary key for the clinical assay version entity.']
  approved_reagent_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Assay versions specify approved suppliers for critical reagents as part of analytical validation and supply chain risk management. Enables supplier qualification tracking, dual-source strategies, and ']
  assay_development_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.assay_development. Business justification: Each clinical assay version originates from specific assay development work. Critical for design history traceability, validation study linkage, technology transfer documentation, and CLIA/CAP accredi']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Clinical assay versions are commercial products requiring catalog linkage for pricing, regulatory classification, lifecycle management, and revenue recognition. Essential for product lifecycle trackin']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Assay version changes (pipeline updates, panel modifications, LOD changes, reference genome updates) require change control for impact assessment, validation, training, and regulatory submission. 21 C']
  clinical_assay_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical assay in the IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) test catalog. Links this version to the base assay definition.']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.controlled_document. Business justification: Assay versions must reference their governing SOPs, protocols, and validation documents for regulatory compliance (CLIA/CAP/FDA). Critical for version control and traceability of analytical methods to']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assay development and validation activities are charged to R&D cost centers for project accounting, budget control, and capitalization eligibility determination per ASC 730. Enables tracking developme']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory director or authorized personnel who approved this assay version for clinical use. Required for regulatory compliance and audit trail.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Each assay version is analytically validated against a specific reference genome. Critical for regulatory submissions, analytical validation documentation, and pipeline version control. Replaces text ']
  library_prep_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Each assay version locks specific library prep kit as material for regulatory validation and reproducibility. Critical for version control, change management, regulatory submissions (510k/PMA), and en']
  metadata_schema_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.metadata_schema. Business justification: Assay versions define bioinformatics pipeline output formats (VCF, BAM) that must align with metadata schemas. Ensures pipeline outputs are schema-compliant for downstream analysis and data sharing vi']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Clinical assay versions are manufactured as reagent batches. Traceability required for lot genealogy, adverse event investigations, and post-market surveillance per 21 CFR Part 820.65. Enables root ca']
  rd_capitalization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.rd_capitalization. Business justification: Assay versions reaching technical feasibility and regulatory approval milestones trigger capitalization decisions per ASC 730. Link enables tracking which assay development costs are capitalized to in']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory submission (FDA 510(k), PMA, or EU IVDR technical documentation) associated with this assay version. Links to regulatory approval documentation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Assay versions require specific SKU linkage for lot traceability, regulatory compliance (21 CFR Part 820), inventory management, and configuration control. Critical for CLIA/CAP compliance and post-ma']
  analytical_validation_date date [note: 'Date when analytical validation study was completed and approved for this assay version. Required for regulatory submissions and laboratory accreditation.']
  analytical_validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the analytical validation study for this assay version. Completed and approved status required before version can be activated for clinical use. Tracks compliance with CLIA and CAP requirements.']
  annotation_database_version varchar [note: 'Version of the variant annotation database used for clinical interpretation (e.g., ClinVar version, dbSNP build, COSMIC version). Database version affects variant classification and clinical significance assignment.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assay version was formally approved for clinical use. Part of the regulatory audit trail required for CLIA and CAP compliance.']
  bioinformatics_pipeline_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the bioinformatics analysis pipeline used for data processing, variant calling, and annotation. Links to the pipeline version entity for full pipeline configuration details.']
  change_rationale varchar [note: 'Business and technical justification for creating this new assay version. Documents reasons such as improved analytical performance, updated reference genome, regulatory requirement, or bug fix. Required for regulatory audit trail.']
  clia_complexity varchar [note: 'CLIA complexity categorization for this assay version. Waived tests have lowest complexity; high complexity tests require specialized personnel and quality systems. Determines laboratory accreditation requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assay version record was first created in the system. Part of the data lineage and audit trail.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this assay version became or will become active for clinical use. All patient samples processed on or after this date use this version configuration.']
  flow_cell_type varchar [note: 'Type of flow cell used in the sequencing platform for this assay version. Different flow cell types have different throughput and read length capabilities.']
  intended_use_statement varchar [note: 'Regulatory-approved statement describing the clinical purpose and intended patient population for this assay version. Required for IVD labeling and marketing materials.']
  library_preparation_protocol varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the library preparation protocol used for sample processing in this assay version. Links to detailed SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) documentation.']
  lod_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the LOD value (e.g., percent allele fraction, copies per microliter, ng/mL). Provides context for interpreting the LOD value.']
  lod_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Analytical sensitivity expressed as the lowest quantity of analyte (e.g., variant allele fraction, copy number) that can be reliably detected. Critical performance characteristic for IVD assays.']
  minimum_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Minimum sequencing read depth required for confident variant calling in this assay version. Expressed as number of reads covering a genomic position. Critical QC (Quality Control) parameter for NGS (Next-Generation Sequencing) assays.']
  minimum_phred_score varchar [note: 'Minimum base quality score (Phred score) threshold for variant calling. Higher scores indicate higher confidence in base calls. Typical clinical thresholds are Q30 or higher.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assay version record was last updated. Tracks configuration changes and supports change control processes.']
  multiplexing_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy used for pooling multiple samples in a single sequencing run. Single index uses one barcode; dual index uses two barcodes for higher sample throughput and reduced index hopping.']
  panel_gene_list_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the curated gene list or panel used in this assay configuration. Tracks which genes are included in targeted sequencing or array-based assays. Essential for clinical validity documentation.']
  retired_date date [note: 'Date when this assay version was retired from active clinical use. Null if the version is still active or planned. Critical for traceability of which version was used for historical patient results.']
  sensitivity_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of true positive results correctly identified by the assay (true positives divided by true positives plus false negatives). Key analytical performance metric for clinical validity.']
  sequencing_platform varchar [note: 'Type of sequencing instrument or platform used for this assay version (e.g., Illumina NovaSeq, Illumina MiSeq, Ion Torrent). Platform-specific parameters affect data quality and analysis pipelines.']
  specificity_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of true negative results correctly identified by the assay (true negatives divided by true negatives plus false positives). Key analytical performance metric for clinical validity.']
  target_read_length varchar [note: 'Target length of sequencing reads in base pairs for this assay version. Affects ability to detect structural variants and map reads to repetitive regions.']
  test_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the test: IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) for FDA-cleared or CE-marked tests, LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) for CLIA-validated tests, or RUO (Research Use Only) for non-clinical tests.']
  turnaround_time_hours varchar [note: 'Target time from sample receipt to report delivery for this assay version, measured in hours. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking and operational planning.']
  variant_calling_algorithm varchar [note: 'Name and version of the primary variant calling algorithm used in this assay version (e.g., GATK HaplotypeCaller, FreeBayes, VarScan). Algorithm choice affects sensitivity and specificity of variant detection.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the clinical assay configuration (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Follows semantic versioning convention to track major and minor changes.']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this assay version. Draft indicates under development; validation indicates undergoing analytical validation; active indicates approved for clinical use; deprecated indicates superseded but still in use; retired indicates no longer in use; withdrawn indicates removed from catalog due to regulatory or quality issues.']

  Note: 'Tracks versioned configurations of each clinical assay in the IVD test catalog — capturing assay version number, effective date, retired date, reference genome build (GRCh38/hg38, T2T), panel gene list version, bioinformatics pipeline version, analytical validation status, and change rationale. Enables traceability of which assay version was used for any given patient result. Supports regulatory audit and post-market surveillance requirements.'
}
