Business justification: Research consent records link to regulatory submissions (IND, IDE, 510k validation studies) when patient samples are used for regulatory evidence generation. Required for IRB compliance and FDA audit ']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the detailed audit trail log for this consent record. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and regulatory inspections.']
  biobanking_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patient consented to long-term storage of biological samples for future research or testing. Required for biorepository management.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date when the patient provided informed consent. Used to determine consent validity period and regulatory compliance timelines.']
  consent_document_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the consent document. Used for audit trails and regulatory submissions.']
  consent_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the consent expires and must be renewed. Null for consents without expiration. Required for time-limited research protocols.']
  consent_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-3 three-letter language code indicating the language in which consent was obtained. Required for multilingual patient populations and regulatory compliance.']
  consent_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where consent was obtained (clinic name, hospital, telehealth platform). Required for audit trails and regulatory compliance.']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'Method by which consent was obtained from the patient or legal guardian. Determines documentation and audit requirements.']
  consent_scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what the patient consented to, including specific tests, data usage, and sharing permissions. Required for informed consent documentation.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consent record. Determines whether genomic testing and data usage are permissible.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Category of consent provided by the patient. Determines scope of permissible genomic testing and data usage.']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent form template used. Required for tracking consent form changes over time and ensuring regulatory compliance.']
  consenting_party_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual who provided consent. Required for legal documentation and audit trails.']
  consenting_party_type varchar [note: 'Relationship of the person providing consent to the patient. Required for minors, incapacitated patients, or legally authorized representatives.']
  consenting_physician_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physician or healthcare provider who obtained informed consent from the patient. Required for clinical accountability and audit trails.']
  consenting_physician_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the physician who obtained consent. Required for regulatory reporting and provider accountability.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this consent record. Required for accountability and audit trails.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consent record was first created in the system. Required for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  data_sharing_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patient consented to sharing of de-identified genomic data with external databases or research consortia. Required for NIH and GDPR compliance.']
  gdpr_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consent record meets GDPR requirements for EU patients. Required for international genomic testing operations.']
  germline_testing_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patient consented to germline genomic testing (inherited variants). Critical for test ordering and result interpretation.']
  incidental_findings_return_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patient consented to receive incidental or secondary findings from genomic testing. Required per ACMG guidelines for return of results.']
  interpreter_used_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a medical interpreter was used during the consent process. Required for documenting informed consent quality and regulatory compliance.']
  irb_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the IRB approved the consent form and protocol. Used to validate consent form currency and regulatory compliance.']
  irb_protocol_number varchar [note: 'IRB protocol number under which this consent was obtained. Required for research use and clinical trial genomic testing.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this consent record. Required for accountability and change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consent record was last modified. Required for audit trails and change tracking.']
  phi_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consent record meets all HIPAA PHI protection requirements. Used for compliance audits and data governance.']
  recontact_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patient consented to be recontacted for future research opportunities or updated findings. Required for longitudinal genomic studies.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the regulatory jurisdiction governing this consent. Required for multi-country genomic testing operations.']
  research_use_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patient consented to use of genomic data for research purposes beyond clinical care. Required for NIH data sharing compliance.']
  signature_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the stored signature artifact (electronic or scanned paper). Used for audit and legal verification purposes.']
  somatic_testing_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether patient consented to somatic genomic testing (tumor/acquired variants). Required for oncology genomic testing workflows.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date when patient withdrew consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Required for GDPR right to withdraw and HIPAA compliance.']
  withdrawal_method varchar [note: 'Method by which patient communicated withdrawal of consent. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance. Null if consent not withdrawn.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why patient withdrew consent. Used for quality improvement and regulatory reporting. Null if consent not withdrawn.']
  witness_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the witness who observed the consent process. Required for certain consent types and regulatory jurisdictions. Null if witness not required.']
  witness_signature_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the stored witness signature artifact. Required for certain consent types. Null if witness not required.']

  Note: 'PHI-compliant record of patient informed consent for clinical genomic testing — capturing consent type (germline testing, somatic testing, research use, biobanking, return of incidental findings), consent version, consent date, method of consent (written/electronic), patient or guardian signature reference, IRB protocol number, withdrawal status, and withdrawal date. Required for HIPAA compliance, GDPR data subject rights, and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy adherence.'
}

Table clinical.assay_qc_run {
  assay_qc_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality control run record. Primary key for the assay QC run entity.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: QC runs validate assay performance within specifications established in the regulatory approval (LOD, sensitivity, specificity). Required for demonstrating continued compliance with approval condition']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing instrument or analysis platform on which the QC run was performed. Critical for instrument-specific performance tracking and calibration.']
  bioinformatics_pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.pipeline_run. Business justification: Assay QC runs validate clinical test performance using bioinformatics pipeline outputs (positive/negative controls, coverage metrics, contamination checks). Links QC metrics to the pipeline execution ']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: QC runs validate specific commercial test products; catalog linkage required for lot release decisions, regulatory submissions (validation reports), and product-specific quality metrics tracking per C']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: QC runs consume reagents, controls, and labor charged to laboratory operations cost centers. Link enables cost-per-test analysis, operational budgeting, and variance analysis between planned and actua']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: QC run failures (coverage depth, contamination, control failures, Westgard rule violations) are the primary source of quality deviations in clinical genomics labs. Must link for root cause analysis, C']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified laboratory scientist who reviewed and approved the QC run results. Required for CLIA compliance and CAP accreditation.']
  flow_cell_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the flow cell used in the sequencing run. Tracks consumable lot-level performance and enables traceability for NGS quality issues.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: QC metrics (coverage depth, on-target rate) are reference-genome-dependent. Required for lot release documentation, trending analysis, and troubleshooting QC failures. Links QC runs to specific refere']
  ivd_test_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical test catalog entry for which this QC run was performed. Links to the specific assay version and test definition.']
  primary_assay_sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the positive control sample included in the QC run. Used to verify assay sensitivity and detect false negatives.']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: QC runs consume reagent batches; lot-to-lot performance tracking required for release decisions and trending analysis. Replaces denormalized reagent_lot_number with proper FK for batch genealogy and s']
  qc_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: QC runs validate specific reagent materials/lots before clinical use; material-level tracking enables lot release decisions, regulatory compliance (CLIA/CAP), and investigation of QC failures linked t']
  reagent_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Clinical QC runs must trace to specific reagent lots (library prep kits, sequencing reagents) for regulatory compliance, lot release decisions, and root cause analysis when QC failures occur. Critical']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_run. Business justification: Clinical assay QC runs validate sequencing run quality for diagnostic use. Regulatory requirement for CLIA/CAP compliance - QC controls must be traceable to the sequencing run they validate. Essential']
  acceptance_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the acceptance criteria document used to evaluate this QC run. Ensures traceability to validated QC thresholds.']
  capa_initiated_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a CAPA workflow was initiated as a result of this QC run failure or out-of-specification result.']
  capa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number linking this QC run to the associated CAPA record in the quality management system. Enables traceability of quality issues to resolution.']
  cluster_density decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of clusters per square millimeter on the flow cell. Optimal density ensures adequate signal intensity without overcrowding that degrades quality.']
  contamination_check_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage of cross-sample contamination detected through allele frequency analysis or sample fingerprinting. Values above threshold trigger QC failure.']
  coverage_uniformity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of target bases covered at a uniform depth, indicating consistency of sequencing across the target region. Low uniformity may indicate technical issues.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the laboratory personnel who created this QC run record. Part of the audit trail required for CLIA and CAP compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this QC run record was first created in the laboratory information management system.']
  duplicate_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sequencing reads identified as PCR or optical duplicates. High duplicate rates reduce effective coverage and may indicate library complexity issues.']
  error_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'Estimated sequencing error rate based on PhiX control or known reference sequences. Monitors instrument and reagent performance over time.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the QC run failed, including which metrics or controls did not meet acceptance criteria. Used for root cause analysis and CAPA.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the laboratory personnel who last modified this QC run record. Maintains complete audit trail for regulatory inspections.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this QC run record was last updated. Supports audit trail and change tracking for regulatory compliance.']
  library_prep_kit_lot varchar [note: 'Lot number of the library preparation kit used for sample processing prior to sequencing. Tracks consumable quality impact on QC metrics.']
  lot_release_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this QC run supports lot release decisions for reagents or consumables. Used in manufacturing quality control workflows.']
  mean_coverage_depth decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of sequencing reads covering each base position across the target region. Key metric for assessing sequencing quality and variant calling confidence.']
  negative_control_result varchar [note: 'Pass/fail determination for the negative control sample. Failure indicates potential contamination or specificity issues.']
  no_template_control_result varchar [note: 'Pass/fail determination for the no-template control. Detects reagent contamination or carryover from previous runs.']
  on_target_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sequencing reads that align to the intended target regions. Low on-target rates indicate library preparation or capture efficiency issues.']
  positive_control_result varchar [note: 'Pass/fail determination for the positive control sample. Failure indicates potential assay sensitivity issues requiring investigation.']
  q30_score_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of bases with Phred quality score of 30 or higher, indicating base call accuracy of 99.9% or better. Industry standard metric for sequencing quality.']
  qc_run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the QC run, typically formatted as a human-readable run number used in laboratory documentation and CLIA compliance records.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail determination for the QC run based on acceptance criteria evaluation. Determines whether patient samples from this run can be reported.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this QC run data is included in regulatory submissions to FDA, EMA, or other governing bodies for assay validation or post-market surveillance.']
  review_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments from the reviewing scientist documenting observations, conditional pass justifications, or follow-up actions required.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reviewing scientist completed their evaluation and signed off on the QC run. Establishes audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  run_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the QC run completed all processing steps and generated final metrics.']
  run_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the QC run was executed in the clinical laboratory.']
  run_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the QC run was initiated on the sequencing instrument or analysis platform.']
  westgard_rule_evaluation varchar [note: 'Result of Westgard multi-rule statistical QC evaluation applied to control samples. Documents which Westgard rules (1-2s, 1-3s, 2-2s, R-4s, 4-1s, 10x) were triggered, if any.']

  Note: 'Quality control run record for a clinical assay batch or sequencing run — capturing QC run ID, assay version, run date, instrument ID, reagent lot numbers, control sample results (positive, negative, no-template), QC metrics (coverage uniformity, on-target rate, duplicate rate, mean coverage depth, contamination check), Westgard rule evaluation, pass/fail determination per acceptance criteria, and reviewing scientist sign-off. Supports CLIA QC documentation, Westgard multi-rule monitoring, and lot release decisions. Links failed QC runs to CAPA workflows.'
}

Table clinical.incidental_finding {
  incidental_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the incidental finding record. Primary key for this entity.']
  acmg_classification_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.acmg_classification_rule. Business justification: Documents ACMG evidence codes applied to incidental findings classification. Required for clinical validation, patient notification decisions, and regulatory compliance. Links classification to struct']
  gene_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_model. Business justification: ACMG secondary findings are gene-specific (SF v3.2 list). Must link to reference gene model for standardized reporting and automated detection pipelines. Acmg_gene text field should be replaced with F']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the patient for whom this incidental finding was identified. Protected Health Information (PHI) under HIPAA.']
  policy_controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.controlled_document. Business justification: Incidental finding reporting decisions must reference the specific version of ACMG Secondary Findings policy (v2.0, v3.0, v3.2) used. Regulatory and ethical requirement for defensibility of disclosure']
  primary_incidental_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the genetic counselor assigned to discuss this incidental finding with the patient. Required for reportable findings per ACMG recommendations.']
  quaternary_incidental_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory director or designee who approved and signed out the incidental finding report.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Incidental findings discovered during research studies link to projects for IRB adverse event reporting, protocol-specific return-of-results policies, publication disclosure, and research ethics compl']
  tertiary_incidental_reviewed_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory professional who performed peer review of the variant interpretation.']
  test_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical test order during which this incidental finding was identified.']
  acmg_classification varchar [note: 'ACMG/AMP variant classification tier indicating clinical significance. Pathogenic and likely pathogenic variants are medically actionable.']
  allele_frequency decimal(10,8) [note: 'Population allele frequency of this variant in reference databases such as gnomAD. Expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1.']
  alternate_allele varchar [note: 'Alternate allele sequence observed in the patient sample at the variant position.']
  associated_condition varchar [note: 'Medical condition or disease associated with this incidental finding. Examples include hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome, Lynch syndrome, familial hypercholesterolemia.']
  chromosome varchar [note: 'Chromosome on which the variant is located. Values include 1-22, X, Y, or MT (mitochondrial).']
  clinical_action_recommended varchar [note: 'Free-text description of recommended clinical actions based on this incidental finding, such as enhanced surveillance, prophylactic interventions, or specialist referral.']
  clinical_significance varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the clinical significance of the incidental finding, including associated disease risk, penetrance, and recommended clinical actions.']
  clinvar_accession varchar [note: 'ClinVar accession number for this variant if the variant has been submitted to ClinVar. Format is VCV followed by numeric identifier.']
  coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Number of sequencing reads covering the variant position. Higher coverage provides greater confidence in variant calling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incidental finding record was first created in the system.']
  dbsnp_accession varchar [note: 'dbSNP reference SNP cluster ID (rsID) for this variant if available. Format is rs followed by numeric identifier.']
  evidence_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of clinical and scientific evidence supporting the variant classification, including references to published literature, functional studies, and population data.']
  family_cascade_recommended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cascade testing of at-risk family members is recommended based on the clinical significance and inheritance pattern of this finding.']
  filter_status varchar [note: 'Quality filter status applied during variant calling. PASS indicates the variant passed all quality filters; other values indicate specific quality concerns.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Category of incidental finding identified. ACMG secondary finding refers to medically actionable genes per ACMG SF v3.2 list; pharmacogenomic refers to drug-gene interactions; carrier status refers to recessive disease alleles.']
  genomic_position varchar [note: 'Genomic coordinate (base pair position) of the variant on the reference genome build.']
  identified_date date [note: 'Date on which the incidental finding was first identified during bioinformatics analysis or variant interpretation.']
  interpretation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the variant interpretation workflow for this incidental finding. Draft indicates initial interpretation; reviewed indicates peer review complete; approved indicates sign-out by laboratory director; amended indicates post-release update.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incidental finding record was last modified, including updates to interpretation status, reporting decision, or variant classification.']
  patient_consent_preference varchar [note: 'Patient preference for receiving incidental findings as documented in informed consent. Receive all indicates patient wants all secondary findings; receive actionable only limits to medically actionable findings; decline all indicates patient opted out.']
  patient_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the patient was notified of the incidental finding, typically through genetic counseling session or clinical consultation. Null if patient has not yet been notified.']
  phi_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this incidental finding record has been reviewed for HIPAA PHI compliance and appropriate access controls have been applied.']
  quality_score decimal(8,2) [note: 'Phred-scaled quality score for the variant call. Higher scores indicate greater confidence in the variant call.']
  reference_allele varchar [note: 'Reference allele sequence at the variant position as defined by the reference genome.']
  reference_genome_build varchar [note: 'Human reference genome assembly version used for variant calling and coordinate mapping. GRCh37 (hg19) and GRCh38 (hg38) are the most common builds.']
  reported_date date [note: 'Date on which the incidental finding was reported to the patient and ordering physician. Null if reporting decision is do not report.']
  reporting_decision varchar [note: 'Decision on whether to report this incidental finding to the patient and ordering physician. Based on patient consent preference, clinical significance, and laboratory policy.']
  reporting_rationale varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of the rationale for the reporting decision, including consideration of patient consent, clinical actionability, and laboratory policy.']
  variant_allele_fraction decimal(5,4) [note: 'Fraction of sequencing reads supporting the alternate allele at the variant position. Expected to be approximately 0.5 for heterozygous germline variants and 1.0 for homozygous variants.']
  variant_hgvs_notation varchar [note: 'Standardized HGVS nomenclature describing the genomic variant associated with this incidental finding. Format follows HGVS guidelines for genomic, coding, or protein-level notation.']
  zygosity varchar [note: 'Zygosity status of the variant. Heterozygous indicates one copy of the variant allele; homozygous indicates two copies; hemizygous applies to X-linked variants in males.']

  Note: 'Records clinically significant incidental (secondary) findings identified during genomic testing that are outside the primary test indication — capturing finding type (ACMG SF v3.2 gene list, pharmacogenomic, carrier status), variant details, ACMG classification, patient notification preference (per consent), reporting decision (report/not report), reporting date, and genetic counselor assignment. Supports ACMG secondary findings policy compliance and patient communication workflows.'
}

Table clinical.genetic_counseling_session {
  genetic_counseling_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the genetic counseling session record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Genetic counseling sessions are billed to institutional accounts (hospitals, clinics) for revenue recognition and institutional reporting. Required for account-level service utilization tracking, cont']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified genetic counselor who conducted the session.']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the patient receiving genetic counseling.']
  protocol_controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.controlled_document. Business justification: Genetic counseling sessions must follow documented protocols (informed consent scripts, disclosure procedures, ACMG secondary findings policies, psychosocial support guidelines). Required for CLIA/CAP']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Genetic counseling sessions for research participants link to projects for IRB protocol compliance (informed consent process documentation), research billing, and participant support tracking. Require']
  revenue_recognition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_recognition. Business justification: Genetic counseling sessions are billable professional services requiring separate revenue recognition from laboratory testing per ASC 606. Link enables service revenue accounting, performance obligati']
  test_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical genomic test order associated with this counseling session.']
  acmg_classification varchar [note: 'ACMG/AMP variant classification for the secondary or incidental finding, indicating the clinical significance of the genomic variant.']
  billing_code varchar [note: 'CPT or HCPCS billing code associated with the genetic counseling session for reimbursement purposes.']
  cascade_screening_recommended_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cascade screening of at-risk family members was recommended based on the findings discussed in this session.']
  clinical_significance varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the clinical significance and health implications of the identified variant, including associated disease risk, penetrance, and actionability.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which informed consent was obtained from the patient during this counseling session.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Status of informed consent for genetic testing or for disclosure of secondary findings, as documented during this counseling session.']
  counselor_certification_number varchar [note: 'Professional certification number of the genetic counselor, typically issued by the American Board of Genetic Counseling (ABGC) or equivalent certifying body.']
  counselor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the genetic counselor who conducted the session.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the system user who created this genetic counseling session record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this genetic counseling session record was first created in the system.']
  family_members_present varchar [note: 'Description of family members or support persons present during the counseling session, including their relationship to the patient.']
  follow_up_actions varchar [note: 'Documented follow-up actions required or recommended, including additional testing, family cascade screening, surveillance protocols, or repeat counseling sessions.']
  gene_symbol varchar [note: 'Official gene symbol (HGNC nomenclature) for the gene in which the secondary or incidental variant was identified.']
  incidental_finding_identified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an incidental or secondary genomic finding was identified and discussed during this session.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the system user who most recently modified this genetic counseling session record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this genetic counseling session record was most recently updated.']
  patient_notification_preference varchar [note: 'Patient\'s documented preference regarding notification of secondary or incidental genomic findings, captured during informed consent or counseling.']
  patient_understanding_assessment varchar [note: 'Genetic counselor\'s assessment of the patient\'s comprehension of the information presented during the session.']
  phi_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this genetic counseling session record has been reviewed and confirmed to be compliant with HIPAA Protected Health Information handling requirements.']
  psychosocial_support_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether psychosocial support or counseling was provided to address emotional, psychological, or social concerns related to genetic testing or results.']
  referral_recommendations varchar [note: 'Clinical referrals recommended by the genetic counselor, such as referrals to medical genetics specialists, oncologists, cardiologists, or other healthcare providers for further evaluation and management.']
  reporting_decision varchar [note: 'Decision made regarding whether the secondary or incidental finding will be formally reported to the patient, based on clinical actionability, patient preference, and laboratory policy.']
  secondary_finding_category varchar [note: 'Category of secondary or incidental finding identified, such as ACMG SF v3.2 gene list findings, pharmacogenomic variants, or carrier status for recessive conditions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ACMG SF v3.2 gene|pharmacogenomic|carrier status|cancer predisposition|cardiovascular condition|metabolic disorder|other — promote to reference product]']
  session_date date [note: 'Date on which the genetic counseling session was conducted or scheduled.']
  session_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the genetic counseling session measured in minutes.']
  session_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the genetic counseling session concluded.']
  session_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the in-person genetic counseling session was conducted, or platform used for telehealth sessions.']
  session_modality varchar [note: 'Mode of delivery for the genetic counseling session.']
  session_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed clinical notes documenting the content, discussions, patient questions, counselor observations, and outcomes of the genetic counseling session. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI).']
  session_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the counseling session, typically formatted as a sequential or structured code for tracking and reference.']
  session_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the genetic counseling session began.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the genetic counseling session.']
  session_type varchar [note: 'Type of genetic counseling session conducted. Pre-test counseling occurs before testing to discuss implications and consent; post-test counseling reviews results; result disclosure communicates findings; family planning addresses reproductive implications; incidental and secondary finding reviews address unexpected genomic discoveries per ACMG guidelines.']
  topics_covered varchar [note: 'Comprehensive list or narrative of topics discussed during the genetic counseling session, including inheritance patterns, disease risk, testing options, result interpretation, and psychosocial implications.']
  variant_hgvs_notation varchar [note: 'Standardized HGVS notation describing the genomic variant associated with the secondary or incidental finding discussed in this session.']

  Note: 'Records clinical follow-up activities including genetic counseling sessions and incidental/secondary finding management associated with clinical genomic test orders or result delivery. Captures session type (pre-test counseling, post-test counseling, result disclosure, family planning, incidental finding review), session date, counselor identity, patient/family members present, topics covered, incidental findings identified (ACMG SF v3.2 gene list, pharmacogenomic, carrier status), variant details for secondary findings, ACMG classification, patient notification preference, reporting decision, referral recommendations, follow-up actions, and session notes. Supports ACMG secondary findings policy compliance, precision medicine workflows, and patient-centered care documentation.'
}

Table clinical.authorized_orderer {
  authorized_orderer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for authorized_orderer']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Ordering physicians are credentialed and affiliated with healthcare institution accounts for test authorization, billing coordination, and regulatory compliance. Essential for clinical lab operations:']
  kol_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.kol_engagement. Business justification: Ordering physicians are frequently KOLs. Commercial teams track which KOLs are active orderers to prioritize engagement investments, measure influence-to-revenue conversion, and optimize KOL program R']
  billing_npi varchar [note: 'NPI used for billing and reimbursement purposes. May differ from ordering NPI if billing is handled by a group practice or institution.']
  cap_accredited_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the provider is affiliated with a CAP-accredited laboratory or institution. Used for quality assurance and accreditation reporting.']
  clia_ordering_privilege boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the provider is authorized to order high-complexity CLIA tests. True if provider meets CLIA personnel qualifications for ordering genomic assays.']
  consent_on_file_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a signed provider agreement and consent for test ordering is on file. Required before first test order can be accepted.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the system user who created the authorized orderer record. Audit trail for accountability and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authorized orderer record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  credentialing_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current credentialing verification expires and re-verification is required. Triggers proactive outreach for credential renewal.']
  credentialing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special credentialing circumstances, verification sources, or exceptions granted during the credentialing process.']
  credentialing_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the providers credentials, licenses, and ordering privileges were last verified by the laboratory credentialing team. CLIA requires periodic re-verification.']
  credentials varchar [note: 'Professional credentials and degree designation of the ordering provider. Determines ordering authority scope for high-complexity genomic testing under CLIA regulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MD|DO|PhD|MS|CGC|LCGC|NP|PA|DVM|PharmD — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dea_number varchar [note: 'DEA registration number for providers authorized to prescribe controlled substances. Optional field used for comprehensive provider identity verification.']
  ehr_facility_code varchar [note: 'Unique facility or organization identifier within the EHR system. Used for HL7 message routing and result interface mapping.']
  ehr_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the EHR system used by the provider or institution (e.g., Epic, Cerner, Allscripts). Required for HL7 interface configuration and automated result delivery.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the ordering provider. Used for electronic report delivery, test status notifications, and critical result alerts.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for secure report delivery. Legacy delivery channel still required by many institutions for PHI-compliant document transmission.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the authorized ordering provider as registered with state medical board and credentialing authority.']
  irb_protocol_number varchar [note: 'IRB protocol number if the provider is ordering tests under a research protocol. Links test orders to approved research studies for compliance tracking.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the system user who last modified the authorized orderer record. Audit trail for change tracking and compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authorized orderer record was last updated. Tracks changes to credentialing status, contact information, or ordering privileges.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name of the authorized ordering provider as registered with state medical board and credentialing authority.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the authorized ordering provider. Optional field for full legal name matching.']
  npi varchar [note: 'Ten-digit unique identification number issued by the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) to healthcare providers in the United States. Required for CLIA-compliant test ordering and billing.']
  ordering_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the providers authorization to order clinical genomic tests. Active status required for test order acceptance. Inactive or revoked status blocks new orders.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the ordering provider. Required for critical result notification and clinical consultation per CLIA requirements.']
  report_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for delivering clinical genomic test reports to the ordering provider. Determines routing logic for automated report distribution.']
  specialty varchar [note: 'Primary medical specialty or practice area of the ordering provider (e.g., Medical Genetics, Oncology, Cardiology, Genetic Counseling). Used for test menu filtering and clinical decision support.']
  state_license_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the state medical license. Monitored for proactive re-credentialing and to prevent test orders from providers with lapsed licenses.']
  state_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued medical license number for the ordering provider. Required for verification of ordering authority and CLIA compliance validation.']
  state_license_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code of the jurisdiction that issued the medical license. Determines geographic ordering authority and cross-state practice limitations.']
  subspecialty varchar [note: 'Subspecialty or fellowship-trained area of focus within the primary specialty (e.g., Molecular Genetic Pathology, Pediatric Oncology). Provides additional context for test appropriateness validation.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Federal tax identification number (EIN or SSN) for the provider or practice. Used for billing, invoicing, and 1099 reporting.']
  test_menu_access_level varchar [note: 'Level of access to the clinical genomic test catalog based on provider credentials and specialty. Determines which tests the provider is authorized to order.']

  Note: 'Master registry of physicians, genetic counselors, and clinical institutions authorized to order clinical genomic tests — capturing provider NPI, name, credentials, specialty, institutional affiliation, CLIA ordering privileges, state licensure verification, preferred report delivery method (EHR/fax/portal), active/inactive status, and credentialing verification date. Required for CLIA compliance (only authorized providers may order high-complexity tests) and report delivery routing. Distinct from customer domain accounts — this is the clinical credentialing and ordering authority registry.'
}

Table clinical.proficiency_testing {
  proficiency_testing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the proficiency testing event record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Proficiency testing programs are purchased and managed at institutional account level for multi-site laboratory networks. Required for account-level PT compliance tracking, CAP/CLIA accreditation mana']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: Proficiency testing programs are tied to specific approved tests to demonstrate ongoing compliance with approval conditions and CLIA requirements. CAP/CLIA inspectors verify PT results match the scope']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the corrective action plan or CAPA record initiated in response to unsatisfactory PT results.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Proficiency testing programs incur enrollment fees, sample costs, and labor charged to laboratory quality cost centers. Link enables regulatory compliance cost tracking, quality budget management, and']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Proficiency testing failures trigger quality deviations requiring immediate investigation and corrective action. CLIA regulatory requirement (42 CFR 493.1236). PT already links to CAPA, but deviation ']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Proficiency testing sample sets are purchased products from PT providers (CAP, EMQN). Linking PT records to procurement orders enables compliance spend tracking, vendor management, and regulatory audi']
  performing_laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory location or facility that performed the proficiency testing.']
  primary_proficiency_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory personnel who performed the PT testing.']
  pt_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: PT samples are purchased from external PT program suppliers (CAP, EMQN, etc.); tracking supplier enables procurement management, performance correlation with supplier quality, and regulatory complianc']
  quaternary_proficiency_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the proficiency testing record.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory director or technical supervisor who reviewed the PT results.']
  tertiary_proficiency_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the proficiency testing record.']
  acceptable_performance_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score percentage required for satisfactory performance as defined by the PT program.']
  analyte_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code for the analyte or test method being assessed (e.g., CLIA analyte code, CAP test code).']
  cap_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'CAP accreditation number of the laboratory, if CAP-accredited.']
  clia_certificate_number varchar [note: 'CLIA certificate number of the laboratory performing the proficiency testing.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context related to the proficiency testing event.']
  corrective_action_completion_date date [note: 'Date the corrective action plan was completed and verified.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action is required following unsatisfactory PT performance (True/False).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proficiency testing record was first created in the system.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the laboratory enrolled in the proficiency testing event.']
  enrollment_period varchar [note: 'The enrollment period or cycle identifier for the PT event (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-Survey-A).']
  grading_outcome varchar [note: 'Overall grading outcome assigned by the PT program: satisfactory, unsatisfactory, pending review, or not graded.']
  grading_report_received_date date [note: 'Date the laboratory received the grading report from the PT program provider.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proficiency testing record was last modified.']
  number_of_challenges_correct varchar [note: 'Number of PT challenges or samples for which the laboratory provided correct results.']
  number_of_challenges_incorrect varchar [note: 'Number of PT challenges or samples for which the laboratory provided incorrect results.']
  number_of_samples varchar [note: 'Total number of samples or challenges included in the PT sample set.']
  pt_documentation_location varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or physical location where PT records and supporting documentation are stored.']
  pt_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of external quality assessment event: proficiency testing (formal PT), alternative assessment (AA), or inter-laboratory comparison.']
  pt_program_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or identifier assigned by the PT program provider to uniquely identify the program.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date regulatory authorities were notified of unsatisfactory PT results, if applicable.']
  regulatory_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unsatisfactory PT results must be reported to regulatory authorities (e.g., CMS, state health department) per CLIA requirements (True/False).']
  results_submitted_date date [note: 'Date the laboratory submitted PT results to the program provider.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date the PT results and grading report were reviewed by the laboratory director or technical supervisor.']
  sample_received_date date [note: 'Date the proficiency testing sample set was received by the laboratory.']
  sample_set_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the PT sample set or challenge set received by the laboratory.']
  score_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score achieved by the laboratory expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00).']
  submission_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether PT results were submitted on time, late, or not submitted by the deadline.']
  submission_deadline_date date [note: 'Deadline date by which PT results must be submitted to the program provider.']
  test_system_evaluated varchar [note: 'The specific test system, assay, or analyte being evaluated in this PT event (e.g., WGS variant calling, SNP genotyping, CNV detection).']
  testing_completed_date date [note: 'Date the laboratory completed testing of the PT samples.']
  unsatisfactory_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation or root cause for unsatisfactory PT performance, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Records external proficiency testing (PT) and alternative assessment (AA) events required for CLIA/CAP accreditation — capturing PT program name (CAP PT, AAFP, ACOG), analyte/test system evaluated, enrollment period, sample set received, results submitted, graded outcome (satisfactory/unsatisfactory), corrective action reference, and submission deadline compliance. SSOT for laboratory PT compliance tracking.'
}

Table clinical.clinical_pipeline_run {
  clinical_pipeline_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the clinical-grade bioinformatics pipeline execution record. Primary key for this GxP-validated pipeline run subject to 21 CFR Part 11 controls.']
  assay_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the validated clinical assay version used for this pipeline run. Ensures traceability to the analytically validated test configuration.']
  bioinformatics_pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original pipeline run ID if this execution is a reanalysis or rerun. Maintains lineage for repeated analyses of the same specimen.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Pipeline runs execute specific commercial assay products; catalog linkage essential for regulatory audit trails (21 CFR Part 11), version control, product performance monitoring, and post-market surve']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Bioinformatics pipeline compute costs (cloud infrastructure, storage) are allocated to cost centers for IT chargeback, departmental budget tracking, and cost-per-sample analysis. Essential for managin']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Bioinformatics pipeline failures (alignment errors, variant calling issues, data integrity problems, compute environment failures) trigger quality deviations that must be traceable to the specific run']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Pipeline runs must track reference genome used for alignment and variant calling. Essential for reproducibility, troubleshooting failed runs, and reprocessing with updated references. Replaces text re']
  pipeline_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.pipeline_version. Business justification: Clinical pipeline runs execute specific validated bioinformatics pipeline versions. Critical for GxP compliance, validation traceability, change control, and ensuring clinical results use only analyti']
  primary_clinical_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory personnel or automated system account that initiated this pipeline run. Part of 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature requirements.']
  quality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.quality_assessment. Business justification: Pipeline runs are assessed for quality metrics (coverage, error rates, QC pass/fail) as part of automated quality monitoring. Production bioinformatics QC workflows require linking runs to their quali']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Bioinformatics pipeline runs for research samples link to projects for compute cost allocation, data provenance tracking, publication methods documentation, and research data management. Essential for']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.sample_specimen. Business justification: Pipeline runs process specimen data; tracking specimen enables reprocessing and quality investigations. Real business need: bioinformatics workflow management, troubleshooting failed runs, and specime']
  sequencing_reagent_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Bioinformatics pipelines require full traceability to upstream wet-lab reagent lots for regulatory compliance and quality investigations. Enables end-to-end lot genealogy from sequencing reagents thro']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_run. Business justification: Clinical bioinformatics pipelines process data from specific sequencing runs. Fundamental link between raw sequencing operations and clinical analysis workflows. Required for tracking data lineage, QC']
  test_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical test order that triggered this pipeline execution. Links the computational analysis to the patient specimen and clinical context.']
  clinically_significant_variants varchar [note: 'Number of variants flagged as clinically significant based on annotation databases and clinical interpretation rules. Subset of total variants requiring clinical review.']
  compute_environment_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the validated compute environment configuration including software versions, container images, and system libraries. Ensures reproducibility per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  compute_instance_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific compute instance or cluster node that executed this pipeline run. Supports infrastructure troubleshooting and audit trails.']
  compute_resource_type varchar [note: 'Type of high-performance computing infrastructure used for this pipeline execution. Tracks whether analysis was performed on-premise or in cloud environments.']
  coverage_uniformity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of target bases covered at or above the minimum required depth. Measures evenness of sequencing coverage across the target region.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pipeline run record was first created in the system. Part of the immutable audit trail.']
  data_retention_date date [note: 'Date until which this pipeline run data and associated genomic files must be retained per regulatory and legal requirements. Typically 2-10 years post-analysis.']
  duplicate_read_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sequencing reads identified as PCR or optical duplicates. High duplication rates may indicate library preparation issues.']
  error_log_path varchar [note: 'Storage location URI for detailed error logs if the pipeline run failed. Supports troubleshooting and root cause analysis.']
  execution_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pipeline execution completed or failed. Used to calculate processing time and meet TAT requirements.']
  execution_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pipeline execution was initiated. Part of the audit trail for turnaround time tracking and regulatory compliance.']
  input_fastq_r1_path varchar [note: 'Storage location URI for the forward read FASTQ file input to this pipeline run. Part of the raw sequencing data lineage.']
  input_fastq_r2_path varchar [note: 'Storage location URI for the reverse read FASTQ file input to this pipeline run. Applicable for paired-end sequencing workflows.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pipeline run record was last updated. Tracks status changes and review activities throughout the lifecycle.']
  mean_base_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average Phred quality score across all base calls in the sequencing run. Indicates overall sequencing accuracy and data quality.']
  mean_coverage_depth decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of sequencing reads covering each base position across the target region. Key QC metric for analytical validity.']
  on_target_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sequencing reads that align to the intended target regions. Measures capture efficiency for targeted sequencing assays.']
  output_bam_path varchar [note: 'Storage location URI for the aligned reads BAM file produced by this pipeline run. Clinical-grade alignment output.']
  output_vcf_path varchar [note: 'Storage location URI for the variant calls VCF file produced by this pipeline run. Clinical-grade variant calling output subject to interpretation.']
  phi_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that this pipeline run was executed in a HIPAA-compliant environment with appropriate PHI safeguards. Required for patient-identifiable clinical data.']
  pipeline_parameters_json varchar [note: 'JSON-serialized configuration parameters used for this pipeline execution. Locked per validation protocol to ensure consistent analytical performance.']
  processing_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes from execution start to completion. Key metric for operational efficiency and TAT compliance.']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of quality control failure if qc_pass_flag is false. Documents specific metrics that fell below acceptance criteria.']
  qc_pass_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this pipeline run met all predefined quality control thresholds for clinical reporting. False indicates QC failure requiring investigation.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pipeline QC results were reviewed and approved by qualified personnel. Part of the clinical validation workflow.']
  run_identifier varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for this pipeline execution. Used for operational tracking and audit trail references.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the clinical pipeline execution. Tracks progression from initiation through completion or failure.']
  total_variants_called varchar [note: 'Total number of genetic variants identified by the variant calling algorithm in this pipeline run. Includes SNPs, indels, and CNVs per assay scope.']

  Note: 'GxP-validated bioinformatics pipeline execution record for clinical specimen analysis — capturing pipeline run ID, assay version, validated pipeline version, input FASTQ references, output BAM/VCF locations, pipeline parameters (locked per validation), QC metrics (coverage depth, uniformity, duplicate rate, on-target %), run status, compute environment hash, and completion timestamp. This is the clinical-grade execution record subject to 21 CFR Part 11 controls, distinct from research pipeline runs in the bioinformatics domain. Supports result traceability from raw reads to clinical-grade variant calls.'
}

  Note: 'Pharmacogenomics clinical report record capturing drug-gene interaction results, star-allele/diplotype calls, metabolizer phenotype classifications, and actionable prescribing recommendations delivered to ordering clinicians for precision medicine decision support'
