Table clinical.clinical_enrollment {
  clinical_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for clinical_enrollment']
  enrolling_investigator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee record of the investigator or research coordinator who enrolled this patient into the study. Used for enrollment attribution and audit trail.']
  participant_patient_id varchar [note: 'De-identified study-specific participant identifier assigned to the patient for this research project. Used to protect patient identity in research datasets while maintaining linkage to clinical records.']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the clinical patient master record who is enrolled in the research study']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the research project master record in which the patient is enrolled']
  biospecimen_collection_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the patient has consented to biospecimen collection and storage for this research project. Distinct from clinical specimen consent.']
  cohort_assignment varchar [note: 'The specific cohort, arm, or subgroup within the research project to which this patient is assigned. Used for stratified enrollment tracking and analysis group management.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the patient completed all protocol-required activities for this research project. Null if enrollment is still active, withdrawn, or screen-failed. Used for completion rate tracking.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the patient\'s research-specific consent for this project. Distinct from clinical consent in patient master record. Tracks consent lifecycle for this specific study enrollment.']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the research consent form signed by the patient for this specific project enrollment. Used to track which consent language and data use terms the patient agreed to.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this enrollment record was created in the research enrollment system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_use_authorization_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the patient has authorized use of their clinical genomic data for this specific research project beyond the primary clinical indication. Required for secondary use compliance.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the patient was officially enrolled into the research project after consent was obtained and eligibility confirmed. Used for enrollment tracking and IRB reporting.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this patient-project enrollment record. Primary key for the enrollment association.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this patient enrollment in the research project: active (currently participating), completed (finished study protocol), withdrawn (patient withdrew consent), screen_failed (did not meet eligibility), excluded (removed by investigator).']
  irb_protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the IRB-approved protocol under which this patient was enrolled. Used to track protocol amendments and ensure patients are enrolled under current approved version.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  randomization_arm varchar [note: 'The randomization arm or treatment group assigned to this patient in interventional studies. Null for observational studies. Used for blinded trial management.']
  screening_date date [note: 'Date when the patient was screened for eligibility for this research project. Precedes enrollment_date. Used for screening funnel analysis and recruitment tracking.']
  site varchar [note: 'The clinical site or research center where this patient was enrolled into the study. Used for multi-site study management and site-level enrollment tracking.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date when the patient withdrew consent or was withdrawn from the research project. Null if enrollment is still active or completed normally. Required for IRB adverse event reporting.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Coded reason for patient withdrawal from the study: adverse_event, patient_request, lost_to_followup, protocol_violation, investigator_decision, death, other. Used for safety monitoring and IRB reporting.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment event between a clinical patient and a research project. It captures the operational relationship when a patient consents to participate in an R&D study, tracking enrollment lifecycle, consent versions, cohort assignments, and withdrawal events. Each record links one patient to one research project with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific enrollment relationship. Required for IRB reporting, enrollment tracking, and data use authorization compliance.. Existence Justification: In genomics biotech clinical research operations, patients participate in multiple R&D projects over time (longitudinal cohorts, multi-protocol enrollment for different genomic studies), and research projects enroll multiple patients as study participants. The business actively manages Research Enrollment as an operational entity - research coordinators and principal investigators create enrollment records, track consent versions, assign patients to cohorts/randomization arms, monitor enrollment status, and report withdrawals to IRBs. This is not an analytical correlation but a core operational process with dedicated workflows in clinical trial management systems.'
}

Table clinical.test_service_agreement {
  test_service_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this test service agreement record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account that has contracted for this test']
  ivd_test_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the clinical test catalog entry covered by this service agreement']
  auto_reflex_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic reflex testing rules are enabled for this account-test combination. Some accounts may opt out of reflex testing for cost control or workflow reasons. True=reflex rules apply, False=reflex testing disabled for this account.']
  billing_code_override varchar [note: 'Account-specific billing code or reference number to use for this test instead of standard CPT code. Used when account requires custom billing identifiers for their internal systems. Null if standard CPT code applies.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this test service agreement became active and the contracted terms took effect. Used for contract lifecycle management and historical pricing queries. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  contract_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this test service agreement expires or is scheduled for renewal. Null indicates evergreen agreement. Used for contract renewal workflows and revenue forecasting. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  contract_owner_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the sales or account management user responsible for managing this test service agreement. Used for contract renewal notifications and escalations.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this test service agreement. draft=under negotiation, active=in force, suspended=temporarily inactive, expired=past expiry date, terminated=cancelled before expiry, renewal_pending=approaching expiry with renewal in progress.']
  contracted_price_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Account-specific negotiated price for this test in USD. May differ from catalog list price based on volume commitments, strategic relationship, or competitive factors. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this test service agreement record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test service agreement record was first created in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test service agreement record was last updated.']
  minimum_annual_volume varchar [note: 'Minimum number of test orders per year committed by the account to qualify for contracted pricing. Used for volume commitment tracking and pricing tier enforcement. Null if no minimum commitment.']
  preferred_specimen_type varchar [note: 'Account-specific preferred specimen type for this test (e.g., whole blood, saliva, buccal swab). May be a subset of accepted specimen types in the catalog based on account laboratory capabilities or workflow preferences. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  sla_turnaround_hours varchar [note: 'Account-specific committed turnaround time from specimen receipt to report delivery for this test, measured in hours. May differ from catalog standard TAT based on account tier or negotiated service levels. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  volume_discount_tier varchar [note: 'Volume-based pricing tier applicable to this account-test combination (e.g., standard, bronze, silver, gold, platinum, enterprise). Determines pricing adjustments based on committed or historical test volumes. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual agreement between a clinical test catalog entry and a customer account. It captures account-specific pricing, service level commitments, volume discount tiers, and specimen handling preferences negotiated for each test-account combination. Each record links one test catalog entry to one account with contract terms that exist only in the context of this commercial relationship.. Existence Justification: In genomics biotechnology commercial operations, clinical laboratories and institutional customers negotiate account-specific pricing, service level agreements, and operational terms for each test offered. A single test catalog entry (e.g., WGS Oncology Panel v2.1) is contracted to hundreds of customer accounts with varying negotiated prices, volume discount tiers, TAT commitments, and specimen preferences. Conversely, each customer account contracts for multiple tests from the catalog with distinct terms per test. The business actively manages these test service agreements as contractual entities with lifecycle states (draft, active, renewal), and commercial teams query \'show me all contracted tests for Account X\' and \'show me all accounts contracted for Test Y\'.'
}

Table clinical.orderer_authorization {
  orderer_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this physician authorization record. Primary key.']
  authorized_orderer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the authorized orderer (physician, genetic counselor, or clinical institution) who holds ordering privileges under this service agreement.']
  service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer service agreement under which this physician is authorized to order tests.']
  authorization_date date [note: 'Date when the physician was authorized to order tests under this specific service agreement. Captured from detection phase relationship data.']
  authorization_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this physician\'s authorization under this specific service agreement expires and must be renewed or re-verified. May differ from the physician\'s general credentialing_expiration_date or the service agreement\'s end_date. Captured from detection phase relationship data.']
  authorization_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special authorization conditions, restrictions, institutional requirements, or verification details specific to this physician-agreement combination.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the physician\'s ordering privileges under this service agreement: active (authorized to order), suspended (temporarily restricted), expired (authorization period ended), revoked (privileges withdrawn), pending_verification (credentialing in progress). Captured from detection phase relationship data.']
  authorized_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the internal credentialing specialist or clinical operations manager who approved this physician\'s authorization under this service agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this authorization record was first created in the system. Automatically populated.']
  institutional_credential_verified_date date [note: 'Date when the physician\'s credentials were verified specifically for this institutional service agreement. Separate from the physician\'s general credentialing_verification_date, as institutional agreements may require additional verification steps.']
  ordering_limit_per_month bigint [note: 'Maximum number of test orders this physician may submit per month under this service agreement. Used for evaluation agreements, beta programs, or volume-capped institutional contracts. Null indicates no limit. Captured from detection phase relationship data.']
  test_menu_access_scope varchar [note: 'Specific test menu access level or scope granted to this physician under this service agreement. May differ from the physician\'s general test_menu_access_level based on institutional agreement terms (e.g., \'oncology_panel_only\', \'full_clinical_menu\', \'hereditary_cancer_tests\'). Captured from detection phase relationship data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this authorization record was last modified. Automatically updated on any change to authorization status, scope, or limits.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the authorization relationship between authorized orderers (physicians, genetic counselors) and institutional service agreements. It captures the specific ordering privileges, test menu access scope, and authorization terms that exist when a physician is credentialed to order tests under a particular customer service agreement. Each record links one authorized orderer to one service agreement with authorization-specific attributes including authorization dates, status, test menu access scope, ordering limits, and expiration dates. This is a critical compliance and operational entity — physicians may hold ordering privileges under multiple institutional agreements (multi-site affiliations, hospital network memberships), and each service agreement authorizes multiple physicians based on institutional credentialing.. Existence Justification: In genomics biotech clinical operations, physicians frequently hold ordering privileges under multiple institutional service agreements due to multi-site hospital affiliations, academic medical center networks, and participation in evaluation/beta programs. Conversely, each institutional service agreement (hospital system contract, research institution agreement) authorizes multiple physicians based on institutional credentialing rosters. The authorization relationship itself carries critical operational data: authorization dates, status (active/suspended/expired), test menu access scope specific to that agreement, monthly ordering limits for evaluation programs, and authorization expiry dates that may differ from both the physician\'s general credentialing and the service agreement\'s term.'
}

Table clinical.orderer_territory_assignment {
  orderer_territory_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this orderer-territory assignment record. Primary key.']
  authorized_orderer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the authorized orderer in the clinical credentialing registry']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sales territory']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when this orderer-territory assignment ended or will end. Null for currently active assignments. Used to track historical territory coverage changes.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'Date when this orderer-territory assignment became effective. Used to track when an orderer began practicing in locations covered by this territory.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this orderer-territory assignment. Active assignments are operational, inactive are historical, pending are future-dated assignments.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory assignment priority: primary (main practice location), secondary (additional practice location), tertiary (occasional coverage). Used for sales resource prioritization and account ownership determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for documenting special circumstances, coverage arrangements, or territory-specific context for this orderer assignment.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Strategic priority classification for this orderer within this specific territory. Used for account planning and resource allocation decisions by territory managers.']
  relationship_strength_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score (0-100) representing the strength of the commercial relationship between this orderer and the territory sales team. Based on ordering volume, engagement frequency, and strategic account value. Used for account planning and territory performance analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was last modified. Audit trail field.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the commercial territory assignment relationship between authorized clinical orderers and sales territories. It captures the operational reality that ordering physicians may practice across multiple geographic locations (each covered by different territories) and territories contain many authorized orderers. Each record links one authorized orderer to one territory with assignment dates, coverage type (primary/secondary), and relationship strength metrics used for account planning and sales resource allocation.. Existence Justification: In genomics biotech commercial operations, authorized ordering physicians frequently practice at multiple locations that span different sales territories (e.g., an academic physician with hospital affiliations in 3 cities, each covered by different territory managers). Simultaneously, each territory contains many authorized orderers. The business actively manages these assignments to determine account ownership, allocate sales resources, and track relationship strength for pipeline planning and quota assignment.'
}

Table clinical.clinical_assay {
  clinical_assay_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for clinical_assay']
  predecessor_clinical_assay_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on clinical_assay (predecessor_clinical_assay_id)']
  analytical_validity varchar [note: 'Evidence level supporting the analytical performance of the assay.']
  assay_category varchar [note: 'High‑level clinical domain the assay serves.']
  assay_code varchar [note: 'External catalog code or identifier used to reference the assay in laboratory information systems.']
  assay_development_phase varchar [note: 'Current stage of the assay in the product lifecycle.']
  assay_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the clinical assay.']
  assay_owner varchar [note: 'Internal team or department responsible for assay maintenance.']
  assay_subcategory varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the primary assay category.']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'Technology category of the assay (e.g., next‑generation sequencing, polymerase chain reaction).']
  assay_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the assay, reflecting updates to protocol or analysis pipeline.']
  billing_cpt_code varchar [note: 'Current CPT code used for billing the assay to insurers.']
  clia_capability varchar [note: 'CLIA classification indicating the complexity level of the assay.']
  clinical_validity varchar [note: 'Evidence level supporting the clinical relevance of the assay results.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assay record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the assay price.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the assay purpose, methodology, and clinical utility.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to detect variants (e.g., hybrid capture, amplicon, SNP array).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the assay version became active for clinical ordering.']
  interpretation_guidelines varchar [note: 'Standardized rules for interpreting assay results and reporting.']
  is_approved boolean [note: 'True if the assay has received all required regulatory approvals for clinical use.']
  is_companion_assay boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assay is a companion diagnostic for a specific therapy.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the assay within the laboratory workflow.']
  limit_of_detection decimal(10,4) [note: 'Lowest variant allele frequency that can be reliably detected, expressed as a proportion.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional comments, caveats, or version‑specific remarks.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Sequencing or analysis platform on which the assay is performed (e.g., Illumina NovaSeq, ThermoFisher Ion Torrent).']
  price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard list price for the assay before discounts.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the assay for clinical use.']
  reimbursement_status varchar [note: 'Insurance reimbursement eligibility for the assay.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date on which the assay was withdrawn from clinical use, if applicable.']
  sample_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific collection, volume, or preservation requirements for the specimen.']
  sensitivity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of true positive variants correctly identified, expressed as a percentage.']
  specificity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of true negative results correctly reported, expressed as a percentage.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Biological specimen accepted for the assay.']
  storage_requirements varchar [note: 'Temperature and handling conditions required for assay reagents and samples.']
  target_gene varchar [note: 'Gene(s) or genomic region that the assay is designed to detect or analyze.']
  target_region varchar [note: 'Specific genomic coordinates or exons targeted by the assay.']
  technology varchar [note: 'Underlying laboratory technology (e.g., sequencing, PCR, microarray).']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of calendar days from sample receipt to result delivery.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the assay record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for clinical_assay. Referenced by clinical_assay_id.'
}

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the laboratory record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for performing_laboratory. Referenced by performing_laboratory_id.'
