  primary_finished_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality control (QC) personnel who performed the initial batch review and quality assessment.']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Finished goods release decision is made for a specific production batch. Cardinality: 1 release : 1 production_batch (typically 1:1, though re-release scenarios possible). Adding production_batch_id F']
  retention_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.retention_policy. Business justification: Finished goods release documentation retention for regulatory compliance, product liability, and recall management. Legal requirement for pharmaceutical and diagnostic products.']
  variant_database_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.variant_database_version. Business justification: Finished goods release for clinical diagnostic products must document which variant database version the product is validated against for regulatory compliance, clinical claims support, and certificat']
  veeva_vault_document_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the batch release record stored in Veeva Vault regulatory document management system.']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) is required based on quality issues identified during batch review.']
  coa_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the Certificate of Analysis was formally issued for the released batch.']
  coa_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the Certificate of Analysis document that accompanies the released batch, certifying product quality and specifications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the finished goods release record was first created in the system.']
  distribution_authorization_date date [note: 'Date when the batch was formally authorized for commercial distribution or internal use following quality release approval.']
  electronic_signature_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the electronic signature applied to the release record, ensuring compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 requirements for electronic records and signatures.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the finished goods batch is no longer suitable for use, based on stability studies and shelf-life validation.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) confirming that the batch was manufactured in accordance with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) requirements.']
  intended_use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the product\'s intended use: RUO (Research Use Only), IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), LDT (Laboratory Developed Test), clinical trial, or internal use.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the finished goods release record was last updated or modified.']
  outstanding_deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of unresolved quality deviations or non-conformances associated with this batch at the time of release decision.']
  qc_test_results_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of quality control test results and acceptance criteria verification performed on the batch.']
  quality_approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Qualified Person or QA manager who authorized the release decision.']
  quality_reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the quality reviewer who conducted the batch quality assessment.']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether the batch is under regulatory hold, preventing distribution pending regulatory clearance or investigation.']
  rejected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of finished goods units rejected and not approved for distribution due to quality non-conformance.']
  release_criteria_checklist_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether all mandatory release criteria and quality checkpoints have been completed and verified.']
  release_decision_date date [note: 'Date when the final quality release decision was made by the authorized quality personnel.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the batch release decision, including any conditional release terms or distribution restrictions.']
  release_record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the finished goods release record, used for traceability and regulatory documentation.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the release record in the quality review and approval process.']
  release_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the release decision: full release (approved for distribution), conditional release (approved with restrictions), reject (not approved), quarantine (hold pending investigation), or rework (return to production).']
  released_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of finished goods units approved for release and distribution from this batch (may be less than batch size if partial release or rejection occurred).']
  sap_qm_inspection_lot varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection lot number associated with the finished goods quality inspection and usage decision.']
  storage_condition_requirement varchar [note: 'Required storage conditions for the finished goods batch (e.g., temperature range, humidity, light protection) to maintain product stability and quality.']

  Note: 'Records the formal quality release decision for a finished goods batch, authorizing it for commercial distribution or internal use. Captures release record number, batch number, product code, product name, manufacturing site, release type (full release, conditional release, reject), release decision date, quality reviewer ID, quality approver ID (QP/QA), electronic signature reference (21 CFR Part 11), COA number, COA issue date, release criteria checklist completion status, outstanding deviation count, regulatory hold flag, and distribution authorization date. Integrates with SAP QM usage decision and Veeva Vault batch release workflow.'
}

Table manufacturing.env_monitoring_result {
  env_monitoring_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the environmental monitoring result record. Primary key for this entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the monitoring instrument or equipment used to perform the measurement (e.g., particle counter, temperature probe, air sampler).']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.capa. Business justification: Repeated environmental failures or trends trigger CAPAs for contamination control improvement. Facility qualification requirement for maintaining environmental control in genomics biotech manufacturin']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Environmental monitoring excursions (alert/action limit breaches) generate quality deviations. Cleanroom monitoring requirement for GMP manufacturing in genomics-biotechnology to document contaminatio']
  manufacturing_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing site or facility where the environmental monitoring was performed.']
  monitoring_event_id varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the environmental monitoring event. Externally-known unique code for traceability and audit purposes.']
  monitoring_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific location within the manufacturing facility where the environmental monitoring was performed.']
  primary_env_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified operator who performed the environmental monitoring sampling or measurement.']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Environmental monitoring results are associated with production batches for GMP traceability. Cardinality: N monitoring results : 1 production_batch (multiple EM samples during batch production). Addi']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing work order active during this environmental monitoring event. Links monitoring data to production context.']
  action_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The action threshold that requires immediate corrective action and investigation when exceeded. Action limits represent the maximum acceptable deviation from specification.']
  action_limit_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the measured value exceeded the action limit. True if action limit was breached, false otherwise.']
  alert_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The alert threshold that triggers investigation when exceeded. Alert limits are set below action limits to provide early warning of potential environmental control drift.']
  alert_limit_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the measured value exceeded the alert limit. True if alert limit was breached, false otherwise.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled calibration due date for the monitoring instrument at the time of measurement. Ensures measurement was taken with a calibrated instrument.']
  clean_room_classification varchar [note: 'ISO 14644-1 classification of the clean room based on airborne particulate cleanliness (ISO 3 through ISO 8).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes regarding the environmental monitoring result, including any observations, anomalies, or contextual information.']
  corrective_action_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier linking to the corrective action or CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) record initiated in response to this monitoring result, if applicable.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether corrective action is required based on the monitoring result. True if corrective action is needed, false otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this environmental monitoring result record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  gmp_grade varchar [note: 'EU GMP Annex 1 grade classification for the manufacturing area (Grade A, B, C, or D) indicating the level of environmental control required.']
  incubation_period_hours varchar [note: 'Duration in hours for which viable particulate samples were incubated before colony counting. Applicable only to viable monitoring; null for non-viable and physical parameter monitoring.']
  incubation_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature in degrees Celsius at which viable particulate samples were incubated. Applicable only to viable monitoring.']
  instrument_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the monitoring instrument used. Critical for traceability and calibration verification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this environmental monitoring result record was last modified. Audit trail field for change tracking.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The quantitative result of the environmental monitoring measurement. Interpretation depends on monitoring type and unit of measure.']
  monitoring_type varchar [note: 'Type of environmental parameter being monitored: viable particulate (microbial), non-viable particulate, temperature, humidity, differential pressure, or HVAC airflow.']
  organism_identified varchar [note: 'Genus and species of microorganism identified from viable particulate monitoring, if applicable. Used for contamination source investigation and trending.']
  regulatory_standard_applied varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory standard or guideline applied for this environmental monitoring (e.g., EU GMP Annex 1, ISO 14644-1, FDA Aseptic Processing Guidance).']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Pass/fail status of the monitoring result: pass (within specification), alert_exceeded (alert limit breached), action_exceeded (action limit breached), or fail (specification limit exceeded).']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the environmental monitoring result was reviewed and approved by QC personnel.']
  reviewed_by_qc_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the QC personnel who reviewed and approved the environmental monitoring result.']
  sampled_by_operator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the operator who performed the environmental monitoring sampling. Included for audit trail and traceability.']
  sampling_datetime timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the environmental monitoring sample was collected or measurement was taken. This is the principal business event timestamp for this monitoring result.']
  specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The target or maximum allowable value for this monitoring parameter as defined in the environmental control specification for the location and monitoring type.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the measured value: CFU/m3 (colony forming units per cubic meter for viable), particles/m3 (for non-viable), degrees Celsius or Fahrenheit (temperature), percent relative humidity, Pascals (pressure), feet per minute (airflow). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CFU/m3|particles/m3|°C|°F|%RH|Pa|fpm — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records environmental monitoring (EM) measurements taken in GMP-classified manufacturing areas (clean rooms, fill-finish suites, ISO-classified zones) to ensure compliance with environmental specifications. Captures monitoring event ID, monitoring location, clean room classification (ISO class, EU GMP grade A/B/C/D), monitoring type (viable particulate, non-viable particulate, temperature, humidity, differential pressure, HVAC), measured value, unit, specification limit, pass/fail result, sampling date/time, sampled-by operator, incubation period (for viable monitoring), organism identified (if applicable), alert/action limit breach flag, and corrective action reference. Critical for GMP compliance and product quality assurance per EU GMP Annex 1 and ISO 14644.'
}

Table manufacturing.packaging_execution {
  packaging_execution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the packaging execution record. Primary key for this entity.']

Business justification: Packaging operations reference commercial products for label version control, regulatory compliance, and serialization. Packaging line clearance procedures verify correct catalog_item is packaged, pre']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Standard Operating Procedure document that defines the packaging process.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Packaging operations have cost center assignments for labor and overhead allocation in biotech manufacturing. Enables accurate cost tracking for packaging activities and supports COGS calculation for ']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Packaging deviations (label reconciliation failures, line clearance issues, mix-ups) documented as quality deviations. Critical for product identity and traceability in genomics-biotechnology packagin']
  labeling_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.labeling. Business justification: Packaging operations must use the approved labeling version for the product and target market. Critical for genomics IVD compliance - wrong labeling version can trigger field safety actions and regula']
  manufacturing_bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Bill of Materials (BOM) defining the packaging configuration (kit components, primary packaging materials, secondary packaging materials, inserts, labels).']
  manufacturing_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing site or plant where the packaging operation was executed.']
  primary_packaging_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who performed the packaging operation.']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Packaging operations are performed on a specific production batch. Cardinality: N packaging executions : 1 production_batch (one batch may have multiple packaging runs for different pack sizes). Addin']
  tertiary_packaging_qc_released_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QC personnel who authorized the quality release.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the packaging line or work center where the packaging operation was executed.']

  batch_record_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the GMP batch record document that governs this packaging execution.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this packaging execution record was first created in the system.']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of deviations or non-conformances recorded during this packaging execution.']
  environmental_monitoring_zone varchar [note: 'Cleanroom or environmental monitoring zone classification where packaging was performed (e.g., A, B, C, D per EU GMP Annex 1).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date assigned to the packaged product batch. Critical for shelf-life management and regulatory compliance.']
  label_destroyed_count varchar [note: 'Total number of labels destroyed or discarded during this execution (damaged, misprinted, or unused). Part of GMP label reconciliation.']
  label_issued_count varchar [note: 'Total number of labels issued to the packaging line for this execution. Part of GMP label reconciliation.']
  label_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the label reconciliation process verifying that issued = used + destroyed. GMP requirement for label control.']
  label_used_count varchar [note: 'Total number of labels actually applied to products during this execution. Part of GMP label reconciliation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this packaging execution record was last updated in the system.']
  line_clearance_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether line clearance was confirmed before packaging started. GMP requirement to prevent cross-contamination and mix-ups.']
  line_clearance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when line clearance was confirmed and documented.']
  packaging_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packaging operation was completed or stopped.']
  packaging_operator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the operator who executed the packaging operation.']
  packaging_order_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the packaging order. Externally-known unique number assigned to this packaging operation.']
  packaging_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packaging operation began. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  packaging_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the packaging execution operation.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the packaging operation: primary packaging (vials, cartridges, tubes), secondary packaging (boxes, cartons), labeling, or kit assembly.']
  qc_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the packaged product is on QC hold pending quality release.']
  qc_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packaged batch was released by Quality Control for distribution.']
  quantity_packaged decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units successfully packaged during this execution.']
  quantity_rejected decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units rejected during packaging due to defects, damage, or quality failures.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the packaged product: Research Use Only (RUO), In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD), CE-IVD marked, FDA cleared, or investigational.']
  storage_condition_requirement varchar [note: 'Required storage conditions for the packaged product (e.g., 2-8°C, -20°C, room temperature, protect from light).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the packaged quantity (each, kit, box, vial, tube, cartridge, plate). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KIT|BOX|VIAL|TUBE|CARTRIDGE|PLATE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records the execution of packaging operations for finished products — including primary packaging (vials, cartridges, tubes), secondary packaging (kits, boxes), and labeling activities. Captures packaging order number, work order reference, product code, batch number, packaging configuration (kit BOM), packaging line, packaging start/end date/time, quantity packaged, quantity rejected, label version used, label reconciliation count (issued vs used vs destroyed), packaging operator, line clearance confirmation, and packaging status (in-progress, complete, on-hold). Ensures GMP label control and packaging traceability for reagent kits, arrays, and consumables.'
}

  instrument_build_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the instrument build record. Primary key for tracking the complete assembly and build history of a manufactured genomics instrument.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: Sequencing instruments and array scanners are Class II/III medical devices. Each instrument build must reference the 510k or CE mark under which it\'s manufactured to ensure configuration matches appro']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.capa. Business justification: Instrument build quality issues trigger CAPAs for product quality improvement. Manufacturing quality system requirement for genomics sequencing/array instrument production.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Instrument build records document assembly of commercial sequencing instruments (catalog items). Service history, warranty tracking, and FDA device history records require direct link from build recor']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Instrument build deviations (assembly errors, test failures, calibration issues) tracked for manufacturing quality. Required for genomics instrument manufacturing quality control and traceability.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Completed instrument builds become capitalized fixed assets. Linking build records to asset master enables full lifecycle traceability from manufacturing through depreciation and supports cost capital']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Instrument build records for sequencers must document which genome builds the instrument firmware/software version supports for factory acceptance testing, quality release, and customer compatibility ']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the quality inspector who performed the final inspection. Used for quality accountability and audit trail.']
  manufacturing_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_bom. Business justification: Instrument builds follow a specific hardware BOM defining assembly structure. Cardinality: N builds : 1 BOM (many instruments built from same BOM). Adding manufacturing_bom_header_id FK links build re']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.opportunity. Business justification: Genomics instruments (sequencers, array scanners, gene editors) are often built to order or allocated to specific customer opportunities. Links build records to commercial deals for revenue recognitio']
  primary_instrument_assembly_technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the primary technician responsible for instrument assembly. Used for traceability, quality accountability, and workforce performance tracking.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing work order that authorized and governs this instrument build. Links to the production planning and execution system.']
  assembly_end_date date [note: 'Date when physical assembly of the instrument was completed. Used to calculate assembly cycle time and transition to testing phase.']
  assembly_start_date date [note: 'Date when physical assembly of the instrument began on the manufacturing floor. Marks the beginning of the build cycle for production tracking and TAT calculation.']
  assembly_technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary assembly technician for human-readable reference in build records and quality documentation.']
  batch_record_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Device History Record (DHR) or batch record documenting all manufacturing steps, materials, and quality checks for this instrument build. Required for GMP compliance and regulatory traceability.']
  build_cycle_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time in hours from assembly start to final release. Key performance indicator for manufacturing efficiency and capacity planning.']
  build_record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the instrument build record. Used for traceability and reference in manufacturing documentation and quality systems.']
  build_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the instrument build process. Tracks progression from initial assembly through final acceptance testing and release to finished goods inventory. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|in_progress|assembly_complete|testing|calibration|inspection|fat_complete|released|on_hold|rejected — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  calibration_certificate_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier for the calibration certificate issued for this instrument. Provides traceability to calibration standards and measurement uncertainty.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when instrument calibration was completed. Establishes the baseline for instrument performance and future recalibration schedules.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of instrument calibration activities. Ensures optical, fluidic, thermal, and mechanical systems meet specified tolerances and performance criteria.']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deviations during this build triggered the need for formal CAPA investigation and corrective action. True if CAPA is required, False otherwise.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this instrument build that does not fit structured fields.']
  compute_module_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the embedded compute sub-assembly installed in this instrument. Handles data acquisition, real-time processing, and instrument control. Provides component-level traceability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this build record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of manufacturing deviations or non-conformances recorded during this instrument build. Used for quality trending and CAPA trigger analysis.']
  fat_completion_date date [note: 'Date when Factory Acceptance Test was successfully completed. Authorizes release of the instrument to finished goods inventory or direct shipment to customer.']
  fat_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier for the FAT report containing detailed test results, performance data, and acceptance signatures. Delivered to customer as part of instrument documentation package.']
  fat_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the Factory Acceptance Test performed before instrument shipment. Comprehensive end-to-end validation that the instrument meets all customer specifications and performance requirements.']
  final_inspection_date date [note: 'Date when final quality inspection was completed. Represents the last quality gate before Factory Acceptance Test.']
  final_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the final quality inspection before release. Verifies cosmetic condition, labeling, documentation completeness, and overall build quality.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the embedded firmware installed on the instrument during manufacturing. Controls low-level hardware operations and instrument control systems.']
  fluidics_module_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the fluidics control sub-assembly installed in this instrument. Manages reagent delivery and flow cell operations. Provides component-level traceability.']
  functional_test_date date [note: 'Date when functional testing was completed. Used for build cycle tracking and quality milestone documentation.']
  functional_test_protocol_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier for the functional test protocol executed during this build. Links to controlled quality documentation in the document management system.']
  functional_test_status varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of functional testing performed on the assembled instrument. Verifies that all subsystems operate according to design specifications.']
  instrument_model varchar [note: 'Model designation of the instrument being built. Examples include sequencing platforms (NGS instruments), array scanners, PCR/qPCR systems, or liquid handling robots.']
  instrument_serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned to the finished instrument unit. This identifier follows the instrument throughout its lifecycle from manufacturing through field deployment and service.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this build record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']
  manufacturing_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing facility where this instrument was built. Used for site-level quality trending, regulatory reporting, and supply chain traceability.']
  optical_module_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the optical detection sub-assembly installed in this instrument. Critical for sequencing and array scanning platforms. Provides component-level traceability.']
  production_line_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific production line or work center where assembly was performed. Used for line-level performance analysis and capacity planning.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument was officially released from manufacturing to finished goods inventory or shipped to customer. Represents final quality approval and handoff to supply chain.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the instrument control and data acquisition software installed during factory configuration. Includes operating system and application software.']

  Note: 'Tracks the assembly and build history of manufactured sequencing instruments, array scanners, PCR/qPCR platforms, and liquid handlers. Captures build record number, instrument model, serial number assigned, work order reference, build configuration (hardware BOM version, firmware version, software version), assembly start/end date, assembly technician, sub-assembly components with serial numbers (optical module, fluidics module, compute module), functional test results, calibration results, final inspection outcome, and factory acceptance test (FAT) status. Provides full instrument genealogy from component to finished instrument. Integrates with instrument domain for lifecycle handoff.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_routing {
  production_routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production routing master record. Primary key for the routing entity.']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master SOP document that governs the execution of this routing. Links routing to controlled documentation for GMP compliance.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Production routings for genomic assay manufacturing specify genome build compatibility as a critical process parameter for quality control checkpoints, validation steps, and manufacturing instructions']
  manufacturing_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this routing is valid and executed. Routings are plant-specific in SAP PP.']
  primary_production_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing or process engineer responsible for maintaining and updating this routing. Accountability for routing accuracy and optimization.']
  primary_production_sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product or material that this routing produces. Links to the master product catalog.']
  product_bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Bill of Materials that defines the components and materials consumed during execution of this routing. Links routing to material requirements.']
  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.protocol. Business justification: Production routings are industrialized versions of research protocols. Tech transfer process scales lab protocols to manufacturing SOPs. Essential for traceability in regulatory filings, process devia']
  tertiary_production_responsible_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  alternative_routing_number varchar [note: 'Reference to an alternative routing that can be used if this routing is unavailable. Enables flexible production planning and capacity management.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this routing was formally approved for production use. Part of the GMP document control audit trail.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the routing in the document control workflow. Only approved routings can be released for production use.']
  base_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Reference quantity for which the standard times in this routing are defined. Actual times are scaled proportionally based on order quantity.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the base quantity and lot sizes (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters). ISO standard unit codes.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the change control record that authorized the creation or last modification of this routing. Required for GMP traceability and audit compliance.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or context about this routing. Used for operational communication and knowledge transfer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  gmp_classification varchar [note: 'GMP criticality classification of the routing. GMP-critical routings require enhanced documentation, validation, and electronic signatures per FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GMP Annex 11.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing record was last updated. Tracks the currency of the routing data and supports change management.']
  last_validation_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent process validation study for this routing was completed and approved. Used to track validation currency.']
  lot_size_from decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Used in SAP PP to select the appropriate routing based on order quantity.']
  lot_size_to decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Enables lot-size-dependent routing selection in SAP PP.']
  mes_integration_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier used to link this routing to the shop-floor MES system for real-time production execution and data collection.']
  next_revalidation_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the routing must undergo revalidation to maintain its validated status. Periodic revalidation is required per GMP regulations.']
  operation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of manufacturing operations defined in this routing. Each operation represents a discrete production step with its own work center and times.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character plant code identifying the manufacturing site. Denormalized for operational reporting.']
  process_validation_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this routing requires formal process validation studies before use in commercial production. Required for IVD and RUO products per FDA and ISO 13485.']
  process_validation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of process validation for this routing. Validated status is required before the routing can be used for commercial GMP production.']
  regulatory_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory submission (510(k), PMA, CE-IVD dossier) that includes this routing as part of the manufacturing process description. Critical for IVD products.']
  requires_batch_record boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether production using this routing requires a formal batch manufacturing record (BMR) for traceability and compliance. Mandatory for GMP-regulated products.']
  requires_electronic_signature boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether operations in this routing require electronic signatures for GMP compliance. True for IVD and regulated product routings per FDA 21 CFR Part 11.']
  routing_group varchar [note: 'Group key that categorizes related routings together. Used in SAP PP to organize routings by product family or process type.']
  routing_group_counter varchar [note: 'Sequential counter within the routing group to distinguish multiple routings for the same material. Part of the composite routing key in SAP PP.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the routing. Externally-known unique code used in SAP PP for routing identification and work order generation.']
  routing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing. Active routings are available for work order generation, released routings are approved for production, locked routings are temporarily unavailable, and obsolete routings are retired.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the routing by its usage pattern. Standard routings are used for regular production, rate routings for repetitive manufacturing, reference routings as templates, and special routings for one-time or custom orders.']
  routing_usage varchar [note: 'SAP PP usage indicator defining the purpose of the routing (1=production, 2=maintenance, 3=quality inspection, etc.). Controls which business processes can reference this routing.']
  total_labor_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Sum of all labor times across all operations in the routing for the base quantity. Labor time represents manual work and scales with lot size.']
  total_machine_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Sum of all machine processing times across all operations in the routing for the base quantity. Machine time is equipment-dependent and scales with lot size.']
  total_setup_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Sum of all setup times across all operations in the routing. Setup time is independent of lot size and occurs once per production run.']
  total_standard_lead_time_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total manufacturing lead time in days required to complete all operations in this routing, including setup, processing, queue, and move times. Used for production planning and scheduling.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this routing becomes effective and can be used for work order generation. Part of the routing validity period.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this routing remains valid. Nullable for routings with indefinite validity. After this date, the routing cannot be used for new work orders.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this routing. Incremented with each approved change to maintain change history and support regulatory traceability.']

  Note: 'Master data defining the sequence of manufacturing operations (routing) required to produce a specific product, including standard times, work center assignments, and control keys. Captures routing number, routing group, material/product, plant, routing status (active, inactive), valid-from/valid-to dates, total standard lead time, operation sequence with operation numbers, work center per operation, standard setup/machine/labor times, GMP-critical operation flags, electronic signature requirement per operation, and MES integration reference. Serves as the manufacturing process template for work order generation and shop-floor execution. Integrates with SAP PP routing (CA01/CA02).'
}

Table manufacturing.work_center_qualification {
  work_center_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this work center qualification record. Primary key.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who approved this qualification. Typically a quality assurance manager or manufacturing director with GMP approval authority.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the catalog item being qualified for production at this work center']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the work center qualified to produce this catalog item']
  last_production_run_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent production run for this catalog item at this work center. Used to track active qualifications and identify dormant product-work center combinations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing product-specific considerations, constraints, or special handling requirements for producing this catalog item at this work center.']
  production_volume_capacity decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum production volume capacity for this catalog item at this work center, expressed in the catalog item\'s unit of measure. Used for production planning and capacity allocation.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when this product-work center combination completed qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ) and was approved for commercial production.']
  qualification_protocol_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the qualification protocol document (IQ/OQ/PQ) used to validate this product-work center combination. Links to quality management system.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory qualification status for producing this catalog item at this work center. Values: qualified (approved for production), in_qualification (validation in progress), suspended (temporarily not authorized), expired (requalification required), not_qualified (failed validation).']
  requalification_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for periodic requalification of this product-work center combination per GMP requirements. Triggers requalification workflow.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Effective start date for this qualification. Production of this catalog item at this work center is authorized beginning on this date.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Expiration date for this qualification. Requalification required before this date to continue production. Nullable for qualifications without expiration.']
  yield_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected production yield rate as a percentage for this catalog item when manufactured at this work center. Reflects product-specific performance metrics used for material planning and cost estimation.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the GMP qualification relationship between catalog items and manufacturing work centers. It captures the regulatory validation status, capacity constraints, and performance metrics required for each product-work center combination. Each record links one catalog item to one work center with qualification-specific attributes that exist only in the context of this manufacturing authorization relationship.. Existence Justification: In genomics manufacturing, work center qualification is a formal GMP compliance process where each catalog item (sequencing kit, reagent, instrument component) must be individually validated for production at each work center. A single catalog item like a NovaSeq reagent kit may be qualified for production at multiple work centers (e.g., Reagent Fill Line 1, Reagent Fill Line 2, Backup Production Line), and each work center is qualified to produce multiple different catalog items. The qualification relationship itself carries critical regulatory data including qualification status, validation dates, product-specific capacity constraints, and yield rates that belong to neither the catalog item nor the work center alone.'
}

Table manufacturing.qualification_participation {
  qualification_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this participation record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee participating in the qualification']
  equipment_qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the equipment qualification record (IQ/OQ/PQ event)']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'Level of approval authority exercised by this employee in this qualification (Execute, Review, Approve, None). Determines regulatory accountability.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Quality Assurance representative who approved the qualification report. [Moved from equipment_qualification: This is a STRING name field representing ONE QA approver. The FK approver_employee_id already captures this relationship as 1:N. This STRING field is redundant and should be deprecated. Not moving to association because it represents a single approver relationship, not M:N participation.]']
  comments varchar [note: 'Comments or observations provided by this employee during their participation in the qualification activity.']
  electronic_signature_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the electronic signature applied by this employee for this qualification participation. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  participation_date date [note: 'Date when this employee performed their role in the qualification activity. May differ from overall qualification completion date.']
  qualification_phase varchar [note: 'The phase of qualification during which this participation occurred (IQ, OQ, PQ, Review, Approval). Allows tracking personnel involvement across different qualification stages.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the engineer responsible for executing and documenting the qualification activity. [Moved from equipment_qualification: This is a STRING name field that represents ONE specific employee\'s role. However, the FK employee_id already captures the responsible engineer relationship. This STRING field is redundant with the FK and should be deprecated in favor of the FK relationship. Not moving to association because it represents a single 1:N relationship, not M:N participation data.]']
  role_in_qualification varchar [note: 'The specific role this employee performed in the qualification activity (e.g., Responsible Engineer, QA Reviewer, QA Approver, Technical SME, Witness, Test Executor). Determines signature authority and responsibility level.']
  signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the electronic signature was applied. Required for audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation of personnel in equipment qualification activities (IQ/OQ/PQ). It captures the role, signature authority, and participation details for each employee involved in a qualification event. Each record links one equipment qualification to one employee with attributes that exist only in the context of this regulatory participation relationship.. Existence Justification: Equipment qualification in GMP-regulated genomics manufacturing is a cross-functional regulatory process requiring documented participation from multiple personnel with different roles and signature authorities. A single qualification event (IQ/OQ/PQ) involves a responsible engineer, QA reviewers, QA approvers, technical SMEs, and witnesses, each providing electronic signatures at different phases. Each employee participates in multiple qualifications over time, and the business must track who participated in which qualification, in what role, with what signature authority, for regulatory audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and GMP Annex 15.'
}

Table manufacturing.manufacturing_site {
  manufacturing_site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for manufacturing_site']
  parent_manufacturing_site_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on manufacturing_site (parent_manufacturing_site_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the site’s street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the site’s street address (optional).']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the manufacturing site is located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the site ceased operations (if applicable).']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of other regulatory certifications held by the site (e.g., ISO 13485, ISO 9001).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the site location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the site record was first created in the data lake.']
  critical_equipment varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated identifiers of equipment critical for GMP compliance.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level applied to the site record for governance.']
  environmental_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site meets environmental regulatory requirements.']
  equipment_count varchar [note: 'Total count of major equipment pieces at the site.']
  floor_area_sqm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total usable floor area of the manufacturing facility.']
  gmp_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site holds current GMP certification.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent internal or external audit.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the site in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the site in decimal degrees.']
  next_audit_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled audit.']
  number_of_shifts varchar [note: 'Number of production shifts operated per day.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date when the site began operations.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the site address.']
  production_capacity_units_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum number of production units the site can output per day.']
  production_capacity_units_per_month decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum number of production units the site can output per month.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region used for reporting and planning.']
  shift_hours_per_day decimal(4,2) [note: 'Length of each shift in hours.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the site in ERP/MES systems.']
  site_manager_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the site manager.']
  site_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person responsible for site operations.']
  site_manager_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the site manager.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the manufacturing site.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Category of the site based on its primary function.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the site location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the site.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the site record.']
  waste_disposal_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used for disposing of manufacturing waste.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for manufacturing_site. Referenced by manufacturing_site_id.'
}

  start_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign is scheduled to start or actually started.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign.']
  target_yield decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target product yield quantity for the campaign.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the campaign definition.']
  yield_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for yield values.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for manufacturing_campaign. Referenced by campaign_id.'
}

Table manufacturing.monitoring_event {
  monitoring_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for monitoring_event']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context or notes about the event.']
  event_payload varchar [note: 'Raw measurement value or data payload captured by the event.']
  event_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level indicating the impact of the event.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the event record.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the monitoring event was observed on the shop floor.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the monitoring event.']
  is_test_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event was generated during a test or validation run.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier linked to the event, used for traceability.']
  recorded_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the system or operator that recorded the event.']
  source_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment, instrument, or system that generated the event.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Category of the event source (e.g., instrument, software, sensor, machine).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit associated with the event payload (e.g., °C, %, ppm).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the event record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for monitoring_event. Referenced by monitoring_event_id.'
}

  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the location.']
  temperature_setpoint_c double [note: 'Target temperature for climate‑controlled locations, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the location indicating its primary function (e.g., cleanroom, laboratory, warehouse).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the monitoring location record.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the monitoring location address.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for monitoring_location. Referenced by monitoring_location_id.'
}

  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration state of the equipment.']
  capacity_value varchar [note: 'Maximum number of samples or units the equipment can process per run.']
  cleaning_status varchar [note: 'Current cleaning state of the equipment.']
  cleanroom_class varchar [note: 'Cleanroom ISO class of the environment housing the equipment.']

  qualification_date date [note: 'Date the equipment was qualified for production use.']
  qualification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date the current qualification expires.']
  risk_classification varchar [note: 'Risk level assigned to the equipment based on impact and failure probability.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number uniquely identifying the unit.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the primary control software running on the equipment.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the equipment.']
  throughput_per_day varchar [note: 'Typical number of samples processed in a 24‑hour period.']
  usage_hours_total decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative operating hours since installation.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'GMP validation state of the equipment.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the manufacturer warranty ends.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for equipment. Referenced by equipment_id.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_line {
  production_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for production_line']

  parent_production_line_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on production_line (parent_production_line_id)']

  capacity_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum number of samples or units the line can process in one hour.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the line\'s capacity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the production line record was first created in the system.']
  current_batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch currently being processed on the line.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned retirement date of the line (null if indefinite).']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average energy consumption per hour of operation.']
  equipment_model varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model designation of the line\'s core equipment.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent scheduled maintenance activity.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle phase of the production line from planning to retirement.']

  line_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the production line used in reports and dashboards.']
