Table quality.nonconformance {
  nonconformance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the nonconformance record (NCR). Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: NCRs for finished genomics products (sequencing kits, reagents, arrays) shipped to customers require traceability to affected accounts for recall execution, customer notification, and regulatory repor']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: NCRs for released genomics products (sequencing kits, reagents) are tracked by approval/market for regulatory reporting obligations. Disposition decisions consider approval-specific specifications. Es']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_asset. Business justification: Manufacturing QC tracks instrument-related nonconformances for defect trending, supplier quality analysis, and calibration/maintenance correlation. Essential for GxP-regulated production environments ']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.audit. Business justification: Nonconformances can be identified during audits. One NCR can be identified in one audit; one audit can identify multiple NCRs. Standard 1:N relationship.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: NCRs for out-of-spec batches, contamination, or labeling errors must link to the affected batch for disposition decisions, quarantine management, and CAPA investigations. Critical for GMP traceability']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the associated CAPA record if corrective or preventive action was initiated.']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Nonconformances can trigger change controls to prevent recurrence. One NCR can trigger one change control; one change control can address multiple NCRs. Standard 1:N relationship.']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.controlled_document. Business justification: Nonconformances reference the specification or procedure that was not met. One NCR references one primary document; one document can be referenced by multiple NCRs. Standard 1:N relationship. Note: sp']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: NCR investigations and rework activities consume resources (labor, materials, retesting) charged to cost centers for quality cost accounting, scrap/rework variance analysis, and cost-of-poor-quality (']
  customer_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the originating customer complaint record if the nonconformance was detected post-sale.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Nonconformances can be linked to quality deviations when the NCR is caused by a deviation from procedure. One NCR can be related to one deviation; one deviation can result in multiple NCRs. Standard 1']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Nonconformances in genomic testing (incorrect variant calls, alignment failures) must document the genome build to assess root cause and determine if reference data version contributed to the failure.']
  inbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_delivery. Business justification: Cold chain excursions, carrier damage, or customs delays detected at delivery trigger NCRs. Link required for temperature log review, carrier claims, and supplier performance impact assessment.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.opportunity. Business justification: NCRs may be raised against products sold via specific opportunities (custom orders, beta units). Linking enables warranty claim tracking, customer-specific quality issue analysis, and opportunity-leve']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: NCRs for incoming material defects (wrong spec, damaged goods, missing CoA) must trace to the originating PO for supplier notification, debit memos, and supplier performance scorecards.']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Nonconformances are often triggered by failing QC results. One NCR can be triggered by one QC result; one QC result can trigger multiple NCRs if multiple parameters fail. Standard 1:N relationship.']
  quality_issue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.quality_issue. Business justification: NCRs for data-related nonconformances (e.g., missing metadata, failed validation, data integrity issues) link to data quality issue tracking. Required for disposition decisions and demonstrating corre']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the supplier or vendor if the nonconformance originated from externally sourced material.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse_location. Business justification: Storage condition deviations (temperature excursion, humidity breach) are location-specific. NCR must reference warehouse_location for calibration verification, environmental monitoring review, and im']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this nonconformance triggers a formal CAPA investigation and action plan.']
  closed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the quality professional who closed the nonconformance record after verification of completion.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the nonconformance record was formally closed after all disposition and corrective actions were completed.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate containment actions taken to prevent further use or distribution of the nonconforming material.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the nonconformance record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the financial impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  detected_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or system that detected the nonconformance.']
  detected_date date [note: 'Date when the nonconformance was first identified and documented.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Origin point where the nonconformance was identified: incoming inspection, in-process QC, final release testing, customer complaint, supplier notification, or internal audit.']
  disposition_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the quality authority who approved the disposition decision.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date when the disposition decision was finalized and approved.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision on how to handle the nonconforming material: use as-is (with concession), rework, reject, scrap, return to supplier, or quarantine pending further investigation.']
  disposition_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed rationale and technical justification for the disposition decision, including risk assessment and impact analysis.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Description of how the material or product failed to meet specifications (e.g., out-of-specification result, physical defect, labeling error, contamination).']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial impact of the nonconformance, including scrap cost, rework cost, and potential liability exposure.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection lot number associated with the quality control testing that identified the nonconformance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the nonconformance record, supporting audit trail requirements.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number of the nonconforming material for traceability.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the affected material, product, reagent, or component.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material master number or SKU of the affected nonconforming item.']
  measured_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual measured or observed value that triggered the nonconformance, stored as string to accommodate various data types.']
  ncr_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the nonconformance report, typically formatted as NCR-YYYYNNNN for external reference and traceability.']
  ncr_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nonconforming item: material, product, component, reagent, instrument, or raw material.']
  nonconformance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the nonconformance record: open, under investigation, pending disposition, closed, or cancelled.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant or manufacturing site code where the nonconformance was detected or where the material was produced.']
  quality_notification_number varchar [note: 'SAP QM notification number linking this NCR to the SAP Quality Management module for integrated workflow.']
  quantity_affected decimal(15,3) [note: 'Numeric quantity of nonconforming material or units identified, expressed in the material\'s base unit of measure.']
  regulatory_report_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the regulatory report filed (e.g., MDR report number, field safety notice number) if reportable.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this nonconformance requires reporting to regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA, notified body) per applicable regulations.']
  root_cause_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the root cause analysis findings, identifying the underlying reason for the nonconformance.']
  specification_limit varchar [note: 'Acceptance criteria or specification limit that was not met (e.g., ≥95%, 7.0-7.5, <10 CFU/mL).']
  specification_parameter varchar [note: 'Specific quality parameter or attribute that was out of specification (e.g., purity, concentration, pH, sterility).']
  specification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality specification, acceptance criteria, or SOP that the material failed to meet.']
  supplier_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier was formally notified of the nonconformance, if applicable.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the affected quantity (e.g., EA for each, L for liters, KG for kilograms).']

  Note: 'Nonconforming material or product record (NCR) capturing instances where a product, reagent, instrument component, or raw material fails to meet defined specifications or acceptance criteria. Tracks nonconformance source (incoming inspection, in-process QC, final release, customer complaint), affected material/lot/batch, failure mode, quantity affected, disposition decision (use-as-is, rework, reject/scrap, return to supplier), and regulatory reportability assessment. Distinct from deviation (process departure) — NCR is material/product-focused. Supports SAP QM module integration and ISO 13485 Clause 8.3 nonconforming product control.'
}

Table quality.qc_result {
  qc_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality control test result record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analytical instrument or equipment used to perform the QC test.']
  gene_annotation_track_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_annotation_track. Business justification: QC results for gene expression assays and panel sequencing must reference the annotation track used for feature counting, coverage metrics, and transcript quantification. Essential for result interpre']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: QC results for genomic assays (NGS, arrays) must reference the genome build used for alignment and variant calling. Critical for regulatory submissions, result interpretation, and traceability. Requir']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'FK to reagent.lot.lot_id — QC results for reagent lots must reference the reagent.lot entity — this was the consolidation path when qc_test_result was merged into qc_result. Enables lot-level quality trending and release decisi']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material, reagent, consumable, or instrument being tested.']
  primary_qc_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory technician or quality control analyst who performed the test.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical sample or specimen that was tested.']
  stability_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.stability_study. Business justification: QC results are generated as part of stability studies at various time points. One QC result can be part of one stability study; one stability study generates multiple QC results over time. Standard 1:']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: QC results can be part of validation studies (method validation, assay validation). One QC result can be part of one validation study; one validation study generates multiple QC results. Standard 1:N ']
  variant_database_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.variant_database_version. Business justification: QC results for variant detection assays must reference the specific variant database version (ClinVar, gnomAD, COSMIC) used for annotation and clinical interpretation. Essential for result reproducibi']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the instrument used for testing was in a valid calibration state at the time of the test.']
  coa_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this QC result is included in the official Certificate of Analysis for the lot.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for analyst observations, notes, or contextual information about the test execution or result.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this QC result record was first created in the system.']
  deviation_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of any deviation or non-conformance associated with this QC result, if applicable.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration or retest date assigned to the material lot based on stability data and QC results.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'The lot number assigned to the batch of material or product being inspected. Links to the manufacturing lot or batch under quality control.']
  laboratory_location varchar [note: 'The physical laboratory or testing facility where the QC test was performed.']
  lot_disposition varchar [note: 'The final quality decision for the lot based on cumulative QC results: released for use, rejected, or held in quarantine.']
  lower_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum acceptable value for the test characteristic as defined in the product specification or quality standard.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The quantitative result obtained from the QC test or inspection.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this QC result record was last modified.']
  out_of_specification_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that this result exceeded specification limits and triggered an OOS investigation.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this result must be reported to regulatory authorities (e.g., FDA, EMA) as part of compliance obligations.']
  replicate_number varchar [note: 'The replicate or repeat number when multiple measurements are taken from the same sample for statistical validity.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'The disposition of the QC test result indicating whether the measured value meets acceptance criteria.']
  retest_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this result requires retesting due to inconclusive or borderline results.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the QC result was reviewed and approved by quality assurance.']
  sop_number varchar [note: 'The controlled document number of the SOP that governed the execution of this test.']
  sop_version varchar [note: 'The version of the SOP that was in effect when the test was performed.']
  specification_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the product specification or quality standard applied to evaluate this result.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The ideal or nominal value for the test characteristic, representing the center of the specification range.']
  test_category varchar [note: 'The analytical category or discipline of the QC test method.']
  test_characteristic varchar [note: 'The specific quality characteristic or parameter being measured (e.g., purity, concentration, pH, viscosity, sterility).']
  test_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the QC test was performed.']
  test_method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the standardized test method or analytical procedure used for this QC measurement.']
  test_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the QC test was executed, supporting audit trail and chronological traceability.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the QC test based on the stage of production or purpose of testing.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the measured value is expressed (e.g., mg/mL, %, pH units, CFU/mL).']
  upper_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum acceptable value for the test characteristic as defined in the product specification or quality standard.']

  Note: 'Quality control test result record capturing individual QC measurements and inspection outcomes for reagents, consumables, instruments, and in-process materials — including inspection lot context, test method, measured value, acceptance criteria, and pass/fail disposition'
}

Table quality.oos_investigation {
  oos_investigation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the OOS investigation record. Primary key.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated CAPA record if corrective or preventive actions were initiated.']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: OOS investigations can trigger change controls when systemic issues are identified. One OOS investigation can trigger one change control; one change control can address multiple investigations. Standa']
  complaint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.complaint. Business justification: OOS investigations can be triggered by customer complaints about product quality. One OOS investigation can be related to one complaint; one complaint can trigger multiple OOS investigations. Standard']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: OOS investigations can be linked to quality deviations when the OOS result is caused by a procedural deviation. One OOS investigation can be related to one deviation; one deviation can trigger multipl']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: OOS investigations can result in nonconformance records when material is determined to be non-conforming. One OOS investigation can result in one NCR; one NCR can be investigated by multiple OOS inves']
  primary_oos_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality assurance or laboratory personnel who initiated the investigation.']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating QC test result that triggered this OOS investigation.']
  quality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.quality_assessment. Business justification: OOS investigations require data quality assessment to rule out laboratory error vs. true OOS per FDA guidance. Phase I investigation must assess data integrity, calculation errors, and system suitabil']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sample that was tested and produced the OOS result.']
  tertiary_oos_qa_reviewer_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the QA professional who reviewed and approved the investigation findings.']
  test_method_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the analytical test method or assay protocol used when the OOS result was obtained.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal CAPA is required based on investigation findings.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the OOS investigation was formally closed after all required actions were completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OOS investigation record was first created in the system.']
  deviation_magnitude decimal(18,6) [note: 'Calculated magnitude of deviation from the specification limit, expressed as absolute difference or percentage.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the lot or batch under investigation based on investigation findings.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the potential impact on product quality, patient safety, and other released lots or batches.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'Date when the OOS investigation was formally initiated following detection of the out-of-specification result.']
  invalidation_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed scientific justification for invalidating the OOS result, required when result_invalidated is true. Must demonstrate clear laboratory error per FDA guidance.']
  investigation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the OOS investigation, typically formatted as OOS-YYYY-NNNNNN for external reference and regulatory reporting.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OOS investigation workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initiated|phase_i_in_progress|phase_i_complete|phase_ii_in_progress|phase_ii_complete|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  investigation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigation: Out-of-Specification (OOS), Out-of-Trend (OOT), atypical result, or failed specification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OOS investigation record was last modified.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number of the product or reagent under investigation.']
  observed_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual measured or observed test result value that fell outside specification limits.']
  phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the investigation: Phase I (laboratory investigation for analyst error, instrument malfunction, calculation error) or Phase II (full investigation for process failure, raw material failure, method failure).']
  phase_i_assignable_cause_found boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an assignable laboratory cause was identified during Phase I investigation.']
  phase_i_completion_date date [note: 'Date when Phase I laboratory investigation was completed.']
  phase_i_findings varchar [note: 'Detailed findings from Phase I laboratory investigation including assessment of analyst error, instrument malfunction, calculation error, or other laboratory-related causes.']
  phase_ii_assignable_cause_found boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an assignable manufacturing or process cause was identified during Phase II investigation.']
  phase_ii_completion_date date [note: 'Date when Phase II full investigation was completed.']
  phase_ii_findings varchar [note: 'Detailed findings from Phase II full investigation including assessment of process failure, raw material failure, method failure, or other manufacturing-related causes.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) or product identifier for the item under investigation.']
  qa_review_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation was reviewed and approved by quality assurance.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date when the OOS investigation was reported to regulatory authorities, if applicable.']
  regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OOS investigation findings require reporting to regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA, etc.).']
  result_invalidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the original OOS result was invalidated based on investigation findings demonstrating laboratory error.']
  retest_authorization_date date [note: 'Date when retesting was authorized by quality assurance.']
  retest_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether retesting of the sample was authorized as part of the investigation.']
  retest_results_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of retest results including number of retests performed and outcomes.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of the identified root cause for the OOS result. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: analyst_error|instrument_malfunction|calculation_error|process_failure|raw_material_failure|method_failure|environmental_condition|no_cause_identified|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the identified root cause and supporting evidence.']
  specification_limit_lower decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower acceptance criterion or specification limit for the test parameter.']
  specification_limit_upper decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper acceptance criterion or specification limit for the test parameter.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the observed value and specification limits (e.g., mg/mL, %, CFU/mL, ng/µL).']

  Note: 'Out-of-Specification (OOS) investigation record initiated when a QC result falls outside established acceptance criteria. Captures Phase I (laboratory investigation — analyst error, instrument malfunction, calculation error) and Phase II (full investigation — process failure, raw material failure, method failure) findings, invalidation justification if applicable, retesting authorization, assignable cause determination, and regulatory reportability assessment. Supports FDA OOS guidance (2006) compliance, CLIA proficiency testing failure investigation, and CAP accreditation requirements for laboratory investigations.'
}

Table quality.complaint {
  complaint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer complaint record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer (research institution, clinical laboratory, biopharma partner, or agricultural genomics client) who submitted the complaint.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: Genomics product complaints are analyzed by regulatory approval/market for vigilance reporting (EU MDR, FDA MDR). Complaint trending by approval is required for periodic safety update reports (PSUR), ']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_asset. Business justification: Customer complaints about instrument performance require structured link for field failure analysis, warranty claim validation, MDR/vigilance reporting, and service contract management. Critical for p']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Customer complaints citing performance issues or defects require batch-level traceability for investigation, recall assessment, and MDR/vigilance reporting. Lot_number denormalization replaced by prop']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.campaign. Business justification: Complaints may surface from campaign-driven purchases (conference demos, promotional trials). Linking enables campaign effectiveness assessment, adverse event tracking for promotional materials, and r']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPA record initiated in response to this complaint, if applicable.']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Customer complaints can trigger change controls to address product or process issues. One complaint can trigger one change control; one change control can address multiple complaints. Standard 1:N rel']

  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Customer complaints can be linked to quality deviations when the complaint is caused by a deviation from procedure. One complaint can be related to one deviation; one deviation can result in multiple ']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Critical for root cause analysis and MDR/vigilance ']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal quality investigation record initiated for this complaint, if applicable.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Complaints reference specific product codes for trend analysis, recall scope determination, and MDR reporting. Material master provides regulatory classification, hazard data, and manufacturer part nu']

  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product implicated in the complaint (sequencing instrument, reagent kit, consumable, software, or service).']
  support_case_id varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM case identifier linking this complaint to the customer service case management system.']

  closure_notes varchar [note: 'Final notes documenting the closure rationale, resolution summary, and any follow-up actions.']
  complaint_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the complaint type based on the nature of the quality issue reported. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reagent_performance|instrument_malfunction|sequencing_quality|bioinformatics_pipeline_error|labeling_packaging|documentation|delivery_shipping|customer_service|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  complaint_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the complaint as reported by the customer, including symptoms, context, and impact.']

  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective and preventive action (CAPA) is required based on investigation findings.']

  diagnostic_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the complaint\'s impact on diagnostic test results, clinical decision-making, or patient care pathways.']
  investigation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal quality investigation is required for this complaint.']
  mdr_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complaint meets FDA Medical Device Reporting (MDR) criteria and requires regulatory submission.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this complaint record was last modified.']
  patient_safety_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complaint has potential or actual impact on patient safety or clinical diagnostic outcomes.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the complaint for workflow management and resource allocation.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the complaint was first received by the organization.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the complaint was logged into the quality management system.']

  vigilance_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complaint meets EU vigilance reporting requirements under IVDR.']

  Note: 'Customer complaint record for quality-related issues reported by research institutions, clinical laboratories, biopharma partners, and agricultural genomics clients. Captures complaint source, product/lot implicated, complaint category (reagent performance, instrument malfunction, sequencing quality, bioinformatics pipeline error, labeling/packaging), patient safety or diagnostic impact assessment, MDR/vigilance reportability determination, investigation findings, and customer response. Integrates with Salesforce CRM case management and Veeva Vault for regulatory reporting. Supports FDA 21 CFR Part 820.198, EU IVDR Article 87, and ISO 13485 Clause 8.2.2 complaint handling requirements.'
}

  study_start_date date [note: 'Date when the validation study execution began.']
  study_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the validation study: draft (protocol being prepared), protocol_approved (ready to execute), in_progress (study execution underway), data_collection_complete (all testing finished), under_review (analysis and report under QA review), approved (study concluded successfully), rejected (study failed acceptance criteria), cancelled (study terminated before completion). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|protocol_approved|in_progress|data_collection_complete|under_review|approved|rejected|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  study_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the validation study summarizing the scope and purpose.']
  validation_conclusion varchar [note: 'Overall conclusion of the validation study: passed (all acceptance criteria met), failed (one or more criteria not met), conditional_pass (passed with minor deviations or additional controls), inconclusive (insufficient data or study issues prevent conclusion).']
  validation_subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification of the validation study within the primary type, such as IQ (Installation Qualification), OQ (Operational Qualification), PQ (Performance Qualification) for equipment qualification, or full validation vs partial validation for methods.']
  validation_type varchar [note: 'Category of validation study being performed: method validation (analytical method), assay validation (clinical assay performance), software validation (CSV - Computer System Validation), equipment qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ - Installation/Operational/Performance Qualification), process validation (manufacturing process), or cleaning validation (equipment cleaning procedures).']

  Note: 'Analytical and process validation study master record covering method validation, assay validation, software validation (CSV), equipment qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ), and process validation for GxP-regulated activities. Captures validation type, applicable regulatory standard (FDA 21 CFR Part 11, ICH Q2(R1), CLSI EP guidelines), validation protocol document reference, acceptance criteria, study execution dates, statistical analysis approach, validation conclusion, and approval chain. Supports clinical assay analytical validation for CLIA/CAP LDT submissions and IVD performance studies for FDA/CE-IVD submissions.'
}

Table quality.stability_study {
  stability_study_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stability study record. Primary key for the stability study entity.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: ICH stability studies are conducted on specific production batches to establish shelf life. Protocol requires batch manufacturing date, storage conditions, and CoA linkage. Essential for regulatory su']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Stability studies are often performed as part of change control for product formulation or storage changes. One stability study can be part of one change control; one change control can require multip']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Stability studies are multi-year resource commitments (laboratory time, materials, storage, testing) requiring cost center allocation for R&D expense tracking, budget management, and regulatory submis']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Stability protocols are material-specific (each SKU has unique formulation, container closure). Material master holds storage conditions, shelf life, and regulatory status. Replaces denormalized produ']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.opportunity. Business justification: Stability studies may be commissioned for specific customer opportunities (custom formulations, extended dating for export markets, temperature excursion studies). Linking enables project costing, del']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the regulatory submission (510(k), PMA, CE-IVD technical file) that includes this stability study data. Links stability evidence to market authorization applications.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed specification of stability-indicating assay acceptance criteria that the product must meet at each time point. Includes parameters such as potency, purity, impurities, pH, appearance, and functional performance thresholds. Failure to meet criteria indicates end of shelf life.']
  assigned_shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Shelf life in months assigned to the product based on stability study results. Determined by the longest time point where all acceptance criteria are met with appropriate statistical confidence. Appears on product labeling and Certificate of Analysis (COA).']
  batch_scale varchar [note: 'Manufacturing scale of the lot enrolled in the stability study. Pilot batches are small-scale development batches; production batches are commercial-scale; validation batches are used for process validation and initial regulatory submission.']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stability study results triggered a CAPA investigation. True when out-of-specification results, unexpected degradation, or shelf-life failures require root cause analysis and corrective action.']
  coa_reference_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stability study is referenced on the product Certificate of Analysis (COA) for expiry dating justification. True for primary stability studies supporting shelf-life claims.']
  container_closure_system varchar [note: 'Description of the primary packaging configuration used in the stability study. Includes container type, material, size, and closure mechanism. Must match commercial packaging to ensure representative stability data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stability study record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data governance and compliance.']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of quality deviations recorded during the stability study execution. Includes storage excursions, missed time points, and protocol deviations. Zero indicates study conducted per protocol.']
  expiry_date_format varchar [note: 'Format specification for expiry date labeling on product packaging. Day-level precision for short shelf-life products; month-level precision for products with shelf life greater than 12 months per regulatory guidance.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stability study was conducted under GMP conditions per 21 CFR Part 211. True for studies supporting regulatory submissions and commercial products; false for research-use-only (RUO) product development studies.']
  ich_climatic_zone varchar [note: 'ICH climatic zone classification for the intended market. Zone I: temperate climate; Zone II: subtropical/Mediterranean; Zone III: hot/dry; Zone IV: hot/humid; Zone IVa: hot/humid with high temperature; Zone IVb: hot/very humid. Determines storage condition requirements.']
  lims_study_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier for the stability study in LabVantage LIMS. Used for integration with laboratory workflow, sample scheduling, and test result capture.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the stability study record. Maintains complete audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the stability study record. Tracks data currency and change history.']
  principal_investigator varchar [note: 'Name of the scientist or quality professional responsible for stability study design, execution, and data interpretation. Accountable for study integrity and regulatory compliance.']
  regulatory_purpose varchar [note: 'Regulatory driver for conducting the stability study. Initial registration supports new product approval; post-approval commitment fulfills regulatory agency requirements; annual stability ensures ongoing compliance; change control evaluates impact of manufacturing changes; shelf-life extension supports expiry date extension; market surveillance monitors commercial product stability.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of product units enrolled in the stability study. Must be sufficient to support all planned time points and replicate testing per ICH guidelines.']
  sap_qm_inspection_lot varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection lot number linked to the stability study. Connects stability data to lot release decisions and quality notifications in SAP S/4HANA QM module.']
  stability_indicating_assays varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of analytical test methods used to assess product stability. Examples: HPLC for purity, qPCR for enzyme activity, spectrophotometry for concentration, visual inspection for appearance, pH measurement. These assays must be validated to detect degradation.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Environmental storage condition specification for the stability study including temperature, relative humidity, and light exposure. Examples: 25°C ± 2°C / 60% RH ± 5%, 40°C ± 2°C / 75% RH ± 5%, -20°C ± 5°C, 2-8°C. Follows ICH climatic zone definitions.']
  storage_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target relative humidity percentage for the stability study storage condition. Null for frozen storage conditions where humidity control is not applicable.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the stability study. Numeric representation extracted from storage condition for analytical purposes.']
  study_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the final time point testing was completed and study data analysis finalized. Null for ongoing studies.']
  study_duration_months varchar [note: 'Total planned duration of the stability study in months. Typical durations: 12-36 months for real-time studies, 6 months for accelerated studies, 12 months for intermediate studies per ICH guidelines.']
  study_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional study information, observations, and context. Includes notes on study design rationale, deviations, special handling requirements, and lessons learned.']
  study_protocol_number varchar [note: 'Unique protocol identifier assigned to the stability study following ICH Q1A/Q1B guidelines. Used for regulatory submission and traceability.']
  study_sponsor varchar [note: 'Business unit or department sponsoring the stability study. Typically Quality Assurance, Regulatory Affairs, or Product Development. Responsible for study oversight and funding.']
  study_start_date date [note: 'Date when the stability study was initiated and samples were placed on stability storage. Marks time zero for all time point calculations.']
  study_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stability study. Planned indicates study design approved but not initiated; active indicates ongoing testing; on-hold indicates temporary suspension; completed indicates all time points tested and data analyzed; cancelled indicates study terminated; under-review indicates final data review and shelf-life determination in progress.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stability study program. Real-time studies establish shelf life under recommended storage conditions; accelerated studies predict shelf life under elevated stress; intermediate studies bridge real-time and accelerated; stress studies determine degradation pathways; in-use studies assess stability after package opening; photostability studies evaluate light exposure effects.']
  time_point_schedule varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of planned time points in months for sample testing. Example: 0,3,6,9,12,18,24,36 for long-term real-time study. Defines the pull schedule for stability sample testing.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault for the stability study protocol and final report. Ensures controlled document management and regulatory audit trail.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the stability study record. Supports audit trail and accountability requirements.']

  Note: 'Stability study master record tracking real-time and accelerated stability programs for reagent kits, consumables, flow cells, and library preparation components. Captures study design (ICH Q1A/Q1B protocol), storage conditions (temperature, humidity, light), time points, acceptance criteria per stability-indicating assay, pull schedule, and shelf-life determination. Tracks ongoing stability commitments for post-market surveillance and supports expiry dating on COAs. Integrates with LabVantage LIMS for stability sample scheduling and SAP QM for lot release decisions.'
}

Table quality.change_control {
  change_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the change control record. Primary key for the change control entity.']
  change_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.controlled_document. Business justification: Change controls often affect controlled documents (SOPs, specifications, protocols). One change control can affect one primary document; one document can be affected by multiple change controls. Stand']
  gene_annotation_track_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_annotation_track. Business justification: Changes to gene annotation versions require change control to assess impact on panel design, coverage metrics, and transcript quantification. Critical for maintaining assay performance and regulatory ']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Changes to reference genome versions in production pipelines require formal change control with validation, impact assessment, and regulatory notification. Critical for maintaining GxP compliance and ']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee assigned as the change owner, responsible for coordinating impact assessment, approval routing, and implementation. Links to workforce or employee master data.']
  primary_change_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who initiated the change control request. Links to workforce or employee master data.']
  quality_training_curriculum_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.training_curriculum. Business justification: Change controls can require training curriculum updates when procedures or processes change. One change control can affect one curriculum; one curriculum can be affected by multiple change controls. S']
  quaternary_change_initiator_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  tertiary_change_regulatory_approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Regulatory Affairs representative who approved the change control. Links to workforce or employee master data. Null if Regulatory approval is not yet obtained or not required.']
  variant_database_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.variant_database_version. Business justification: Updates to variant databases (ClinVar, gnomAD) in clinical pipelines require change control to assess impact on variant interpretation, reclassifications, and patient reporting. Required for CAP/CLIA ']
  actual_implementation_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the change was fully implemented. Null if implementation is not yet complete.']
  affected_equipment_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured list of equipment asset IDs or instrument serial numbers impacted by the change. Used for equipment qualification and maintenance planning.']
  affected_facility_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured list of facility locations, cleanrooms, or manufacturing sites impacted by the change. Used for facility qualification and environmental monitoring.']
  affected_product_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured list of product SKUs, catalog numbers, or product families impacted by the change. Used for traceability and scope definition.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal cross-functional approval is required for this change control. True for GxP-impactful changes requiring Quality Assurance, Regulatory Affairs, and functional area approval; False for administrative changes with simplified routing.']
  approval_routing_path varchar [note: 'Structured representation of the approval workflow path, listing required approvers by role or department (e.g., Quality Assurance, Regulatory Affairs, Manufacturing, Engineering, R&D). May be comma-separated or JSON-encoded.']
  change_category varchar [note: 'Functional category of the change control request. Defines the operational domain affected by the change: manufacturing process modifications, equipment qualification changes, raw material or reagent changes, software system updates, facility modifications, analytical method validation changes, or controlled document revisions (SOP, specification, protocol). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturing_process|equipment|material|software|facility|analytical_method|document_revision — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_control_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the proposed change, including current state, proposed future state, rationale, and business justification. Provides comprehensive context for impact assessment and approval decision-making.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the change control request, typically formatted as CC-NNNNNN. Used for cross-functional communication and regulatory traceability.']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the change control request. Tracks progression from initiation through approval, implementation, and closure. Draft: initial creation. Submitted: routed for review. Under Review: impact assessment and approval routing in progress. Approved: authorized for implementation. Rejected: not approved. Implementation: change being executed. Completed: implementation finished, awaiting effectiveness check. Closed: effectiveness verified and change control archived. Cancelled: withdrawn before completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|implementation|completed|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change based on regulatory and business impact. Major: significant process/product changes requiring regulatory submission. Minor: low-impact changes with limited scope. Critical: urgent changes affecting patient safety or product quality. Administrative: documentation-only changes with no GxP impact.']
  closure_comments varchar [note: 'Final comments or notes recorded at the time of change control closure, summarizing outcomes, lessons learned, or follow-up actions.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the change control was formally closed after successful implementation and effectiveness verification. Null if the change control is not yet closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the change control record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  document_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change requires updates to controlled documents (SOPs, specifications, protocols, work instructions, batch records, device master records). True if document updates are required; False otherwise.']
  effectiveness_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the post-implementation effectiveness check was completed. Null if effectiveness check is not yet performed or not required.']
  effectiveness_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-implementation effectiveness check is required to verify that the change achieved its intended objectives and did not introduce unintended consequences. True if effectiveness check is required; False otherwise.']
  effectiveness_check_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the post-implementation effectiveness check. Effective: change achieved intended objectives. Partially Effective: some objectives met, additional action required. Ineffective: objectives not met, corrective action required. Not Applicable: effectiveness check not required or not yet performed.']
  impact_assessment_summary varchar [note: 'Consolidated summary of cross-functional impact assessment results, covering regulatory, validation, training, documentation, supply chain, and operational impacts. Informs approval decision and implementation planning.']
  implementation_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed plan describing how the approved change will be executed, including tasks, responsibilities, timelines, resource requirements, and success criteria. May reference linked project plans or work orders.']
  initiator_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional area of the change initiator (e.g., Manufacturing, Quality Assurance, R&D, Regulatory Affairs, Engineering). Used for cross-functional impact analysis and routing.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business and technical rationale for the proposed change. May include quality improvement objectives, cost reduction, regulatory compliance requirements, risk mitigation, process optimization, or corrective action linkage.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the change control record was last modified. Updated on any field change. Used for audit trail and version control.']
  planned_implementation_date date [note: 'Target date for completing the implementation of the approved change. Used for scheduling, resource planning, and tracking.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the change control request. Critical: immediate action required (safety, regulatory, or business continuity). High: significant impact, expedited review. Medium: standard processing. Low: minimal impact, routine processing.']
  qa_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which Quality Assurance approved the change control. Null if QA approval is not yet obtained or not required.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which Regulatory Affairs approved the change control. Null if Regulatory approval is not yet obtained or not required.']
  regulatory_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change requires regulatory notification or submission (PAS, CBE-30, CBE-0, annual report). True if regulatory submission is required; False otherwise.']
  regulatory_submission_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory submission required for the change. PAS (Prior Approval Supplement): major changes requiring pre-approval. CBE-30 (Changes Being Effected in 30 Days): moderate changes with 30-day notification. CBE-0 (Changes Being Effected): minor changes with immediate notification. Annual Report: reportable in annual submission. None: no regulatory submission required.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment classification based on potential impact to product quality, patient safety, regulatory compliance, and business operations. Determines the rigor of impact assessment and approval routing requirements.']
  title varchar [note: 'Concise, descriptive title summarizing the nature and scope of the proposed change. Used for quick identification in dashboards, reports, and approval workflows.']
  training_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change requires training or retraining of personnel (SOP training, equipment training, software training). True if training is required; False otherwise.']
  validation_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change requires revalidation, requalification, or verification activities (process validation, equipment qualification, software validation, analytical method validation). True if validation activities are required; False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Unified quality change control record governing all GxP-impactful changes — including manufacturing processes, equipment, materials, software, facilities, analytical methods, and controlled document revisions. Manages the complete change lifecycle: initiation, impact assessment (regulatory, validation, training, document), cross-functional approval routing, implementation authorization, and post-change effectiveness verification. Encompasses both document-level changes (SOP revisions, specification updates) and broader operational changes requiring regulatory submission (PAS, CBE-30, CBE-0). Integrates with Veeva Vault for change control workflow, MasterControl for document revision routing, and SAP for ERP-side change implementation. Supports FDA 21 CFR Part 820.40, ICH Q10, and ISO 13485 Clause 7.3.9.'
}

Table quality.risk_assessment {
  risk_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for risk_assessment']
  attached_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the stored assessment report or supporting documentation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or group responsible for managing the risk.']
  previous_risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on risk_assessment (previous_risk_assessment_id)']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who performed the latest review of the assessment.']
  assessment_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the assessment in reports and SOPs.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk assessment was performed.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the assessment based on the area of the organization it evaluates.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the assessment against the referenced regulatory requirement.']
  control_effectiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rating (0‑10) of how effective existing controls are at mitigating the risk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk assessment becomes active.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk assessment expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric rating (0‑10) of the expected impact if the risk materialises.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the risk is deemed critical for immediate action.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review or update of the assessment.']
  likelihood_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric rating (0‑10) of the probability that the risk will occur.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Textual description of actions planned or taken to mitigate the identified risk.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the risk assessment.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next periodic review of the risk assessment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments captured by the assessor.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation, guideline, or standard that drives the assessment (e.g., 21 CFR Part 11).']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the risk being assessed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk level derived from severity, likelihood and controls.']
  severity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric rating (0‑10) of the potential impact severity.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the assessment record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for risk_assessment. Referenced by risk_assessment_id.'
}

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the protocol record was created in the system.']

  reviewer varchar [note: 'Name of the person who reviewed and approved the protocol.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the protocol.']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Sample types applicable to the protocol.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the protocol.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the protocol (e.g., analytical, manufacturing, software, equipment).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the protocol record.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date when the protocol was validated.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the protocol validation process.']
  version_number decimal(5,2) [note: 'Version number of the protocol.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for qualification_protocol. Referenced by qualification_protocol_id.'
}

Table quality.test_method {
  test_method_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for test_method']
  superseded_test_method_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on test_method (superseded_test_method_id)']
  applicable_sample_type varchar [note: 'Sample matrix(s) the method can analyze.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the method for use.']
  assay_target varchar [note: 'Gene, protein, or biomarker the assay is designed to detect.']
  category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping such as nucleic‑acid, protein, or cell‑based assay.']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the test method in lab systems.']
  compliance_standards varchar [note: 'List of standards (e.g., GxP, ISO 13485) the method complies with.']
  cost_per_test decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to run a single test using this method.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test method record was first created.']
  detection_limit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Lowest analyte concentration reliably detected by the method.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the SOP or validation documentation for the method.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the method becomes officially usable.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the method is retired or must be re‑validated.']
  instrument_model varchar [note: 'Model of the instrument required to execute the method.']
  is_clia_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the method is CLIA‑certified.']
  is_fda_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the method complies with FDA 21 CFR Part 11.']
  is_gxp_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the method meets GxP requirements.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date the method was last reviewed for relevance and compliance.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot identifier for the assay kit.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that produces the assay kit or reagents.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Vendor‑assigned part number for the assay kit.']
  method_owner varchar [note: 'Department or team responsible for the method.']
  method_type varchar [note: 'Category of the method (e.g., PCR, NGS, Microarray).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the test method.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special considerations.']
  regulatory_approval_number varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the regulatory authority for the approved method.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory agency that granted approval.']
  requires_training boolean [note: 'Indicates whether personnel must complete specific training to use the method.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between scheduled method reviews.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for using the method in clinical testing.']
  sensitivity decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of true positives correctly identified (percentage).']
  specificity decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of true negatives correctly identified (percentage).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the method.']
  training_version varchar [note: 'Version of the training curriculum associated with the method.']
  turnaround_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical time from sample receipt to result reporting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit associated with the detection limit (e.g., copies/mL).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the test method record.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date the method was last validated.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation state of the method.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the method specification.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for test_method. Referenced by test_method_id.'
}

  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the audit report.']
  total_nonconformities varchar [note: 'Total number of non-conformities identified.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit report record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit_report. Referenced by report_id.'
}

  audit_objectives varchar [note: 'Key objectives and goals that the audit program aims to achieve.']
  compliance_standards varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or industry standards the audit program addresses. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GxP|ISO13485|FDA21CFRPart11|CLIA|CAP|HIPAA|GDPR — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit program record was first created in the system.']

  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the program indicating whether it is internal, external, or a combination.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the audit program based on impact and likelihood.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the audit program – internal, external, or both.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit program.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status, such as why a program is retired or pending.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the audit program record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the audit program record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the audit program definition, incremented on each change.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the audit program record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit_program. Referenced by program_id.'
}

Table quality.investigation {

  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned corrective actions to address the root cause.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the investigation record was initially created in the system.']

  investigation_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the investigation, used in reports and tracking.']
  investigation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the investigation indicating its origin or nature.']

  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the identified root cause for the issue under investigation.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Impact severity assigned to the investigation based on risk assessment.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investigation.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title summarizing the investigation purpose or issue.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the investigation record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when verification of corrective actions was performed.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of verification that corrective actions were effective.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for investigation. Referenced by investigation_id.'
}
