  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating reflecting potential impact on product quality or compliance.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation formally began.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investigation.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title of the investigation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the investigation record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for internal_investigation. Referenced by internal_investigation_id.'
}

Table regulatory.submission {
  submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory submission record. Primary key for the submission entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each regulatory submission incurs significant costs (fees, consultant time, document preparation) allocated to specific regulatory affairs cost centers. Required for submission cost tracking, budget v']
  lead_regulatory_affairs_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Every regulatory submission requires an accountable regulatory affairs professional as lead. Critical for submission strategy execution, agency interactions, workload planning, and regulatory affairs ']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.opportunity. Business justification: Major regulatory submissions (510k, PMA, CE Mark) are commercial milestones tracked in sales forecasts. Sales teams monitor submission status and target decision dates to forecast product launch timin']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory authority formally accepted the submission for substantive review. Marks the start of the official review clock for submissions with defined review timelines.']
  adverse_event_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether post-market adverse event reporting obligations apply to this submission. True if MDR, vigilance, or adverse event reporting is required, False otherwise.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory authority granted approval, clearance, or market authorization for the submission. Null if the submission is not yet approved or was denied.']
  clinical_data_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether clinical performance data or clinical evidence is required as part of the submission. True if clinical studies or clinical performance evaluation is mandatory, False otherwise.']
  clinical_study_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier(s) for the clinical studies or clinical performance evaluations included in the submission. May include ClinicalTrials.gov NCT numbers, internal study codes, or published literature references.']
  competent_authority varchar [note: 'The specific competent authority or notified body responsible for reviewing and approving the submission within the target jurisdiction. For EU IVDR submissions, identifies the notified body number and name.']
  correspondence_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or link to the log of all correspondence exchanged with the regulatory authority regarding this submission (e.g., information requests, deficiency letters, responses, meeting minutes). Typically managed in Veeva Vault or document control system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this submission record was first created in the regulatory data system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.']
  device_classification varchar [note: 'The regulatory risk classification assigned to the device or IVD under the target jurisdiction\'s classification system (e.g., FDA Class I/II/III, IVDR Class A/B/C/D, GHTF/IMDRF risk class). Determines the level of regulatory control and submission pathway.']
  dossier_reference varchar [note: 'The internal or external reference number for the regulatory dossier or technical file associated with this submission. Links to the controlled document set in Veeva Vault or other document management system.']
  expedited_review_granted boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the regulatory authority granted expedited, priority, or breakthrough designation review status. True if granted, False if denied or not applicable.']
  expedited_review_requested boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether expedited, priority, or breakthrough designation review was requested from the regulatory authority. True if expedited review was requested, False otherwise.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The regulatory submission fee amount paid or payable to the regulatory authority for processing the submission. Expressed in the currency of the target jurisdiction.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the submission fee amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  gmp_certification_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether GMP or ISO 13485 certification is required as part of the submission. True if quality system certification must be demonstrated, False otherwise.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'The intended use or indications for use statement for the product as declared in the regulatory submission. Defines the clinical or research purpose, target population, and claims for the IVD or genomic product.']
  iso_13485_certificate_number varchar [note: 'The certificate number for the organization\'s ISO 13485 quality management system certification, if applicable and required for the submission. Null if not required or not yet obtained.']
  labeling_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether product labeling (IFU, package insert, device labels) has been verified for compliance with target jurisdiction requirements. True if labeling review is complete and compliant, False otherwise.']
  language varchar [note: 'The primary language in which the regulatory submission dossier is prepared and submitted to the authority. Reflects jurisdiction-specific language requirements (e.g., English for FDA, local language for EU member states).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this submission record was last updated or modified. Audit trail field for tracking changes and data lineage.']
  local_representative_name varchar [note: 'The name of the authorized representative or legal manufacturer representative appointed in the target jurisdiction, if required. Null if no representative is required or not yet appointed.']
  local_representative_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the target jurisdiction requires the appointment of a local authorized representative or legal manufacturer representative for the submission. True if required, False otherwise.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who last modified this submission record. Audit trail field for accountability and compliance.']
  notified_body_number varchar [note: 'For EU CE-IVD and IVDR submissions, the four-digit identification number assigned to the notified body conducting conformity assessment. Null for non-EU submissions or self-declared conformity.']
  post_market_surveillance_plan varchar [note: 'Reference to the post-market surveillance plan or post-market clinical follow-up plan submitted as part of the regulatory dossier. Required for IVDR and certain FDA submissions.']
  predicate_device varchar [note: 'For FDA 510(k) submissions, the legally marketed device to which substantial equivalence is claimed. Includes the predicate device name and 510(k) number. Null for non-510(k) submissions.']
  priority varchar [note: 'The business priority level assigned to this regulatory submission. Reflects strategic importance, revenue impact, or time-to-market urgency (critical, high, medium, low).']
  product_scope varchar [note: 'A description of the product(s) or device(s) covered by this regulatory submission. Identifies the IVD assay, sequencing platform, reagent kit, instrument, or gene-editing tool that is the subject of the filing.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'The device registration or listing number assigned by the regulatory authority upon approval or clearance (e.g., FDA establishment registration number, EUDAMED UDI-DI, Health Canada device license number). Null if not yet assigned.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory framework or legislation under which the submission is made (e.g., FDA 21 CFR Part 807, IVDR 2017/746, IVDD 98/79/EC, Medical Device Regulation 2017/745, Health Canada MDEL). Defines the legal basis for the submission.']
  regulatory_pathway varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory pathway or route to market classification under which the submission is filed. Provides additional granularity beyond submission type (e.g., 510(k) Traditional, 510(k) Special, 510(k) Abbreviated, Class I exempt, Class II, Class III PMA, IVDR Class A/B/C/D).']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory submission was officially filed with the target regulatory authority. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the submission lifecycle.']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known regulatory submission number or dossier reference assigned by the submitting organization or regulatory authority (e.g., FDA 510(k) number K123456, PMA number P210001, CE-IVD technical file reference).']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory submission in the approval workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through regulatory authority review to final decision (draft, submitted, under review, additional information requested, approved, cleared, denied, withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|additional_info_requested|approved|cleared|denied|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'The regulatory pathway classification for the submission. Indicates the type of regulatory filing made to the health authority (e.g., FDA 510(k) premarket notification, PMA premarket approval, De Novo classification request, CE-IVD technical file, IVDR registration, EMA dossier, ICH CTD).']
  target_agency varchar [note: 'The primary health authority or regulatory agency to which the submission is directed (e.g., FDA, EMA, Health Canada, TGA, PMDA, ANVISA, NMPA). Identifies the competent authority responsible for reviewing and approving the submission.']
  target_decision_date date [note: 'The planned or regulatory-mandated target date by which the authority is expected to issue a decision on the submission. Used for tracking review timelines and SLA compliance.']
  target_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The country, region, or trading bloc for which regulatory approval is sought (e.g., USA, European Union, Canada, Australia, Japan, Brazil, China). Defines the geographic scope of the regulatory submission.']
  udi_di varchar [note: 'The Unique Device Identification Device Identifier (UDI-DI) assigned to the product covered by this submission. Required for FDA and EU IVDR submissions. Null if not yet assigned.']

  Note: 'Master record for every regulatory submission made to a health authority — FDA 510(k), PMA, De Novo, CE-IVD technical file, EMA dossier, ICH CTD, IVDR registration, and country-specific IVD filings. Captures submission type, target agency, target jurisdiction (country/region/trading bloc), submission date, dossier reference, regulatory pathway classification, product scope, predicate device (for 510(k)), lead regulatory affairs owner, and current lifecycle status. Includes jurisdiction-level metadata: applicable regulatory framework, competent authority, IVD classification system, language requirements, and local representative requirement. SSOT for all regulatory filing identities across global jurisdictions.'
}

Table regulatory.submission_event {
  submission_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each discrete regulatory submission event. Primary key for the submission event record.']
  quality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.quality_assessment. Business justification: Each submission event (eCTD sequence, amendment, response to deficiency) undergoes quality assessment before transmission to verify package integrity, document completeness, and technical validation. ']
  submission_contact_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Submission contact must be a real employee for agency correspondence, deficiency responses, and accountability. Links submission events to qualified regulatory affairs personnel with appropriate train']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory dossier package that this submission event belongs to. Links to the master dossier record containing all submission materials.']
  actual_decision_date date [note: 'Actual date when the regulatory agency issued a final decision (approval, rejection, or other disposition) on this submission. Nullable if decision is pending.']
  agency_receipt_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Unique confirmation or tracking number issued by the regulatory agency upon receipt of the submission. Provides proof of delivery and agency acknowledgment.']
  agency_receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory agency officially acknowledged receipt of the submission. Used to establish filing date and review clock start.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Official approval or clearance number issued by the regulatory agency upon successful submission (e.g., FDA 510(k) number, PMA number, CE certificate number). Nullable if not approved.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this submission event record in the system. Audit trail for accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this submission event record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  decision_letter_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document link to the official agency decision letter or notification. Links to controlled document repository.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Final regulatory decision rendered by the agency on this submission (e.g., approved, approved with conditions, rejected, withdrawn, pending).']
  deficiency_letter_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory agency issued a deficiency letter or additional information request for this submission. Nullable if no deficiency was issued.']
  deficiency_letter_received_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a deficiency letter or additional information request was received from the agency in response to this submission.']
  deficiency_response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline date by which the sponsor must respond to the agency deficiency letter. Nullable if no deficiency was issued.']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned to each eCTD submission within a regulatory application. Used to maintain chronological order of submissions per ICH eCTD specification.']
  fee_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory user fee was paid to the agency. Required for establishing submission acceptance and review clock start.']
  fee_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory user fee payment associated with this submission event.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this submission event record in the system. Audit trail for accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this submission event record was last modified in the system. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  regulatory_correspondent_name varchar [note: 'Name of the official regulatory correspondent or authorized representative designated for agency communications regarding this submission.']
  review_clock_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days allocated by the agency for review of this submission under applicable regulatory timelines (e.g., 90 days for FDA 510(k), 180 days for PMA).']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the submission was officially transmitted to the regulatory agency. Represents the business event timestamp for the submission action.']
  submission_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Regulatory user fee amount charged by the agency for processing this submission. Amount in the currency specified by submission_fee_currency_code.']
  submission_fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the submission fee (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'Technical format or standard used to structure the submission package (e.g., eCTD, NeeS-R, paper, PDF, XML).']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Technical channel or mechanism used to transmit the submission to the regulatory agency (e.g., eCTD gateway, eSTAR, paper, electronic portal).']
  submission_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special circumstances, or internal notes related to this submission event.']
  submission_package_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the complete dossier package associated with this submission event. Links to the controlled document repository (e.g., Veeva Vault document ID).']
  submission_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this submission event within the dossier lifecycle. Tracks sequential revisions and amendments (e.g., v1.0, v1.1, v2.0).']
  submission_volume_count varchar [note: 'Number of physical or logical volumes comprising the submission package. Used for large submissions split across multiple files or binders.']
  target_decision_date date [note: 'Expected date by which the regulatory agency should issue a decision on this submission, based on statutory or performance goal timelines.']
  total_document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual documents included in this submission event. Provides a measure of submission complexity and completeness.']
  total_page_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pages across all documents in the submission package. Used for resource planning and review estimation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each discrete regulatory submission action — initial filing, amendment, supplement, response to deficiency letter, withdrawal, or resubmission. Tracks submission date, submission method (eCTD gateway, paper, eSTAR), agency receipt confirmation, submission version, and associated dossier package reference. Enables full audit trail of all submission activities per dossier.'
}

Essential for program financial']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Approved genomic tests have the reference genome build locked in their approved product specification and labeling. Changes to reference genome require regulatory variation or supplement submission to']

  variant_database_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.variant_database. Business justification: Approved genomic tests may have specific variant database versions locked in their approved specifications, particularly for oncology panels. Database updates require change control and regulatory not']
  annual_report_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next annual report, periodic safety update report (PSUR), or post-market surveillance report is due to the regulatory authority.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory approval, clearance, or registration was officially granted by the health authority.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Official approval, clearance, or registration number issued by the regulatory authority. Examples: FDA 510(k) number (K123456), PMA number (P123456), CE certificate number, IVDR certificate number, EMA authorization number.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory approval. Indicates whether the approval is currently valid and in force.']
  approval_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory approval or clearance granted. Indicates the regulatory pathway used for market authorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 510k_clearance|pma_approval|ce_ivd_certificate|ivdr_certificate|ema_marketing_authorization|country_registration|de_novo_classification|humanitarian_device_exemption — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approved_indications varchar [note: 'Specific clinical indications, disease states, or genetic markers that the product is approved to detect, diagnose, or analyze.']
  clia_complexity varchar [note: 'CLIA complexity categorization for the approved test: waived, moderate complexity, or high complexity. Determines laboratory requirements for performing the test.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory approval record was first created in the system.']
  device_classification varchar [note: 'Risk-based classification of the medical device or IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) according to regulatory framework. FDA uses Class I/II/III; EU IVDR uses Class A/B/C/D. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: class_i|class_ii|class_iii|class_a|class_b|class_c|class_d — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document control number for the official approval letter or certificate stored in the document management system (Veeva Vault).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the regulatory approval becomes legally effective and the product may be marketed in the jurisdiction.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory approval expires and requires renewal or re-certification. Null for approvals without expiration.']
  holder_address varchar [note: 'Registered business address of the approval holder as recorded with the regulatory authority.']
  holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the organization or entity that holds the regulatory approval. The manufacturer or sponsor responsible for the product.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Official intended use or indications for use statement as approved by the regulatory authority. Describes the clinical purpose and target population.']
  jurisdiction_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the jurisdiction where the approval is valid. Examples: USA, GBR, DEU, AUS, CAN.']
  jurisdiction_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or regulatory zone for the approval. Examples: European Union, North America, Asia-Pacific. Used for multi-country approvals like CE-IVD.']
  labeling_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific labeling, instructions for use (IFU), or package insert requirements mandated by the regulatory authority.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection or audit conducted by the health authority related to this approval.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this regulatory approval record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory approval record was last modified or updated.']
  next_renewal_date date [note: 'Date by which the next renewal application must be submitted or the approval must be renewed. Null if no renewal is required.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or internal observations related to the regulatory approval. Free-text field for regulatory affairs team use.']
  post_market_surveillance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether post-market surveillance, post-approval studies, or periodic safety update reports are required as a condition of approval.']
  predicate_device varchar [note: 'For 510(k) clearances, the legally marketed device to which substantial equivalence was demonstrated. Includes predicate device name and 510(k) number.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Internal product code or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for the approved product. Links the approval to the product master data.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Commercial or trade name of the product as approved by the regulatory authority. The name under which the product is marketed.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or health agency that granted the approval. Examples: FDA (U.S. Food and Drug Administration), EMA (European Medicines Agency), MHRA (Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency), TGA (Therapeutic Goods Administration).']
  regulatory_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary regulatory affairs contact person for this approval.']
  regulatory_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary regulatory affairs contact person responsible for this approval.']
  regulatory_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary regulatory affairs contact person for this approval.']
  regulatory_pathway varchar [note: 'Regulatory submission pathway or route used to obtain the approval. Examples: 510(k) premarket notification, PMA premarket approval, De Novo classification, CE conformity assessment.']
  renewal_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which the approval must be renewed. Null if no renewal is required.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the approval requires periodic renewal or re-certification. True if renewal is required, False if approval is perpetual.']
  ruo_ivd_designation varchar [note: 'Designation indicating whether the product is approved for clinical diagnostic use (IVD), research use only (RUO), or as a laboratory developed test (LDT).']
  technical_file_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the technical file or design dossier submitted to support the approval. Links to the regulatory documentation repository.']

  Note: 'Master record of regulatory approvals, clearances, and registrations granted by health authorities — FDA 510(k) clearance, PMA approval, CE-IVD certificate, IVDR certificate of conformity, EMA marketing authorization, and country-specific IVD registrations. Captures approval date, approval number, notified body or agency, approved indications, conditions of approval, expiry date, and renewal obligations. SSOT for all granted regulatory authorizations.'
}

Critical for managing FDA/EMA interactions, meeting res']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fda|ema|notified_body|clia|cap|health_canada|tga|pmda|nmpa|anvisa|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  correspondence_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the correspondence based on its purpose and stage in the regulatory lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_submission|deficiency_letter|additional_information_request|interactive_review_meeting|agency_question|agency_response|scientific_advice|notified_body_query|post_approval_correspondence|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the correspondence has been escalated to senior management or executive leadership due to complexity or risk.']
  impact_on_submission_strategy varchar [note: 'Assessment of how the agency feedback or correspondence outcome affects the overall regulatory submission strategy, timelines, or approach.']
  interaction_subtype varchar [note: 'Detailed subtype of the interaction, providing granular classification (e.g., FDA Q-Sub for quality questions, Pre-Sub for pre-submission meetings, AIR for Additional Information Request). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: q_sub|pre_sub|pre_application_consultation|deficiency_response|air_response|meeting_request|meeting_minutes|clarification_request|withdrawal_notice|approval_letter|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential internal notes or commentary regarding the correspondence, strategy considerations, or follow-up actions.']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: usa|eu|canada|australia|japan|china|brazil|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  key_questions_posed varchar [note: 'Summary of the primary questions or issues raised by the sponsor or agency during the correspondence or meeting.']
  meeting_date date [note: 'Date of the regulatory meeting or teleconference, if the correspondence relates to a scheduled interaction (e.g., Pre-Sub meeting, EMA scientific advice session).']
  meeting_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the meeting format (e.g., FDA Type A/B/C meeting, EMA scientific advice, notified body pre-application consultation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_submission|interactive_review|type_c|type_b|scientific_advice|pre_application|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified the correspondence record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the correspondence record.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the correspondence based on urgency, regulatory impact, or business criticality.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Internal product code or SKU identifying the specific product or product family related to the correspondence.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Name of the IVD product, sequencing platform, or assay that is the subject of the correspondence.']
  regulatory_pathway varchar [note: 'The regulatory pathway or submission type associated with the correspondence (e.g., FDA 510(k), PMA, De Novo, CE-IVD, IVDR, EMA Centralized, LDT, RUO). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 510k|pma|de_novo|ce_ivd|ivdr|ema_centralized|ldt|ruo|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which a response to the agency correspondence is required (e.g., deficiency letter response deadline, AIR due date).']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the response to the agency correspondence, tracking whether action is pending, in progress, or completed.']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor\'s response to the agency correspondence was officially submitted.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief subject line or title summarizing the topic of the correspondence.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created the correspondence record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of all formal communications and interactions with regulatory agencies — including pre-submission engagements (FDA Q-Sub/Pre-Sub meetings, EMA scientific advice, notified body pre-application consultations, CLIA/CAP consultation interactions), FDA deficiency letters, additional information requests (AIRs), interactive review meetings, EMA questions, notified body queries, and agency responses. Captures correspondence type, interaction subtype (pre-submission, during-review, post-approval), direction (inbound/outbound), agency contact, meeting date, agenda topics, key questions posed, agency feedback received, impact on submission strategy, due date for response, response status, and linked submission. SSOT for all regulatory agency dialogue — from proactive pre-submission engagement through post-approval interactions.'
}

Table regulatory.document {
  document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory document record. Primary key.']

Business justification: Regulatory document creation, review, and maintenance costs are allocated to regulatory affairs or quality cost centers. Enables cost tracking for document management operations and supports activity-']
  dpia_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.dpia. Business justification: Regulatory documents containing PHI/PII (clinical study reports, patient-level data, genomic datasets) require DPIA for GDPR Article 35 and HIPAA compliance. Essential for privacy impact assessment in']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or device to which this regulatory document applies. Links document to specific product master record.']
  metadata_schema_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.metadata_schema. Business justification: Regulatory documents (eCTD modules, clinical study reports) must conform to structured metadata schemas (CDISC, ICH standards) for submission validation and agency acceptance. Critical for document li']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: GxP compliance requires every regulatory document to have an accountable employee owner for lifecycle management, audit trails, and regulatory inspections. Document ownership is fundamental to 21 CFR ']
  quality_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.quality_rule. Business justification: Regulatory documents undergo automated quality checks (version control, approval status, completeness, signature verification) before submission. Quality rules enforce regulatory document standards pe']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory submission dossier (FDA 510(k), PMA, CE-IVD technical file, etc.) to which this document is attached or referenced.']
  superseded_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the previous document version that this current version replaces. Maintains document lineage and version history.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the document received final approval and was authorized for use.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state indicating whether the document has received required sign-offs from designated approvers per the approval matrix.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who provided final approval for the document to become effective.']
  archive_location varchar [note: 'Physical or electronic location where the document is archived after it becomes obsolete (e.g., Veeva Vault archive folder, offsite storage facility).']
  audit_trail_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of key audit events for this document (creation, approvals, revisions, access) maintained in Veeva Vault for compliance traceability.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who authored or prepared the current version of the document.']
  checksum_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'SHA-256 cryptographic hash of the document file, ensuring integrity and detecting unauthorized modifications.']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Technical File|Clinical Evaluation Report|Performance Evaluation Report|Risk Management File|Instructions For Use|Labeling Artwork|Regulatory SOP|Design History File|Device Master Record|Certificate of Analysis|Declaration of Conformity|Essential Requirements Checklist — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the approved document becomes officially effective and enforceable within the quality management system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the document is scheduled to expire or become obsolete if not renewed or superseded.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Electronic file format of the document as stored in Veeva Vault (e.g., PDF for controlled final versions, DOCX for working drafts).']
  file_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the document file in megabytes, used for storage management and submission package size planning.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the regulatory jurisdiction or market for which this document is prepared and applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|GBR|DEU|FRA|ITA|ESP|JPN|CHN|AUS|BRA|IND|KOR|SGP|CHE|NLD|SWE|NOR|DNK|FIN|BEL|AUT|IRL|NZL|MEX|ZAF|ISR|TUR|POL|CZE|HUN|ROU|GRC|PRT|HRV|SVN|SVK|BGR|LTU|LVA|EST|CYP|MLT|LUX — 44 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the primary language in which the document is written (e.g., en, de, fr, es, ja).']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the document in its controlled lifecycle from creation through approval, effectiveness, and eventual obsolescence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|In Review|Approved|Effective|Superseded|Obsolete|Archived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory document record was last modified in the data system.']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pages in the document, used for completeness verification and submission package assembly.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Governing regulatory framework or standard under which the document is prepared and maintained (e.g., FDA premarket submission, IVDR technical documentation, ISO quality standard).']
  related_document_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of document numbers or identifiers for related or cross-referenced regulatory documents (e.g., supporting SOPs, risk assessments, test reports).']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the document must be retained after obsolescence per regulatory requirements and internal retention policy.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the document must undergo periodic review to ensure continued accuracy, relevance, and regulatory compliance.']
  revision_description varchar [note: 'Summary of changes made in the current version compared to the previous version. Documents the nature and scope of revisions.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional granularity within the document type (e.g., IFU-Professional, IFU-Patient, CER-Initial, CER-Update).']
  title varchar [note: 'Full official title of the regulatory document as it appears in the controlled document header.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether personnel must complete formal training on this document before performing related activities (True) or not (False).']
  vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault system of record. External system key for document control integration.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the document following major.minor versioning convention. Incremented per change control procedures.']

  Note: 'Master record for controlled regulatory documents managed in Veeva Vault — technical files, clinical evaluation reports (CER), performance evaluation reports (PER), risk management files (ISO 14971), instructions for use (IFU), labeling artwork, and regulatory SOPs. Captures document type, Veeva Vault document ID, version, effective date, document owner, approval status, and applicable submission references. Integrates with Veeva Vault as the system of record for document control.'
}

Table regulatory.labeling {
  labeling_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the labeling record. Primary key for the labeling master data product.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: labeling tracks product labeling and IFU assets subject to regulatory control. Each labeling version is associated with a specific regulatory approval (the approved product for which labeling is creat']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the change control record that authorized this labeling version. Links to the quality management system change control process.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Product labeling for genomic IVD tests must state the reference genome build used (e.g., \'GRCh38\'). Required for IVD regulatory compliance under FDA 21 CFR 809 and EU IVDR Annex I.']
  pharmacogenomics_marker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.pharmacogenomics_marker. Business justification: PGx test labeling must specify which pharmacogenomic markers are detected and their clinical associations per FDA 21 CFR 809.10. Required for IFU and professional labeling compliance.']
  product_sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU that this labeling asset applies to. Links labeling to specific reagent kits, instruments, consumables, or IVD products.']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Batch-specific labeling (lot number, expiry date, storage conditions) must reference the actual production batch for traceability. Required for genomics IVD products to comply with 21 CFR 820 and EU M']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the labeling asset received regulatory approval from the applicable authority (FDA, Notified Body, or internal Quality Assurance). Null if not yet approved.']
  authorized_representative varchar [note: 'Name and address of the authorized representative in the European Union, if applicable. Required for non-EU manufacturers marketing IVD products in the EU under IVDR.']
  barcode_type varchar [note: 'Type of barcode or machine-readable identifier printed on the labeling. Supports supply chain automation and inventory management.']
  ce_mark_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this labeling must display the CE-IVD mark for European market distribution. True for IVD products marketed in the EU under IVDR; false for RUO or non-EU products.']
  change_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the change that resulted in this labeling version. Major changes typically require regulatory notification or re-approval; minor changes may be implemented under quality system authority; administrative changes are non-substantive corrections.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification and rationale for the labeling change. Documents why the revision was necessary, including regulatory requirements, safety updates, product improvements, or error corrections.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of the specific changes made in this labeling version compared to the previous version. Supports regulatory submission documentation and internal change tracking.']
  checksum_md5 varchar [note: 'MD5 hash of the labeling file content. Ensures file integrity and detects unauthorized modifications in the controlled document environment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labeling record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  customer_support_contact varchar [note: 'Customer support phone number, email, or website URL printed on the labeling for technical assistance and inquiries.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique document control number assigned by the quality management system. Used for traceability and change control across the labeling lifecycle.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this labeling version becomes effective and must be used for product distribution. Marks the start of the labeling lifecycle in the market.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this labeling version is no longer valid for use. After this date, the labeling must be replaced with a newer version. Null for currently active labeling with no planned obsolescence.']
  expiry_date_format varchar [note: 'Specification for the expiry date format that will be printed on this labeling. Defines date representation (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD, MM/YYYY) for product shelf-life indication.']
  fda_establishment_identifier varchar [note: 'FDA Establishment Identifier (FEI) or registration number that must appear on the labeling for US-marketed products. Required for IVD products under FDA jurisdiction.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Digital file format of the labeling asset. Supports document control and publishing workflows.']
  file_path varchar [note: 'File system path or document management system URI where the digital labeling asset is stored. Links to the controlled document repository (e.g., Veeva Vault).']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the digital labeling file in bytes. Used for storage management and document transfer validation.']
  hazard_symbols varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of hazard symbols or pictograms that must appear on the labeling. Supports chemical safety and regulatory compliance for reagents and consumables.']
  impacted_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of market regions or countries affected by this labeling change. Used to coordinate multi-market labeling updates and regulatory notifications.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'Actual date on which this labeling version was implemented in production and distribution systems. May differ from effective_date due to operational lead times or inventory depletion strategies.']
  intended_use_statement varchar [note: 'Regulatory intended use statement for the product as documented in the labeling. Critical for IVD and RUO product classification and regulatory compliance.']
  labeling_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the labeling asset. Distinguishes between physical labels, package inserts, Instructions for Use (IFU) documents, carton artwork, digital labeling content, and Safety Data Sheets (SDS).']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language variant of the labeling content. Supports multi-market and multi-lingual labeling requirements.']
  lot_number_format varchar [note: 'Specification for the lot number format that will be printed on this labeling. Defines the structure and content of lot identifiers for traceability and quality control.']
  manufacturer_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the manufacturing facility as it appears on the labeling. Required for regulatory compliance and customer contact.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the manufacturer as it appears on the labeling. Required for regulatory compliance and traceability.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who last modified this labeling record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labeling record was last modified in the system. Part of the audit trail for regulatory compliance and change control.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which regulatory notification was submitted to the applicable authority for this labeling change. Null if no notification was required or if not yet submitted.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this labeling change requires formal notification to regulatory authorities (FDA, Notified Body, or other competent authority). True for major changes; false for minor or administrative changes.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory approval status of the labeling asset. Tracks progression from draft through regulatory review to approval, effectiveness, and eventual obsolescence.']
  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'Storage temperature and environmental conditions specified on the labeling. Critical for reagent stability and product quality. Examples: 2-8°C, -20°C, room temperature.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title or name of the labeling asset. Human-readable identifier used in regulatory submissions and internal documentation.']
  udi_di varchar [note: 'Unique Device Identification Device Identifier assigned to this product and printed on the labeling. Required for medical devices and IVD products under FDA UDI regulations.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the labeling asset. Tracks revisions and updates to labeling content over time. Format typically follows semantic versioning or document control numbering scheme.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who created this labeling record in the system. Supports audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master record for all product labeling and IFU (Instructions for Use) assets subject to regulatory control, including their change history. Covers instrument labels, reagent kit inserts, IFU documents, carton artwork, and digital labeling. Captures labeling version, applicable markets/jurisdictions, language variants, regulatory approval status, effective date, linked product SKU, and full change history — including change reason, change classification (major/minor), impacted markets, regulatory notification requirements, change control reference, and implementation date. Tracks labeling lifecycle and compliance with FDA 21 CFR Part 801, EU IVDR Annex I, and global jurisdiction-specific labeling requirements.'
}

Table regulatory.adverse_event_report {
  adverse_event_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the adverse event report. Primary key for post-market surveillance tracking.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: adverse_event_report tracks post-market adverse events for approved products. Each AER is associated with a specific regulatory approval (the approved product involved in the event). Adding regulatory']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated CAPA record in the quality management system. Links adverse event to corrective and preventive action workflow.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Adverse event investigation and reporting costs are allocated to regulatory affairs or quality assurance cost centers. Required for pharmacovigilance program cost tracking and quality operations finan']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: AER authorship tracking is mandatory for regulatory compliance, FDA MDR reporting, and audit trails. Links AER creation to qualified employees with appropriate training in adverse event reporting and ']
  quality_issue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.quality_issue. Business justification: Adverse event data quality issues (incomplete patient information, missing device identifiers, inconsistent event descriptions) trigger investigation and remediation per pharmacovigilance requirements']
  veeva_vault_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the controlled regulatory document in Veeva Vault system. Links to the official regulatory submission package.']
  affected_lot_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of all lot numbers affected by the adverse event or corrective action. Used for recall scope determination.']
  affected_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic markets or jurisdictions affected by the adverse event or corrective action (e.g., USA, DEU, GBR, FRA).']
  agency_acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory agency acknowledged receipt of the adverse event report.']
  agency_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or tracking identifier assigned by the regulatory agency (FDA, EMA, competent authority) upon receipt of the report.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the adverse event report and all associated corrective actions were closed. Marks completion of the post-market surveillance lifecycle.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action taken to address the adverse event and prevent recurrence. Links to CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) system.']
  corrective_action_type varchar [note: 'Type of corrective action taken in response to the adverse event. Recall, FSCA (Field Safety Corrective Action), FSN (Field Safety Notice), product modification, labeling change, or no action required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adverse event report record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  customer_notification_date date [note: 'Date when affected customers were notified of the adverse event or corrective action.']
  customer_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of customer notification for field safety corrective actions or recalls. Tracks whether affected customers have been notified.']
  device_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number or batch number of the device involved in the adverse event. Critical for traceability and recall scope determination.']
  device_product_code varchar [note: 'Product code or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) of the device involved in the adverse event. Links to product master for device identification.']
  device_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the specific device unit involved in the adverse event, if applicable. Enables unit-level traceability.']
  event_date date [note: 'Date when the adverse event or serious incident occurred. Principal business event timestamp for post-market surveillance.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the adverse event, serious incident, or field safety issue. Includes patient/user impact, device behavior, and circumstances of the event.']
  follow_up_status varchar [note: 'Status of any follow-up reporting or supplemental information requested by the regulatory agency.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction or competent authority to which the report was submitted (e.g., USA-FDA, DEU-BfArM, GBR-MHRA). Uses 3-letter country codes with authority suffix.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the adverse event report record. Audit trail for accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adverse event report record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates.']
  patient_impact varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the impact on the patient or user. Death, serious injury, malfunction with no injury, or no impact. Determines reportability and urgency.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date when regulatory authorities were notified of the corrective action (e.g., recall notification, FSCA notification).']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the adverse event report. External reference number used in regulatory submissions and agency correspondence.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the adverse event report in the post-market surveillance workflow.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the adverse event report. MDR (Medical Device Report) for FDA filings, serious incident for EU IVDR, FSCA (Field Safety Corrective Action), FSN (Field Safety Notice), recall, or market withdrawal.']
  reportability_determination varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the event meets regulatory criteria for mandatory reporting to health authorities (FDA, EMA, competent authorities).']
  reportability_rationale varchar [note: 'Business justification for the reportability determination. Documents the regulatory assessment and decision-making process.']
  reporter_contact varchar [note: 'Contact information (email or phone) for the individual who reported the adverse event. Used for follow-up inquiries.']
  reporter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or organization who reported the adverse event. May be confidential depending on jurisdiction.']
  reporter_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party who reported the adverse event. Customer, field service engineer, internal QA, regulatory authority, or healthcare provider.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the adverse event based on investigation. Links to quality investigation and CAPA processes.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the root cause for trending and analytics. Design defect, manufacturing defect, labeling issue, user error, environmental factor, or unknown.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the adverse event report was submitted to the regulatory agency (FDA, EMA, competent authority).']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Method used to submit the report to the regulatory agency. Electronic (e.g., FDA eSubmitter), paper, or agency portal.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for the complete post-market vigilance and field safety workflow — adverse event reports, serious incident reports, field safety corrective actions (FSCA), and field safety notices (FSN). Covers FDA MDR filings, EU IVDR serious incident reports, product recalls, market withdrawals, hazard alerts, and corrective field actions. Captures event date, event description, device involved, patient/user impact, reportability determination, report submission date, agency acknowledgment, follow-up status, action type, affected lot numbers, affected markets, root cause, corrective action description, regulatory notification dates, customer notification status, and closure date. Integrates with quality CAPA and supply chain recall management. SSOT for all post-market safety signals from detection through corrective action closure.'
}

Table regulatory.post_market_surveillance {
  post_market_surveillance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the post-market surveillance plan or report record.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory approval record this PMS plan is associated with.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Post-market surveillance programs are ongoing cost centers tracking complaint monitoring, literature review, and registry participation costs. Essential for PMS program budgeting and regulatory compli']
  notified_body_id varchar [note: 'FK to regulatory.notified_body']
  responsible_person_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: PMS plans require a designated responsible person employee for regulatory accountability under MDR/IVDR. Links PMS activities to qualified employees with appropriate training, essential for regulatory']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory submission (510(k), PMA, CE technical file) that this PMS plan is linked to or was submitted as part of.']
  adverse_event_count varchar [note: 'Total number of adverse events or incidents reported for this product during the current PMS reporting period.']
  benefit_risk_assessment_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the benefit-risk analysis conducted as part of the PMS evaluation, documenting whether the product continues to meet safety and performance requirements.']
  clinical_follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) or post-market performance follow-up (PMPF) studies are required as part of this PMS plan.']
  complaint_data_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer complaint data is included as a data source in this PMS plan.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions taken or planned in response to PMS findings, including design modifications, labeling changes, or field safety actions.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective actions (design changes, labeling updates, field actions) are required based on PMS findings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PMS record was first created in the system.']
  data_sources varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of data sources used for PMS data collection: complaint data, adverse event reports, scientific literature, clinical registries, post-market clinical follow-up studies, customer feedback, field performance data.']
  device_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory risk classification of the device: Class I, IIa, IIb, III (IVDR/EU), or Class A, B, C (FDA/US). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: class_i|class_iia|class_iib|class_iii|class_a|class_b|class_c — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the controlled document or file location (e.g., Veeva Vault document ID) where the full PMS plan or report is stored.']
  field_safety_notice_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Field Safety Notice (FSN) or Field Safety Corrective Action (FSCA) has been issued for this product during the PMS period.']
  jurisdiction_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the regulatory jurisdiction where this PMS plan applies (e.g., USA, DEU, GBR, FRA).']
  last_report_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent PMS report was submitted to the regulatory authority.']
  literature_review_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether systematic scientific literature review is included as a data source in this PMS plan.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PMS record was last modified or updated.']
  next_report_due_date date [note: 'Date when the next periodic PMS report (PSUR, PMPF, or post-approval study report) is due for submission to the regulatory authority.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding this PMS plan or report, including internal observations or regulatory authority feedback.']
  plan_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this PMS plan version became effective and data collection activities commenced.']
  plan_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this PMS plan version expires or is scheduled for review and renewal. Null for ongoing plans without fixed end date.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number or identifier of the PMS plan document, used to track revisions and updates over time.']
  pmpf_study_protocol_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or identifier for the PMPF study protocol associated with this PMS plan, if applicable.']
  pms_plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the PMS plan or report, used for external reference and regulatory submissions.']
  pms_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PMS plan or report: draft (in preparation), active (ongoing data collection), under review (internal QA), approved (ready for submission), submitted (filed with authority), closed (completed), suspended (temporarily halted). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|active|under_review|approved|submitted|closed|suspended — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pms_type varchar [note: 'Type of post-market surveillance activity: PMS plan (IVDR general plan), PSUR (Periodic Safety Update Report), PMPF plan (Post-Market Performance Follow-up), post-approval study (FDA commitment), vigilance report, or field safety corrective action.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Internal or regulatory product code (SKU, catalog number, or FDA product code) for the device under surveillance.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Name of the IVD product or device covered by this PMS plan.']
  recall_initiated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a product recall or market withdrawal was initiated during the PMS period.']
  registry_data_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether clinical registry or real-world evidence registry data is included in this PMS plan.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or competent authority overseeing this PMS activity (e.g., FDA, MHRA, BfArM, ANSM).']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which PMS reports (PSUR, PMPF updates) must be generated and submitted to regulatory authorities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|biennial|event_driven|continuous — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional unit responsible for managing this PMS plan (e.g., Regulatory Affairs, Quality Assurance, Post-Market Surveillance).']
  risk_management_file_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the risk management file (per ISO 14971) that this PMS plan supports and updates with post-market data.']
  serious_adverse_event_count varchar [note: 'Number of serious adverse events (death, serious injury, or serious public health threat) reported during the current PMS reporting period.']
  technical_file_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the technical documentation or technical file that includes this PMS plan as a required component.']

  Note: 'Master record for post-market surveillance (PMS) plans and reports per product — IVDR PMS plans, periodic safety update reports (PSUR), post-market performance follow-up (PMPF) plans, and FDA post-approval study commitments. Captures PMS plan version, reporting frequency, data sources (complaint data, literature, registry), next due date, responsible owner, and regulatory submission linkage. SSOT for ongoing post-market regulatory obligations per product.'
}

Table regulatory.field_safety_action {
  field_safety_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the field safety corrective action or field safety notice record.']
  action_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Field safety actions must have an accountable employee owner for execution tracking, FDA recall management, and regulatory compliance. Links FSA to qualified employees responsible for corrective actio']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: field_safety_action tracks field safety corrective actions (FSCA) and field safety notices (FSN) for products. Each FSA is associated with a specific regulatory approval (the approved product subject ']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this field safety action to the originating CAPA record in the quality management system, establishing traceability to root cause investigation and corrective action planning.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product subject to the field safety action, linking to the master product catalog.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Field safety actions (recalls, FSNs) incur investigation, notification, and remediation costs tracked to quality or regulatory cost centers. Required for financial impact tracking, reserve estimation,']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the field safety action, typically formatted as FSA-NNNNNN for external tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the field safety action indicating progress through investigation, notification, recovery, and closure phases.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the field safety action: recall (product return), market withdrawal (removal from market), safety alert (hazard notification), field correction (on-site fix), customer notification (informational), or product removal (permanent discontinuation).']
  affected_lot_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of lot numbers or batch identifiers affected by the field safety action, enabling precise traceability and targeted recovery.']
  affected_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes representing the geographic markets where the affected product was distributed and the field safety action applies.']
  affected_product_code varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) or catalog number of the affected product for precise identification in supply chain and inventory systems.']
  affected_product_name varchar [note: 'Commercial name of the product affected by the field safety action, captured for reporting and customer communication purposes.']
  affected_serial_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or range of device serial numbers affected by the field safety action, used for instrument-level traceability.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the field safety action was formally closed after all corrective activities, customer notifications, and product recovery were completed and verified.']
  competent_authority_notification_date date [note: 'Date when national competent authorities in affected EU member states were notified of the field safety action via the European Database on Medical Devices (EUDAMED).']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective actions taken to address the root cause, including product modifications, process changes, customer instructions, and preventive measures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this field safety action record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  customer_notification_date date [note: 'Date when affected customers were first notified of the field safety action, marking the start of customer communication activities.']
  customer_notification_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to notify affected customers of the field safety action: email, postal mail, phone, fax, web portal, field representative visit, or multiple channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|postal_mail|phone|fax|web_portal|field_representative|multiple — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  customer_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of customer notification activities indicating whether all affected customers have been successfully contacted and informed of the field safety action.']
  customers_notified_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customers who have been successfully notified of the field safety action, tracked for regulatory reporting and effectiveness monitoring.']
  effectiveness_check_date date [note: 'Date when the effectiveness check was performed to verify that corrective actions successfully resolved the issue and prevented recurrence.']
  effectiveness_check_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the effectiveness check assessment: effective (corrective action successful), partially effective (additional actions needed), ineffective (corrective action failed), or pending (check not yet completed).']
  effectiveness_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal effectiveness check is required to verify that the field safety action successfully addressed the root cause and prevented recurrence.']
  estimated_completion_date date [note: 'Projected date for completing all field safety action activities including customer notification, product recovery, and regulatory reporting, used for planning and regulatory commitment tracking.']
  fda_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) was formally notified of the field safety action, as required under 21 CFR 806 for medical device recalls.']
  fda_recall_number varchar [note: 'Official FDA-assigned recall number for tracking in the FDA recall database, typically formatted as Z-YYYY-NNNN where Z is the recall class.']
  financial_impact_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total financial impact of the field safety action including product recovery costs, customer credits, regulatory penalties, and operational expenses, captured in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  fsn_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Field Safety Notice document issued to customers and regulatory authorities, used for cross-referencing in regulatory correspondence.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'FDA recall classification indicating the severity of the health hazard: Class I (serious injury or death), Class II (temporary or reversible health consequences), Class III (unlikely to cause adverse health consequences).']
  initiation_date date [note: 'Date when the field safety action was formally initiated and documented, marking the start of the corrective action process.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this field safety action record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change control requirements for regulatory compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special circumstances, lessons learned, or supplementary information relevant to the field safety action that does not fit structured fields.']
  notified_body_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the EU Notified Body was notified of the field safety action, as required under IVDR Article 83 for CE-marked in vitro diagnostic devices.']
  recovery_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of affected units successfully recovered or corrected, calculated as (units_recovered / units_distributed) * 100, used to track field safety action effectiveness.']
  regulatory_contact_email varchar [note: 'Business email address of the regulatory contact for agency correspondence regarding the field safety action.']
  regulatory_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the regulatory affairs contact person responsible for regulatory authority communications and submissions related to this field safety action.']
  root_cause_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of the root cause analysis findings that led to the field safety action, including failure mode, contributing factors, and investigation conclusions.']
  units_distributed_count varchar [note: 'Total number of affected product units that were distributed to customers and are subject to the field safety action.']
  units_recovered_count varchar [note: 'Number of affected product units that have been successfully recovered, returned, or corrected as part of the field safety action.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for field safety corrective actions (FSCA) and field safety notices (FSN) — product recalls, market withdrawals, hazard alerts, and corrective field actions initiated in response to safety signals or regulatory requirements. Captures action type, affected lot numbers, affected markets, root cause, corrective action description, regulatory notification dates, customer notification status, and closure date. Integrates with quality CAPA and supply chain recall management.'
}

Each registration is associated with a specific regulatory approval (the approved product being registered). Adding']

  jurisdiction_region varchar [note: 'Sub-national region, state, or province within the jurisdiction country if applicable (e.g., California for US state-level registrations, Ontario for Canadian provincial requirements). Nullable for national-level registrations.']

  manufacturer_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the manufacturer location.
