Requisition layer: captures requisition number, position reference, job profile, hiring manager, recruiter, requisition type, target start date, approved headcount, sourcing channels, status, and time-to-fill metrics. Candidate layer: manages applicant/candidate master records including candidate name, source channel (LinkedIn, referral, job board, campus), application date, applied position, candidate status through pipeline stages (applied/screened/interviewed/offered/hired/rejected/withdrawn), interview progression, offer details, background check status, and EEO data. Candidate records are distinct from employee records created upon hire. Sourced from Oracle Cloud HCM Recruiting.'
}

Table workforce.candidate {
  candidate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the candidate record in the talent acquisition pipeline. Primary key.']
  candidate_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee record created upon hire. Links the candidate record to the employee master record.']
  candidate_hiring_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hiring manager responsible for the position to which the candidate applied.']
  candidate_recruiter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recruiter or talent acquisition specialist managing this candidate.']
  candidate_referral_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who referred this candidate, if the source channel was employee referral.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_profile. Business justification: Candidates apply for specific job profiles. Currently applied_position_title is a free-text STRING, which creates data quality issues and prevents proper matching/reporting. Adding job_profile_id FK n']
  requisition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the job requisition to which the candidate applied.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date when the candidate submitted their application for the position.']
  background_check_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the background check was completed.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the background check process for the candidate (not initiated, in progress, completed, cleared, flagged).']
  candidate_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier assigned to the candidate for tracking and reference purposes across recruiting workflows.']
  candidate_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the candidate in the recruiting pipeline (applied, screened, interviewed, offered, hired, rejected, withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: applied|screened|interviewed|offered|hired|rejected|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cover_letter_file_path varchar [note: 'File path or storage location reference for the candidate\'s cover letter document, if provided.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the candidate record was first created in the system.']
  current_employer varchar [note: 'Name of the candidate\'s current or most recent employer.']
  current_job_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role the candidate currently holds or most recently held.']
  eeo_disability_status varchar [note: 'Self-reported disability status for Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) reporting purposes. Collected voluntarily and stored separately for compliance.']
  eeo_ethnicity varchar [note: 'Self-reported ethnicity for Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) reporting purposes. Collected voluntarily and stored separately for compliance.']
  eeo_gender varchar [note: 'Self-reported gender for Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) reporting purposes. Collected voluntarily and stored separately for compliance.']
  eeo_veteran_status varchar [note: 'Self-reported veteran status for Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) reporting purposes. Collected voluntarily and stored separately for compliance.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for candidate communication throughout the recruiting process.']
  expected_start_date date [note: 'Expected or agreed-upon start date for the candidate if hired.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the candidate as provided in the application.']
  highest_education_level varchar [note: 'Highest level of education attained by the candidate (e.g., high school, bachelor\'s degree, master\'s degree, PhD).']
  hire_date date [note: 'Actual date when the candidate was hired and transitioned to employee status.']
  interview_stage varchar [note: 'Current interview stage or phase the candidate is in (e.g., phone screen, technical interview, panel interview, final interview).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the candidate record was last updated or modified.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name of the candidate as provided in the application.']
  location_preference varchar [note: 'Geographic location or site preference indicated by the candidate for the position.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the candidate, if provided.']
  offer_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date when the candidate accepted the job offer.']
  offer_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total compensation amount offered to the candidate (base salary or total package value).']
  offer_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the offer amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  offer_date date [note: 'Date when a formal job offer was extended to the candidate.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the candidate.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the candidate was formally rejected or removed from consideration.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or category for candidate rejection if applicable (e.g., qualifications not met, position filled, candidate withdrew).']
  resume_file_path varchar [note: 'File path or storage location reference for the candidate\'s resume or curriculum vitae document.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the candidate was sourced or applied (e.g., LinkedIn, employee referral, job board, campus recruiting, career site, recruiting agency).']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date when the candidate withdrew their application from the recruiting process.']
  work_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Candidate\'s work authorization status in the hiring country (e.g., citizen, permanent resident, requires sponsorship).']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total years of relevant professional experience reported by the candidate.']

  Note: 'Applicant/candidate master record in the talent acquisition pipeline — capturing candidate name, source channel (LinkedIn, referral, job board, campus), application date, applied position, candidate status (applied/screened/interviewed/offered/hired/rejected/withdrawn), interview stage, offer details, background check status, and EEO data. Sourced from Oracle Cloud HCM Recruiting. Distinct from the employee record created upon hire.'
}

Table workforce.absence_record {
  absence_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the absence record. Primary key.']
  absence_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the absence plan or policy under which this absence is recorded (e.g., annual Paid Time Off (PTO) plan, sick leave plan, Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) plan). Links to absence plan configuration.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (typically manager or Human Resources (HR) representative) who approved or rejected the absence request.']
  primary_absence_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who is absent. Links to the employee master record.']
  workforce_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee assignment or position providing coverage during this absence. Used for workforce planning and continuity management.']
  absence_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed reason or description for the absence event. May include medical condition category, family care reason, or other contextual information.']
  absence_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the absence request. Tracks workflow from submission through approval and completion.']
  absence_type_code varchar [note: 'Classification of the absence event. Includes planned leave (Paid Time Off (PTO), vacation, parental leave, sabbatical) and unplanned absences (sick leave, Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA), disability leave, bereavement). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PTO|VACATION|SICK|FMLA|PARENTAL|BEREAVEMENT|DISABILITY — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  accrual_balance_after decimal(10,2) [note: 'Employee\'s absence accrual balance (in hours or days) after this absence was deducted. Used for audit trail and balance reconciliation.']
  accrual_balance_before decimal(10,2) [note: 'Employee\'s absence accrual balance (in hours or days) before this absence was taken. Used for audit trail and balance reconciliation.']
  approval_comments varchar [note: 'Comments or notes provided by the approver during the approval or rejection process.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the absence request was approved or rejected by the approver.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the absence request. Tracks manager or Human Resources (HR) approval decision.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the absence request was cancelled by the employee or system administrator.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the absence request was cancelled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this absence record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  disability_leave_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence is classified as short-term or long-term disability leave.']
  duration_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the absence measured in calendar days. Supports fractional days for partial-day absences.']
  duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the absence measured in hours. Used for hourly tracking and partial-day leave calculations.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last scheduled date of the absence period. Represents the planned return date minus one day.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier for this absence record (e.g., Oracle Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) absence ID, third-party leave management system case number). Used for cross-system reconciliation.']
  fmla_designated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence qualifies for and is designated as Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) protected leave under U.S. federal law.']
  fmla_hours_used decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) hours consumed by this absence event. Tracked against the employee\'s 12-week (480-hour) annual Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) entitlement.']
  gxp_qualification_impact_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the impact of the absence on the employee\'s Good Practice Regulations (GxP) qualification status, including specific training or requalification activities required before resuming Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) or Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) duties.']
  gxp_requalification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee requires Good Practice Regulations (GxP) requalification or retraining upon return to work due to extended absence from Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) or Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) regulated environment. Triggered when absence exceeds regulatory qualification maintenance thresholds.']
  intermittent_leave_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence is part of an intermittent leave pattern (e.g., recurring medical appointments, reduced work schedule under Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA)).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this absence record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  medical_certification_date date [note: 'Date when medical certification or documentation was received by Human Resources (HR).']
  medical_certification_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required medical certification or documentation has been received and validated by Human Resources (HR).']
  medical_certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether medical certification or documentation is required for this absence (e.g., doctor\'s note for extended sick leave, Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) certification).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about the absence event. May include special circumstances, accommodation details, or Human Resources (HR) case management notes.']
  paid_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the absence is paid or unpaid. True for paid leave (Paid Time Off (PTO), vacation, paid sick leave), false for unpaid leave (unpaid Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA), leave without pay).']
  payroll_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence has an impact on payroll processing (e.g., unpaid leave deduction, paid time off usage).']
  payroll_processed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payroll impact of this absence has been processed in the payroll system.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'The actual date the employee returned to work following the absence. May differ from planned end date if absence is extended or shortened.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the system or channel through which the absence record was created (Oracle Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM), manual Human Resources (HR) entry, timekeeping integration, employee self-service portal).']
  start_date date [note: 'The first date of the absence period. Represents when the employee begins their leave.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the employee or their representative submitted the absence request.']
  workers_compensation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence is related to a workers compensation claim due to work-related injury or illness.']

  Note: 'Tracks employee absence events — planned leave (PTO, vacation, parental leave, sabbatical) and unplanned absences (sick leave, FMLA, disability leave). Captures absence type, start date, end date, duration in hours/days, approval status, FMLA designation, return-to-work date, and impact on GxP qualification status (e.g., requalification required after extended absence from GMP environment). Sourced from Oracle Cloud HCM Absence Management.'
}

Table workforce.headcount_plan {
  headcount_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the headcount plan record. Primary key for the headcount planning entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center that will bear the financial burden of this headcount plan. Links to the finance cost_center master data for General Ledger (GL) integration.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary work location (site, office, or facility) for this headcount plan entry. Supports geographic workforce distribution analysis.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit for which this headcount plan applies. Links to the org_unit master data.']
  primary_headcount_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who approved this headcount plan record. Supports audit trail and governance requirements.']
  tertiary_headcount_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'The user identifier of the person who last modified this headcount plan record. Supports audit trail and data governance.']
  actual_filled_headcount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual number of Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) positions filled as of the reporting date. Sourced from Oracle Cloud HCM active assignments.']
  approved_headcount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The approved number of Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) positions after budget review and executive approval. May differ from planned_headcount due to budget constraints or strategic adjustments.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this headcount plan record was approved. Supports audit trail and Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) compliance.']
  budget_allocation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total budget amount allocated for this headcount plan entry, including salary, benefits, and overhead. Used for Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) spend vs. budget variance reporting.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 4217 currency code for the budget allocation amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'The business unit code for this headcount plan entry. Aligns with corporate organizational structure and Profit and Loss (P&L) reporting.']
  clia_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the headcount in this plan entry requires Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) certification or qualification. Relevant for clinical genomics and In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) laboratory personnel.']
  contractor_fte_equivalent decimal(10,2) [note: 'The Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) count of contractors and contingent workers supporting this org unit and job family. Enables total workforce capacity analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this headcount plan record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'The employment type classification for this headcount plan entry. Distinguishes between permanent employees, contractors, and temporary staff.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year (1-12). Provides monthly granularity for headcount planning and Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) tracking.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter within the fiscal year for this headcount plan (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4). Enables quarterly workforce planning and variance analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year for which this headcount plan applies (e.g., 2024, 2025). Supports annual workforce planning cycles.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'The functional area classification for this headcount plan entry (e.g., Research and Development (R&D), Manufacturing, Quality, Sales, General and Administrative (G&A)). Used for functional cost allocation and Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) reporting.']
  gxp_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the headcount in this plan entry is subject to Good Practice Regulations (GxP) (Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP), Good Laboratory Practice (GLP), Good Clinical Practice (GCP)) compliance requirements. Drives training and qualification planning.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'The job family classification for this headcount plan entry (e.g., Research and Development (R&D), Manufacturing, Quality Assurance (QA), Field Application Scientist, Bioinformatics, Clinical Operations, Sales, Marketing, Finance, Human Resources (HR)). Aligns with workforce planning categories.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'The job level or grade for this headcount plan entry (e.g., Individual Contributor, Senior, Lead, Manager, Director, Vice President (VP), Executive). Used for workforce stratification and compensation planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this headcount plan record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  open_requisition_count varchar [note: 'The number of open job requisitions for this org unit, job family, and fiscal period. Indicates active recruiting efforts to close the headcount gap.']
  plan_effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this headcount plan record ceases to be effective. Null indicates an open-ended plan. Supports temporal workforce planning and historical analysis.']
  plan_effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this headcount plan record becomes effective. Supports temporal workforce planning and historical analysis.']
  plan_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this headcount plan entry. Captures business context, assumptions, or special considerations.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the headcount plan record. Tracks the approval workflow and activation state of the plan.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier for this headcount plan (e.g., Original Budget, Revised Q2, Board Approved). Supports multiple planning scenarios and revisions throughout the fiscal year.']
  planned_headcount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The planned number of Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) positions for this org unit, job family, and fiscal period. Represents the initial workforce plan target.']
  rd_capitalization_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the headcount in this plan entry is eligible for Research and Development (R&D) capitalization under Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) or International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS). Supports financial reporting and Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) compliance.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this headcount plan record originated (e.g., Oracle Cloud HCM, SAP S/4HANA, Workday, custom workforce planning tool). Supports data lineage and integration traceability.']
  variance_to_plan decimal(10,2) [note: 'The variance between approved_headcount and actual_filled_headcount (calculated as actual_filled_headcount minus approved_headcount). Negative values indicate understaffing; positive values indicate overstaffing.']

  Note: 'FTE planning and headcount budget records by org unit, job family, and fiscal period — capturing planned headcount, approved headcount, actual filled headcount, open requisition count, contractor FTE equivalents, and variance to plan. Supports annual workforce planning cycles, R&D headcount capitalization decisions, and GMP staffing compliance. Enables CFO/CHRO reporting on FTE spend vs. budget.'
}

Table workforce.lifecycle_event {
  lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the HR lifecycle event record. Primary key.']
  alt5_lifecycle_final_approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who provided final approval for this lifecycle event.']
  new_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work location after this lifecycle event. Null for termination events.']
  primary_lifecycle_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who is the subject of this lifecycle event.']
  primary_lifecycle_prior_job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job profile assigned to the employee before this event. Null for new hire events.']
  primary_lifecycle_prior_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit the employee belonged to before this event. Null for new hire events.']
  primary_lifecycle_prior_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee\'s position before this lifecycle event. Null for new hire events.']
  prior_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work location before this lifecycle event. Null for new hire events.']
  quaternary_lifecycle_initiating_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager who initiated this lifecycle event. Critical for SOX compliance audit trail.']
  quinary_lifecycle_hr_business_partner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the HR business partner responsible for processing this lifecycle event.']
  tertiary_lifecycle_new_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee\'s direct manager after this lifecycle event. Null for termination events.']
  approval_chain varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of employee IDs representing the approval workflow sequence for this event. Provides complete audit trail for SOX compliance.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when final approval was granted for this lifecycle event. Critical for SOX audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this lifecycle event record was first created in the system. Distinct from event_effective_date which represents the business event date.']
  event_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, context, or special instructions related to this lifecycle event.']
  event_effective_date date [note: 'The date when this lifecycle event becomes effective for the employee. This is the business event date, distinct from system processing timestamps. Critical for SOX compliance and GxP personnel qualification continuity per 21 CFR Part 211.25.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the lifecycle event, typically system-generated sequence number from Oracle Cloud HCM.']
  event_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the lifecycle event. Maps to Oracle Cloud HCM action reason lookup values.']
  event_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual explanation of the reason for the lifecycle event. Provides context beyond the standardized reason code.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the lifecycle event. Tracks progression through approval and execution stages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|in_progress|completed|cancelled|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the HR lifecycle event. Defines the nature of the personnel action being recorded. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_hire|internal_transfer|promotion|demotion|role_change|location_change|leave_of_absence_start|return_from_leave|termination|offboarding — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this lifecycle event record was last updated in the system.']
  new_clia_qualified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee requires CLIA personnel qualification in their new role. Null for termination events.']
  new_employment_type varchar [note: 'Employment classification after this lifecycle event. Null for termination events.']
  new_fte_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Full-time equivalent allocation percentage after this event. 100.00 represents full-time. Null for termination events.']
  new_gxp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee\'s new role is designated as GxP-critical requiring regulatory qualification per 21 CFR Part 211.25. Null for termination events.']
  prior_clia_qualified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee held CLIA personnel qualification in their prior role. Null for new hire events.']
  prior_employment_type varchar [note: 'Employment classification before this lifecycle event. Null for new hire events.']
  prior_fte_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Full-time equivalent allocation percentage before this event. 100.00 represents full-time. Null for new hire events.']
  prior_gxp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee\'s prior role was designated as GxP-critical requiring regulatory qualification per 21 CFR Part 211.25. Null for new hire events.']
  requalification_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which training requalification must be completed following this lifecycle event. Null if requalification is not required.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this lifecycle event record originated, typically Oracle Cloud HCM instance identifier.']
  source_system_event_code varchar [note: 'Native event identifier from the source system (Oracle Cloud HCM action ID) for traceability and reconciliation.']
  sox_control_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle event is subject to SOX personnel change control requirements due to financial system access or authority changes.']
  system_access_change_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle event requires changes to system access permissions and roles. Critical for SOX IT general controls.']
  training_requalification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle event triggers mandatory GxP training requalification per 21 CFR Part 211.25.']

  Note: 'Captures discrete HR lifecycle events providing a complete audit trail for each employee — new hire onboarding, internal transfer, promotion, demotion, role change, location change, leave of absence initiation, return from leave, and termination/offboarding. Each event captures event type, effective date, prior state, new state, initiating manager, HR business partner, approval chain, and event reason code. Critical for SOX compliance (personnel change controls), GxP personnel qualification continuity (21 CFR Part 211.25), and CLIA personnel change documentation. Sourced from Oracle Cloud HCM.'
}

Table workforce.lab_qualification {
  lab_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the laboratory qualification record. Primary key for the lab_qualification product.']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualification documentation stored in the document management system (e.g., Veeva Vault, MasterControl). Links to assessment forms, certificates, and training records.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Lab qualifications for variant calling, NGS pipeline operation, and clinical bioinformatics must specify the genome build the employee is qualified to use. CLIA personnel qualification records require']
  genomic_access_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.genomic_access_control. Business justification: Lab qualifications determine technical access rights to specific genomic analysis platforms (e.g., Illumina BaseSpace, bioinformatics pipelines) and associated datasets. Operational necessity: only qu']
  primary_lab_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who holds this laboratory qualification. Links to the employee master record in the workforce domain.']
  quality_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.quality_rule. Business justification: Lab qualifications (instrument-specific, assay-specific) authorize personnel to perform quality-controlled genomic workflows. Quality rules enforce that only qualified personnel execute critical data ']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'Specific assay or test method for which the qualification applies. Examples: WGS (Whole Genome Sequencing), WES (Whole Exome Sequencing), SNP Array, CNV Detection, CRISPR Editing Protocol. Null for non-assay-specific qualifications.']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score achieved during the qualification assessment, expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00). Null if the assessment was pass/fail without numerical scoring.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who conducted the qualification assessment. Denormalized for audit trail and reporting purposes.']
  assessor_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the assessor at the time of qualification. Examples: Senior Scientist, Lab Manager, Technical Director, Quality Assurance Manager.']
  biosafety_level varchar [note: 'Biosafety containment level for which the individual is authorized. BSL-1 for minimal hazard, BSL-2 for moderate risk, BSL-3 for serious or potentially lethal agents. Not applicable for non-biosafety qualifications.']
  cap_accreditation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this qualification is required for CAP laboratory accreditation. True for qualifications mandated by CAP inspection checklists; false otherwise.']
  cleanroom_class varchar [note: 'ISO cleanroom classification for which the individual is qualified. ISO 5 (Class 100) through ISO 8 (Class 100,000) per ISO 14644-1. Not applicable for non-cleanroom qualifications.']
  clia_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this qualification is required for CLIA-regulated clinical laboratory operations. True for qualifications mandated by CLIA personnel standards; false otherwise.']
  competency_assessment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assess competency for this qualification. Practical demonstration for hands-on skills; written exam for knowledge; observation for supervised performance; simulation for controlled scenarios; combination for multi-method assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this qualification record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification expires and requalification is required. Null for qualifications with no expiration (permanent authorization).']
  gxp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this qualification is critical for GxP compliance (Good Manufacturing Practice, Good Laboratory Practice, Good Clinical Practice). True for qualifications required for regulated activities; false otherwise.']
  independent_runs_required varchar [note: 'Number of successful independent runs required to demonstrate competency. Null for non-operational qualifications.']
  instrument_model varchar [note: 'Specific instrument model or platform for which the qualification applies. Examples: Illumina NovaSeq 6000, Illumina NextSeq 2000, Applied Biosystems QuantStudio 7, CRISPR Cas9 System. Null for non-instrument qualifications.']
  iso_13485_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this qualification is required for ISO 13485 medical device quality management system compliance. True for qualifications mandated by ISO 13485 personnel competency requirements; false otherwise.']
  lab_scope varchar [note: 'Laboratory area, facility, or functional scope to which this qualification applies. Examples: NGS Production Lab, qPCR Lab, Gene Editing Suite, BSL-2 Containment Area, Cleanroom Zone A.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this qualification record was last updated. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance.']
  last_requalification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent requalification assessment. Null if the individual has not yet been requalified since initial qualification.']
  next_requalification_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date by which the next requalification must be completed. Derived from last qualification date plus requalification frequency. Null for qualifications not requiring requalification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, special conditions, limitations, or observations related to the qualification. Examples: Qualified with restrictions, requires co-signer for first 10 runs, authorized for research use only.']
  passing_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to pass the qualification assessment, expressed as a percentage. Typically 80.00 or higher for GxP-regulated qualifications.']
  qualification_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or credential number issued upon successful qualification. Used for audit trail and verification purposes.']
  qualification_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific qualification. Examples: NGS-NOVASEQ-OPR, QPCR-OPR, CRISPR-EDIT-L2, BSL2-AUTH, CR-GOWN-A.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date on which the individual successfully completed the qualification and was authorized. Represents the effective start of competency.']
  qualification_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the laboratory qualification. Examples: NovaSeq 6000 Instrument Operator, qPCR Operator Qualification, CRISPR Gene Editing Lab Level 2, Biosafety Level 2 Authorization, Cleanroom Gowning Class A.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification. Active indicates the individual is currently authorized; expired indicates the qualification has lapsed; suspended indicates temporary hold; pending_requalification indicates renewal in process; revoked indicates permanent removal; in_progress indicates initial qualification underway.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Category of laboratory qualification. Distinguishes between instrument operation, lab technique competency, biosafety clearance, cleanroom access, assay-specific authorization, and equipment maintenance qualification.']
  requalification_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required requalification assessments. Common values: 12, 24, 36. Null for qualifications that do not require periodic requalification.']
  requalification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of requalification requirement. Not required for permanent qualifications; upcoming when approaching expiry; overdue when past expiry; in progress during reassessment; completed when renewed.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification was revoked due to performance issues, safety violations, or other disqualifying events. Null for qualifications that have not been revoked.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the qualification was revoked. Examples: Failed requalification assessment, safety violation, quality incident, extended absence, role change. Null for qualifications that have not been revoked.']
  sop_version varchar [note: 'Version of the Standard Operating Procedure under which the qualification was conducted. Ensures traceability to the specific procedure version used for assessment.']
  supervised_runs_completed varchar [note: 'Number of supervised instrument runs, assays, or procedures completed during the qualification process. Null for non-operational qualifications.']
  suspension_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification suspension was lifted and authorization was reinstated. Null for qualifications that are currently suspended or have not been suspended.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the qualification was temporarily suspended. Examples: Pending investigation, refresher training required, equipment change, procedure update. Null for qualifications that have not been suspended.']
  suspension_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification was temporarily suspended. Null for qualifications that have not been suspended.']
  training_hours_completed decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of training hours completed as part of the qualification process. Includes classroom instruction, hands-on practice, and supervised operation.']

  Note: 'Tracks individual laboratory and instrument-specific qualifications for scientists, engineers, and technicians — e.g., NGS instrument operator qualification (Illumina NovaSeq, NextSeq), qPCR operator qualification, CRISPR editing lab qualification, biosafety level (BSL-1/BSL-2) authorization, and cleanroom gowning qualification. Captures qualification type, instrument/lab scope, qualification date, qualified-by assessor, expiry date, and requalification status. Distinct from GxP training records — this is competency-based lab authorization.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_result {
  payroll_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payroll result record. Primary key for this entity representing a single employee\'s processed payroll for a specific pay period.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center to which this payroll expense is allocated. Critical for labor cost allocation and R&D capitalization analysis per SOX requirements.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee for whom this payroll result was processed. Links to the employee master record in Oracle Cloud HCM.']
  payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll run batch that generated this result. Links to the payroll run header record.']
  workforce_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee assignment record active during this pay period. Captures the position, organization, and cost center context for this payroll result.']
  bonus_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discretionary or performance-based bonus payments included in this pay period. May include annual bonuses, spot awards, or incentive compensation.']
  commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sales commission earnings for field application specialists and commercial staff. Calculated based on revenue attainment and commission plans.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll result record was first created in the system. Part of standard audit trail.']
  dental_insurance_deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee portion of dental insurance premiums deducted from pay. Typically pre-tax under cafeteria plan.']
  federal_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Federal income tax withheld from gross pay per IRS regulations and employee W-4 elections. Required for SOX financial controls and tax reporting.']
  fsa_contribution_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee pre-tax contributions to healthcare or dependent care FSA. Subject to IRS annual limits and use-it-or-lose-it rules.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for posting this payroll expense. Maps to chart of accounts for financial reporting and SOX controls.']
  gross_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total earnings before any deductions or withholdings. Includes regular pay, overtime, shift differentials, bonuses, and other earnings components.']
  health_insurance_deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee portion of health insurance premiums deducted from pay. May be pre-tax under Section 125 cafeteria plan.']
  hours_worked decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total hours worked during the pay period including regular and overtime hours. Used for labor productivity analysis and FTE calculations.']
  hsa_contribution_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee pre-tax contributions to HSA for high-deductible health plan participants. Subject to IRS annual limits.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll result record was last updated. Tracks changes for audit and compliance purposes.']
  local_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Local or municipal income tax withheld where applicable. Includes city, county, or district taxes based on work or residence location.']
  medicare_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee portion of FICA Medicare tax withheld. Includes additional Medicare tax for high earners per ACA regulations.']
  net_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Take-home pay after all deductions and withholdings. The amount disbursed to the employee via direct deposit or check.']
  other_deductions_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of miscellaneous deductions not captured in standard categories. May include garnishments, union dues, charitable contributions, or loan repayments.']
  overtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hours worked beyond standard schedule at premium pay rates. Subject to FLSA regulations and company overtime policy.']
  overtime_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional earnings for hours worked beyond standard schedule. Calculated at premium rates per labor regulations and company policy.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the pay period covered by this payroll result. Defines the earnings and deductions calculation window.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the pay period covered by this payroll result. Used for period-based reporting and labor cost allocation.']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this payroll result. Typically USD for US operations.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the net pay was disbursed to the employee. Used for cash flow forecasting and SOX financial controls.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which net pay was disbursed to the employee. Direct deposit is standard for most employees.']
  payroll_frequency varchar [note: 'Pay cycle frequency for this employee. Determines pay period duration and payment schedule.']
  payroll_run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this payroll result. Tracks progression from calculation through payment and supports audit trail for SOX compliance.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll result was calculated and finalized. Critical for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  pto_hours_used decimal(8,2) [note: 'Paid time off hours taken during this pay period. Reduces available PTO balance and is included in gross pay calculation.']
  rd_capitalization_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of labor cost eligible for R&D capitalization per accounting standards. Used for financial statement preparation and tax credits.']
  rd_capitalization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of gross pay allocated to R&D capitalization. Determined by employee role, project assignment, and accounting policy.']
  regular_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard hours worked at base pay rate. Excludes overtime and premium hours.']
  regular_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base earnings for standard hours worked during the pay period. Excludes overtime, bonuses, and other supplemental pay.']
  retirement_contribution_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee pre-tax or Roth contributions to 401(k) retirement plan. Voluntary deduction subject to IRS annual limits.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payroll result has been reversed or voided. True if reversed, false if active. Used for audit trail and correction tracking.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for reversing this payroll result. Required for SOX audit trail when corrections are made.']
  shift_differential_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Premium pay for GMP manufacturing staff working non-standard shifts (evening, night, weekend). Critical for labor cost analysis in regulated manufacturing operations.']
  social_security_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee portion of FICA Social Security tax withheld. Calculated at statutory rate up to annual wage base limit.']
  state_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'State income tax withheld based on employee work location and state tax regulations. Varies by jurisdiction.']
  total_deductions_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all tax withholdings and voluntary deductions subtracted from gross pay. Used to calculate net pay.']
  vision_insurance_deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee portion of vision insurance premiums deducted from pay. Typically pre-tax under cafeteria plan.']

  Note: 'Stores processed payroll run results per employee per pay period — capturing gross pay, net pay, tax withholdings (federal, state, local), benefit deductions, voluntary deductions (401k, FSA), overtime pay, shift differential pay for GMP manufacturing staff, and payroll run status. Sourced from Oracle Cloud HCM Payroll. Restricted classification. Supports SOX financial controls and labor cost allocation to cost centers and R&D capitalization.'
}

Table workforce.time_entry {
  time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the time entry record. Primary key for the time entry data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for billable time entries. Used for field application specialist time tracking and customer invoicing.']
  adjusted_time_entry_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on time_entry (adjusted_time_entry_id)']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or supervisor who approved this time entry. Provides audit trail for SOX compliance.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center against which this time is charged. Enables labor cost allocation across organizational units.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location where the work was performed. Supports multi-site labor cost allocation and remote work tracking.']
  payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll run that processed this time entry. Links time to compensation and enables payroll audit trail.']
  primary_time_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who recorded this time entry. Links to the employee master data.']
  rd_capitalization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.rd_capitalization. Business justification: R&D labor capitalization under ASC 985-20/IAS 38 requires linking time entries to capitalization decisions to allocate labor costs to fixed assets (capitalized software/technology). Essential for dete']
  service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service agreement or contract under which billable time is provided. Links time to contractual commitments and SLA tracking.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_order. Business justification: Enables direct labor cost rollup from time entries to work orders and production batches for manufacturing cost accounting and variance analysis. Critical for GMP cost tracking and production costing.']
  workforce_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific employee assignment under which this time was worked. Supports employees with multiple concurrent assignments.']
  activity_code varchar [note: 'Specific activity or task performed during this time period. Supports detailed labor cost analysis and R&D capitalization eligibility determination.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the time entry. Controls payroll processing and financial posting. Critical for SOX internal controls over labor cost.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the time entry was approved. Critical for payroll cutoff and SOX audit trail.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time is billable to a customer or external party. Critical for field application specialist revenue recognition.']
  capitalization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of labor cost from this time entry that should be capitalized as R&D asset. Supports partial capitalization scenarios.']
  clia_qualified_activity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time was spent on CLIA-regulated clinical laboratory activities requiring personnel qualification. Critical for CAP accreditation and CLIA compliance.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes about the time entry. Provides context for unusual entries or approval decisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this time entry record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for labor cost amount. Supports multi-currency operations and global workforce management.']
  entry_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the work was performed. Critical for labor cost allocation and payroll processing.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External system reference identifier for this time entry. Enables cross-system reconciliation and audit trail.']
  gl_posted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether labor cost from this time entry has been posted to the general ledger. Critical for financial close and SOX compliance.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date when labor cost was posted to the general ledger. Used for period-end close and financial reporting.']
  gmp_production_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time was spent on GMP-regulated manufacturing activities. Used for regulatory compliance reporting and labor cost allocation to production batches.']
  gxp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time was spent on GxP-regulated activities requiring documented qualification and training compliance. Critical for FDA and EMA audit trails.']
  hours_worked decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total number of hours worked for this time entry. Supports fractional hours for precise labor cost allocation.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP internal order number for cost collection and allocation. Used for project-based and overhead cost tracking.']
  labor_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total labor cost for this time entry including base pay, overtime premium, and shift differential. Used for cost accounting and R&D capitalization.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this time entry record was last updated. Critical for change tracking and SOX audit trail.']
  payroll_processed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time entry has been processed in payroll. Prevents duplicate payment and supports payroll reconciliation.']
  rd_capitalization_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time entry is eligible for R&D capitalization under ASC 730 or IAS 38. Critical for financial reporting and SOX compliance.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Shift identifier for manufacturing and laboratory operations. Used for shift differential calculation and 24/7 operations management.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system that created this time entry record. Supports data lineage and multi-system integration.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the employee submitted the time entry for approval. Tracks timesheet submission timeliness.']
  time_type_code varchar [note: 'Classification of the time worked. Determines pay rate multiplier and labor cost treatment. Critical for GMP manufacturing staff overtime tracking and SOX-compliant labor reporting.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'Work breakdown structure element for project-based time tracking. Enables hierarchical project cost rollup and R&D capitalization reporting.']

  Note: 'Captures employee time worked against cost centers, projects, and R&D activities — supporting labor cost allocation, R&D capitalization under ASC 730/IAS 38, overtime tracking for GMP manufacturing staff, and billable hours for field application specialists. Records employee reference, date, hours, time type (regular/overtime/on-call), project/activity code, cost center, approval status, and capitalization eligibility flag. Sourced from Oracle Cloud HCM Time and Labor. Critical for biotech R&D capitalization decisions and SOX-compliant labor cost reporting.'
}

Table workforce.genomic_access_grant {
  genomic_access_grant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this access grant record. Primary key.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the Data Access Committee member or authorized approver who granted this access.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee who has been granted access to the genomic dataset']
  genomic_access_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the genomic access control policy governing the dataset being accessed']
  access_tier varchar [note: 'Level of access granted to the employee for this specific dataset, which may differ from the policy\'s default tier based on employee role and approval. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the Data Access Committee or authorized approver granted this access request. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']
  audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this access grant to the audit logging system tracking all data access events by this employee for this dataset. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']
  data_use_agreement_signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee signed the Data Use Agreement acknowledging their responsibilities and limitations for accessing this genomic dataset.']
  data_use_limitation_codes varchar [note: 'GA4GH Data Use Ontology (DUO) codes specifying the permitted uses for this specific access grant, which may be more restrictive than the policy-level limitations. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']
  grant_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee\'s access to the genomic dataset expires and must be renewed or revoked. Explicitly identified in detection phase as access_grant_expiry_date.']
  grant_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee\'s access to the genomic dataset becomes effective. Explicitly identified in detection phase as access_grant_start_date.']
  grant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this access grant indicating whether the employee currently has active access.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which this access grant was revoked before its natural expiry, if applicable. Null for grants that expired naturally or are still active.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for early revocation of access, such as employment termination, role change, IRB protocol violation, or data breach investigation.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee completed required training on genomic data handling, privacy protection, and ethical use before being granted access.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the authorization event granting an employee access to a specific genomic dataset governed by an access control policy. It captures time-bound access permissions, approval workflows, data use limitations, and audit requirements. Each record links one employee to one genomic access control policy with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific access grant, including grant dates, expiration, approval references, and compliance tracking.. Existence Justification: In genomics research and clinical operations, employees require access to multiple genomic datasets (dbGaP studies, internal cohorts, clinical variant databases), and each dataset has multiple authorized users across different roles (researchers, bioinformaticians, clinicians, data stewards). Access grants are actively managed as time-bound authorizations with approval workflows, expiration tracking, audit logging, and compliance requirements tied to IRB protocols and data use agreements. This is an operational M:N relationship where the business manages individual access grants as distinct entities.'
}

Table workforce.genome_qualification {
  genome_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the genome_qualification association']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee who holds this genome assembly qualification']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the genome assembly for which the employee is qualified']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this qualification certification expires and requires renewal. Null for qualifications without expiry. Critical for CLIA compliance tracking.']
  certifying_authority varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or body that certified this qualification (e.g., training manager, CLIA director). Required for audit trail.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Most recent date the employee worked with this genome assembly in a production pipeline or assay. Used to track active vs dormant qualifications and identify retraining needs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about this qualification, including special conditions, limitations, or requalification requirements.']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'Assessed proficiency level of the employee working with this specific genome assembly. Used for project staffing decisions and training needs assessment.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee achieved qualification to work with this genome assembly. Required for CLIA personnel records and audit trails.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the qualification. Active indicates the employee is currently authorized to work with this assembly. Expired/Suspended/Revoked indicate loss of authorization requiring requalification.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date the employee completed required training for this genome assembly. May differ from qualification_date if assessment occurred later.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the qualification relationship between employees and genome assemblies. It captures CLIA-compliant personnel qualification records for bioinformatics staff authorized to work with specific reference genome builds. Each record links one employee to one genome assembly with qualification status, proficiency level, certification dates, and last usage tracking required for regulatory compliance and project staffing decisions.. Existence Justification: In genomics biotechnology operations, bioinformatics employees must be qualified to work with specific genome assemblies for CLIA compliance and quality assurance. A single employee (bioinformatician, scientist) can be qualified on multiple genome builds (GRCh37, GRCh38, T2T-CHM13, GRCm39), and each genome assembly requires multiple qualified personnel for operational continuity, cross-coverage, and 24/7 pipeline support. The qualification relationship itself carries regulatory-critical data including qualification dates, proficiency levels, certification expiry, and last usage tracking.'
}

  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the plan is reviewed for updates.']

  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax treatment applicable to the plan.']
  taxability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the compensation is subject to tax withholding.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compensation plan record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the compensation plan.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for compensation_plan. Referenced by compensation_plan_id.'
}

  type varchar [note: 'Category describing the primary function of the location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the location record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for location. Referenced by location_id.'
}

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the schedule record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for work_schedule. Referenced by work_schedule_id.'
}

Table workforce.benefit_plan {
  benefit_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for benefit_plan']
  superseded_benefit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on benefit_plan (superseded_benefit_plan_id)']
  coinsurance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of costs shared between employee and insurer after deductible.']
  copay_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed amount the employee pays per service encounter.']
  coverage_level varchar [note: 'Scope of coverage (e.g., employee only, employee + spouse, family).']
  deductible_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual amount the employee must pay before benefits apply.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan ends; null if open‑ended.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules defining which employees qualify for the plan.']
  employee_contribution_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of premium paid by the employee.']
  employer_contribution_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of premium paid by the employer.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Last date employees may enroll in the plan.']
  enrollment_start_date date [note: 'First date employees may enroll in the plan.']
  is_gxp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan complies with GxP regulations.']
  out_of_pocket_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum annual amount employee pays after which plan covers 100%.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan based on eligible participant groups.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the benefit plan in HR systems.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing plan benefits and rules.']
  plan_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the official plan document.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the benefit plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of benefits provided by the plan.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the plan for change management.']
  premium_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Periodic amount the employee pays for the plan.']
  premium_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the premium amount.']
  provider_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the benefit provider.']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external vendor delivering the benefits.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the benefit plan record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the benefit plan record.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date the plan received required regulatory approval.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Authority that approved the benefit plan.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit plan.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for benefit_plan. Referenced by benefit_plan_id.'
}

Table workforce.training_item {
  training_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for training_item']
  prerequisite_training_item_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on training_item (prerequisite_training_item_id)']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Method used to evaluate learner performance.']
  author varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team that created the training content.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Name of the certification granted upon successful completion.']
  certification_required boolean [note: 'True if successful completion awards a formal certification.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned unique code used to reference the training item in catalogs and LMS systems.']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'True if the training is required to meet external regulatory compliance.']
  cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary cost to enroll in the training item.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training item record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Accredited credit value associated with the training item.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the training cost.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'How the training is delivered to participants.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the content, objectives, and scope of the training item.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total length of the training item expressed in minutes.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the training item becomes available for enrollment.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the training item is no longer offered (null if open‑ended).']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external Learning Management Systems to reference this training item.']
  format varchar [note: 'Technical format of the training material.']
  gxp_training boolean [note: 'True if the training satisfies Good Laboratory Practice (GLP), Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP), or related regulatory requirements.']
  language varchar [note: 'Natural language in which the training content is presented.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑2 three‑letter code representing the training language.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date the training content was last reviewed for relevance and accuracy.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training item is mandatory for the assigned audience.']
  max_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a learner may attempt the assessment.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the training item.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Department or business unit responsible for the training item.']
  pass_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage required to pass the assessment.']
  prerequisite_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of other training items is required before this one.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the training record must be retained for compliance.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Planned interval in months between mandatory content reviews.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the training item.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Primary employee group(s) for which the training is intended.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category that classifies the training item by its primary focus area.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the training item record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the training content (e.g., v1.2).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for training_item. Referenced by training_item_id.'
}

  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for any manual adjustments applied to the payroll run.']

  company_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (company) for which the payroll run is processed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of compliance validation for the payroll run.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payroll amounts.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the payroll run.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct employee records included in the payroll run.']
  is_gxp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payroll run complies with GxP regulatory requirements.']

  payroll_type varchar [note: 'Category of payroll processing (e.g., regular salary, bonus, overtime).']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the payroll period covered by this run.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the payroll period covered by this run.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payroll run was executed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating HR/Payroll system that generated the payroll run data.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll run.']
  total_benefit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate employer‑paid benefit contributions included in the payroll run.']
  total_deduction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all deductions (taxes, benefits, garnishments) applied to the payroll run.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of gross earnings for all employees in this payroll run before deductions.']
  total_net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net amount paid to employees after all deductions for this payroll run.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate tax withholdings deducted from the gross amount in this payroll run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payroll run record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the payroll run record for audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_run. Referenced by payroll_run_id.'
}

  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the review cycle definition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the review cycle record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the review cycle definition, incremented on changes.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for review_cycle. Referenced by review_cycle_id.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_cycle. Referenced by payroll_cycle_id.'

Table reference.genome_assembly {
  genome_assembly_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reference genome assembly record. Primary key.']
  superseded_by_assembly_genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the genome_assembly_id of the newer assembly that supersedes this one. Null if this assembly is current or has not been superseded.']
  annotation_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the gene annotation track associated with this assembly (e.g., GENCODE v42, Ensembl 109). Gene annotations are versioned independently of the assembly.']
  assembly_accession varchar [note: 'Official accession identifier assigned by the source authority (NCBI/INSDC format: GCA_ for GenBank, GCF_ for RefSeq). Serves as the globally unique external identifier for this assembly build.']
  assembly_alias varchar [note: 'Alternative or legacy name for the assembly (e.g., hg38 for GRCh38, mm10 for GRCm38). Used for cross-referencing with external tools and historical datasets.']
  assembly_level varchar [note: 'Degree of completeness and contiguity of the assembly. Chromosome = fully assembled chromosomes; Scaffold = ordered contigs with gaps; Contig = unordered contiguous sequences; Complete Genome = fully closed, telomere-to-telomere.']
  assembly_method varchar [note: 'Bioinformatics software and algorithm used to assemble the genome (e.g., FALCON, Canu, DISCOVAR, SPAdes). Important for reproducibility and quality assessment.']
  assembly_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the genome assembly (e.g., GRCh38, T2T-CHM13v2.0, GRCm39). This is the canonical label used across all bioinformatics pipelines and publications.']
  assembly_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the assembly. Active = currently used in production pipelines; deprecated = no longer recommended but still accessible; superseded = replaced by a newer version; archived = retained for historical reproducibility only.']
  assembly_type varchar [note: 'Ploidy and structural representation of the assembly. Haploid = single consensus sequence; diploid = phased maternal/paternal; haploid-with-alt-loci = primary assembly plus alternate haplotype scaffolds.']
  bowtie2_index_path varchar [note: 'Path to the Bowtie2 index files for this assembly. Alternative aligner index used in some RNA-seq and ChIP-seq pipelines.']
  bwa_index_path varchar [note: 'Path to the BWA (Burrows-Wheeler Aligner) index files for this assembly. Required for read alignment in NGS pipelines.']
  chromosome_count varchar [note: 'Total number of chromosomes represented in the assembly, including autosomes, sex chromosomes, and mitochondrial DNA where applicable.']
  contig_count varchar [note: 'Total number of contiguous sequences (contigs) in the assembly. Lower contig count indicates higher assembly contiguity and quality.']
  contig_n50_bp varchar [note: 'N50 statistic for contigs: the length at which 50% of the total assembly length is contained in contigs of this size or larger. Key quality metric for assembly contiguity.']
  coverage_depth decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average sequencing coverage depth (fold coverage) used to generate the assembly. Higher coverage generally indicates higher assembly accuracy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this genome assembly record was first created in the Genomics Biotech reference data catalog. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  cytoband_file_path varchar [note: 'Path to the cytoband file defining chromosome banding patterns (Giemsa staining regions). Used for cytogenetic coordinate mapping and visualization.']
  deprecated_date date [note: 'Date on which this assembly was officially deprecated by the source authority or by Genomics Biotech internal policy. Null if the assembly is still active.']
  fasta_file_path varchar [note: 'Cloud storage or file system path to the primary FASTA file containing the assembly sequences. This is the authoritative artifact used by all downstream pipelines.']
  fasta_md5_checksum varchar [note: 'MD5 hash of the FASTA file for integrity verification. Ensures the file has not been corrupted or tampered with during transfer or storage.']
  gc_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of guanine (G) and cytosine (C) bases in the total assembly sequence. Species-specific characteristic used for quality control and comparative genomics.']
  gff_file_path varchar [note: 'Path to the GFF3 file containing genomic features and annotations. Alternative to GTF, used by some annotation pipelines and genome browsers.']
  gtf_file_path varchar [note: 'Path to the GTF file containing gene annotations (exons, transcripts, genes) for this assembly. Used by RNA-seq and variant annotation pipelines.']
  index_file_path varchar [note: 'Path to the FASTA index file (.fai) used by bioinformatics tools (e.g., samtools, GATK) for rapid random access to sequences.']
  is_primary_assembly boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the primary/default assembly for the organism. True = primary assembly used by default in all pipelines; False = alternate or legacy assembly.']
  is_reference_standard boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this assembly is recognized as a community reference standard (e.g., GRCh38 for human). True = reference standard; False = non-standard or custom assembly.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional metadata, known issues, usage recommendations, or special handling instructions for this assembly (e.g., \'Contains unresolved centromeric gaps\', \'Recommended for clinical variant calling\').']
  organism_common_name varchar [note: 'Common or vernacular name of the organism (e.g., human, mouse, thale cress). Used for user-facing displays and non-technical reporting.']
  organism_name varchar [note: 'Scientific name of the organism for which this genome assembly was built (e.g., Homo sapiens, Mus musculus, Arabidopsis thaliana).']
  patch_version varchar [note: 'Incremental patch or update number applied to the base assembly (e.g., p13 for GRCh38.p13). Patches correct errors or add alternate loci without changing the primary coordinate system.']
  ploidy varchar [note: 'Number of complete sets of chromosomes in the organism. Haploid = one set (e.g., bacteria, haploid yeast); diploid = two sets (e.g., human, mouse); polyploid = more than two sets (e.g., wheat, some plants).']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which this assembly version was officially released by the source authority. Critical for reproducibility and version control in genomic analyses.']
  scaffold_count varchar [note: 'Total number of scaffolds in the assembly. Scaffolds are ordered and oriented contigs connected by gaps of estimated size.']
  scaffold_n50_bp varchar [note: 'N50 statistic for scaffolds: the length at which 50% of the total assembly length is contained in scaffolds of this size or larger. Higher values indicate better long-range contiguity.']
  sequence_dictionary_path varchar [note: 'Path to the sequence dictionary file (.dict) required by GATK and Picard tools. Contains metadata about each sequence (chromosome) in the assembly.']
  sequencing_technology varchar [note: 'Primary sequencing platform(s) used to generate the assembly (e.g., Illumina HiSeq, PacBio HiFi, Oxford Nanopore, Sanger). Relevant for understanding assembly characteristics and error profiles.']
  source_authority varchar [note: 'Organization or consortium responsible for generating and maintaining this assembly (e.g., GRC = Genome Reference Consortium, T2T = Telomere-to-Telomere Consortium).']
  submitter_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or consortium that submitted the assembly to the public database (e.g., Genome Reference Consortium, Broad Institute, Sanger Institute).']
  taxon_number varchar [note: 'NCBI Taxonomy database identifier for the organism. Enables linkage to external biological databases and phylogenetic classification.']
  total_sequence_length_bp varchar [note: 'Total number of base pairs across all sequences in the assembly, including chromosomes, scaffolds, contigs, and unplaced sequences. Measured in base pairs (bp).']
  ungapped_length_bp varchar [note: 'Total number of base pairs excluding gaps (N-runs). Represents the actual sequenced and assembled nucleotide content.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this genome assembly record was last modified. Tracks metadata updates, status changes, and artifact path corrections.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of reference genome assemblies used across all Genomics Biotech pipelines and assays.
