  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user or system process that last modified this genomic region record.']
  region_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the genomic region, including its biological function, clinical relevance, or technical purpose.']
  region_identifier varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier or accession number for the region (e.g., vendor catalog number, ENCODE identifier, dbSNP rsID for SNP regions).']
  region_length_bp varchar [note: 'Length of the genomic region in base pairs, calculated as end_position minus start_position.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the genomic region (e.g., BRCA1_exon2, chr1_centromere, ACMG_SF_v3.2_gene_panel).']
  region_set_name varchar [note: 'Name of the parent region set or collection to which this region belongs (e.g., Agilent SureSelect v8, ACMG SF v3.2, ENCODE Blacklist v2).']
  region_set_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the parent region set (e.g., v8, v3.2, 2023-Q1).']
  region_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the genomic region record indicating whether it is approved for use in production pipelines.']
  region_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the genomic region by its biological, clinical, or technical function. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exome_capture_target|gene_panel|blacklist_region|artifact_region|segmental_duplication|repeat_masked_region|centromere|telomere|coding_exon|regulatory_element|structural_variant|copy_number_variant — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the region set: IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), RUO (Research Use Only), LDT (Laboratory Developed Test), or not applicable.']
  repeat_masked_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this region is masked due to repetitive DNA sequences (True if repeat-masked, False otherwise).']
  source_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the authoritative body or database that curated or published the region definition (e.g., NCBI, ENCODE, ACMG, gnomAD, ClinVar).']
  source_publication varchar [note: 'PubMed ID (PMID) or DOI of the scientific publication that describes or validates this genomic region.']
  source_vendor varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor or manufacturer that provided the region definition (e.g., Agilent, Illumina, Twist Bioscience, Roche).']
  start_position varchar [note: 'Zero-based or one-based start coordinate of the genomic region on the reference genome assembly.']
  strand varchar [note: 'DNA strand orientation of the genomic region: + (forward/sense), - (reverse/antisense), or . (unstranded/not applicable).']
  target_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Recommended or expected sequencing coverage depth (number of reads) for this genomic region in quality control assessments.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this genomic region record, supporting version control and audit trail for regulatory compliance.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of curated genomic regions and region sets of biological, clinical, or technical significance. Encompasses exome capture targets, clinically actionable gene panels, blacklist/artifact regions, segmental duplications, repeat-masked regions, centromeres/telomeres, and named region sets (e.g., Agilent SureSelect v8, ACMG SF v3.2, ENCODE blacklist). Captures region identity, type classification, coordinates (chromosome/start/end/strand), genome build, parent set membership, associated gene, clinical significance, BED file path, source vendor/authority, version, and approval status. Foundational reference for assay design, coverage QC, variant filtering, and artifact exclusion in NGS workflows.'
}

Table reference.acmg_classification_rule {
  acmg_classification_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ACMG/AMP variant classification evidence rule record. Primary key.']
  acmg_classification_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this evidence code specification. Active rules are used in production variant interpretation; deprecated rules are retained for historical audit but not applied to new cases.']
  allelic_data_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of requirements related to allelic phase, compound heterozygosity, or trans configuration for this evidence code. Applicable to PM3, BP2 criteria.']
  applicable_gene_contexts varchar [note: 'Description of gene or disease mechanism contexts where this evidence code is applicable (e.g., \'genes where loss-of-function is a known disease mechanism\', \'genes with established functional domains\'). May reference specific gene lists or disease categories.']
  applicable_variant_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of variant types to which this evidence code applies (e.g., \'nonsense, frameshift, canonical splice site\', \'missense\', \'in-frame indel\'). Defines the molecular consequence scope.']
  authoritative_source varchar [note: 'The authoritative body or publication that defines this evidence code (e.g., \'ACMG/AMP 2015 Guidelines\', \'ClinGen Hearing Loss Expert Panel\', \'ClinGen CDH1 Variant Curation Expert Panel\').']
  automation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this evidence code can be fully or partially automated in computational variant annotation pipelines (True) or requires manual expert review (False).']
  clingen_refinement_specification varchar [note: 'Reference to ClinGen-specific refinements, gene-disease-specific adaptations, or expert panel recommendations that modify or clarify the application of this evidence code for particular contexts.']
  computational_prediction_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of in silico prediction tool requirements and consensus thresholds for this evidence code (e.g., \'multiple lines of computational evidence support deleterious effect\'). Applicable to PP3, BP4 criteria.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this evidence code record was first created in the reference data system. Supports audit trail and data lineage for regulatory compliance.']
  data_source_requirements varchar [note: 'List or description of required reference databases, population frequency datasets, functional assay repositories, or literature sources needed to apply this evidence code (e.g., \'gnomAD, ClinVar, HGMD, functional studies\').']
  de_novo_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of de novo variant occurrence requirements for this evidence code, including maternity/paternity confirmation standards. Applicable to PS2, PM6 criteria.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this version of the evidence code specification became effective and should be applied in variant interpretation workflows.']
  evidence_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of whether this evidence code supports pathogenic or benign interpretation of variants.']
  evidence_code varchar [note: 'The standardized ACMG/AMP evidence code identifier (e.g., PVS1, PS1, PM1, PP1, BA1, BS1, BP1). Defines the specific criterion used for variant pathogenicity assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PVS1|PS1|PS2|PS3|PS4|PM1|PM2|PM3|PM4|PM5|PM6|PP1|PP2|PP3|PP4|PP5|BA1|BS1|BS2|BS3|BS4|BP1|BP2|BP3|BP4|BP5|BP6|BP7 — 28 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  evidence_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the evidence criterion, including the biological or clinical rationale, applicability conditions, and interpretation guidance.']
  evidence_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable short name or title of the evidence criterion (e.g., \'Null variant in a gene where LOF is a known mechanism\', \'Same amino acid change as established pathogenic variant\').']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date after which this version of the evidence code specification is superseded or no longer valid. Null if currently active.']
  frequency_threshold decimal(10,6) [note: 'Numeric threshold for population allele frequency (MAF) used in applying this evidence code, if applicable. For example, BA1 uses a threshold of >5% in population databases. Null if not frequency-based.']
  functional_assay_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of functional or experimental evidence requirements for this criterion (e.g., \'well-established functional studies showing damaging effect\', \'validated assay demonstrating normal function\'). Applicable to PS3, BS3, and related codes.']
  implementation_logic varchar [note: 'Structured or semi-structured description of the computational or manual logic required to evaluate this evidence code. May include decision trees, data source requirements, thresholds, or algorithmic steps for automated variant annotation pipelines.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this evidence code record. Tracks changes to specifications, refinements, or metadata over time.']
  manual_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether application of this evidence code mandates manual review by a clinical molecular geneticist or variant scientist, even if computational pre-screening is performed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional implementation guidance, edge case handling, known limitations, or clarifications for variant scientists and bioinformaticians applying this evidence code.']
  phenotype_specificity_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of patient phenotype matching requirements for this evidence code, including specificity of clinical features to the gene-disease association. Applicable to PP4 criterion.']
  publication_reference varchar [note: 'Citation or PubMed identifier (PMID) for the primary publication or guideline document that defines this evidence code.']
  reputable_source_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of requirements for reputable source reporting of this variant as pathogenic or benign, including acceptable databases and curation standards. Applicable to PP5, BP6 criteria.']
  segregation_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of family segregation analysis requirements for this evidence code, including minimum number of affected family members or LOD score thresholds. Applicable to PP1 and related criteria.']
  strength_level varchar [note: 'The weight or strength tier assigned to this evidence code (very strong, strong, moderate, supporting, or stand-alone for benign criteria). Determines the contribution of this criterion to the final pathogenicity classification.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this evidence code specification, tracking updates to interpretation guidelines, ClinGen refinements, or expert panel recommendations over time.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of ACMG/AMP variant classification evidence criteria (PVS1 through BP7) with their strength tiers, applicable variant types, gene/disease contexts, and ClinGen-refined specifications. Each record defines one evidence code\'s logic, applicability conditions, and implementation rules for automated and manual variant classification. Authoritative reference ensuring consistent, auditable application of pathogenicity classification standards across all clinical variant interpretation workflows.'
}

Table reference.ontology_term {
  ontology_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ontology term record. Primary key.']
  parent_term_ontology_term_id varchar [note: 'The term identifier of the immediate parent term in the ontology hierarchy (e.g., for HP:0001250 \'Seizure\', the parent might be HP:0012638 \'Abnormality of nervous system physiology\'). Supports hierarchical navigation and subsumption reasoning. May be null for root terms.']
  primary_replacement_term_ontology_term_id varchar [note: 'The term identifier of the replacement or successor term if this term has been deprecated. Null if not deprecated or if no replacement is specified.']
  child_term_count varchar [note: 'The number of immediate child terms (direct descendants) of this term in the ontology hierarchy. Supports navigation and ontology structure analysis.']
  comment varchar [note: 'Free-text editorial comment or note about the term, its usage, or its relationship to other terms. May include curator notes, usage warnings, or clarifications.']
  created_date date [note: 'The date when this term was first introduced into the source ontology (format: yyyy-MM-dd). Supports temporal analysis and version tracking.']
  cross_references varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of equivalent or related term identifiers in other ontologies or coding systems (e.g., \'UMLS:C0036572|SNOMEDCT:91175000|MeSH:D012640\'). Enables interoperability and cross-ontology mapping.']
  curation_status varchar [note: 'The internal curation or review status of this term within the enterprise reference data governance workflow. Indicates whether the term has been validated for use in production genomics pipelines.']
  deprecated_date date [note: 'The date when this term was deprecated or obsoleted (format: yyyy-MM-dd). Null if the term is not deprecated. Supports regulatory audit trails and data quality monitoring.']
  evidence_code varchar [note: 'The evidence code or provenance indicator describing the basis for the terms inclusion or annotation (e.g., \'IEA\' for Inferred from Electronic Annotation, \'TAS\' for Traceable Author Statement in GO). Supports data quality assessment.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The depth or level of this term in the ontology hierarchy, with root terms at level 0. Supports hierarchical queries and subsumption analysis.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ontology term record was ingested into the enterprise reference data repository (format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports data lineage and audit trails.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this term has been deprecated or obsoleted in the source ontology. True if deprecated, False if active.']
  is_root_term boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this term is a root or top-level term in the ontology hierarchy (i.e., has no parent term). True if root, False otherwise.']
  last_modified_date date [note: 'The date when this term was last updated or modified in the source ontology (format: yyyy-MM-dd). Critical for change tracking and data lineage.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ontology term record was last updated in the enterprise reference data repository (format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Critical for change data capture and incremental processing.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'The most recent date when this term was used in a production annotation, variant interpretation, or clinical report (format: yyyy-MM-dd). Supports term lifecycle management and archival decisions.']
  namespace varchar [note: 'The namespace or sub-ontology within the source ontology to which this term belongs (e.g., \'biological_process\', \'molecular_function\', \'cellular_component\' for GO terms). Provides additional categorization within large ontologies.']
  ontology_source varchar [note: 'The source ontology or terminology system from which this term originates (e.g., HPO for Human Phenotype Ontology, OMIM for Online Mendelian Inheritance in Man, SNOMED CT for Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine Clinical Terms, SO for Sequence Ontology). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HPO|OMIM|MedGen|SNOMED CT|ICD-10|ICD-11|LOINC|SO|GO|MONDO|ORDO|GARD|NCIT|UBERON|CL|CHEBI|RxNorm|CPT|HGNC — 19 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ontology_version varchar [note: 'The version or release identifier of the source ontology from which this term was extracted (e.g., \'2023-10-09\', \'v2023.4\', \'Release 20231101\'). Critical for reproducibility and regulatory traceability.']
  regulatory_applicability varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of regulatory contexts or jurisdictions for which this term is applicable or required (e.g., \'FDA|EMA|CLIA|CAP\'). Supports compliance-driven term selection for clinical reporting.']
  source_url varchar [note: 'The canonical URL or persistent identifier (e.g., PURL, DOI) for accessing the authoritative definition and metadata for this term in the source ontology (e.g., \'http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/HP_0001250\').']
  subset_membership varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of subset or slim identifiers to which this term belongs (e.g., \'goslim_generic|goslim_plant\' for GO terms, \'hpo_slim\' for HPO). Subsets are curated collections of terms for specific use cases or communities.']
  synonyms varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of alternative names, aliases, or synonyms for the term (e.g., \'epileptic seizure|convulsion|fit\'). Supports flexible search and mapping from clinical free-text.']
  term_accession varchar [note: 'The canonical identifier for the ontology term as assigned by the source ontology (e.g., HP:0001250 for HPO, OMIM:600354, SNOMED:73211009). Format is typically prefix:numeric_code.']
  term_definition varchar [note: 'The formal textual definition of the ontology term as provided by the source ontology. Describes the precise meaning, scope, and usage of the term.']
  term_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable label or preferred name for the ontology term (e.g., \'Seizure\', \'Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy\', \'Single nucleotide variant\').']
  term_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the ontology term within the reference data governance framework. Active terms are approved for use in production workflows; deprecated terms are retained for historical traceability but should not be used for new annotations.']
  term_type varchar [note: 'The ontological type or role of this term within the ontology structure (e.g., class for concepts, property for relationships, individual for instances). Supports ontology reasoning and semantic validation.']
  usage_frequency varchar [note: 'The count of how many times this term has been used in annotations, variant interpretations, or clinical reports within the enterprise. Supports term prioritization and quality metrics.']
  use_case_category varchar [note: 'The primary domain or use case category for which this term is applicable (e.g., phenotype for HPO terms, disease for OMIM/MONDO, variant for SO terms, observation for LOINC). Supports filtering and context-specific term selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phenotype|disease|variant|sequence|gene|pathway|drug|procedure|observation|specimen — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of standardized ontology terms used across genomics workflows — including HPO (Human Phenotype Ontology), OMIM, MedGen, SNOMED CT, ICD-10, LOINC, and SO (Sequence Ontology) terms. Captures term ID, term name, ontology source, ontology version, term definition, parent term ID (for hierarchy), synonyms, cross-references to other ontologies, deprecated flag, replacement term ID, and applicable use cases (phenotype/disease/variant/sequence). Enables consistent phenotype-genotype mapping, clinical reporting standardization, and interoperability with external clinical and research systems.'
}

Table reference.pharmacogenomics_marker {
  pharmacogenomics_marker_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pharmacogenomics marker record. Primary key. Inferred role: MASTER_RESOURCE (reference catalog of PGx variant markers).']
  gene_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_model. Business justification: Pharmacogenomics markers are associated with specific genes (e.g., CYP2D6, TPMT). The gene_symbol and gene_name string attributes should be replaced with a proper FK to gene_model for referential inte']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Pharmacogenomics markers are positioned on a specific reference genome assembly. The reference_genome_build string attribute should be replaced with a proper FK to genome_assembly for referential inte']
  alternate_allele varchar [note: 'Alternate (variant) allele sequence (e.g., G, T, ACGTT). Null for star alleles or diplotypes that represent multi-variant haplotypes.']
  associated_drugs varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of drugs for which this PGx marker has documented gene-drug interactions and clinical actionability (e.g., codeine, tamoxifen, clopidogrel, warfarin, fluorouracil). Enables drug-specific PGx reporting.']
  chromosome varchar [note: 'Chromosome location of the variant (e.g., chr22, 10, X, MT). Follows UCSC or Ensembl chromosome naming conventions.']
  clinical_actionability_status varchar [note: 'Current clinical actionability classification: actionable (guideline-supported dosing or therapy change), informative (known association but no specific guideline), research_only (insufficient evidence for clinical use), or uncertain (conflicting evidence). Lifecycle status field for PGx marker.']
  clinical_annotation varchar [note: 'Free-text clinical interpretation and actionability guidance for the marker, including dosing recommendations, contraindications, or alternative therapy suggestions (e.g., Consider 50% dose reduction for CYP2D6 poor metabolizers on codeine; avoid fluorouracil in DPYD deficiency).']
  clinvar_variation_number varchar [note: 'ClinVar variation ID for the variant, if the variant has clinical significance annotation in ClinVar (e.g., 12345). Null if not present in ClinVar.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PGx marker record was first created in the system (format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.']
  curation_date date [note: 'Date when this PGx marker record was curated or last reviewed by the data stewardship team (format: yyyy-MM-dd). Ensures currency and auditability of reference data.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Primary data source or database from which this PGx marker record was curated (e.g., PharmGKB, CPIC, DPWG, FDA Table of PGx Biomarkers, PharmVar). Supports data lineage and provenance tracking.']
  diplotype varchar [note: 'Two-allele genotype combination representing the diplotype (e.g., *1/*4, *2/*3A). Captures the paired allele configuration that determines metabolizer phenotype. Null for single-variant markers.']
  ema_pgx_label_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this marker is referenced in EMA-approved drug labeling with pharmacogenomic information (True) or not (False). Supports EU regulatory compliance.']
  evidence_level varchar [note: 'CPIC evidence level classification for the gene-drug interaction: 1A (high evidence, strong recommendation), 1B (high evidence, moderate recommendation), 2A (moderate evidence, strong recommendation), 2B (moderate evidence, moderate recommendation), 3 (weak evidence), 4 (preliminary evidence). Drives clinical decision support priority.']
  fda_pgx_label_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this marker is referenced in FDA-approved drug labeling with pharmacogenomic information (True) or not (False). Supports regulatory-grade PGx reporting.']
  functional_impact varchar [note: 'Predicted or experimentally validated functional impact of the variant on gene/protein function: no_function (null allele), decreased_function, normal_function, increased_function, or uncertain_function. Drives phenotype prediction.']
  genomic_position varchar [note: 'Genomic coordinate (base pair position) of the variant on the reference genome assembly (GRCh38/hg38). For star alleles or haplotypes, this may represent the lead variant position.']
  guideline_source varchar [note: 'Primary authoritative source for the PGx guideline or recommendation: CPIC (Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium), DPWG (Dutch Pharmacogenetics Working Group), PharmGKB, FDA (U.S. Food and Drug Administration), EMA (European Medicines Agency), or other.']
  guideline_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier or publication date of the source guideline (e.g., CPIC v2.1, 2021-08-15). Ensures traceability and reproducibility of PGx recommendations.']
  hgvs_notation varchar [note: 'Standardized HGVS nomenclature for the variant (e.g., NC_000022.11:g.42130692G>A, NM_000546.5:c.215C>G, NP_000537.3:p.Arg72Pro). Supports genomic (g.), coding (c.), and protein (p.) representations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PGx marker record was last modified (format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Audit trail field for change tracking and data governance.']
  marker_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the PGx marker, typically combining gene symbol and variant notation (e.g., CYP2D6_rs1065852, DPYD_c.1905+1G>A). Used for cross-reference with PharmGKB, CPIC, and FDA labeling.']
  marker_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the pharmacogenomics marker, often including gene symbol and variant designation (e.g., CYP2D6*4, TPMT c.719A>G).']
  pharmacogenomics_marker_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the PGx marker record: active (current and approved for use), deprecated (superseded by newer version), under_review (pending curation or validation), or retired (no longer clinically relevant). Ensures data quality and governance.']
  pharmgkb_accession varchar [note: 'PharmGKB accession identifier for the variant, gene, or drug-gene interaction (e.g., PA166153631). Enables cross-reference to the PharmGKB knowledge base.']
  population_frequency decimal(8,6) [note: 'Global or population-specific minor allele frequency (MAF) for the variant, sourced from gnomAD, 1000 Genomes, or other population databases. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.123456 for 12.3456%). Null if frequency data unavailable.']
  population_group varchar [note: 'Population or ancestry group for which the allele frequency is reported (e.g., Global, European (Non-Finnish), East Asian, African/African American, Latino/Admixed American). Supports population-stratified PGx analysis.']
  predicted_phenotype varchar [note: 'Predicted metabolizer or functional phenotype associated with the marker or diplotype (e.g., Poor Metabolizer, Intermediate Metabolizer, Normal Metabolizer, Rapid Metabolizer, Ultrarapid Metabolizer; or Decreased Function, Normal Function, Increased Function). Core clinical actionability field.']
  reference_allele varchar [note: 'Reference genome allele sequence at the variant position (e.g., A, G, ACGT). Null for star alleles or diplotypes that represent multi-variant haplotypes.']
  rsid varchar [note: 'dbSNP reference SNP cluster identifier (rsID) for the variant, if applicable (e.g., rs1065852, rs3918290). Null for star alleles or complex haplotypes not represented by a single SNP.']
  star_allele_designation varchar [note: 'Named star allele designation for pharmacogenes with established allele nomenclature (e.g., *1, *2, *4, *17 for CYP2D6; *2, *3A for TPMT). Null for variants not part of a star allele system.']
  variant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pharmacogenomic variant type: SNP (Single Nucleotide Polymorphism), CNV (Copy Number Variation), indel (insertion/deletion), star_allele (named allele designation), haplotype (phased variant combination), or diplotype (two-allele genotype).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this PGx marker record (e.g., v1.0, v2.1). Supports versioned reference data management and reproducible genomic analysis pipelines.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of pharmacogenomics (PGx) variant markers and star-allele/diplotype definitions sourced from PharmGKB, CPIC, DPWG, and FDA PGx labeling. Each record captures a clinically actionable gene-drug interaction: gene symbol, defining variant(s), diplotype designation (e.g., CYP2D6*1/*4), predicted metabolizer phenotype, associated drug(s), evidence level (CPIC 1A-4), guideline reference, and clinical actionability status. Enables PGx-informed clinical reporting, drug dosing recommendations, and companion diagnostic assay design.'
}

Table reference.gene_tag_assignment {
  gene_tag_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this gene-tag assignment record. Primary key.']
  catalog_tag_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the catalog tag being applied']
  gene_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the gene model being tagged']
  assigned_by varchar [note: 'Username or system identifier of the user or automated process that created this tag assignment.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The date when this tag was assigned to the gene. Tracks when the classification was established.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this tag assignment. Values: active, pending_review, approved, deprecated, rejected.']
  classification_algorithm varchar [note: 'Name or version identifier of the algorithm, rule set, or curation process that assigned this tag. Examples: CancerGeneCensus_v95, PharmGKB_import_2024Q1, manual_curation.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Quantitative confidence score (0.0 to 1.0) indicating the certainty or strength of evidence for this tag assignment. Used for algorithmic or curated classifications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this tag assignment is considered active and valid for use in data catalog search and filtering.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date on which this tag assignment was deprecated or superseded. Null for currently active assignments. Supports temporal tracking of classification changes.']
  evidence_source varchar [note: 'Reference to the external database, publication, or internal analysis that provides evidence for this tag assignment. Examples: PMID:12345678, ClinVar, internal_variant_analysis.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this tag assignment, including rationale, caveats, or review comments.']
  review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tag assignment requires manual review or approval by a data steward before being published in the data catalog.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the classification assignment between gene_model and catalog_tag. It captures the controlled tagging of genes with genomics-specific classification labels (cancer gene, pharmacogene, ACMG secondary finding, essential gene) for data catalog discovery and faceted search. Each record links one gene_model to one catalog_tag with metadata about the assignment provenance, confidence, and lifecycle.. Existence Justification: In genomics data catalog operations, a single gene model is tagged with multiple catalog tags (e.g., cancer_gene, pharmacogene, essential_gene, ACMG_secondary_finding) to enable faceted search and discovery, and each catalog tag applies to many genes. The business actively manages these tag assignments as a classification and curation workflow, with assignment dates, confidence scores, algorithm provenance, review status, and temporal validity tracking. This is a recognized operational entity in genomics data governance.'
}

Table reference.variant_database_qualification {
  variant_database_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the variant_database_qualification association']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee who holds the qualification for this variant database']
  qualified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the supervisor or trainer who assessed and certified this qualification. Required for CLIA documentation and audit trail.']
  variant_database_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the variant database for which the employee is qualified']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score from the qualification assessment or competency test (0.00 to 100.00). Used to document proficiency level and identify personnel requiring additional training.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the qualification certification. Active certifications allow work assignment; expired or suspended certifications require retraining. Values: Active, Expired, Suspended, Pending Renewal.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this qualification record was created in the system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this qualification expires and requires renewal or requalification. Driven by CLIA competency assessment requirements and internal quality management policies.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this qualification record was last modified.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Most recent date when the employee actively used this variant database in annotation or interpretation work. Used to track ongoing competency and identify personnel requiring refresher training.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special conditions, limitations, or observations related to this qualification (e.g., \'Qualified for somatic variants only\', \'Requires supervision for COSMIC interpretation\').']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'Current proficiency level of the employee for this specific variant database. Determines work assignment eligibility and supervision requirements. Values: Trainee, Qualified, Expert, Supervisor.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the employee successfully completed qualification training and assessment for this variant database. Used for CLIA competency documentation and requalification scheduling.']
  training_version varchar [note: 'Version of the variant database on which the employee was trained and qualified. Critical for determining whether retraining is required when database versions are updated with significant schema or content changes.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the qualification and competency certification between clinical variant scientists and variant databases used in annotation pipelines. It captures CLIA-mandated competency assessment, training completion, proficiency levels, and ongoing usage tracking required for regulatory compliance in clinical genomics laboratories. Each record links one employee to one variant database with qualification metadata that exists only in the context of this competency relationship.. Existence Justification: In clinical genomics laboratories operating under CLIA regulations, variant scientists must be individually qualified to interpret variants using specific databases (ClinVar, gnomAD, COSMIC, etc.). Each scientist holds qualifications for multiple databases, and each database requires multiple qualified interpreters to ensure operational coverage. The qualification relationship is actively managed through training programs, competency assessments, proficiency tracking, and periodic recertification—making it a core operational business process, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table commercial.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing campaign record. Primary key.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Marketing campaigns in genomics biotech target specific products (e.g., new sequencer launch campaign, reagent kit promotion). Campaign ROI tracking and product marketing planning require linking camp']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Campaigns typically target product families (e.g., "NGS Platform" campaign) rather than individual SKUs. Strategic marketing planning, budget allocation, and campaign performance analysis by product l']
  market_segment_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.market_segment_target. Business justification: Marketing campaigns target specific market segments. Adding market_segment_target_id FK links the campaign to the authoritative segment definition, enabling segment-based campaign performance analysis']
  parent_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent or umbrella campaign if this campaign is part of a larger multi-wave or integrated marketing initiative. Null for standalone campaigns.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when the campaign execution concluded. May differ from planned end date due to extensions or early termination.']
  actual_lead_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of qualified leads generated by the campaign to date.']
  actual_opportunity_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of sales opportunities created or influenced by the campaign to date.']
  actual_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual revenue pipeline value attributed to the campaign in USD, tracked through opportunity influence.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the campaign execution commenced. May differ from planned start date due to operational adjustments.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the campaign plan and budget: pending (awaiting management review), approved (authorized to proceed), rejected (not approved for execution).']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the executive or manager who approved the campaign plan and budget. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign was formally approved for execution. Null if not yet approved.']
  budget_allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total financial budget allocated for this campaign in USD, covering all channels and activities.']
  budget_spent_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount spent to date on campaign execution in USD, tracked against allocated budget.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the campaign, used in marketing materials, tracking URLs, and cross-system references.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the campaign objectives, target messaging, and strategic rationale.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the marketing campaign (e.g., \'Q1 2024 NGS Product Launch\', \'ASHG 2024 Conference Sponsorship\').']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign: draft (initial planning), planned (approved but not started), active (currently executing), completed (finished and closed), cancelled (terminated before completion), on_hold (temporarily paused).']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign execution model: digital (online ads, social media), field_marketing (regional events, roadshows), conference (industry event sponsorship such as ASHG, AGBT), email (targeted email campaigns), webinar (virtual educational sessions), direct_mail (physical collateral distribution).']
  channel_mix varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of marketing channels utilized in this campaign (e.g., \'email, social_media, webinar, conference_booth, direct_mail\').']
  conference_location varchar [note: 'City and country where the conference is held (e.g., \'San Diego, USA\', \'Barcelona, Spain\'). Null for non-conference campaigns.']
  conference_name varchar [note: 'Name of the industry conference or event if this campaign is conference-based (e.g., \'ASHG 2024\', \'AGBT 2024\', \'ASCO Annual Meeting\'). Null for non-conference campaigns.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for budget amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of the campaign: global (worldwide), north_america (USA, Canada, Mexico), emea (Europe, Middle East, Africa), apac (Asia-Pacific), latam (Latin America), usa (United States only), multi_region (custom combination). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: global|north_america|emea|apac|latam|usa|multi_region — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP internal order number used for detailed budget tracking and cost allocation for this campaign.']
  kol_engagement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this campaign includes targeted engagement with key opinion leaders in the genomics research or clinical community (True) or not (False).']
  last_modified_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who last modified this campaign record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional campaign context, lessons learned, or operational notes.']
  owner_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the campaign owner for coordination and reporting.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the marketing manager or campaign lead responsible for planning, execution, and performance tracking.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the campaign is intended to conclude.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the campaign is intended to begin execution.']
  target_lead_count varchar [note: 'Planned number of qualified leads the campaign aims to generate.']
  target_opportunity_count varchar [note: 'Planned number of sales opportunities the campaign aims to create or influence.']
  target_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned revenue pipeline value the campaign aims to generate in USD.']

  Note: 'Master record for all commercial marketing campaigns executed by Genomics Biotech — including product launch campaigns, genomics conference sponsorships, digital demand-generation programs, field marketing events, and key opinion leader (KOL) engagement initiatives. Captures campaign name, type (digital, field, conference, email, webinar), target audience segment, associated product family, campaign owner, planned and actual start/end dates, budget allocation, channel mix, geographic scope, and campaign status lifecycle (draft, active, completed, cancelled).'
}

Table commercial.campaign_response {
  campaign_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign response record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the account associated with the responding contact. Null for anonymous or non-account responses.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated this response.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contact who responded to the campaign. Null for anonymous responses.']
  converted_to_lead_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lead record created as a result of this campaign response. Null if no lead was created.']
  converted_to_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the opportunity record created as a result of this campaign response. Null if no opportunity was created.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative assigned to follow up on this campaign response.']
  original_campaign_response_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on campaign_response (original_campaign_response_id)']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales territory to which this response is assigned for follow-up.']
  application_area varchar [note: 'The genomics application area or use case the respondent is interested in.']
  campaign_asset_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific campaign asset or content piece that triggered the response (e.g., whitepaper title, webinar topic).']
  consent_to_contact boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the respondent provided explicit consent to be contacted for marketing or sales purposes.']
  conversion_outcome varchar [note: 'The downstream conversion result or action taken as a result of this campaign response. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lead_created|opportunity_created|meeting_scheduled|quote_requested|trial_started|no_conversion|disqualified — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the response was converted to a lead or opportunity. Null if not yet converted.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign response record was first created in the system.']
  follow_up_status varchar [note: 'Status of the sales follow-up activity for this campaign response.']
  follow_up_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sales team first followed up on this campaign response. Null if not yet followed up.']
  is_anonymous boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the response was captured anonymously (true) or from an identified contact (false).']
  lead_score_after_response varchar [note: 'Total lead score of the contact after this response was recorded.']
  lead_score_delta varchar [note: 'Change in lead score attributed to this campaign response. Positive values indicate increased engagement.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign response record was last modified.']
  opt_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the respondent provided marketing consent or opt-in. Null if consent not provided.']
  product_interest varchar [note: 'Specific genomics product, service, or solution the respondent expressed interest in (e.g., NGS platform, WGS service, CRISPR tool).']
  research_focus varchar [note: 'Specific research focus or scientific area of interest indicated by the respondent (e.g., oncology, rare disease, microbiome).']
  respondent_company varchar [note: 'Company or organization name of the respondent as captured at the time of response.']
  respondent_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the respondent\'s geographic location.']
  respondent_email varchar [note: 'Email address provided by the respondent at the time of response. May be used for anonymous lead capture.']
  respondent_first_name varchar [note: 'First name of the respondent as captured at the time of response.']
  respondent_job_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the respondent as provided at the time of response.']
  respondent_last_name varchar [note: 'Last name of the respondent as captured at the time of response.']
  response_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or medium through which the response was captured (e.g., email, web form, conference booth). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|web|mobile_app|social_media|conference|webinar|direct_mail|phone|partner_portal|field_event — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  response_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for this campaign response, used for tracking and attribution across systems.']
  response_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments captured about this campaign response, including respondent questions or special requests.']
  response_source varchar [note: 'Specific source or referrer that drove the response (e.g., LinkedIn ad, Google search, partner referral, conference name).']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign response indicating progression through the lead qualification funnel.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the prospect or customer responded to the campaign. Principal business event timestamp.']
  response_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of engagement or action taken by the respondent in response to the campaign. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: webinar_registration|content_download|conference_booth_visit|email_click|demo_request|whitepaper_download|trial_signup|contact_form|phone_inquiry|event_attendance — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter from the campaign URL used for digital attribution tracking.']
  utm_content varchar [note: 'UTM content parameter from the campaign URL used for A/B testing and content differentiation.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter from the campaign URL used for digital attribution tracking.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source parameter from the campaign URL used for digital attribution tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual prospect or customer responses to a marketing campaign — including webinar registrations, content downloads, conference booth interactions, email click-throughs, and demo requests. Captures response date/time, response channel, respondent identity (contact or anonymous), campaign attribution, response type, lead score delta, and conversion outcome. Serves as the raw engagement signal feeding lead generation and pipeline attribution for genomics product campaigns.'
}

Table commercial.lead {
  lead_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lead record. Primary key for the lead entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the account record if this lead has been associated with an existing customer account.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the marketing campaign associated with this lead.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the contact record created upon lead conversion.']
  converted_from_lead_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lead (converted_from_lead_id)']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the user (SDR, BDR, or sales rep) who owns this lead.']
  market_segment_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.market_segment_target. Business justification: Leads are generated for and qualified against specific market segments. Adding market_segment_target_id FK links the lead to the target segment definition, enabling segment-based lead routing, qualifi']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the opportunity record created upon lead conversion.']
  annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated annual revenue of the lead\'s organization, used for firmographic scoring and segmentation.']
  assigned_sdr_bdr varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the SDR or BDR assigned to work and qualify this lead.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the lead is located.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date when marketing consent was obtained or last updated.']
  consent_marketing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lead has provided consent to receive marketing communications, per GDPR and privacy regulations.']
  contact_first_name varchar [note: 'First name of the individual prospect contact.']
  contact_last_name varchar [note: 'Last name of the individual prospect contact.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'Date when the lead was converted to an opportunity, contact, and/or account.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the lead\'s country location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lead record was first created in the CRM system.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or rationale for disqualifying the lead (e.g., budget constraints, no authority, wrong fit, unresponsive).']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the lead contact, used for outreach and communication.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Number of employees at the lead\'s organization, used for firmographic profiling.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or territory where the lead is located (north_america, europe, asia_pacific, latin_america, middle_east_africa).']
  industry varchar [note: 'Industry sector or vertical of the lead\'s organization (e.g., pharmaceuticals, academic research, clinical diagnostics, agriculture).']
  interest_area varchar [note: 'Expressed area of interest or product category the lead is exploring (NGS, arrays, CRISPR, diagnostics, bioinformatics, reagents, instruments). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NGS|arrays|CRISPR|diagnostics|bioinformatics|reagents|instruments — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_converted boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the lead has been converted to an opportunity, contact, and/or account.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Professional job title or role of the lead contact within their organization.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent activity or interaction with the lead (email, call, meeting, web visit).']
  lead_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or notes about the lead, including context from the initial inquiry or interaction.']
  lead_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the lead, typically auto-generated in format LED-########.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the lead was generated (e.g., conference, web inquiry, content syndication, partner referral, field marketing event, trade show).']
  lead_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lead (new, working, qualified, disqualified, converted, nurture). Tracks progression through the sales funnel.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lead record was last modified or updated.']
  organization_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or company associated with the lead prospect.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number of the lead contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the lead\'s location.']
  quality varchar [note: 'Assessed quality of the lead based on fit, engagement, and qualification criteria (high, medium, low).']
  rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the lead\'s potential or priority (hot, warm, cold).']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Specific partner, individual, or entity that referred this lead, if applicable.']
  score varchar [note: 'Numeric score representing the lead\'s likelihood to convert, typically calculated based on engagement, firmographics, and behavioral signals.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the lead is located.']
  street_address varchar [note: 'Street address of the lead\'s organization or contact location.']
  website varchar [note: 'Website URL of the lead\'s organization.']

  Note: 'Master record representing an unqualified commercial prospect generated through inbound or outbound demand-generation activities — including conference leads, web inquiries, content syndication, partner referrals, and field marketing events. Captures lead source, lead status (new, working, qualified, disqualified, converted), prospect organization name, contact name, email, phone, job title, expressed interest area (NGS, arrays, CRISPR, diagnostics), geographic region, assigned SDR/BDR, lead score, and conversion date to opportunity. SSOT for top-of-funnel prospect identity prior to CRM opportunity creation.'
}

Table commercial.opportunity {
  opportunity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the commercial opportunity record. Primary key for the opportunity entity.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Opportunities are categorized by primary product family for pipeline analysis, forecasting, and quota attainment tracking. Sales operations and executive reporting require structured product family se']
  market_segment_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.market_segment_target. Business justification: Opportunities are pursued within specific market segments. Adding market_segment_target_id FK links the opportunity to the authoritative segment definition, enabling segment-based pipeline analytics, ']
  opportunity_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Commercial opportunities must link to customer accounts for pipeline reporting, territory assignment validation, and account-level forecasting.']
  opportunity_partner_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the channel partner account involved in this opportunity, if applicable. Used for partner-sourced or co-sell deals.']
  parent_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on opportunity (parent_opportunity_id)']
  price_book_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.price_book. Business justification: Opportunities use specific price books for quoting based on customer segment, geography, and sales channel. Adding price_book_id FK links the opportunity to the price book being used, enabling pricing']
  primary_opportunity_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with this opportunity. Links to the account master record in the customer domain.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory to which this opportunity is assigned. Used for territory performance analysis and quota management.']
  actual_close_date date [note: 'Actual date when the opportunity was closed. Populated only after the opportunity reaches closed-won or closed-lost stage.']
  application_area varchar [note: 'Primary application area or use case for the genomics solution being pursued. Used for market segment analysis and product-market fit assessment.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the opportunity received final management approval for special terms or pricing. Populated only when approval is required and granted.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of management approval for special pricing, terms, or deal structure. Required for deals exceeding standard authority limits.']
  arr decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual recurring revenue value for subscription-based products and services (e.g., bioinformatics software licenses, service contracts). Expressed in USD.']
  close_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the opportunity will be closed (won or lost). Used for revenue forecasting and pipeline management.']
  competitive_landscape varchar [note: 'Description of competitive dynamics for this opportunity, including known competitors, their positioning, and our competitive advantages. Used for win/loss analysis.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the contract or service agreement in months. Used for calculating ARR and contract lifecycle management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was first created in the system. Used for opportunity age analysis and pipeline velocity metrics.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the opportunity financial values. Supports multi-currency deal management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CNY|AUD|CAD|CHF|SGD|INR — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  days_in_current_stage varchar [note: 'Number of days the opportunity has been in its current stage. Calculated field used for pipeline velocity analysis and identifying stalled opportunities.']
  deal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deal based on customer relationship status. New logo = first purchase, expansion = additional products to existing customer, renewal = contract renewal, upgrade = replacement of existing product.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total discount percentage applied to list price for this opportunity. Used for pricing analysis and margin management.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter in which the opportunity is expected to close, based on close date. Format: Q#-FY#### (e.g., Q2-FY2024). Used for quarterly revenue forecasting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the opportunity is expected to close, based on close date. Used for annual revenue planning and performance tracking.']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'Sales forecast category indicating confidence level for revenue recognition. Pipeline = early stage, best case = possible, commit = highly likely, closed = won.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the opportunity has been closed (won or lost). True = closed, False = still active in pipeline.']
  is_won boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the opportunity was won. True = closed-won, False = closed-lost or still open. Only meaningful when is_closed is True.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was last updated. Used for tracking sales activity and pipeline hygiene.']
  last_stage_change_date date [note: 'Date when the opportunity last moved to a different stage. Used for calculating stage duration and identifying stalled deals.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Original source or channel through which this opportunity was generated. Used for marketing attribution and ROI analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inbound_web|trade_show|partner_referral|direct_outreach|existing_customer|conference|publication — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_step varchar [note: 'Description of the next action or milestone required to advance the opportunity. Used for pipeline hygiene and sales coaching.']
  opportunity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the opportunity scope, customer requirements, proposed solution, and strategic importance. Free-text field for context and notes.']
  opportunity_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or title of the opportunity, typically describing the customer and product scope (e.g., \'Acme Labs - NovaSeq 6000 Expansion\').']
  opportunity_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the opportunity, used in communications and reporting. Format: OPP-########.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment terms for the opportunity. Defines when and how the customer will pay for products and services.']
  primary_competitor varchar [note: 'Name of the primary competitor identified in this opportunity. Used for competitive intelligence and market share analysis.']
  probability decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability of winning this opportunity, expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00). Used for weighted pipeline forecasting.']
  regulatory_pathway varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the products or services in this opportunity. RUO = Research Use Only, IVD = In Vitro Diagnostic, LDT = Laboratory Developed Test, CE-IVD = European conformity for diagnostics.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this opportunity data originated. Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the opportunity in the sales pipeline lifecycle. Represents progression from initial prospecting through closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospecting|qualification|solution_design|proposal|negotiation|closed_won|closed_lost — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tcv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contract value including all one-time and recurring revenue over the contract lifetime. Includes instruments, reagents, consumables, and services. Expressed in USD.']
  win_loss_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the primary win or loss reason. Used for structured win/loss analysis and trend identification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: price|product_features|service_quality|competitor|timing|budget|relationship|technical_fit — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  win_loss_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the opportunity was won or lost. Captured at closure for continuous improvement and competitive analysis.']

  Note: 'Core commercial opportunity record representing a qualified sales pursuit for Genomics Biotech products and services — sequencing instruments, reagent kits, array platforms, CRISPR tools, bioinformatics software licenses, and professional services. Captures opportunity name, stage (prospecting, qualification, solution design, proposal, negotiation, closed-won, closed-lost), close date, ARR/TCV, probability, primary product family, competitive landscape, deal type (new logo, expansion, renewal), assigned account executive, territory, and win/loss reason. SSOT for pipeline management and revenue forecasting.'
}

Table commercial.opportunity_line {
  opportunity_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the opportunity line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: Opportunity lines for regulated genomics products must reference the specific regulatory approval under which the product can be sold in the customer\'s jurisdiction. Essential for compliance validatio']
  assay_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.clinical_assay_version. Business justification: Specific assay versions are sometimes specified in commercial contracts (e.g., "NGS Panel v3.2" for regulatory-locked protocols). Version-level tracking enables accurate delivery commitments, change c']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Optional identifier linking multiple line items that are sold together as a configured bundle or package deal. Null for standalone line items.']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Opportunity lines must reference specific reagent catalog items for accurate quoting, pricing validation, and revenue recognition. Essential for quote-to-order flow and product-level sales analytics i']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the catalog product, instrument, reagent kit, or service offering being sold in this line item.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this opportunity line item record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  device_identifier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.device_identifier. Business justification: UDI (Unique Device Identifier) is required on commercial transactions for regulated IVD products per FDA/EU MDR requirements. Opportunity lines must reference the correct UDI-DI for traceability, regu']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field application scientist or sales engineer providing technical support for this product line in the opportunity.']
  gene_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_model. Business justification: Genomics biotech sells gene panels and targeted sequencing products. Opportunity lines must track which genes are included in custom panel configurations for pricing, regulatory documentation, and tec']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Sequencing products and bioinformatics pipelines are sold with specific genome build compatibility (GRCh37/GRCh38). Sales quotes must specify reference genome for customer compatibility verification a']
  installed_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.installed_instrument. Business justification: Opportunity lines for service contract renewals, instrument upgrades, or consumable subscriptions reference specific installed instruments. Sales teams configure quotes based on installed base serial ']
  ivd_test_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_catalog. Business justification: Specific IVD tests from clinical catalog are sold as line items in commercial opportunities. Essential for product-level revenue forecasting, catalog performance analysis, and linking commercial pipel']
  metadata_schema_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.metadata_schema. Business justification: Each genomics product (sequencing platform, array kit) generates data conforming to specific metadata schemas (FASTQ, VCF, IDAT, BED). Product configuration and customer onboarding require schema spec']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_model. Business justification: Opportunity line items for capital equipment sales reference specific instrument models (e.g., NovaSeq 6000, MiSeq). Critical for product mix analysis, sales forecasting by platform, competitive win/l']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this opportunity line item record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent commercial opportunity that contains this line item. Links this product line to the overall deal.']
  partner_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the channel partner or distributor account involved in this line item sale. Null for direct sales. Used for partner commission calculation.']
  substituted_opportunity_line_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on opportunity_line (substituted_opportunity_line_id)']
  application_area varchar [note: 'The primary genomics application area or use case for this product line. Enables segmentation by scientific domain and customer workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clinical_genomics|research_genomics|agricultural_genomics|population_health|oncology|rare_disease|reproductive_health|microbiology|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when pricing or discount approval was granted for this line item. Null if approval is not required or still pending.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of pricing or discount approval workflow for this line item. Large discounts or non-standard pricing may require management approval.']
  commission_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The sales commission percentage applicable to this line item. May vary by product family, sales territory, or partner involvement. Confidential compensation data.']
  competitor_product varchar [note: 'Name of the competing product or platform that this line item is being positioned against. Used for competitive analysis and win/loss tracking.']
  configuration_attributes varchar [note: 'JSON or delimited string capturing product configuration options selected for this line. Examples: flow cell type, read length, multiplexing capacity, software modules enabled.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'The duration in months for subscription or service contracts associated with this line item. Null for one-time product sales. Common values: 12, 24, 36 months.']
  cost_of_goods_sold decimal(18,2) [note: 'The standard cost to manufacture or procure this product per unit. Used for margin analysis and profitability reporting. Confidential financial data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this opportunity line item record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format with timezone.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CNY.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the list price to arrive at the sales price. Calculated as ((list_price - sales_price) / list_price) * 100.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'The expected delivery or fulfillment date for this line item based on current lead times and production schedules. Used for customer expectation management.']
  gross_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The gross profit margin percentage for this line item. Calculated as ((sales_price - cost_of_goods_sold) / sales_price) * 100. Confidential financial metric.']
  is_primary_product boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this line item represents the primary product in the opportunity (e.g., the main sequencing instrument) versus ancillary products (reagents, accessories, services).']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The number of days from order placement to expected delivery for this product. Varies by product type: instruments may have 60-90 day lead times, consumables 7-14 days.']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about this line item. May include special terms, customer-specific configurations, or delivery instructions.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the parent opportunity. Used for ordering and display purposes in multi-product deals.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this individual opportunity line item. Tracks the progression from initial quote through approval and ordering. Line status may differ from overall opportunity status. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|quoted|negotiation|approved|ordered|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  line_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount for this line item before taxes. Calculated as quantity * sales_price. Represents the extended line value.']
  list_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The published catalog list price per unit in the opportunity currency. Represents the standard price before any discounts or negotiations.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this opportunity line item record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization. Follows ISO 8601 format with timezone.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The number of units being quoted or sold in this line item. May represent instrument count, reagent kit boxes, software licenses, or service hours depending on product type.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory status of the product being sold. Research Use Only (RUO) products cannot be used for clinical diagnostics; In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) products are clinically validated and approved.']
  requires_installation boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this product requires professional installation services. True for sequencing instruments and array scanners; false for reagent kits and software.']
  revenue_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Defines how revenue from this line will be recognized over time. Critical for subscription-based bioinformatics software and reagent rental programs.']
  revenue_type varchar [note: 'Classification of revenue recognition type for this line item. Determines accounting treatment and revenue recognition schedule per ASC 606 / IFRS 15.']
  sales_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The negotiated sales price per unit after discounts, volume pricing, or contract terms have been applied. This is the actual price the customer will pay.']
  service_end_date date [note: 'The planned or actual end date for service delivery, subscription access, or maintenance coverage. Null for perpetual licenses or one-time sales.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'The planned or actual start date for service delivery, subscription access, or maintenance coverage for this line item. Used for revenue recognition and fulfillment scheduling.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantity field. Genomics-specific units include runs (sequencing runs), lanes (flow cell lanes), samples, or gigabases of sequencing output. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|box|kit|license|subscription|hour|run|sample|lane|gigabase — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Individual product line item within a commercial opportunity, representing a specific catalog item, SKU, or service offering being pursued in the deal. Captures catalog item reference, quantity, list price, negotiated price, discount percentage, product family, application area (clinical genomics, research, agricultural), and line-level status. Enables multi-product opportunity modeling for complex genomics deals combining instruments, reagent subscriptions, and bioinformatics software.'
}

Table commercial.territory {
  territory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales territory record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or account manager assigned primary responsibility for this territory. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  parent_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent territory in a hierarchical territory structure, enabling rollup reporting from sub-territories to regional or national territories. Null for top-level territories.']
  account_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer accounts currently assigned to this territory. Updated periodically from account assignment rules.']
  annual_quota_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual sales quota target assigned to this territory in USD. Used for performance tracking and commission calculation.']
  assignment_rule varchar [note: 'Business rule or criteria used to automatically assign accounts to this territory (e.g., \'All academic institutions in postal codes 02101-02199 with annual revenue > $500K\'). Supports automated territory management.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or metropolitan area for highly localized territory definitions. Used for dense urban markets requiring granular territory segmentation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country for this territory (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, CHN, JPN).']
  coverage_model varchar [note: 'Defines how accounts in this territory are covered: exclusive (single territory ownership), shared (multiple territories can claim same accounts), or overlay (specialized coverage layered on top of base territory).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this territory record was first created in the system. Audit trail for territory lifecycle tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this territory definition expires or was retired. Null for currently active territories. Enables historical territory analysis and transition management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this territory definition became or will become active for account assignment and quota tracking. Supports territory restructuring with historical accuracy.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code for the primary business language in this territory (e.g., \'en\', \'de\', \'fr\', \'ja\', \'zh\'). Used for localized marketing and support materials.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Primary market segment focus for this territory, aligned with Genomics Biotech\'s core customer verticals. Determines product portfolio emphasis and sales approach. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: academic_research|clinical_labs|biopharma|agricultural_genomics|diagnostics|government|healthcare_systems — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this territory record. Audit trail for change accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this territory record. Enables change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional territory context, special instructions, coverage exceptions, or strategic considerations not captured in structured fields.']
  postal_code_range varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or range of postal codes included in this territory for precise geographic boundary definition (e.g., \'02101-02199,02201\').']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Strategic priority classification for resource allocation and management attention. Strategic territories receive highest investment and executive focus.']
  product_focus varchar [note: 'Primary product lines or solution categories emphasized in this territory (e.g., \'NGS Sequencing Systems\', \'Array-Based Genotyping\', \'CRISPR Gene Editing Tools\', \'Bioinformatics Software\'). Comma-separated for multi-product territories.']
  quota_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the quota amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Enables multi-currency quota management for international territories.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broader geographic region classification (e.g., \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'Asia Pacific\', \'Latin America\') used for regional sales rollup and management hierarchy.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory jurisdiction governing product sales and clinical use in this territory (e.g., \'FDA\', \'EMA\', \'NMPA\', \'TGA\'). Critical for compliance with IVD and medical device regulations.']
