  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel through which this territory is served: direct (Genomics Biotech sales force), distributor (third-party distribution), OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer partnership), partner (channel partner), online (e-commerce), or hybrid (multiple channels).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative subdivision within the country for geographically-defined territories. Null for non-geographic territory types.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the territory used in sales operations and reporting. Typically alphanumeric format assigned by sales operations.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the sales territory, typically reflecting geographic or market segment focus (e.g., \'Northeast Academic Research\', \'West Coast Clinical Labs\').']
  territory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the territory. Active territories are operational and assigned; inactive territories are no longer in use; pending territories are being set up; suspended territories are temporarily paused; archived territories are historical records.']
  territory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of territory structure: geographic (region-based), named_account (specific high-value accounts), vertical (industry segment), hybrid (combination), strategic (key accounts), or overlay (specialized coverage).']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the territory\'s primary location (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\', \'Asia/Tokyo\'). Used for scheduling and activity tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this territory record. Audit trail for accountability.']

  Note: 'Master record defining commercial sales territories used to organize and assign account coverage responsibilities across Genomics Biotech\'s global sales organization. Captures territory name, territory code, geographic scope (country, region, state/province), market segment focus (academic research, clinical labs, biopharma, agricultural genomics), assigned territory manager, territory type (named account, geographic, vertical), parent territory for hierarchical rollup, and active status. Enables territory-based pipeline reporting, quota assignment, and account routing.'
}

Table commercial.territory_assignment {
  territory_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the territory assignment record. Primary key for the territory assignment entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account being assigned to the territory. Nullable when the assignment is for a sales representative without a specific account.']
  authorized_orderer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.authorized_orderer. Business justification: Ordering physicians are assigned to sales territories for coverage planning and quota allocation. Territory managers need to know which orderers are in their geography for account planning, relationsh']
  previous_territory_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on territory_assignment (previous_territory_assignment_id)']
  primary_territory_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative assigned to the territory. Nullable when the assignment is for an account without a specific representative.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory to which the account or representative is assigned. Links to the territory master entity.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this territory assignment was formally approved by management. Used for compliance and audit trail.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about the territory assignment, including special instructions, coverage agreements, transition plans, or historical context. Used for knowledge transfer and audit trail.']
  assignment_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for this assignment when multiple representatives are assigned to the same territory or account. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for commission crediting and conflict resolution. Typically ranges from 1 (highest) to 10 (lowest).']
  assignment_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason or trigger for creating this territory assignment. Territory realignment indicates periodic territory restructuring; new hire indicates assignment for a newly onboarded representative; promotion indicates role elevation; transfer indicates lateral move; account growth indicates expansion of coverage; strategic coverage indicates deliberate overlay for key accounts; backfill indicates replacement for departed representative; organizational change indicates structural reorganization; merger acquisition indicates integration activity; performance based indicates assignment driven by performance metrics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: territory_realignment|new_hire|promotion|transfer|account_growth|strategic_coverage|backfill|organizational_change|merger_acquisition|performance_based — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assignment_role varchar [note: 'The functional role of the sales representative in this territory assignment. Account Executive (AE) handles sales; Field Application Engineer (FAE) provides technical pre-sales support; Customer Success Manager (CSM) manages post-sales relationships; Clinical Specialist supports clinical assay applications; Technical Support provides troubleshooting; Sales Engineer provides technical sales expertise; Territory Manager oversees territory strategy; Regional Director manages multiple territories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: account_executive|field_application_engineer|customer_success_manager|clinical_specialist|technical_support|sales_engineer|territory_manager|regional_director — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the territory assignment. Active indicates the assignment is currently in effect; inactive indicates the assignment has been deactivated; pending indicates the assignment is scheduled but not yet effective; suspended indicates temporary hold; expired indicates the assignment has passed its end date.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory assignment indicating the nature of the relationship. Primary indicates the main account owner or territory owner; overlay indicates additional coverage; specialist indicates subject matter expert coverage (e.g., Field Application Engineer (FAE), Clinical Specialist); support indicates secondary support role; backup indicates contingency coverage; temporary indicates short-term assignment.']
  commission_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this territory assignment qualifies the representative for commission on sales within the territory or account. True indicates commission eligibility; False indicates non-commission assignment (e.g., support roles).']
  commission_split_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of commission allocated to this representative for sales in this territory or account when multiple representatives share commission. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 50.00 for 50%). Must sum to 100.00 across all commission-eligible assignments for the same territory/account.']
  coverage_model varchar [note: 'The sales coverage model applied to this territory assignment. Direct indicates direct sales by company representatives; channel indicates sales through distribution partners; hybrid indicates combination of direct and channel; inside sales indicates remote/phone-based coverage; field sales indicates in-person territory coverage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this territory assignment record was first created in the lakehouse. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_segment_focus varchar [note: 'The customer segment or market vertical that this territory assignment targets. Academic research includes universities and research institutes; clinical diagnostics includes hospitals and diagnostic labs; pharmaceutical biotech includes drug development companies; agricultural genomics includes crop and livestock genomics; government includes public health and regulatory agencies; Contract Research Organization (CRO) includes outsourced research services; reference laboratory includes high-throughput testing facilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: academic_research|clinical_diagnostics|pharmaceutical_biotech|agricultural_genomics|government|contract_research_organization|reference_laboratory — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this territory assignment ends. Nullable for open-ended assignments. Used for historical territory coverage analysis and commission cutoff.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this territory assignment becomes active and the representative or account is officially assigned to the territory. Used for historical tracking and commission crediting.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the geographic boundaries or scope of this territory assignment. May include country codes, state/province names, postal code ranges, or named regions (e.g., \'USA-CA-Northern\', \'EMEA-UK-London\', \'APAC-Japan\').']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this territory assignment is currently active and in effect. True indicates the assignment is active; False indicates the assignment is inactive or historical. Derived from assignment_status and effective dates but maintained for query performance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this territory assignment record was last modified in the lakehouse. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  product_focus varchar [note: 'The product line, product family, or application area that this territory assignment focuses on. Examples include \'NGS Sequencing Systems\', \'Array-Based Genotyping\', \'CRISPR Gene Editing Tools\', \'Clinical IVD Assays\', \'Research Use Only (RUO) Reagents\'. Used for specialist and overlay assignments.']
  quota_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the territory or account quota allocated to this representative. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 100.00 for full quota ownership, 50.00 for shared quota). Used for performance tracking and quota attainment calculations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the operational system from which this territory assignment record originated. Typically \'Salesforce CRM\' or \'SAP SD\' for genomics biotech companies. Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this territory assignment record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and bidirectional synchronization.']

  Note: 'Association record linking customer accounts and sales representatives to territories, capturing the effective assignment period and assignment type (primary, overlay, specialist). Tracks assignment start date, end date, assignment reason, assigned rep role (AE, FAE, CSM, specialist), and whether the assignment is active. Enables historical territory coverage analysis, commission crediting, and account ownership auditing across territory realignments.'
}

Table commercial.sales_quota {
  sales_quota_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales quota record. Primary key for the sales quota entity.']
  compensation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the incentive compensation plan that governs commission and bonus calculations for this quota. Links quota attainment to payout rules.']
  parent_quota_sales_quota_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent quota record in a hierarchical quota structure (e.g., a regional director\'s quota may be the parent of multiple territory manager quotas). Null for top-level quotas.']
  primary_sales_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative, territory manager, or regional director to whom this quota is assigned.']
  quaternary_sales_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this quota record.']
  revised_sales_quota_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sales_quota (revised_sales_quota_id)']
  sales_holder_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  tertiary_sales_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this quota record in the system.']
  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this quota was activated and became effective for attainment tracking. Null if not yet activated.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status for this quota record. Pending: awaiting management approval; Approved: authorized for activation; Rejected: not approved and requires revision.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this quota was approved by management. Null if not yet approved.']
  base_quota_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The baseline or standard quota amount before any adjustments, accelerators, or stretch targets are applied. Used to calculate attainment against the original target.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this quota period was closed and final attainment was calculated. Null if still active or not yet closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this quota record was first created in the system.']
  customer_segment_scope varchar [note: 'The customer segment or market vertical to which this quota applies. Null or \'all_segments\' if quota applies across all customer types.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter within the fiscal year for which this quota applies. Values: Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4 for quarterly quotas, or FY for full-year quotas.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year for which this quota is assigned, represented as a four-digit year (e.g., 2024).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this quota record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or clarifications regarding this quota assignment (e.g., carve-outs, special conditions, strategic focus areas).']
  product_family_scope varchar [note: 'The product family or product line to which this quota applies (e.g., NGS Sequencing Instruments, Microarray Platforms, CRISPR Gene Editing Tools, Reagents and Consumables, Bioinformatics Software). Null if quota applies to all products.']
  quota_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the parent quota (e.g., regional or team quota) allocated to this individual quota holder. Used in hierarchical quota rollup structures.']
  quota_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The target value assigned to the quota holder for the specified period. For revenue and ARR quotas, this is a monetary amount. For unit and count quotas, this is a numeric quantity.']
  quota_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary quota amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Null for non-monetary quotas such as unit or count targets.']
  quota_holder_role varchar [note: 'The sales role or position of the quota holder within the commercial organization hierarchy.']
  quota_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the quota plan, typically following format QTA-YYYY-NNNNNN where YYYY is fiscal year and NNNNNN is sequential number.']
  quota_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the period for which this quota is effective, typically the end of the fiscal quarter or year.']
  quota_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the period for which this quota is effective, typically the start of the fiscal quarter or year.']
  quota_source varchar [note: 'The methodology used to derive this quota: top-down allocation (executive-driven targets cascaded down), bottoms-up plan (aggregated from territory forecasts), hybrid (combination of both), carryover (rolled from prior period), or stretch target (aspirational goal beyond base quota).']
  quota_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quota record. Draft: under construction; Approved: finalized but not yet active; Active: currently in effect; Closed: period completed; Suspended: temporarily inactive; Cancelled: voided before completion.']
  quota_type varchar [note: 'The category of quota target being measured: revenue (total bookings), instrument units (sequencer/array platform sales count), reagent ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue from reagent subscriptions), consumable units (kits and consumables volume), new logo count (new customer acquisition), or service contract value (support and maintenance contracts).']
  quota_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the quota amount is expressed: currency (for revenue quotas), units (for instrument/consumable volume), count (for new logos), or ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue for subscription products).']
  stretch_quota_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'An aspirational quota target above the base quota, often tied to accelerated commission rates or bonus tiers. Null if no stretch target is defined.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic territory or sales region to which this quota applies (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, US East, US West, specific country or state). Null if quota applies globally.']

  Note: 'Quota plan record defining revenue, unit, and activity targets assigned to individual sales representatives, territory managers, and regional directors for a given fiscal period. Captures quota holder identity, quota type (revenue, instrument units, reagent ARR, new logo count), fiscal year, fiscal quarter, quota amount, currency, product family scope, territory scope, quota status (draft, approved, active, closed), and quota source (top-down allocation, bottoms-up plan). Enables attainment tracking and incentive compensation calculation.'
}

Table commercial.channel_partner {
  channel_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the channel partner record. Primary key for the channel partner entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Genomics Biotech employee (Partner Account Manager or Channel Manager) responsible for managing the relationship with this partner.']
  market_segment_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.market_segment_target. Business justification: Channel partners often specialize in or focus on specific market segments based on their expertise, customer base, and go-to-market capabilities. Adding market_segment_target_id FK links the partner t']
  parent_channel_partner_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on channel_partner (parent_channel_partner_id)']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales territory or region to which this partner is assigned for reporting and commission purposes.']
  agreement_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the partnership agreement became legally effective and the partner was authorized to begin operations.']
  agreement_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current partnership agreement expires or is scheduled for renewal. Null for evergreen agreements.']
  annual_revenue_commitment_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual annual revenue commitment or quota the partner has agreed to achieve, expressed in US Dollars. Used for tier qualification and performance tracking.']
  authorized_product_lines varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product lines, families, or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) categories the partner is authorized to sell (e.g., NGS Sequencing Instruments, Array Platforms, CRISPR Kits, Reagents and Consumables).']
  certification_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the partners current certification expires and requires renewal or recertification.']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'Highest technical or sales certification level achieved by the partner organization (e.g., NGS Certified, CRISPR Specialist, Clinical Genomics Expert). Multiple certifications may be concatenated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the channel partner record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field.']
  credit_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding accounts receivable credit limit approved for the partner, expressed in US Dollars.']
  deal_registration_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the partner is authorized to register deals for opportunity protection and additional discounts. True if enabled, False if disabled.']
  discount_tier_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard discount percentage off list price that the partner receives based on their tier and volume commitments. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 25.00 for 25%).']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet unique identifier for the partner organization, used for credit assessment and business verification.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic territories, regions, or countries where the partner is authorized to sell or distribute Genomics Biotech products. May include multiple countries or regions separated by semicolons.']
  headquarters_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the partner organization headquarters or principal place of business.']
  headquarters_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, floor, building, or additional location details.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the partner headquarters location.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the partner headquarters location (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, CHN).']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the headquarters address.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or primary administrative subdivision of the headquarters location.']
  mdf_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the partner is eligible to receive Market Development Funds for co-marketing activities. True if eligible, False if not.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the channel partner record was last updated. Updated automatically on every modification.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the partner completed onboarding process including training, certification, and system access provisioning.']
  partner_legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal registered name of the channel partner organization as it appears on partnership agreements and regulatory filings.']
  partner_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, special handling instructions, strategic relationship context, or historical information about the partner.']
  partner_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the channel partner, used in contracts, invoices, and partner portal. Format: CP- prefix followed by alphanumeric code.']
  partner_portal_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the partner has active access to the partner portal for order management, training, and marketing resources. True if enabled, False if disabled.']
  partner_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the partnership. Active (full operations), Probation (performance review), Suspended (temporary hold), Terminated (ended), Pending Approval (onboarding in progress).']
  partner_tier varchar [note: 'Partner program tier indicating level of investment, certification, and benefits. Platinum (highest strategic partners), Gold (certified high-volume), Silver (certified standard), Bronze (emerging), Registered (entry level).']
  partner_trade_name varchar [note: 'Doing-business-as (DBA) or trade name used by the partner in the market, may differ from legal name.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the partner business model: distributor (bulk wholesale), reseller (direct sales), VAR (Value-Added Reseller with services), OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer integration), referral (lead generation only), or system integrator (solution provider).']
  partnership_agreement_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the master partnership agreement or contract governing the relationship, stored in contract management system.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms in days for invoices issued to the partner (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days net).']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary partner contact for operational communications, order processing, and escalations.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the partner organization responsible for the partnership relationship.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the partner contact, including country code and extension if applicable.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number, VAT number, or equivalent government-issued business tax identifier for the partner organization in their primary jurisdiction.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the partnership was terminated or ended. Null for active partnerships.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or cause for partnership termination (e.g., performance issues, strategic realignment, partner request, compliance violation). Null for active partnerships.']

  Note: 'Master record for authorized channel partners, distributors, resellers, and value-added resellers (VARs) engaged to sell or distribute Genomics Biotech products in specific geographies or market segments. Captures partner organization name, partner type (distributor, reseller, OEM, referral), tier (platinum, gold, silver), geographic coverage, authorized product lines, partnership agreement reference, partner status (active, probation, terminated), primary partner contact, and onboarding date. SSOT for commercial channel partner identity distinct from supply-side suppliers.'
}

Table commercial.partner_deal_registration {
  partner_deal_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the partner deal registration record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the end-customer account associated with this registered deal.']
  amended_partner_deal_registration_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on partner_deal_registration (amended_partner_deal_registration_id)']
  channel_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the channel partner who registered the deal.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the channel manager who approved or rejected the deal registration.']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the deal registration record.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the direct sales opportunity linked to this partner deal registration, if applicable.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales territory where the end-customer is located.']
  application_area varchar [note: 'Scientific or clinical application area for the registered deal (e.g., Precision Medicine, Agricultural Genomics, Drug Development).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the deal registration was approved by the channel manager.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the deal registration was approved.']
  approving_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the channel manager who approved or rejected the deal registration.']
  co_sell_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this registered deal is eligible for co-selling support from Genomics Biotech direct sales team.']
  conflict_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Status of channel conflict resolution if multiple partners or direct sales registered the same opportunity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deal registration record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the estimated deal value.']
  deal_protection_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when deal protection expires and the registration is no longer valid.']
  deal_protection_start_date date [note: 'Date when deal protection begins for the registered partner.']
  deal_stage varchar [note: 'Current sales stage of the registered deal.']
  end_customer_city varchar [note: 'City where the end-customer is located.']
  end_customer_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the end-customer location.']
  end_customer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the end-customer organization for whom the partner is registering the deal.']
  end_customer_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the end-customer is located.']
  estimated_close_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the partner expects to close the deal with the end-customer.']
  estimated_deal_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total value of the registered deal in the specified currency.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deal registration record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments provided by the partner or channel manager regarding the deal registration.']
  partner_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the partner representative who submitted the deal registration.']
  partner_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the partner representative who submitted the deal registration.']
  partner_incentive_tier varchar [note: 'Incentive tier applicable to this deal registration based on partner program level.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Product family or category being registered in this deal (e.g., NGS Sequencing, Array Solutions, Gene Editing Tools).']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'Specific product SKU being registered in this deal.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the partner submitted the deal registration.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique registration number assigned to this partner deal registration.']
  registration_source varchar [note: 'Channel or system through which the partner submitted the deal registration.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the partner deal registration.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the partner submitted the deal registration.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the deal registration was rejected.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided for why the deal registration was rejected.']
  win_probability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability of winning the deal, expressed as a percentage.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a channel partner\'s formal registration of a sales opportunity or deal with Genomics Biotech, establishing deal protection and co-sell eligibility. Captures registration date, partner identity, end-customer account, registered opportunity details (product, estimated value, close date), registration status (submitted, approved, rejected, expired), approval date, approving manager, deal protection expiry date, and associated direct opportunity linkage. Enables channel conflict management and partner incentive program administration.'
}

Business justification: Market segments in genomics are defined by gene focus (oncology segments focus on cancer genes, cardiology on cardiac genes).   strategic_priority_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic importance classification of the market segment. Tier 1 segments receive highest investment and focus; tier 2 are growth opportunities; tier 3 are maintenance; watch list are monitored for future potential.']
  target_account_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of target accounts (organizations) within this market segment. Used for territory sizing and coverage planning.']
  typical_customer_profile varchar [note: 'Description of the typical customer characteristics within this segment, including organization type, size, research focus, budget range, and purchasing behavior patterns.']
  typical_sales_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from initial contact to closed-won opportunity for this segment. Used for pipeline forecasting and resource planning.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the commercial market segments and sub-segments that Genomics Biotech actively targets — including academic research institutions, clinical reference laboratories, biopharma/biotech R&D, agricultural genomics, direct-to-consumer genomics, and government/public health. Captures segment name, segment code, segment description, primary application areas (WGS, WES, SNP arrays, CRISPR screening), estimated addressable market size, strategic priority tier, and associated product families. Used for campaign targeting, territory design, and pipeline segmentation.'
}

Table commercial.competitive_intel {
  competitive_intel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the competitive intelligence record.']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Competitive intelligence tracks which specific reagent products compete with competitor offerings for battlecard development, win/loss analysis, and product positioning. Essential for reagent-specific']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Competitive intelligence is gathered at the product level - tracking which competitor sequencers/arrays compete with which catalog items. Essential for sales battlecards, win/loss analysis, and produc']
  gene_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_model. Business justification: Competitive genomics products are differentiated by gene coverage (competitor panel includes 100 genes vs 150). Tracking which genes are covered by competing products is essential for battlecards, com']
  market_segment_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.market_segment_target. Business justification: Competitive intelligence is gathered and maintained for specific market segments. Adding market_segment_target_id FK links the intel record to the authoritative segment definition, enabling segment-sp']
  superseded_competitive_intel_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on competitive_intel (superseded_competitive_intel_id)']
  battlecard_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL linking this intelligence record to the corresponding competitive battlecard document or sales enablement content in the CRM or content management system.']
  competing_product_name varchar [note: 'Specific name or model designation of the competitor\'s product that directly competes with Genomics Biotech offerings (e.g., NovaSeq 6000, PromethION, Sequel IIe).']
  competitive_intel_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this competitive intelligence record indicating whether it is actively maintained, archived, under review for updates, or obsolete.']
  competitive_positioning_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the competitor\'s market positioning, messaging strategy, target customer segments, and go-to-market approach for this product.']
  competitor_code varchar [note: 'Internal short code or abbreviation used to identify the competitor in sales and marketing systems (e.g., ILMN, ONT, PACB).']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal or trade name of the competing genomics or biotechnology company (e.g., Illumina, Oxford Nanopore, Pacific Biosciences, 10x Genomics, Agilent, Twist Bioscience).']
  competitor_strengths varchar [note: 'Documented strengths and advantages of the competitor\'s product or market position that sales teams must address in competitive situations.']
  competitor_weaknesses varchar [note: 'Documented weaknesses, limitations, or vulnerabilities in the competitor\'s product, service delivery, or market approach that Genomics Biotech can exploit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this competitive intelligence record was first created in the system, following format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic region or country where this competitive intelligence is most relevant, using three-letter ISO country codes or regional designations (e.g., USA, EUR, CHN, global).']
  intelligence_confidence_level varchar [note: 'Assessed confidence level in the accuracy and reliability of this competitive intelligence based on source credibility and corroboration.']
  intelligence_owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the intelligence owner for questions, updates, or collaboration on competitive strategy.']
  intelligence_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the product marketing manager, competitive intelligence analyst, or sales leader responsible for maintaining and updating this competitive intelligence record.']
  intelligence_source varchar [note: 'Primary source or channel through which this competitive intelligence was gathered (e.g., sales team feedback, customer interviews, industry conferences, analyst reports, public SEC filings, competitor websites). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_feedback|customer_interview|conference|analyst_report|public_filing|website|press_release|partner_channel|trade_publication|social_media|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  key_differentiators varchar [note: 'Structured list of primary technical, commercial, or service differentiators that distinguish Genomics Biotech products from this competitor\'s offerings (e.g., read length, accuracy, throughput, TAT, cost per sample, support model).']
  last_updated_date date [note: 'Date when this competitive intelligence record was last reviewed and updated with new information. Critical for ensuring battlecard content remains current.']
  list_price_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Published or estimated list price for the competing product in the reference currency. May be instrument price, per-sample cost, or annual subscription depending on product category.']
  market_share_estimate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated market share percentage held by this competitor in the relevant product category or geographic segment, based on industry analyst reports and internal analysis.']
  modified_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the user who last modified this competitive intelligence record, supporting audit trail and data governance requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this competitive intelligence record, following format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review and refresh of this competitive intelligence to ensure ongoing accuracy and relevance.']
  performance_specifications varchar [note: 'Key technical performance metrics for the competing product including throughput, accuracy, read length, coverage depth, turnaround time, or other relevant specifications for head-to-head comparison.']
  price_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the list price amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  pricing_intelligence varchar [note: 'Gathered intelligence on competitor pricing models, list prices, discount structures, bundling strategies, and cost-per-sample economics. Critical for deal strategy and pricing decisions.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the competing product or service offering within the genomics and biotechnology value chain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sequencing_platform|array_platform|library_prep|reagent_consumable|bioinformatics_software|gene_editing_tool|clinical_assay|instrument|service — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recent_competitive_activity varchar [note: 'Summary of recent competitive moves, product launches, pricing changes, partnership announcements, or market activities by this competitor that impact Genomics Biotech\'s competitive position.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Known regulatory clearances, certifications, or approvals held by the competing product (e.g., FDA 510(k), CE-IVD, CLIA-waived, RUO only). Critical for clinical and IVD product competition.']
  sales_strategy_notes varchar [note: 'Recommended sales strategies, objection handling techniques, and positioning guidance for sales representatives when competing against this competitor in genomics platform deals.']
  source_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed citation or reference for the intelligence source, including document name, URL, conference name, analyst firm, or sales representative who provided the input.']
  technology_platform varchar [note: 'Underlying technology or chemistry used by the competing product (e.g., sequencing by synthesis, nanopore sequencing, single-molecule real-time sequencing, microarray hybridization, CRISPR-Cas9).']
  threat_level varchar [note: 'Assessed level of competitive threat posed by this competitor and product to Genomics Biotech\'s market position, revenue, or strategic objectives.']
  win_loss_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of competitive sales opportunities won by Genomics Biotech against this specific competitor, calculated as (wins / (wins + losses)) * 100. Used to measure competitive strength.']

  Note: 'Master record capturing structured competitive intelligence on rival genomics and biotechnology companies — including Illumina, Oxford Nanopore, Pacific Biosciences, 10x Genomics, Agilent, and Twist Bioscience. Captures competitor name, product category, competing product name, competitive positioning notes, win/loss rate against this competitor, key differentiators, pricing intelligence, market share estimate, intelligence source, and last updated date. Feeds competitive battlecard content and informs sales strategy for genomics platform deals.'
}

Table commercial.win_loss_review {
  win_loss_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the win/loss review record. Primary key for the win_loss_review product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with the opportunity. Links to the account master record in the CRM system.']
  competitive_intel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.competitive_intel. Business justification: Win/loss reviews reference specific competitive intelligence records for the competitor selected. Adding competitive_intel_id FK links the review to the authoritative competitive intel record, enablin']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the closed commercial opportunity that this win/loss review analyzes. Links to the opportunity record in the CRM system.']
  quality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.quality_assessment. Business justification: Lost genomics deals often cite data quality concerns (accuracy, reproducibility, error rates) as decision factors. Win/loss analysis should reference quality assessment results for competitive intelli']
  revised_win_loss_review_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on win_loss_review (revised_win_loss_review_id)']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory in which the opportunity was managed.']
  account_executive_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the account executive who owned the opportunity and participated in the win/loss review.']
  action_items varchar [note: 'Specific follow-up actions identified during the review to improve future win rates or address product/service gaps.']
  application_area varchar [note: 'Intended genomics application area for the customer (e.g., WGS, WES, RNA-Seq, targeted panels, agricultural genomics, clinical diagnostics).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the win/loss review was approved by the sales manager or commercial operations leader.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the customer account location (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, CHN).']
  created_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who created the win/loss review record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the win/loss review record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the deal size amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_interview_conducted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal post-decision interview was conducted with the customer to gather feedback. True if interview occurred, False otherwise.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Market segment classification of the customer (e.g., academic research, clinical diagnostics, pharmaceutical, agricultural, government).']
  customer_stated_factors varchar [note: 'Free-text capture of the decision factors as explicitly stated by the customer during post-decision interviews or feedback sessions.']
  deal_size_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the opportunity at the time of decision, representing the potential or actual revenue from the deal.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date when the customer made the final purchase decision or when the opportunity was closed. Represents the real-world business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the commercial opportunity: won (Genomics Biotech secured the deal), lost (competitor won), no_decision (customer deferred or did not proceed), or cancelled (opportunity withdrawn).']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region of the customer account (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, Latin America).']
  interview_date date [note: 'Date when the customer interview was conducted, if applicable. Nullable for reviews without formal customer interviews.']
  key_learnings varchar [note: 'Summary of key insights and lessons learned from the win or loss that can inform future sales strategy and product development.']
  modified_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who last modified the win/loss review record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the win/loss review record was last modified. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes and observations captured during the win/loss review process.']
  pricing_competitiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Subjective assessment of how competitive Genomics Biotech pricing was relative to alternatives, based on customer feedback and market intelligence.']
  primary_reason_category varchar [note: 'Top-level category classifying the primary reason for the win or loss outcome. Used for systematic analysis of commercial performance drivers.']
  primary_reason_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the primary reason for the win or loss, providing specific context beyond the category classification.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'High-level product family classification for the primary product or service in the opportunity (e.g., sequencing instruments, reagent kits, array products).']
  product_fit_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of how well the Genomics Biotech product or service met the customers technical and application requirements.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Specific product line within the product family (e.g., NovaSeq, MiSeq, TruSeq, Infinium).']
  relationship_strength_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the strength of the pre-existing relationship between Genomics Biotech and the customer account.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the win/loss review was conducted and documented by the sales team or commercial operations.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the win/loss review, formatted as WLR-YYYYNNNN where YYYY is year and NNNN is sequence number.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the win/loss review record: draft (in progress), submitted (awaiting approval), approved (finalized), or archived (historical record).']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who conducted the win/loss review (may be sales operations, commercial analyst, or sales manager).']
  sales_cycle_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from opportunity creation to decision date, representing the length of the sales cycle.']
  sales_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the sales manager who reviewed and approved the win/loss analysis.']
  secondary_reason_category varchar [note: 'Secondary contributing factor category for the win or loss outcome. Nullable field for cases where multiple factors influenced the decision.']
  secondary_reason_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the secondary contributing reason, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the structured post-decision analysis of a closed commercial opportunity — documenting why Genomics Biotech won or lost a deal for sequencing instruments, reagent kits, arrays, or services. Captures opportunity reference, decision date, outcome (won, lost, no decision), primary win/loss reason category, secondary reasons, competitor selected (if lost), customer-stated decision factors, deal size, product family, and reviewing sales manager. Enables systematic learning from commercial outcomes to improve win rates.'
}

Table commercial.contract {
  contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the commercial contract record. Primary key for the commercial contract entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or channel partner account that is the counterparty to this contract. Links to the master customer account record.']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Volume purchase agreements and reagent supply contracts specify committed reagent products. Enables contract compliance tracking, committed product validation, and reagent-specific terms enforcement i']
  master_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on contract (master_contract_id)']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.opportunity. Business justification: Commercial contracts are the legal outcome of closed-won opportunities. Adding opportunity_id FK links the executed contract back to the originating sales opportunity, enabling win-rate analysis, sale']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory or region to which this contract is assigned for quota and commission purposes. Links to the sales territory master data.']
  service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_agreement. Business justification: Commercial contracts (MSAs, volume purchase agreements) often reference or supersede service agreements for instrument maintenance and support. Legal and sales operations teams cross-reference contrac']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_contract. Business justification: Master commercial contracts with customers may reference underlying supplier contracts for pass-through pricing, supply guarantees, or back-to-back terms. Business process: back-to-back contract manag']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of internal approval workflow for non-standard terms, pricing exceptions, or high-value contracts. Pending indicates awaiting review; approved means all required approvals obtained; rejected means approval denied; conditional means approved with modifications.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the executive or manager who provided final approval for the contract. Typically required for contracts exceeding standard authority limits or containing non-standard terms.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when final internal approval was granted. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews for an additional term unless either party provides notice of non-renewal. True means contract will auto-renew; false means contract expires at term end unless explicitly renewed.']
  confidentiality_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes confidentiality or non-disclosure provisions protecting proprietary information, trade secrets, or sensitive business data. True means confidentiality obligations exist; false means no specific confidentiality terms beyond standard commercial practice.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the contract, used in communications with customers and partners. Typically follows a structured format including year, region, or contract type prefix.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract. Draft indicates contract is being negotiated; pending approval awaits internal sign-off; executed means signed by all parties; active indicates contract is in force; expired means term has ended; terminated indicates early cancellation; suspended indicates temporary hold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|executed|active|expired|terminated|suspended — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract based on the primary commercial relationship it governs. Instrument purchase agreements cover sequencing systems and hardware; reagent supply agreements govern consumables and kits; software license agreements cover bioinformatics platforms; MSAs establish framework terms; evaluation agreements enable product trials; maintenance agreements cover post-warranty service.']
  counterparty_legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the customer or partner organization as it appears on the executed contract. Must match official registration documents for contract enforceability.']
  counterparty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contracting party based on their relationship to Genomics Biotech. Direct customers purchase for their own use; channel partners resell; distributors provide regional coverage; OEM partners integrate products; academic institutions include universities and research centers; government entities include public health and regulatory bodies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contract record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in the contract. Determines the currency for invoicing and payment.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'Agreed-upon method for resolving disputes arising from the contract. Arbitration is common for commercial contracts to avoid court proceedings; mediation involves a neutral third party; litigation means court-based resolution.']
  document_repository_url varchar [note: 'Link to the executed contract document stored in the document management system (e.g., Veeva Vault). Provides access to the full legal agreement and any amendments.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract terms become binding and enforceable. Typically the date of final signature or a future date specified in the contract. Marks the start of the contract term.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract grants exclusive rights to the counterparty within a defined scope (territory, product line, or customer segment). True means exclusive rights granted; false means non-exclusive. Common in channel partner and distributor agreements.']
  executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contract was fully executed (signed by all parties). Marks the point at which the contract becomes legally binding, which may differ from the effective date.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract term ends unless renewed or extended. Null for perpetual contracts or contracts with no fixed term. Used for contract renewal planning and revenue forecasting.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of the contract. Typically specified as a state or country (e.g., State of California, England and Wales). Critical for dispute resolution.']
  insurance_requirements varchar [note: 'Summary of insurance coverage requirements specified in the contract, such as general liability, professional liability, product liability, or cyber liability insurance. May include minimum coverage amounts and certificate of insurance requirements.']
  ip_ownership_terms varchar [note: 'Summary of intellectual property ownership and licensing terms, particularly relevant for R&D collaborations, custom development, or co-marketing agreements. Specifies who owns IP created under the contract and any license grants.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contract record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  liability_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount of liability that either party may be held responsible for under the contract, excluding certain carve-outs (e.g., IP infringement, gross negligence). Null if no liability cap is specified. Typically expressed as a multiple of contract value or a fixed amount.']
  minimum_purchase_commitment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary value or quantity that the customer commits to purchase over the contract term. Used in volume discount agreements and channel partner contracts. Null if no minimum commitment exists.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special terms, negotiation history, or internal notes relevant to contract management. Not part of the legal contract but useful for operational context.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred or required method of payment specified in the contract. Wire transfer and ACH are common for large transactions; credit card for smaller purchases; purchase order for institutional buyers; letter of credit for international or high-value deals.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms governing when payment is due after invoice. Net terms indicate days until payment due; installment and milestone-based indicate structured payment schedules; subscription terms indicate recurring billing cycles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_30|net_45|net_60|net_90|due_on_receipt|installment|milestone_based|subscription_monthly|subscription_annual — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  product_scope varchar [note: 'Summary description of the products, services, or solutions covered by this contract. May include instrument models, reagent SKUs, software platforms, or service packages. Provides high-level context without listing every line item.']
  regulatory_compliance_terms varchar [note: 'Summary of regulatory compliance obligations specified in the contract, such as HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA) terms, GDPR data processing terms, FDA Quality System Regulation (QSR) requirements, or GxP compliance for clinical products. Critical for IVD and clinical genomics contracts.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiration that either party must provide written notice to prevent auto-renewal or to initiate renewal discussions. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days. Null if contract does not auto-renew.']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'Summary of service level commitments including response times, uptime guarantees, turnaround times (TAT), and performance metrics. Common in maintenance agreements and software license agreements. May reference detailed SLA document.']
  termination_for_convenience_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether either party may terminate the contract without cause by providing advance notice. True means termination for convenience is allowed; false means contract can only be terminated for cause (breach, insolvency, etc.).']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance written notice required to terminate the contract for convenience or non-renewal. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days. Null if termination for convenience is not permitted.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or market region where the contract terms apply. May be global, regional (e.g., EMEA, APAC), country-specific, or limited to specific sites. Important for channel partner and distributor agreements.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the contract over its full term, including all products, services, and recurring fees. Represents the maximum revenue potential from this contract. Used for pipeline reporting and revenue forecasting.']
  volume_discount_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier or discount level applicable to this contract based on purchase volume, strategic relationship, or negotiated terms. Higher tiers typically receive better pricing. Standard indicates list pricing with no volume discount.']
  warranty_terms varchar [note: 'Summary of warranty coverage provided under the contract, including duration, scope, and exclusions. For instrument purchase agreements, typically includes hardware warranty period and terms. For reagents, may reference shelf life and quality guarantees.']

  Note: 'Master record for executed commercial contracts governing the sale, licensing, and service delivery of Genomics Biotech products — including instrument purchase agreements, reagent supply agreements, software license agreements, master service agreements (MSAs), and evaluation agreements. Captures contract number, contract type, counterparty (customer or channel partner), effective date, expiration date, auto-renewal flag, total contract value, payment terms, governing jurisdiction, contract status (draft, executed, active, expired, terminated), and key commercial terms summary. SSOT for commercial contractual commitments distinct from regulatory or R&D agreements.'
}

  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that holds this subscription agreement.']

This links commercial subscription management to the authoritative reagent product catalog. The produc']
