  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.commercial_contract. Business justification: Reagent subscriptions are often governed by master commercial contracts that define terms, pricing frameworks, and service levels. Adding commercial_contract_id FK links the subscription to its govern']
  installed_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.installed_instrument. Business justification: Reagent subscriptions in genomics are instrument-specific (e.g., flow cells for specific sequencer models). Business tracks which instrument serial number consumes which subscription to manage volume ']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Subscription fulfillment must track which specific lots are shipped to customers for traceability, recall management, and quality complaints. Critical for regulatory compliance and customer-specific l']
  parent_subscription_reagent_subscription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent or master subscription if this subscription is part of a hierarchical subscription structure or enterprise agreement.']
  price_book_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.price_book. Business justification: Reagent subscriptions are priced according to specific price books (often customer-specific or tier-based). Adding price_book_id FK links the subscription to its pricing source, enabling subscription ']
  primary_reagent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales or account management employee responsible for this subscription relationship.']
  quality_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.quality_rule. Business justification: Subscription-based reagent supply (flow cells, sequencing kits, arrays) includes contractual quality specifications that map to automated quality rules. Lot acceptance criteria are subscription terms.']
  renewed_reagent_subscription_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on reagent_subscription (renewed_reagent_subscription_id)']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory responsible for managing this subscription account.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.service_contract. Business justification: Bundled service+reagent contracts are common in genomics (e.g., instrument maintenance plus committed reagent volumes). Linking enables tracking bundled deals, enforcing contract dependencies (reagent']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Reagent subscriptions are for specific SKUs (e.g., flow cell kits, library prep reagents). Subscription management, consumption tracking, and renewal forecasting require linking to the actual SKU for ']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Subscriptions may be fulfilled by third-party contract manufacturers or reagent suppliers under supply agreements. Business process: subscription supply chain management, supplier performance against ']
  activation_date date [note: 'The date when the subscription was activated and became operational for order fulfillment.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription automatically renews at the end of its term (True) or requires explicit renewal action (False).']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'The cadence at which the customer is invoiced for subscription charges (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annually).']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date when the subscription was cancelled by the customer or provider, if applicable.']
  cancellation_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required from the customer to cancel the subscription without penalty.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or explanation for subscription cancellation, used for churn analysis and customer retention insights.']
  committed_annual_volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'The contracted quantity of reagent or consumable units the customer commits to purchase annually (e.g., flow cell count, kit quantity).']
  consumed_volume_to_date decimal(15,3) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of reagent or consumable units consumed by the customer since subscription start, tracked against committed volume.']
  consumption_tracking_method varchar [note: 'The method used to track customer consumption of subscribed reagents and consumables against committed volumes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this subscription record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this subscription (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the standard list price as part of the subscription agreement terms.']
  last_shipment_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent shipment of subscribed reagents or consumables to the customer.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The minimum quantity of reagent or consumable units that must be ordered in a single transaction under this subscription.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this subscription record was last modified or updated.']
  next_scheduled_shipment_date date [note: 'The date of the next planned shipment of subscribed reagents or consumables to the customer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special terms, or operational notes related to the subscription agreement.']
  overage_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is permitted to consume beyond the committed annual volume (True) or is capped at the commitment (False).']
  overage_price_per_unit decimal(15,2) [note: 'The unit price charged for consumption exceeding the committed annual volume, if overage is allowed.']
  pause_end_date date [note: 'The date when a paused subscription is scheduled to resume, if applicable.']
  pause_start_date date [note: 'The date when the subscription was paused or temporarily suspended, if applicable.']
  price_per_unit decimal(15,2) [note: 'The contracted unit price for each reagent or consumable unit under this subscription agreement.']
  remaining_volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of reagent or consumable units remaining in the subscription commitment that have not yet been consumed or shipped.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to subscription end date that renewal notification is sent to the customer.']
  shipment_frequency varchar [note: 'The cadence at which subscribed reagents and consumables are shipped to the customer (e.g., on-demand, monthly scheduled deliveries).']
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'The date when the subscription agreement expires or terminates, after which the subscription terms no longer apply.']
  subscription_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the subscription agreement, used in customer communications and invoicing.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'The date when the subscription agreement becomes effective and the customer can begin ordering under the subscription terms.']
  subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the subscription agreement indicating whether it is actively fulfilling orders or in a non-active state.']
  subscription_tier varchar [note: 'Service level or pricing tier of the subscription, determining volume commitments, pricing, and service benefits.']
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Category of reagent or consumable covered by this subscription agreement (e.g., flow cell subscriptions, library prep kit subscriptions, array consumable programs).']
  total_contract_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the subscription over its full term, calculated as committed volume multiplied by price per unit.']
  volume_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the committed volume is measured (e.g., flow cells, kits, arrays, cartridges). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flow_cell|kit|array|cartridge|reagent_set|lane|run|unit — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record for recurring reagent and consumable supply subscription agreements with customers — including flow cell subscriptions, library prep kit subscriptions, and array consumable programs. Captures subscription ID, customer account, subscribed product family, committed annual volume (e.g., flow cell count, kit quantity), subscription tier, pricing per unit, subscription start/end date, auto-renewal terms, consumption tracking method, and subscription status (active, paused, cancelled, expired). Enables recurring revenue management and reagent pull-through forecasting for genomics platforms.'
}

Table commercial.reagent_subscription_order {
  reagent_subscription_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reagent subscription release order or call-off order record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that holds the subscription agreement and is placing this release order.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corresponding sales order created in the order management system for fulfillment and revenue recognition of this release order.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Release orders must specify exact lots being shipped for lot traceability, expiry management, and customer-specific COA generation. Essential for regulatory compliance and quality documentation in gen']
  previous_reagent_subscription_order_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on reagent_subscription_order (previous_reagent_subscription_order_id)']
  primary_reagent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or account manager responsible for this customer account and release order.']
  primary_reagent_ship_to_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery address where the reagents should be shipped for this release order.']
  quaternary_reagent_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the person or automated process that last modified this release order record.']
  reagent_subscription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent reagent subscription agreement under which this release order is generated.']
  requested_by_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer contact person who submitted or authorized this release order.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory under which this release order is managed for commission and performance tracking.']
  tertiary_reagent_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the person or automated process that created this release order record.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or distribution center assigned to fulfill this release order based on inventory availability and proximity.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date when the reagents were delivered to the customer, captured upon fulfillment completion.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this release order requires internal approval before fulfillment, typically for high-value orders or special terms.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the release order if approval workflow is required.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this release order was approved, if approval was required.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when this release order was cancelled, if applicable.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why this release order was cancelled, if applicable, for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier or shipping company handling the delivery of this release order.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Date confirmed by supply chain and logistics for delivery of the reagents, may differ from requested date based on availability and lead time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this release order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values in this release order.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this release order record was last updated or modified.']
  order_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this release order for internal coordination or customer communication.']
  order_source varchar [note: 'Channel or system through which this release order was submitted, enabling analysis of ordering patterns and channel effectiveness.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this release order for fulfillment scheduling and resource allocation.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Customer-provided purchase order number for their internal procurement tracking and invoice reconciliation.']
  release_order_date date [note: 'Date when the release order was created or submitted by the customer against the subscription agreement.']
  release_order_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the release order or call-off order, externally visible and used for tracking and fulfillment coordination.']
  release_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the release order indicating its progression from creation through fulfillment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|confirmed|in_fulfillment|shipped|delivered|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the customer has requested delivery of the reagents specified in this release order.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Internal requisition or request number from the customer organization that triggered this release order.']
  shipment_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for the shipment associated with this release order, enabling customer visibility into delivery status.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method of shipment selected for delivery of reagents, critical for temperature-sensitive and time-sensitive materials.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for special handling requirements such as cold chain maintenance, hazardous material protocols, or delivery access instructions.']
  subscription_consumption_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total subscription commitment consumed by this release order, calculated as order value divided by total subscription value.']
  total_line_items varchar [note: 'Count of distinct Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) line items included in this release order.']
  total_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of this release order including all line items before taxes and adjustments, used for consumption tracking against subscription commitment.']
  total_quantity_ordered decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all reagent units ordered across all line items in this release order, measured in base unit of measure.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual release orders or call-off orders generated against an active reagent subscription agreement. Captures subscription reference, release order date, ordered SKUs and quantities, requested delivery date, delivery address, order status, and fulfillment linkage to the order domain. Enables tracking of subscription consumption against committed volumes and supports reagent pull-through revenue recognition.'
}

Table commercial.price_book {
  price_book_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price book record. Primary key.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  derived_from_price_book_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_book (derived_from_price_book_id)']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  parent_price_book_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent price book when this price book inherits or overrides pricing from another book, enabling hierarchical pricing structures.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.profit_center']
  applicable_customer_segments varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of customer segment codes or names to which this price book applies (e.g., academic_research, clinical_diagnostics, pharma_r_and_d).']
  applicable_geographies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region codes where this price book is valid (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, EU).']
  applicable_sales_channels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sales channel codes where this price book is used (e.g., direct_sales, distributor, online, oem_partner).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the price book. Not Submitted = not yet submitted for approval; Pending Review = under review by pricing committee; Approved = authorized for use; Rejected = not approved and requires revision.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the price book was approved for commercial use, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone.']
  competitive_benchmark_source varchar [note: 'Source of competitive pricing intelligence used to inform this price book (e.g., market research firm, internal analysis, customer feedback). Confidential business information.']
  created_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who created this price book record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price book record was first created in the system, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all prices in this price book (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  discount_policy varchar [note: 'Type of discount policy applied within this price book. None = list prices only; Volume Based = quantity discounts; Contract Based = negotiated contract pricing; Promotional = temporary promotional pricing; Custom = special pricing arrangements.']
  distribution_channel_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD distribution channel code indicating the route-to-market for products priced under this book (e.g., direct, distributor, online).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this price book expires and is no longer valid for pricing transactions. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this price book becomes active and available for use in pricing transactions.']
  external_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for this price book in external systems such as Salesforce CRM or e-commerce platforms, used for cross-system integration and reconciliation.']
  is_default_price_book boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default price book for a given segment or geography when no specific price book is assigned. True = default; False = non-default.']
  is_public boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this price book is publicly available (e.g., published list prices) or confidential (e.g., negotiated contract pricing). True = public; False = confidential.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this price book was last reviewed for accuracy, competitiveness, and alignment with business strategy.']
  maximum_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable discount percentage that can be applied to prices in this price book, used for discount authorization controls.']
  minimum_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum order value required to use this price book, expressed in the price book currency. Null indicates no minimum.']
  modified_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who last modified this price book record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price book record was last modified, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this price book to ensure continued relevance and competitiveness.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, special instructions, or comments related to the use and maintenance of this price book.']
  price_book_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the price book, used in commercial systems and customer communications.']
  price_book_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the price book purpose, scope, and applicability including business rationale and strategic intent.']
  price_book_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the price book describing its purpose and target segment.']
  price_book_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price book. Draft = under construction; Pending Approval = submitted for review; Active = in use for pricing; Inactive = temporarily disabled; Archived = retired from use.']
  price_book_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price book by customer segment or channel. List = standard published pricing; Academic = research institution pricing; Government = public sector pricing; Biopharma = pharmaceutical and biotech company pricing; Distributor = channel partner pricing; OEM = original equipment manufacturer pricing.']
  pricing_strategy_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes describing the strategic rationale, competitive positioning, and business objectives for this price book. Confidential business information.']
  requires_contract boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether use of this price book requires an active contract or agreement with the customer. True = contract required; False = no contract required.']
  sales_organization_code varchar [note: 'SAP Sales and Distribution (SD) sales organization code that owns and manages this price book.']
  sap_condition_type varchar [note: 'SAP SD pricing condition type code associated with this price book for integration with SAP pricing procedures (e.g., PR00 for list price, K004 for customer-specific price).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the price book, incremented with each revision to track pricing changes over time.']

  Note: 'Master record defining commercial price books used to manage customer-specific, segment-specific, and geography-specific pricing for Genomics Biotech products. Captures price book name, price book type (list, academic, government, biopharma, distributor, OEM), currency, effective date range, applicable customer segments, applicable geographies, approval status, and version number. Serves as the pricing framework container that price book entries reference, enabling multi-tier pricing strategy management across global markets.'
}

Table commercial.price_book_entry {
  price_book_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price book entry record. Primary key for the price book entry entity.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Price book entries must link to reagent catalog items to establish list prices for reagent products. Core commercial master data relationship enabling automated pricing for genomics reagents alongside']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the catalog item or product master record being priced. Links to the product catalog entry for sequencing instruments, reagent kits, array products, CRISPR tools, bioinformatics software licenses, or consumables.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.product_family']
  ivd_test_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_catalog. Business justification: Each IVD test in clinical catalog must have corresponding pricing entries for commercial transactions. Fundamental product-to-price relationship enabling quote generation, contract pricing, and revenu']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_model. Business justification: Price books contain list prices and discount tiers for specific instrument models. Foundational for quote generation, discount approval workflows, regional pricing strategies, and ensuring sales reps ']
  price_book_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent price book that contains this pricing entry. Links to the price book master record defining the commercial pricing structure for a specific customer segment, geography, or channel.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.sku']
  superseded_by_entry_price_book_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the price book entry that supersedes this entry. Used to maintain pricing history and version control when pricing is updated or replaced. Null for current active pricing that has not been superseded.']
  superseded_price_book_entry_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_book_entry (superseded_price_book_entry_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval status for this price book entry. Indicates whether commercial or finance approval is required and the current state of that approval workflow. Not Required for standard pricing, Pending for entries awaiting approval, Approved for authorized pricing, Rejected for declined pricing requests.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price book entry was approved. Null if approval is not required or entry is pending approval. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Optional reference to a specific customer contract or master service agreement that governs this pricing. Used for customer-specific negotiated pricing tied to contractual commitments. Confidential commercial information.']
  cost_of_goods_sold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard cost of goods sold for this catalog item. Used for margin analysis and pricing strategy optimization. Confidential business data used by finance and commercial teams to evaluate pricing profitability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price book entry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the pricing entry. Defines the currency in which list price and unit price are denominated. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CNY.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The target customer segment for this price book entry. Used to align pricing with customer type and use case. Examples include Clinical laboratories, Research institutions, Pharmaceutical companies, Agricultural genomics, Government agencies, Academic institutions, and OEM partners. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Clinical|Research|Pharmaceutical|Agricultural|Government|Academic|OEM|Other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this price book entry expires and is no longer valid for new quotations or orders. Null indicates the pricing is open-ended with no planned expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this price book entry becomes active and available for quotation and order processing. Pricing is valid from this date forward until the effective end date.']
  entry_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the price book entry. Draft entries are under construction, Active entries are available for use, Inactive entries are temporarily disabled, Expired entries have passed their end date, Superseded entries have been replaced by newer pricing, and Pending Approval entries await commercial approval.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic region or country for which this pricing applies. Enables region-specific and country-specific pricing strategies to account for market conditions, regulatory requirements, and competitive positioning. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional designations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Global|North America|EMEA|APAC|LATAM|USA|CAN|GBR|DEU|FRA|CHN|JPN|AUS — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gross_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The gross margin percentage for this price book entry, calculated as (unit_price - cost_of_goods_sold) / unit_price * 100. Used for profitability analysis and pricing governance. Confidential business metric.']
  last_modified_by_name varchar [note: 'The name of the user or system process that last modified this price book entry record. Used for audit trail and accountability in pricing governance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price book entry record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']
  list_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard list price for the catalog item under this price book. Represents the base commercial price before any discounts, volume tiers, or promotional adjustments. Used as the reference price for discount calculations.']
  maximum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'The maximum quantity that can be ordered under this price book entry. Used to cap promotional pricing or enforce allocation limits for constrained products. Null indicates no maximum limit.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'The minimum quantity that must be ordered for this price book entry to apply. Used to enforce volume commitments or packaging constraints. Null or 1 indicates no minimum order requirement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about this price book entry. Used to capture pricing rationale, special terms, competitive positioning notes, or other contextual information for commercial teams.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'The commercial pricing strategy classification for this entry. Indicates whether this is standard list pricing, promotional pricing, volume-based pricing, customer-specific negotiated pricing, channel partner pricing, government contract pricing, or academic/research institution pricing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Standard|Promotional|Volume|Customer Specific|Channel Partner|Government|Academic — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  promotional_code varchar [note: 'Optional promotional code or campaign identifier associated with this pricing entry. Links the price book entry to a specific marketing campaign or promotional offer. Null for standard non-promotional pricing.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which this pricing is offered. Enables channel-specific pricing strategies for direct sales teams, channel partners, distributors, online e-commerce, government contracts, and OEM partnerships.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for pricing and ordering. Defines the quantity basis for the unit price. Examples include Each for instruments, Kit for reagent bundles, Flow Cell for sequencing consumables, License for software, or Run for service-based pricing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Each|Kit|Cartridge|Flow Cell|Chip|License|Run|Sample|Reaction|Test|Hour|Month|Year — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The effective unit price for a single unit of the catalog item. May differ from list price if volume discounts, promotional pricing, or customer-specific pricing applies. This is the price used in quotation and order processing.']
  volume_discount_tier_1_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The discount percentage applied when the order quantity meets or exceeds the tier 1 quantity threshold. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5% discount). Null if no tier 1 discount applies.']
  volume_discount_tier_1_quantity varchar [note: 'The minimum quantity threshold to qualify for the first volume discount tier. Orders meeting or exceeding this quantity receive the tier 1 discount percentage. Null if no volume discounting applies.']
  volume_discount_tier_2_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The discount percentage applied when the order quantity meets or exceeds the tier 2 quantity threshold. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10% discount). Null if no tier 2 discount applies.']
  volume_discount_tier_2_quantity varchar [note: 'The minimum quantity threshold to qualify for the second volume discount tier. Orders meeting or exceeding this quantity receive the tier 2 discount percentage. Null if no tier 2 discount applies.']
  volume_discount_tier_3_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The discount percentage applied when the order quantity meets or exceeds the tier 3 quantity threshold. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15% discount). Null if no tier 3 discount applies.']
  volume_discount_tier_3_quantity varchar [note: 'The minimum quantity threshold to qualify for the third volume discount tier. Orders meeting or exceeding this quantity receive the tier 3 discount percentage. Null if no tier 3 discount applies.']

  Note: 'Individual product pricing entry within a price book, defining the commercial price for a specific catalog item or SKU under a given price book. Captures price book reference, catalog item/SKU reference, unit price, currency, minimum order quantity, volume discount tiers, effective start/end date, and entry status. Enables granular price management for sequencing instruments, reagent kits, array products, CRISPR tools, and bioinformatics software licenses across customer segments and geographies.'
}

Table commercial.discount_approval {
  discount_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the discount approval record. Primary key for the discount approval transaction.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for which the discount is being requested. Links the approval to the customer master record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or executive who reviewed and made the approval decision. Links to the employee or user record of the approver.']
  escalated_discount_approval_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on discount_approval (escalated_discount_approval_id)']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales opportunity for which the discount is being requested. Links the discount approval to the deal pipeline.']
  price_book_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.price_book_entry. Business justification: Discount approvals are for specific product pricing entries in a price book. Adding price_book_entry_id FK links the approval to the specific price book entry being discounted, enabling variance analy']
  primary_discount_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative who submitted the discount approval request. Links to the employee or user record of the requestor.']
  quotation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific quote or proposal document associated with this discount request. May be null if discount is requested before formal quote generation.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory or region associated with this discount request, enabling territory-level discount analytics and governance.']
  approval_comments varchar [note: 'Comments or notes provided by the approver explaining the decision rationale, conditions, or guidance for the sales team.']
  approval_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision made by the approver on the discount request: approved as requested, rejected, or counter-offered with alternative terms.']
  approval_expiration_date date [note: 'Date by which the approved discount must be used in a closed transaction. After this date, the approval becomes invalid and a new request must be submitted.']
  approval_request_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the discount approval request, formatted as DA-YYYYMMDD sequence for traceability and reference in communications.']
  approval_tier_required varchar [note: 'The organizational level required to approve this discount request based on the variance from standard thresholds and deal size, as defined by discount governance policy.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the approver made the final decision on the discount request. Used to calculate approval turnaround time and enforce service level agreements (SLA).']
  approved_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The final discount percentage approved by the decision maker. May differ from the requested percentage if a counter-offer was made. Null if request was rejected.']
  approved_net_price_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final approved net price after applying the approved discount percentage. This is the price authorized for the sales transaction. Null if request was rejected.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the manager or executive who made the approval decision, included for audit trail and governance reporting.']
  approver_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the approver at the time of decision, documenting the organizational authority level that approved the discount.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation provided by the requesting sales representative describing the business rationale for the discount request, including competitive situation, strategic value, or customer circumstances.']
  competitive_situation varchar [note: 'Description of the competitive dynamics influencing the discount request, such as competing vendor offers, price pressure, or market positioning considerations.']
  counter_offer_terms varchar [note: 'Alternative discount terms or conditions proposed by the approver when the original request cannot be approved as submitted. Populated only when approval_decision is counter_offered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this discount approval record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this discount approval record, ensuring consistent financial reporting across geographies.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Market segment classification of the customer account, used to evaluate discount requests against segment-specific pricing strategies and discount policies.']
  deal_closed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the opportunity associated with this discount approval was successfully closed and converted to a booking. Used to measure discount approval effectiveness.']
  deal_size_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of the opportunity or deal for which the discount is being requested. Used to assess discount impact on revenue and margin.']
  discount_variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The difference between the requested discount and the standard threshold, representing the magnitude of the exception being requested. Calculated as requested discount minus standard threshold.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this discount approval record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record for audit and synchronization purposes.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the product type for which discount is requested, enabling analysis of discount patterns by product line. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sequencing_instrument|reagent_kit|consumable|bioinformatics_service|array_product|gene_editing_tool|clinical_assay|support_service — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the genomics product or service for which the discount is being requested, such as sequencing platform model, assay kit name, or service package.']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the genomics product or service for which the discount is requested. May represent sequencing instruments, reagent kits, consumables, or bioinformatics services.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the approver for rejecting the discount request, such as insufficient justification, margin impact, or policy violation. Populated only when approval_decision is rejected.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the discount approval request in the workflow, tracking progression from submission through final decision. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|under_review|approved|rejected|counter_offered|withdrawn|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the discount approval request was submitted by the sales representative. Serves as the starting point for approval turnaround time (TAT) measurement.']
  requested_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The discount percentage requested by the sales representative, expressed as a percentage of list price. This is the discount level requiring approval.']
  requested_net_price_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The proposed net price after applying the requested discount percentage. Calculated as list price minus discount for deal evaluation.']
  requesting_sales_rep_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the sales representative who submitted the discount approval request for audit trail and reporting purposes.']
  standard_discount_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum discount percentage that can be applied without requiring special approval, as defined by commercial policy for this product category and customer segment.']
  strategic_value_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this deal has strategic importance beyond immediate revenue, such as market entry, reference customer, or long-term partnership potential.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal approval workflow for non-standard discounts and special pricing exceptions requested by sales representatives for genomics product deals. Captures request date, requesting rep, opportunity reference, product SKU, requested discount percentage, standard discount threshold, approval tier required (manager, director, VP, CFO), approver identity, approval decision (approved, rejected, counter-offered), approval date, business justification, and deal context. Enforces discount governance and deal desk controls.'
}

Captures activity date, FAS identity, customer account, activity type, product platform demonstrated or supported, duration, outcome, follow-up actions, and associated opportunity or service agreement. Tracks pre-sales and post-sales technical engagement critical to genomics instrument adoption.'
}

Engagement contr']
  gene_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_model. Business justification: Key opinion leaders in genomics have gene-specific expertise (BRCA1/2 experts, TP53 researchers).   kol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master KOL person record. Links to the individual researcher or clinician being engaged.']

Adding market_segment_target_id FK links the engagement to the segment being influenced, enabl']

  primary_kol_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Genomics Biotech employee (typically a commercial or scientific affairs representative) who owns and manages the KOL relationship.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commercial territory to which this KOL engagement is assigned for quota and pipeline tracking purposes.']
  active_program_count varchar [note: 'Number of currently active engagement programs or initiatives in which the KOL is participating.']
  advisory_board_participation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the KOL currently serves on a Genomics Biotech advisory board (scientific, clinical, or commercial).']
  annual_honorarium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total annual honorarium or consulting fees paid to the KOL. Used for budget tracking and Sunshine Act reporting compliance.']
  beta_tester_participation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the KOL is currently participating in beta testing or early access programs for new Genomics Biotech products or platforms.']

  compliance_training_date date [note: 'Date when the KOL most recently completed required compliance training. Supports audit readiness and renewal tracking.']

  engagement_role varchar [note: 'Primary role or capacity in which the KOL engages with Genomics Biotech. Defines the nature of the relationship and expected activities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: advisory_board_member|speaker|beta_tester|reference_site|publication_collaborator|clinical_trial_investigator|consultant — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  expertise_area varchar [note: 'Primary scientific or clinical domain where the KOL has recognized expertise and influence. Aligns engagement activities with KOL specialization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ngs|wgs|wes|single_cell_sequencing|spatial_genomics|clinical_genomics|bioinformatics|cancer_genomics|rare_disease|pharmacogenomics|agricultural_genomics|microbial_genomics — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  first_engagement_date date [note: 'Date when the KOL was first formally engaged by Genomics Biotech. Establishes the start of the relationship timeline.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region where the KOL is based and conducts their work. Used for regional engagement planning and territory alignment.']
  h_index varchar [note: 'Hirsch index measuring the KOL\'s research productivity and citation impact. Used to assess scientific credibility and influence.']
  influence_score varchar [note: 'Quantitative assessment of the KOL\'s influence in the genomics community based on publication impact, citations, conference presence, and social media reach. Scale 0-100.']
  institution_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary academic, clinical, or research institution where the KOL holds an appointment or conducts research.']
  institution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the KOL\'s affiliated institution to segment engagement strategies by organizational context.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Professional title or academic rank of the KOL (e.g., Professor, Director, Principal Investigator, Chief Medical Officer).']
  last_engagement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent engagement activity or interaction with the KOL. Used to track relationship recency and identify dormant relationships.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this KOL engagement record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  next_scheduled_engagement_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned engagement activity or touchpoint with the KOL. Supports proactive relationship management.']
  publication_collaboration_count varchar [note: 'Number of peer-reviewed publications co-authored or collaborated on with Genomics Biotech scientists or featuring company platforms.']
  reference_site_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the KOL\'s institution serves as a reference site for prospective customers or case study development.']
  secondary_engagement_role varchar [note: 'Additional role or capacity in which the KOL may engage, supporting multi-faceted relationships.']
  secondary_expertise_area varchar [note: 'Additional scientific or clinical domain where the KOL has notable expertise, enabling cross-functional engagement opportunities.']
  speaking_engagement_count varchar [note: 'Number of speaking engagements, presentations, or webinars delivered by the KOL on behalf of or featuring Genomics Biotech platforms.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the KOL\'s primary institution is located. Supports sub-national territory management.']
  total_engagement_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of formal engagement activities or interactions with the KOL since first engagement. Indicates depth of relationship.']
  total_publication_count varchar [note: 'Total number of peer-reviewed publications authored or co-authored by the KOL across their career.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking structured engagement with Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) — prominent genomics researchers, clinical genomicists, bioinformaticians, and laboratory directors who influence purchasing decisions and scientific adoption of Genomics Biotech platforms. Captures KOL identity, affiliated institution, area of expertise (NGS, single-cell, spatial genomics, clinical WGS), engagement tier (advisory board, speaker, beta tester, reference site), engagement history summary, active programs, publication collaborations, and assigned commercial relationship owner.'
}

Table commercial.conference_event {
  conference_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the conference or event record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign under which this event participation is organized. Links event to broader campaign strategy and budget.']
  market_segment_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.market_segment_target. Business justification: Conference events are planned to reach specific market segments. Adding market_segment_target_id FK links the event to the target segment definition, enabling segment-based event ROI analysis, lead ge']
  parent_conference_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on conference_event (parent_conference_event_id)']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory responsible for this event. Used for territory-level event planning and lead assignment.']
  actual_lead_volume varchar [note: 'Actual number of qualified leads generated from this event, captured post-event for ROI (Return on Investment) tracking and future planning.']
  application_area varchar [note: 'Scientific or clinical application area emphasized at this event (e.g., oncology, rare disease, population genomics, agricultural genomics, microbial genomics, pharmacogenomics).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for event participation and budget: draft (initial planning), pending_approval (submitted for management review), approved (authorized to proceed), rejected (not approved).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event participation was officially approved.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the manager or executive who approved the event participation and budget allocation.']
  assigned_commercial_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the commercial team responsible for managing Genomics Biotech\'s participation (e.g., North America Sales, EMEA Marketing, APAC Field Applications).']
  booth_number varchar [note: 'Assigned booth or stand number on the exhibition floor (e.g., Booth 1234, Stand A-45). Null if not exhibiting.']
  booth_size_sqm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical size of the exhibition booth in square meters. Used for logistics planning and cost allocation.']
  budget_allocated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated for this event participation, including sponsorship fees, booth costs, travel, marketing materials, and staffing. Used for financial planning and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.']
  budget_spent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual amount spent on this event participation as of the last update. Tracked against allocated budget for variance analysis.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the event is held.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the event location (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, JPN). Use VRT for fully virtual events.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this conference event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for budget and financial amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  end_date date [note: 'Official end date of the event (last day of conference or trade show).']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or short code for the event (e.g., ASHG2024, AGBT2025, JPM-HC-2024).']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the conference, trade show, symposium, or industry event (e.g., American Society of Human Genetics Annual Meeting, Advances in Genome Biology and Technology).']
  event_owner_email varchar [note: 'Corporate email address of the event owner for coordination and communication.']
  event_owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Genomics Biotech employee who is the primary owner and coordinator for this event participation.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the event: planned (initial planning stage), confirmed (registration and logistics finalized), in_progress (event is currently happening), completed (event concluded), cancelled (event will not occur), postponed (event rescheduled to future date).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the event format: conference (multi-day scientific meeting), trade show (commercial exhibition), symposium (focused scientific gathering), webinar series (virtual event series), workshop (hands-on training), or summit (executive-level gathering).']
  event_website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL for the event, providing access to event details, agenda, registration, and logistics information.']
  expected_attendee_count varchar [note: 'Estimated total number of attendees expected at the event, provided by the organizer or based on historical data. Used for market reach assessment.']
  expected_lead_volume varchar [note: 'Projected number of qualified leads expected to be generated from this event, used for pre-event planning and post-event ROI (Return on Investment) measurement.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region classification for the event location, used for regional market analytics and territory planning.']
  kol_engagement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event includes planned engagement with Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) in the genomics or clinical community. True if KOL meetings, advisory boards, or speaker engagements are scheduled.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this conference event record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special requirements, logistics details, or strategic considerations related to this event participation.']
  organizer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or entity organizing the event (e.g., American Society of Human Genetics, European Society of Human Genetics, Cambridge Healthtech Institute).']
  participation_type varchar [note: 'Nature of Genomics Biotech\'s participation in the event: exhibitor (booth presence), sponsor (financial or in-kind sponsorship), speaker (presenting scientific or commercial content), attendee (passive attendance), organizer (co-organizing or hosting), virtual_participant (online-only engagement).']
  presentation_count varchar [note: 'Number of scientific or commercial presentations, posters, or talks delivered by Genomics Biotech representatives at this event.']
  product_focus varchar [note: 'Primary Genomics Biotech product family or solution being promoted at this event (e.g., NGS (Next-Generation Sequencing) platforms, array-based solutions, CRISPR gene editing tools, bioinformatics software, clinical assays).']
  registration_deadline_date date [note: 'Deadline date for event registration, booth reservation, or sponsorship commitment. Used for internal planning and coordination.']
  sponsorship_tier varchar [note: 'Level of sponsorship commitment if Genomics Biotech is a sponsor: platinum (highest tier with maximum visibility), gold, silver, bronze, exhibitor_only (booth without sponsorship), none (no sponsorship).']
  start_date date [note: 'Official start date of the event (first day of conference or trade show).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the event is held (applicable for countries with state/province subdivisions).']
  venue_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physical venue or facility hosting the event (e.g., Walter E. Washington Convention Center, Moscone Center, virtual platform name for online events).']

  Note: 'Master record for scientific conferences, trade shows, and industry events where Genomics Biotech participates commercially — including ASHG, AGBT, ESHG, JPM Healthcare Conference, Bio-IT World, and regional genomics symposia. Captures event name, event type (conference, trade show, symposium, webinar series), organizer, location, dates, Genomics Biotech participation type (exhibitor, sponsor, speaker, attendee), booth size, sponsorship tier, assigned commercial team, budget, and expected lead volume. Enables event ROI tracking and commercial calendar management.'
}

Table commercial.forecast {
  forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the commercial forecast record. Primary key.']
  family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_family. Business justification: Sales forecasts are rolled up by product family for executive reporting, quota setting, and manufacturing planning. Finance and sales operations require structured product family dimension for forecas']
  forecast_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (typically senior sales leader or finance manager) who approved this forecast.']
  forecast_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales manager or territory manager who submitted this forecast.']
  prior_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on forecast (prior_forecast_id)']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory for which this forecast is submitted.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval status of the forecast by senior sales leadership or finance.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this forecast was approved.']
  assumptions varchar [note: 'Key assumptions underlying this forecast (e.g., product launch timing, pricing stability, market conditions).']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'The submitting managers subjective confidence level in achieving this forecast.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary country covered by this forecast.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this forecast record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the forecasted revenue amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period covered by this forecast.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The specific fiscal period identifier combining year and period (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-M03, 2024-W12).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter to which this forecast applies (Q1, Q2, Q3, or Q4).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this forecast applies, represented as a four-digit year (e.g., 2024).']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'The confidence level or stage of the forecast: pipeline (early stage), best_case (optimistic), commit (high confidence), or closed (won deals).']
  forecast_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the forecast submission, typically generated by the CRM or forecast management system.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the forecast submission in the approval workflow.']
  forecasted_instrument_units varchar [note: 'The number of instrument units (sequencers, array scanners, gene editing systems) forecasted to be sold in this period.']
  forecasted_reagent_arr decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annualized recurring revenue from reagents and consumables forecasted for this period, representing the ongoing revenue stream from installed base.']
  forecasted_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total revenue amount forecasted for the period, territory, and product family combination.']
  forecasted_service_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'The revenue from service contracts, maintenance agreements, and technical support forecasted for this period.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region covered by this forecast (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, Latin America).']
  is_latest_version boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the most current version of the forecast for this period and territory.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'The target market segment for this forecast (e.g., Clinical Diagnostics, Research Institutions, Pharmaceutical and Biotech, Agricultural Genomics, Government and Public Health).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this forecast record was last modified in the lakehouse.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or commentary from the submitting manager regarding this forecast.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'The time granularity of the forecast submission (weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annual).']
  prior_period_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual or forecasted revenue from the immediately preceding period, used for period-over-period comparison and trend analysis.']
  risk_factors varchar [note: 'Free-text description of known risks or challenges that could impact achievement of this forecast (e.g., competitive pressure, regulatory delays, supply chain constraints).']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which the forecasted revenue is expected to be realized.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system from which this forecast was captured (e.g., Salesforce CRM, custom forecast management tool).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this forecast record in the source system.']
  start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period covered by this forecast.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this forecast was submitted by the sales manager through the CRM or forecast management system.']
  submitting_manager_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the sales manager who submitted this forecast.']
  submitting_manager_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the sales manager or territory manager who submitted this forecast.']
  variance_to_prior_period decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between the current forecast and the prior period revenue, calculated as current minus prior.']
  variance_to_quota decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between the forecasted revenue and the assigned quota for this territory and period.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'The version number of this forecast submission, incremented when a forecast is revised or resubmitted.']

  Note: 'Periodic commercial revenue forecast record capturing the sales team\'s forward-looking revenue and unit projections by territory, product family, and fiscal period. Captures forecast period (week, month, quarter), forecast category (commit, best case, pipeline), forecasted revenue, forecasted instrument units, forecasted reagent ARR, product family, territory, submitting manager, submission date, and prior period comparison. Distinct from finance.budget — this is the operational sales forecast submitted through the CRM/forecast management process, not the financial plan.'
}

Table commercial.market_development_fund {
  market_development_fund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the market development fund program record. Primary key.']

Adding campaign_id FK links the MDF program to the campaign it supports, enabling MDF ROI tracking, c']
  channel_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the channel partner to whom this MDF program is allocated.']
  market_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  parent_market_development_fund_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on market_development_fund (parent_market_development_fund_id)']
  primary_market_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or manager who approved the MDF program allocation.']
  quaternary_market_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the MDF program record.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales territory associated with this MDF program for regional alignment and reporting.']
  tertiary_market_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the MDF program record.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fund amount allocated to the partner for this MDF program.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the MDF program was officially approved for activation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the MDF program by internal governance or finance teams.']
  balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining available fund balance calculated as allocated minus utilized and committed amounts.']
  claim_submission_deadline date [note: 'Final date by which partners must submit claims for reimbursement under this MDF program.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fund amount committed to pending claims that have been submitted but not yet approved or paid.']

  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center code in the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA CO) to which MDF expenses are allocated.']

  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this MDF program.']
