  partner_tier varchar [note: 'Partner tier or level in the Genomics Biotech partner program, which may influence MDF allocation amounts and terms.']
  performance_target_description varchar [note: 'Description of performance targets or Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) that the partner is expected to achieve through this MDF program (e.g., lead generation targets, event attendance, pipeline creation).']
  product_family_focus varchar [note: 'Genomics product families or solution areas that this MDF program is intended to promote (e.g., NGS platforms, array-based solutions, gene editing tools).']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the MDF program, used for external reference and tracking.']
  program_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date when the MDF program expires and no further claims can be submitted.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the MDF program, identifying the co-marketing initiative or campaign focus.']
  program_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, special terms, restrictions, or operational details about the MDF program.']
  program_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date when the MDF program becomes active and funds are available for partner use.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MDF program indicating its operational state.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the MDF program by its primary marketing objective and activity type.']
  reimbursement_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of eligible marketing expenses that will be reimbursed by Genomics Biotech under this MDF program (e.g., 50% co-funding means partner pays 50%, Genomics Biotech reimburses 50%).']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'Intended customer segments or market verticals that the MDF program aims to reach (e.g., clinical labs, research institutions, pharmaceutical companies).']
  utilized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fund amount utilized to date through approved claims and reimbursements.']

  Note: 'Master record for Market Development Fund (MDF) programs allocated to channel partners to co-fund marketing and demand-generation activities for Genomics Biotech products. Captures fund program name, partner identity, fund allocation amount, currency, program period, eligible activities (events, digital campaigns, training), fund utilization to date, claim submission deadline, approval status, and fund balance. Enables channel marketing investment governance and partner co-marketing program management.'
}

Table commercial.partner_genomic_access_authorization {
  partner_genomic_access_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the partner genomic access authorization record. Primary key.']
  channel_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the channel partner who is being granted access authorization']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data governance officer or administrator who approved this partner access authorization.']
  genomic_access_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the genomic access control policy being authorized']
  access_tier_granted varchar [note: 'Specific access tier granted to this partner for this dataset. May be more restrictive than the dataset\'s maximum tier based on partner agreement and training level. Values: open, registered, controlled, restricted.']
  authorization_date date [note: 'Date when the channel partner was granted authorization to access the genomic dataset under this access control policy. Marks the start of the authorization lifecycle.']
  authorization_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the partner authorization expires and access must be revoked or renewed. Null indicates indefinite authorization subject to policy changes.']
  authorization_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special conditions, restrictions, or context for this authorization.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this authorization. Active (full access), suspended (temporary hold), revoked (terminated), expired (past expiry date), pending_renewal (approaching expiry).']
  compliance_audit_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance audit status for this partner-dataset authorization. Tracks whether the partner is meeting data handling and security requirements. Values: compliant, non_compliant, pending_review, audit_required.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit for this partner-dataset authorization.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Date when the next compliance audit is scheduled or required for this authorization.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date when this authorization was revoked. Null for active authorizations.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for authorization revocation (e.g., compliance violation, partnership termination, policy change, partner request).']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the partner completed required training for handling this class of genomic data. Required for controlled and restricted tier access.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the authorization contract between channel partners and genomic access control policies. It captures the lifecycle of partner access to genomic datasets, including authorization dates, compliance status, and training requirements. Each record links one channel partner to one genomic access control policy with attributes that exist only in the context of this authorization relationship.. Existence Justification: In genomics biotechnology operations, channel partners (distributors, service providers, resellers) require authorized access to multiple genomic datasets for various business purposes: reference data for product demonstrations, training datasets for partner enablement, and customer genomic data when providing services under partnership agreements. Each partner requires multiple authorizations across different datasets with varying access tiers, and each genomic access control policy governs access for multiple partners. The business actively manages these authorizations as a compliance and data governance function, tracking authorization lifecycle, training completion, audit status, and expiry dates.'
}

Table commercial.campaign_allocation {
  campaign_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this cost center-campaign allocation record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign being allocated across cost centers']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the cost center receiving the campaign budget allocation']
  actual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount spent by this cost center on this campaign to date, tracked for budget variance analysis.']
  allocation_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date for this cost center allocation. Null if allocation is active through campaign completion.']
  allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining the rationale for this allocation split or any special considerations.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the campaign budget allocated to this specific cost center. Sum of all allocations for a campaign should equal 100%.']
  allocation_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date for this cost center allocation. Supports time-phased budget allocations where cost center participation changes during campaign lifecycle.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this allocation: planned (budget reserved), active (spending in progress), completed (campaign ended), cancelled (allocation removed).']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of campaign budget allocated to this cost center, calculated as campaign total budget × allocation percentage.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation record was created in the system.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP internal order number used for detailed expense tracking for this specific cost center-campaign allocation.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation record was last updated.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated variance between allocated budget and actual spend for this cost center-campaign combination (actual_spend - budget_amount).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the budget allocation relationship between cost centers and marketing campaigns. It captures how campaign budgets are split across multiple cost centers for financial tracking and variance analysis. Each record links one cost center to one campaign with allocation percentages, budget amounts, and actual spend that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: In genomics biotech marketing operations, campaigns routinely span multiple cost centers with split budget allocations. A product launch campaign may allocate 40% to Product Marketing (cost center NGS-MKT-001), 35% to Field Marketing (FLD-MKT-002), and 25% to Corporate Marketing (CORP-MKT-003). Each cost center participates in multiple campaigns simultaneously. Finance and marketing teams actively manage these allocations for budget tracking, variance analysis, and cost center P&L reporting.'
}

Table commercial.campaign_participation {
  campaign_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign participation record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee participating in the campaign']
  cost_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the employee\'s time/cost allocated to this campaign for financial tracking and cost center allocation purposes. Supports the business need for cost allocation mentioned in detection reasoning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this participation record was created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s participation in this campaign ends. May differ from campaign end date if employee leaves early. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  hours_allocated decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of hours allocated for this employee\'s participation in the campaign. Used for resource planning and cost allocation. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this participation assignment. Tracks whether the participation is planned, confirmed, actively in progress, completed, or cancelled.']
  participation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the participation commitment level. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  role varchar [note: 'The specific role the employee fulfills in this campaign (e.g., presenter at conference, booth staff, technical expert for product demos, KOL liaison). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s participation in this campaign begins. May differ from campaign start date if employee joins mid-campaign. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this participation record was last updated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation relationship between employees and marketing campaigns at Genomics Biotech. It captures the operational assignment of employees to campaigns with specific roles, time allocations, and participation periods. Each record links one employee to one campaign with attributes that exist only in the context of this participation relationship, enabling resource planning, cost allocation, and campaign staffing management.. Existence Justification: In genomics biotech marketing operations, employees participate in multiple campaigns throughout the year (e.g., a Field Application Specialist may support Q1 product launch, attend ASHG conference in Q3, and participate in KOL engagement program in Q4), and each campaign requires multiple employees with different specialized roles (product specialists, bioinformaticians for technical demos, FAS for customer engagement, marketing managers for coordination). The business actively manages these participation assignments for resource planning, tracks hours allocated per employee per campaign for cost allocation to campaign budgets, and maintains participation history with specific roles and time periods.'
}

Table commercial.opportunity_team_member {
  opportunity_team_member_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this opportunity team member assignment record. Primary key for the association.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee who is a member of the opportunity team. References the authoritative employee master record from Oracle Cloud HCM.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the commercial opportunity for which this employee is a team member. References the core opportunity record in the sales pipeline.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this team member assignment. Active = currently working the opportunity, Inactive = assignment ended, Pending Approval = awaiting sales manager approval for commission split changes.']
  contribution_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of credit this team member receives for this opportunity, used for commission splits and performance attribution. Must sum to 100% across all team members for a given opportunity. Account Executive typically receives 60-80%, with remaining split among supporting roles.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when this employee\'s assignment to the opportunity team ended. Null for active team members. Populated when team member is reassigned, leaves company, or opportunity closes.']
  involvement_stage varchar [note: 'The sales stage at which this team member became involved in the opportunity. Used to track when different roles engage (e.g., FAS typically joins at Solution Design stage, Sales Manager at Negotiation stage).']
  is_primary_owner boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this team member is the primary owner of the opportunity (typically the Account Executive). Each opportunity must have exactly one primary owner at any time. Primary owner has ultimate responsibility for advancing the opportunity and receives largest commission split.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when this employee was assigned to the opportunity team. Used to track team composition changes over the opportunity lifecycle and determine commission eligibility periods.']
  team_role varchar [note: 'The specific role this employee plays on the opportunity team. Determines responsibilities, approval authority, and commission eligibility. Examples: Account Executive (relationship owner), Field Application Scientist (technical validation), Sales Engineer (integration design), Sales Manager (approval authority).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the team assignment relationship between employees and commercial opportunities. It captures the multi-role sales team structure required for complex genomics platform sales, where each opportunity requires multiple employees in different roles (account executive, field application scientist, sales engineer, sales manager) and each employee works multiple opportunities concurrently. Each record links one employee to one opportunity with role, involvement stage, contribution percentage for commission splits, and time period attributes.. Existence Justification: In genomics platform sales, complex opportunities require multi-role sales teams where each employee works multiple opportunities concurrently and each opportunity has multiple team members in different roles (account executive, field application scientist, sales engineer, sales manager). The business actively manages these team assignments to track roles, involvement stages, and contribution percentages for commission splits and performance attribution. This is an operational relationship that humans create, update, and delete as team composition changes throughout the opportunity lifecycle.'
}

Table commercial.key_opinion_leader {

  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the key opinion leader record.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Therapeutic area(s) the key opinion leader focuses on (e.g., oncology, rare diseases).']
  title varchar [note: 'Professional title or position held by the key opinion leader.']
  twitter_handle varchar [note: 'Twitter username used for professional communications.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for key_opinion_leader. Referenced by kol_id.'
}

Table data.quality_rule {
  quality_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the data quality rule. Primary key for the quality rule catalog.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_asset. Business justification: Quality rules target specific instruments for automated validation (e.g., Q30 thresholds for NovaSeq 6000). Enables instrument-specific QC rule execution in LIMS workflows. Genomics labs routinely con']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Quality rules monitor reagent specifications (concentration, purity, sterility) for automated QC. Real business need: continuous quality monitoring of reagent products per GMP requirements.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Product specifications define quality acceptance criteria that become automated quality rules. NGS sequencers require Q30 score rules, arrays need probe intensity thresholds. Quality validation protoc']
  gene_annotation_track_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.gene_annotation_track. Business justification: Quality rules validating transcript annotations, exon boundary integrity, or coding sequence completeness must specify annotation release version (GENCODE v38 vs v42 have different transcript sets); c']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Quality rules validating genomic coordinates, variant positions, or alignment metrics must specify reference genome build (GRCh38 vs GRCh37) to ensure correct validation logic; critical for variant ca']
  metadata_schema_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.metadata_schema. Business justification: Quality rules validate data compliance with metadata schemas and standards. Many quality_rules can validate one metadata_schema. This FK links the quality rule to the metadata schema it validates, ens']
  parent_quality_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on quality_rule (parent_quality_rule_id)']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: SKU-level quality rules enforce lot-specific performance criteria. Flow cell lots must meet cluster density thresholds, reagent lots require purity specs. Manufacturing QC, lot release testing, and ce']
  software_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.software_release. Business justification: Software releases include embedded quality rules. Variant callers have built-in quality filters (QUAL>30, DP>10), alignment software has mapping quality thresholds. Release notes document rule changes']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_specification. Business justification: Product specifications define acceptance criteria that become automated quality rules. Spec says read length ≥150bp, quality rule enforces it. Spec defines LOD ≤10 copies/mL, rule validates it. Design']
  variant_database_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.variant_database_version. Business justification: Quality rules checking variant clinical significance or population frequency thresholds must lock to specific ClinVar/gnomAD versions; monthly ClinVar releases reclassify variants, so rules must versi']
  alert_threshold_breach_count varchar [note: 'Number of consecutive executions where this rule failed to meet its threshold. Used to trigger escalation workflows after sustained violations.']
  business_impact varchar [note: 'Description of downstream business consequences if this rule is violated (e.g., regulatory non-compliance, incorrect clinical reports, revenue leakage, patient safety risk).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rule record was first created in the catalog. Immutable audit field required for regulatory compliance.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to detailed documentation, Standard Operating Procedure (SOP), or knowledge base article explaining this rule\'s business context, validation logic, and remediation procedures.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this rule version was retired or superseded. Null for currently active rules. Enables historical rule analysis and compliance reporting.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this rule version became active and began enforcing data quality checks. Supports temporal rule management and compliance auditing.']
  enforcement_action varchar [note: 'Automated action taken when rule fails: block (halt pipeline), warn (continue with warning), quarantine (isolate bad records), log (record only), alert (notify stakeholders).']
  execution_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this rule is evaluated: real-time (streaming/DLT), batch at scheduled intervals, or on-demand for ad-hoc validation.']
  execution_platform varchar [note: 'Technical platform or framework used to execute this rule (e.g., Databricks Delta Live Tables, Great Expectations, AWS Deequ, custom Spark job).']
  fair_principle varchar [note: 'Which FAIR data principle this rule supports for genomic datasets: Findable (metadata completeness), Accessible (access control validation), Interoperable (format/standard compliance), Reusable (provenance/license checks).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule is currently active and being enforced in data pipelines. False for deprecated or temporarily disabled rules.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule is automatically enforced by data pipelines (true) or requires manual validation (false).']
  last_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of this rule. Used to monitor rule execution health and identify stale validations.']
  last_failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of records that failed validation in the most recent rule execution. Used for impact assessment and prioritization.']
  last_pass_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Pass rate (0.0000 to 1.0000) from the most recent rule execution. Represents the proportion of records that satisfied the rule. Used for trend analysis and alerting.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who last modified this rule record. Updated on every change to support audit trail.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this rule record. Updated on every change to support audit trail and change tracking.']
  regulatory_requirement varchar [note: 'Citation of specific regulatory requirement, compliance framework, or industry standard this rule supports (e.g., FDA 21 CFR Part 11, CLIA, CAP, GDPR Article 5, HIPAA Security Rule).']
  remediation_guidance varchar [note: 'Step-by-step instructions for data stewards or engineers to investigate and remediate rule violations. May include links to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) or runbooks.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Business domain category this rule applies to, enabling domain-specific rule management and governance workflows.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Standardized unique code identifier for the rule following pattern DQR-{DOMAIN}-{NUMBER}, enabling cross-system reference and traceability.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of what the rule validates, why it matters, and the expected data behavior. Should be understandable by non-technical stakeholders.']
  rule_expression varchar [note: 'The executable logic or SQL expression that defines the rule validation. May contain Spark SQL syntax, Python validation logic, or declarative constraint definition.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the data quality rule. Should be descriptive and follow organizational naming conventions.']
  rule_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business data steward or domain owner accountable for defining and maintaining this rule.']
  rule_steward_email varchar [note: 'Email contact for the data steward responsible for this rule. Used for automated alerts and remediation workflows.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of data quality dimension this rule enforces: completeness (non-null checks), uniqueness (duplicate detection), validity (format/range checks), consistency (cross-field logic), timeliness (freshness checks), or referential integrity (FK validation).']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the rule logic (format: vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). Incremented when rule expression or thresholds change, enabling audit trail and rollback.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Impact severity of rule violations: critical (blocks pipeline/report release), major (requires immediate remediation), minor (tracked for improvement), warning (informational only).']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of custom tags for rule categorization, search, and filtering (e.g., \'genomic,NGS,FASTQ\', \'clinical,IVD,FDA\', \'PII,GDPR\'). Supports flexible rule discovery and governance workflows.']
  target_asset_name varchar [note: 'Fully qualified name of the target data asset (table, view, or dataset) this rule validates. Format: {catalog}.{schema}.{table} for Databricks Unity Catalog assets.']
  target_column_name varchar [note: 'Specific column or attribute name within the target asset that this rule validates. Null if rule applies to entire asset or multiple columns.']
  target_domain varchar [note: 'The data domain this rule applies to (e.g., Sequencing, Sample Management, Clinical Genomics, Customer). Maps to enterprise data domain taxonomy.']
  threshold_operator varchar [note: 'Comparison operator applied between actual metric and threshold value to determine rule compliance.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the threshold value (e.g., percentage for pass rates, count for record volumes, days for timeliness checks). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percentage|count|ratio|seconds|minutes|hours|days — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  threshold_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric threshold for rule pass/fail determination. Interpretation depends on threshold_operator (e.g., pass rate >= 0.95 means 95% of records must pass).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who created this rule record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of enterprise data quality rules applied to genomic and operational datasets across all domains. Captures rule name, rule type (completeness, uniqueness, validity, consistency, timeliness, referential integrity), target asset or domain, rule expression or logic, severity level (critical/major/minor), expected threshold, remediation guidance, and active status. Supports automated DQ enforcement in Databricks pipelines and FAIR interoperability compliance.'
}

Table data.quality_assessment {
  quality_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the data quality assessment execution run. Primary key.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_asset. Business justification: Instruments undergo periodic data quality assessments for GxP compliance and qualification maintenance. Required for regulatory audits (FDA 21 CFR Part 11). Tracks instrument performance over time aga']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Quality assessments of sequencing runs, BAM/CRAM files, and variant callsets must record which reference genome was used for alignment and variant calling to enable reproducibility audits and regulato']
  instrument_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_run. Business justification: Every sequencing run undergoes quality assessment (cluster density, Q30, PhiX error rate). Core operational workflow in genomics labs. Enables run-level QC reporting and pass/fail decisions for downst']
  previous_quality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on quality_assessment (previous_quality_assessment_id)']
  accuracy_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Data accuracy dimension score, measuring correctness of values against reference data (0.00 to 100.00).']
  assessed_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system or pipeline that executed the assessment (e.g., Databricks_DQ_Pipeline, Great_Expectations, Manual_Review).']
  assessed_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or system account that executed the quality assessment (e.g., data_steward_id, automated_pipeline_service).']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the assessment run, including context, exceptions, or manual review findings.']
  assessment_result varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail result of the quality assessment based on threshold comparison: pass, fail, or conditional_pass.']
  assessment_run_number varchar [note: 'Business-friendly identifier for the quality assessment run, formatted as QA-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.']
  assessment_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of data evaluated: full dataset, sample subset, incremental (new records only), or targeted (specific columns/rules).']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current execution status of the quality assessment run: completed, failed, in_progress, cancelled, or partial.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of quality assessment execution: automated pipeline, manual review, hybrid, scheduled, or on-demand.']
  completeness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Data completeness dimension score, measuring presence of required values (0.00 to 100.00).']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance framework this assessment validates against (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR, CLIA, FDA_21_CFR_Part_11, FAIR_Principles).']
  consistency_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Data consistency dimension score, measuring uniformity across related datasets (0.00 to 100.00).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this quality assessment record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format.']
  critical_failures_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical quality rule failures that require immediate remediation (e.g., PHI exposure, regulatory non-compliance).']
  data_domain varchar [note: 'Business domain to which the assessed asset belongs (e.g., Sequencing, Clinical Genomics, Sample Management).']
  data_steward_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the data steward was notified of assessment results (True/False).']
  execution_duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time taken to complete the quality assessment run, measured in seconds.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality assessment run was executed, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  failure_summary varchar [note: 'Summary description of key quality rule failures and their business impact.']
  fair_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessed genomic dataset meets FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) principles (True/False).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this quality assessment record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when data steward or stakeholders were notified of assessment results, in ISO 8601 format.']
  overall_dq_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite data quality score for this assessment, calculated as percentage of rules passed (0.00 to 100.00).']
  pass_fail_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum data quality score required for the assessment to be considered passing (0.00 to 100.00).']
  phi_exposure_detected_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Protected Health Information exposure or non-compliance was detected during assessment (True/False).']
  records_assessed_count varchar [note: 'Total number of data records evaluated during this quality assessment run.']
  records_failed_count varchar [note: 'Number of data records that failed one or more quality rules during this assessment.']
  remediation_actions_triggered varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of automated remediation actions triggered by this assessment (e.g., data_quarantine, alert_steward, block_pipeline).']
  remediation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data remediation actions are required based on assessment results (True/False).']
  rule_set_name varchar [note: 'Name of the data quality rule set applied during this assessment (e.g., NGS_Data_Validation, Clinical_PHI_Compliance).']
  rule_set_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version of the quality rule set applied (e.g., 1.2.3).']
  rules_failed_count varchar [note: 'Number of quality rules that failed validation during this assessment.']
  rules_passed_count varchar [note: 'Number of quality rules that passed validation during this assessment.']
  rules_warning_count varchar [note: 'Number of quality rules that generated warnings (non-critical issues) during this assessment.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of records in the sample if assessment_scope is sample; null for full assessments.']
  target_asset_name varchar [note: 'Fully qualified name of the data asset being assessed (e.g., database.schema.table or domain.product).']
  target_asset_type varchar [note: 'Type of data asset being assessed: table, view, dataset, domain, product, or pipeline.']
  timeliness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Data timeliness dimension score, measuring currency and availability when needed (0.00 to 100.00).']
  total_rules_executed varchar [note: 'Total number of data quality rules executed during this assessment run.']
  uniqueness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Data uniqueness dimension score, measuring absence of duplicate records (0.00 to 100.00).']
  validity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Data validity dimension score, measuring conformance to defined formats and business rules (0.00 to 100.00).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each data quality assessment execution run against a data asset or domain. Captures assessment run ID, target asset, quality rule set applied, execution timestamp, pass/fail counts per rule, overall DQ score, critical failures, remediation actions triggered, and assessed-by (automated pipeline or manual review). Provides the operational DQ audit trail for genomic datasets, clinical data, and enterprise master data.'
}

Table data.quality_issue {
  quality_issue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the data quality issue record. Primary key.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_asset. Business justification: Quality issues are tracked to specific instruments for trend analysis and preventive maintenance triggers. Enables instrument-level quality trending and service escalation. Required for GxP instrument']
  primary_quality_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that detected or reported the quality issue, enabling accountability and follow-up communication.']
  quality_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data quality rule that was violated or triggered this issue, linking to the rule definition in the data quality framework.']
  root_cause_quality_issue_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on quality_issue (root_cause_quality_issue_id)']
  tertiary_quality_verified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who verified that the quality issue resolution was effective and the data now meets quality standards.']
  actual_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual value found in the data that violated the quality rule, enabling comparison with expected value for root cause analysis (may be NULL, malformed, or out-of-range).']
  affected_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of data records impacted by this quality issue, quantifying the scope of the problem for prioritization and remediation planning.']
  affected_record_identifiers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or JSON array of primary key values for records affected by this issue, enabling direct navigation to problematic data for remediation (truncated if exceeds storage limit).']
  assigned_date date [note: 'Date when the quality issue was assigned to a data steward for remediation, marking the start of active resolution efforts.']
  business_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of the business impact caused by this quality issue, including effects on clinical reporting, regulatory submissions, research accuracy, customer deliverables, or operational decisions.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context about the quality issue, resolution process, or special circumstances that do not fit structured fields.']
  compliance_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this quality issue impacts regulatory compliance (FDA, CLIA, CAP, GDPR, HIPAA) or audit requirements, requiring elevated attention and documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this quality issue record was first created in the data quality management system, establishing the audit trail start point.']
  data_domain varchar [note: 'Business data domain where the quality issue was identified (e.g., Sequencing, Sample Management, Clinical Genomics, Bioinformatics, Quality and Compliance), enabling domain-specific trend analysis.']
  detected_date date [note: 'Date when the quality issue was first identified, establishing the timeline for issue age and Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the quality issue was first identified, enabling detailed audit trail and time-to-resolution metrics.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the quality issue was identified: automated_rule (scheduled DQ check), manual_review (steward inspection), user_report (end-user feedback), reconciliation (cross-system comparison), profiling (data discovery scan).']
  expected_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The correct or expected value according to the data quality rule or business standard, providing a reference for remediation (e.g., required metadata field, valid enumeration value, correct format).']
  fair_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this quality issue violates FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) principles for genomic data sharing and metadata standards (e.g., missing required metadata, non-standard ontology terms).']
  issue_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the quality issue, including specific examples of violations, business context, and potential downstream impact on genomic analysis or regulatory compliance.']
  issue_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the quality issue, formatted as DQ-YYYYMMDD sequence for tracking and reference in communications.']
  issue_type varchar [note: 'Category of data quality dimension violated: completeness (missing data), accuracy (incorrect values), consistency (conflicting data across sources), validity (format/constraint violations), timeliness (stale/outdated data), uniqueness (duplicate records).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this quality issue record, enabling change tracking and audit trail for all modifications throughout the issue lifecycle.']
  preventive_action varchar [note: 'Description of preventive actions implemented to avoid recurrence of similar quality issues (e.g., enhanced validation rules, updated Standard Operating Procedure (SOP), additional training, system configuration changes).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority for remediation based on severity, affected systems, regulatory deadlines, and business impact. Urgent: immediate action required; High: resolve within 24 hours; Normal: standard SLA; Low: backlog.']
  quality_issue_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quality issue: open (newly identified), assigned (steward assigned), in_progress (under remediation), resolved (fix applied pending verification), closed (verified and complete), rejected (false positive or accepted risk).']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the remediation action taken to resolve the quality issue (e.g., corrected missing metadata, updated variant classification, merged duplicate records, fixed referential integrity).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the quality issue was resolved and remediation actions were completed, enabling time-to-resolution metrics and SLA compliance tracking.']
  resolution_method varchar [note: 'Method used to resolve the issue: data_correction (fixed data values), rule_adjustment (modified DQ rule), source_system_fix (corrected at source), accepted_exception (documented business exception), false_positive (rule triggered incorrectly).']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the quality issue was resolved, providing detailed audit trail for compliance and performance analysis.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying root cause of the quality issue for trend analysis and preventive action: data_entry_error, system_integration (ETL/interface issue), process_gap (missing SOP), rule_misconfiguration, data_migration (conversion issue), external_source (vendor data quality).']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the root cause analysis findings, documenting why the quality issue occurred and what systemic factors contributed to the problem.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Business impact severity of the quality issue. Critical: blocks regulatory submission or patient safety; High: impacts clinical reporting; Medium: affects analytics accuracy; Low: cosmetic or minor metadata issues.']
  source_column varchar [note: 'Specific column or field name within the source table where the quality issue was identified, enabling field-level remediation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system or data source where the quality issue was detected (e.g., LabVantage LIMS, Illumina BaseSpace, SAP S/4HANA QM, Benchling, Veeva Vault).']
  source_table varchar [note: 'Fully qualified name of the data asset (database table, file, or dataset) containing the quality issue, enabling precise traceability to the affected data structure.']
  target_resolution_date date [note: 'Target date by which the quality issue should be resolved, based on Service Level Agreement (SLA), severity, and business requirements.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of post-resolution verification: pending (awaiting verification), verified (fix confirmed effective), failed (issue persists or recurred, requires re-work).']
  verified_date date [note: 'Date when the quality issue resolution was verified as effective, completing the issue lifecycle and enabling closure.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of individual data quality issues identified during assessment runs or manual review — including missing genomic metadata, invalid variant classifications, duplicate sample records, broken referential integrity between clinical specimens and test orders, and non-conformant FAIR metadata. Captures issue ID, source asset, rule violated, affected records count, severity, assigned steward, resolution status, resolution date, and root cause category.'
}

Table data.mdm_policy {
  mdm_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the master data governance policy record.']
  superseded_mdm_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on mdm_policy (superseded_mdm_policy_id)']
  access_control_level varchar [note: 'The default data classification level for data governed by this policy: public (no restrictions), internal (internal use only), confidential (business-sensitive), or restricted (PII/PHI/PCI requiring strict access controls).']
  anonymization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data governed by this policy must be anonymized or de-identified before use in research, analytics, or sharing. True if anonymization is required, False otherwise.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the policy was formally approved by the approving authority.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the individual who approved the policy.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'The role, committee, or individual responsible for approving and authorizing this policy (e.g., Chief Data Officer, Data Governance Council, Quality Assurance Director).']
  archival_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data must be archived (moved to long-term storage) rather than deleted after the retention period expires. True if archival is required, False if deletion is permitted.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'The regulatory or industry framework(s) to which this policy is aligned, such as GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation), HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act), GxP (Good Practice Regulations), FAIR (Findable Accessible Interoperable Reusable), CLIA (Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments), CAP (College of American Pathologists), ISO 13485, ISO 15189, or IVDR (In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation). Multiple frameworks may be listed.']
  consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit patient or subject consent is required before data governed by this policy can be collected, processed, or shared. True if consent is mandatory, False otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this policy record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_transfer_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data governed by this policy may be transferred across international borders. True if cross-border transfer is permitted (subject to applicable safeguards), False if data must remain within the originating jurisdiction.']
  data_domain varchar [note: 'The primary data domain to which this policy applies (e.g., Sequencing, Sample Management, Clinical Genomics, Bioinformatics, Quality and Compliance, Customer, Finance).']
  data_quality_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum acceptable data quality score (as a percentage) for data governed by this policy. Applicable for quality-type policies. For example, 95.00 means 95% completeness or accuracy is required.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the policy becomes enforceable and binding.']
  enforcement_mechanism varchar [note: 'The method or system by which this policy is enforced (e.g., automated data quality rules in LIMS, access control lists in data catalog, manual review by QA, workflow gates in MES).']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'The specific master data entity type governed by this policy (e.g., Patient, Sample, Reagent Lot, Genomic Variant, Instrument, Assay, Test Order).']
  exception_process varchar [note: 'The documented process for requesting and approving exceptions to this policy, including escalation path and approval authority.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the policy ceases to be in effect. Null for policies with no defined end date.']
  fair_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy enforces FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) principles for genomic datasets. True if FAIR compliance is required, False otherwise.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the policy was most recently reviewed by the governance authority.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this policy record was last modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next policy review, calculated from last_review_date plus review_cycle_months.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding the policy, including rationale for specific provisions or historical context.']
  phi_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy governs data that contains Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined by HIPAA. True if PHI is in scope, False otherwise.']
  pii_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy governs data that contains Personally Identifiable Information (PII) as defined by GDPR and other privacy regulations. True if PII is in scope, False otherwise.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the policy, following the format MDM-POL-XXXX.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'The official name of the master data governance policy.']
  policy_owner varchar [note: 'The role or department responsible for maintaining and enforcing this policy (e.g., Data Governance Office, Quality Assurance, Regulatory Affairs).']
  policy_scope varchar [note: 'The domain, entity, or data asset to which this policy applies (e.g., Patient, Sample, Reagent Lot, Genomic Variant, Sequencing Run).']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the policy: draft (being developed), active (in force), under_review (being revised), suspended (temporarily inactive), or retired (no longer applicable).']
  policy_text varchar [note: 'The full text of the governance policy, including rules, standards, and procedures that govern the master data entity or domain.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'The category of master data governance policy: retention (data lifecycle), access (authorization controls), quality (data quality rules), privacy (PII/PHI protection), fair (Findable Accessible Interoperable Reusable compliance for genomic datasets), or traceability (chain-of-custody and lineage).']
  policy_url varchar [note: 'The URL or document management system link where the full policy document is stored and accessible.']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'The version number of the policy, following semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Incremented with each revision.']
  related_sop_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) document that provides detailed operational instructions for implementing this policy.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'The number of years that data governed by this policy must be retained before it is eligible for archival or deletion. Applicable for retention-type policies.']
  retired_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this policy was retired and ceased to be active. Null if the policy is still active or under review.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'The frequency in months at which the policy must be reviewed and revalidated (e.g., 12 for annual review, 24 for biennial).']
  traceability_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether full chain-of-custody and data lineage traceability is required for data governed by this policy. True if traceability is mandatory, False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Master data governance policy record defining the rules, standards, and procedures governing master data entities across Genomics Biotech — including patient/subject identity resolution, sample chain-of-custody standards, reagent lot traceability requirements, and genomic data retention policies. Captures policy name, scope (domain or entity), policy type (retention, access, quality, privacy, FAIR), policy text, effective date, review cycle, approving authority, and compliance framework linkage (GDPR, HIPAA, GxP).'
}

Table data.genomic_access_control {
  genomic_access_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the genomic access control policy record. Primary key.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Access control policies for genomic datasets are genome-build-specific; dbGaP/EGA accessions and consent forms reference specific assemblies. Data use agreements and IRB protocols specify allowed geno']
  license_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.license_entitlement. Business justification: Software licenses control access to analysis features and datasets. Clinical interpretation module license restricts access to ClinVar-annotated variants, oncology license enables somatic variant call']
  mdm_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.mdm_policy. Business justification: Genomic access control policies are governed by broader master data management policies. Many genomic_access_control policies can be governed by one mdm_policy. This FK establishes the governance hier']
  parent_genomic_access_control_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on genomic_access_control (parent_genomic_access_control_id)']
  access_tier varchar [note: 'Classification tier defining the level of access restriction applied to the genomic data. Open = publicly accessible, Registered = requires registration, Controlled = requires DAC approval, Restricted = highest security with IRB and consent requirements.']
  approval_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or committee that approved this access control policy.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this access control policy was formally approved by the designated authority.']
  audit_logging_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether comprehensive audit logging of all data access events is required for compliance and security monitoring.']
  authorized_roles varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of roles authorized to access the genomic data under this policy. Examples include Principal Investigator, Bioinformatician, Clinical Geneticist, Data Analyst.']
  authorized_user_groups varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of user groups or organizational units authorized to access the genomic data under this policy. May include research teams, clinical departments, or external collaborators.']
  consent_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit patient or participant consent is required for access to the genomic data governed by this policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this access control policy record was first created in the system.']
  data_access_committee_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for contacting the Data Access Committee for access requests and inquiries.']
  data_access_committee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Data Access Committee responsible for reviewing and approving access requests for controlled-access genomic data.']
  data_use_limitation_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GA4GH Data Use Ontology (DUO) codes specifying permitted and restricted uses of the genomic data. Examples: GRU (General Research Use), HMB (Health/Medical/Biomedical), DS (Disease-Specific Research).']
  data_use_limitation_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the data use limitations and restrictions governing access to the genomic data.']
  dbgap_accession_number varchar [note: 'dbGaP study accession number if the genomic dataset is registered with NIH dbGaP for controlled-access data sharing.']
  deidentification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deidentify the genomic data to protect participant privacy. None indicates identifiable data.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this access control policy expires or is no longer enforceable. Null indicates an open-ended policy.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this access control policy becomes effective and enforceable.']
  ega_accession_number varchar [note: 'EGA study or dataset accession number if the genomic dataset is registered with EGA for controlled-access data sharing in Europe.']
  encryption_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether encryption at rest and in transit is required for the genomic data governed by this policy.']
  fair_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this access control policy is designed to support FAIR principles for genomic data sharing and reuse.']
  ga4gh_passport_compatible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this access control policy is compatible with GA4GH Passport standard for federated identity and access management.']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Geographic or jurisdictional restrictions on data access, specified as comma-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes. Empty if no geographic restrictions apply.']
  irb_approval_number varchar [note: 'IRB protocol approval number authorizing the collection, storage, and access to the genomic data under this policy.']
  irb_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the IRB approval expires, requiring renewal or policy suspension.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this access control policy record was last modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of this access control policy for continued relevance and compliance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this access control policy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this access control policy.']
  phi_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the genomic dataset contains Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined by HIPAA, requiring additional privacy safeguards.']
  policy_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the full policy document detailing the access control requirements and procedures.']
  policy_identifier varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the access control policy, used for external reference and audit trails.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the access control policy describing its purpose and scope.']
  policy_owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the policy owner for inquiries and policy management.']
  policy_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or role responsible for maintaining and enforcing this access control policy.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the access control policy indicating whether it is actively enforced.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the access control policy based on the type of genomic resource being governed.']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this access control policy, incremented with each revision.']
  publication_moratorium_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the publication moratorium expires, allowing public disclosure of research findings.']
  publication_moratorium_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a publication moratorium period is in effect, restricting public disclosure of research findings derived from the genomic data.']
  target_dataset_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the genomic dataset or data asset to which this access control policy applies. May reference WGS/WES datasets, VCF repositories, or clinical genomic databases.']

  Note: 'Master record governing access control policies for genomic datasets — including WGS/WES data, variant call files (VCF), clinical genomic reports, and biobank specimen data. Captures access control policy ID, target dataset or data asset, access tier (open/registered/controlled/restricted), authorized user groups or roles, consent requirement linkage, IRB approval reference, dbGaP/EGA accession if applicable, data use limitation codes (DUL), and policy effective dates. Implements FAIR accessibility and GA4GH Passport-compatible access governance.'
}

Table data.access_request {
  access_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the genomic data access request. Primary key for the access request record.']
  amended_access_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on access_request (amended_access_request_id)']
  data_use_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this access request to the executed Data Use Agreement governing permitted uses and restrictions.']
  dpia_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.dpia. Business justification: Access requests for high-risk genomic data processing may be subject to Data Protection Impact Assessments. Many access_requests can be covered by one DPIA. This FK links the operational access reques']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual researcher or principal investigator submitting the access request.']
  genomic_access_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.genomic_access_control. Business justification: Access requests are submitted against specific genomic access control policies. Many access_requests reference one genomic_access_control policy. The access_request captures request-specific data (req']
  instrument_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_run. Business justification: Access requests for controlled genomic data reference specific runs (dbGaP, EGA submissions). Enables run-level data access governance and consent tracking. Required for NIH Genomic Data Sharing Polic']
  target_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary genomic dataset, clinical data collection, or biobank resource being requested for access.']
  access_duration_months varchar [note: 'Total duration in months for which data access is granted, typically ranging from 12 to 36 months.']
  access_grant_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the approved data access expires and the requester must cease data use or request renewal.']
  access_grant_start_date date [note: 'Effective date when approved data access begins and the requester may commence data use.']
  approval_conditions varchar [note: 'Specific terms, restrictions, or conditions attached to the approved access, such as publication embargo, data destruction requirements, or usage limitations.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Data Access Committee (DAC) member or governance body that authorized the access decision.']
  collaboration_agreement_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a formal collaboration agreement with the data custodian is required as a condition of access.']
  commercial_use_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the requester intends to use the genomic data for commercial purposes or product development.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the access request record was first created in the data governance system.']
  data_return_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the requester must return or destroy the data upon completion of the research or access expiry.']
  data_sensitivity_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the requested dataset sensitivity level determining access control requirements: public (open access), registered (registration required), or controlled (DAC approval required).']
  data_use_statement varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the intended research use, scientific objectives, and analytical methods for the requested genomic data.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date when the final approval or rejection decision was communicated to the requester.']
  dsar_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number if this access request is related to a GDPR Data Subject Access Request for an individual\'s own genomic data.']
  gdpr_lawful_basis varchar [note: 'Legal basis under GDPR Article 6 justifying the processing of personal genomic data for this access request.']
  irb_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the Institutional Review Board or ethics committee approved the research protocol.']
  irb_approval_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the ethics committee or IRB approval authorizing the proposed research involving human subjects data.']
  irb_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current IRB approval expires and requires renewal for continued research.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the access request record was most recently updated.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified the access request record.']
  phi_included_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the requested dataset contains Protected Health Information subject to HIPAA regulations.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for Data Access Committee review, with urgent requests receiving accelerated processing.']
  publication_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the requester is required to publish research findings as a condition of data access.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation provided by the Data Access Committee for rejecting the access request.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the access request, formatted as AR-YYYYNNNN where YYYY is year and NNNN is sequential number.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the access request in the Data Access Committee (DAC) review workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|under_review|approved|rejected|expired|withdrawn|revoked — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the access request indicating whether it is a new access request, renewal of existing access, amendment to approved access, or emergency access request.']
  requester_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the requester for communication regarding the access request.']
  requester_institution varchar [note: 'Name of the academic, research, or clinical institution with which the requester is affiliated.']
  requester_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual researcher or principal investigator submitting the access request.']
  requester_orcid varchar [note: 'ORCID persistent digital identifier for the requester, enabling unique identification across research systems.']
  research_purpose_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the research purpose for the data access request aligned with GA4GH Data Use Ontology categories.']
  review_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the Data Access Committee completed its review and reached a decision on the access request.']
  review_start_date date [note: 'Date when the Data Access Committee (DAC) formally began review of the access request.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the access request was formally submitted to the Data Access Committee (DAC) for review.']
  target_dataset_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the genomic dataset or clinical data collection being requested.']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Target number of days for Data Access Committee to complete review and reach a decision on the access request.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each formal request submitted by internal or external researchers to access controlled genomic datasets, clinical data, or restricted biobank resources. Captures request ID, requester identity, requesting institution, target dataset(s), intended use statement, IRB/ethics approval reference, data use agreement linkage, request submission date, review status (pending/approved/rejected/expired), approving authority, approval date, and access grant expiry. Supports GA4GH Data Access Committee (DAC) workflows and GDPR data subject access request (DSAR) processing.'
}

Table data.fair_assessment {
  fair_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the FAIR compliance assessment record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual or team who conducted the FAIR assessment.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: FAIR F1/F3 assessments require genomic datasets to include unambiguous genome build metadata in persistent identifiers and resource descriptions; NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy mandates genome']
  instrument_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_run. Business justification: Run outputs undergo FAIR assessments before repository submission (SRA, EGA, GEO). Enables run-level FAIR compliance tracking and NIH DMSP reporting. Required for federally-funded genomics research da']
  metadata_schema_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.metadata_schema. Business justification: FAIR assessments evaluate compliance with metadata standards and schemas. Many fair_assessments can evaluate one metadata_schema. The fair_assessment.metadata_standard_used (STRING) should be removed ']
  previous_fair_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fair_assessment (previous_fair_assessment_id)']
  target_data_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the genomic dataset or data asset being assessed for FAIR compliance.']
  accessible_a1_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for A1 principle: Data and metadata are retrievable by their identifier using a standardized communications protocol. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  accessible_a2_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for A2 principle: Metadata are accessible even when the data are no longer available. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the FAIR compliance assessment was conducted.']
  assessment_identifier varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the FAIR assessment, used for external reference and reporting.']
  assessment_methodology varchar [note: 'Standardized methodology or framework used to conduct the FAIR assessment (e.g., FAIRsFAIR, RDA FAIR Maturity Indicators).']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context captured during the FAIR assessment process.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the FAIR assessment record.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the data steward, bioinformatician, or compliance officer who performed the assessment.']
  community_standard_compliance varchar [note: 'Domain-relevant community standards the data asset complies with (e.g., GA4GH standards, MIAME for microarray, MINSEQE for sequencing).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this FAIR assessment record was first created in the system.']
  data_license varchar [note: 'License governing the reuse of the data asset (e.g., CC0, CC-BY, CC-BY-NC, custom institutional license).']
  data_repository_url varchar [note: 'URL of the public or institutional repository where the data asset is registered or deposited (e.g., NCBI SRA, ENA, dbGaP, Zenodo).']
  elixir_fair_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessed data asset meets ELIXIR FAIR data obligations for European genomic data infrastructure.']
  findable_f1_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for F1 principle: Data are assigned globally unique and persistent identifiers. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  findable_f2_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for F2 principle: Data are described with rich metadata. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  findable_f3_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for F3 principle: Metadata clearly and explicitly include the identifier of the data they describe. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  findable_f4_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for F4 principle: Metadata are registered or indexed in a searchable resource. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  identified_gaps varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of specific FAIR principle gaps identified during the assessment (e.g., missing persistent identifiers, incomplete metadata, lack of standardized vocabularies).']
  interoperable_i1_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for I1 principle: Data and metadata use a formal, accessible, shared, and broadly applicable language for knowledge representation. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  interoperable_i2_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for I2 principle: Data and metadata use vocabularies that follow FAIR principles. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  interoperable_i3_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for I3 principle: Data and metadata include qualified references to other data and metadata. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this FAIR assessment record was last updated or modified.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic FAIR compliance reassessment of the data asset.']
  nih_dmsp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessed data asset meets NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy requirements for FAIR data sharing.']
  ontology_terms_used varchar [note: 'List of ontologies or controlled vocabularies used in metadata (e.g., Gene Ontology, SNOMED CT, LOINC, Human Phenotype Ontology).']
  overall_fair_maturity_level varchar [note: 'Aggregate FAIR maturity classification based on all dimension scores, aligned with capability maturity model levels.']
  overall_fair_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite FAIR maturity score calculated as weighted or simple average of all F, A, I, R dimension scores. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  persistent_identifier_assigned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a globally unique and persistent identifier (e.g., DOI, ARK, Handle) has been assigned to the data asset.']
  persistent_identifier_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual persistent identifier value assigned to the data asset (e.g., DOI: 10.1234/example, ARK: ark:/12345/abc).']
  provenance_documented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detailed data provenance (origin, processing history, transformations) is documented for the data asset.']
  recommended_remediation_actions varchar [note: 'Actionable recommendations to address identified FAIR gaps and improve maturity scores (e.g., implement DOI assignment, enrich metadata with ontology terms, publish to public repository).']
  reusable_r1_1_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for R1.1 principle: Data and metadata are released with a clear and accessible data usage license. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  reusable_r1_2_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for R1.2 principle: Data and metadata are associated with detailed provenance. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  reusable_r1_3_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for R1.3 principle: Data and metadata meet domain-relevant community standards. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  reusable_r1_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'FAIR maturity score for R1 principle: Data and metadata are richly described with a plurality of accurate and relevant attributes. Score range 0.00 to 5.00.']
  target_asset_name varchar [note: 'Name of the genomic dataset or data asset being assessed (e.g., WGS cohort, variant database, sequencing run collection).']
  target_asset_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the data asset being assessed for FAIR compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: genomic_dataset|reference_genome|variant_database|sequencing_run|bioinformatics_pipeline|clinical_dataset|research_dataset — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record of FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) compliance assessments conducted on genomic datasets and data assets. Captures assessment ID, target data asset, assessment date, FAIR maturity scores per dimension (F1–F4, A1–A2, I1–I3, R1–R3), overall FAIR maturity level, identified gaps, recommended remediation actions, assessor identity, and assessment methodology (FAIRsFAIR, RDA FAIR Maturity Indicators). Supports NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy compliance and ELIXIR FAIR data obligations.'
}

Table data.metadata_schema {
  metadata_schema_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the metadata schema record. Primary key.']
  genome_assembly_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.genome_assembly. Business justification: Metadata schemas for genomic data assets must specify supported genome builds; coordinate systems, contig naming conventions, and sequence dictionaries differ between assemblies. GA4GH Phenopackets an']
  parent_metadata_schema_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on metadata_schema (parent_metadata_schema_id)']
  parent_schema_metadata_schema_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent or base schema from which this schema inherits or extends. Null if this is a root schema.']
  applicable_data_asset_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of data asset types to which this schema applies (e.g., sequencing runs, variant calls, clinical specimens, bioinformatics pipelines).']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this schema version was formally approved by the data governance board or schema authority.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or committee who approved this schema version for production use.']
  backward_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schema version is backward compatible with previous versions. True if data conforming to older versions remains valid.']
  change_log varchar [note: 'Summary of changes, enhancements, or fixes introduced in this schema version compared to the previous version.']
  controlled_vocabulary_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the controlled vocabulary, ontology, or code system used by this schema (e.g., SNOMED CT, LOINC, HPO, SO). Ensures semantic consistency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this metadata schema record was first created in the system.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'The date on which this schema version was deprecated and marked for retirement. Null if schema is still active.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this schema version became active and approved for use in production systems.']
  fair_compliance_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of how well this schema supports FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) principles for genomic data sharing.']
  interoperability_standard varchar [note: 'The overarching interoperability framework or standard this schema aligns with (e.g., HL7 FHIR, GA4GH, ISO 23494, CDISC). Enables cross-system data exchange.']
  is_extensible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schema allows custom extensions or additional fields beyond the core specification. True if extensible, False if strictly defined.']
  mandatory_fields varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of field names that are required by this schema. Used for validation and compliance checking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this metadata schema record was last modified or updated.']
  optional_fields varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of field names that are optional in this schema. Provides flexibility for diverse use cases.']
  regulatory_alignment varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory frameworks or compliance requirements this schema supports (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, CLIA).']
  retirement_date date [note: 'The date on which this schema version was fully retired and removed from production use. Null if not yet retired.']
  schema_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the purpose, scope, and intended use cases for this metadata schema.']
  schema_documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL pointing to the official documentation, user guide, or specification document for this schema.']
